name: =DREAMS (SINGAPORE) LIMITED
search_text:
=DREAMS (SINGAPORE) LIMITED =DREAMS Singapore is a first-of-its-kind secular, co-ed weekday residential model that provides after-school education and holistic support.

=DREAMS aims to level the playing field by giving our children equal opportunities to dream.

=DREAMS aspires to create a child-centric system of care that empowers promising and deserving children from low-income households to break out of poverty, allowing them to dream of a future where they are not limited by family circumstances. ABOUT US

WHAT: =DREAMS is a residential model that provides after-school enrichment and holistic developmental support so that each child can focus on reaching their potential for a tertiary education.

WHY: While 90% of Singapore’s households live in homes of their own, families living in public rental housing may need socio-economic support, including help with their children’s development. =DREAMS seeks to provide opportunities to the aspiring children of these families, some of whom may be 2nd and 3rd generation occupants of HDB 1-Room (33sqm) and 2-Room (45sqm) Public Rental Flats.

WHO: To qualify for the programme, the child should have secured a place in a secondary school based on his/her PSLE results.

HOW: Successful applicants will benefit from the programme for up to 6 years, from Secondary 1 to Pre-tertiary.

Children under the programme will commute to school themselves and return to our boarding facility after the school day where they will receive after-school academic and holistic support. This includes differentiated programmes designed according to the specific needs of each age band, such as coding classes, eco-citizenship cultivation lessons, leadership and critical-thinking-focused training incorporated into their schedules and offered during term breaks. We are a home-away-from-home for the children during school days, and they return to their family on weekends and school holidays.

We will partner parents and the community to encourage integrated care and foster the kampung spirit.

=DREAMS will be funded through donations and in-kind sponsorships.

WHERE
Located at 99 Haig Road, Singapore 438748, =DREAMS will house up to 80 students in the initial stage, progressively moving to have students in each of the six pre-tertiary academic levels living in the residence. The full-term target is a cohort of 240 students. The boarders will come from different constituencies in Singapore. =DREAMS is a residential model that provides after-school academic and holistic support so that each child can focus on reaching their potential for a tertiary education. =DREAMS will offer a full... name: ACTION THEATRE LIMITED
search_text:
ACTION THEATRE LIMITED ACTION Theatre’s Objectives are as follows:

1. CHAMPION ORIGINALITY: Create and showcase original, homegrown Singaporean stage plays and musicals, enriching the cultural tapestry with fresh, local, regional, and international narratives.

2. CULTIVATE LOCAL TALENTS: Foster a dynamic and skilled talent pool of theatre professionals—including playwrights, directors, designers, and production crew—while nurturing their growth and excellence.

3. BRIDGE CULTURES: Adapt international works of artistic merit with themes that resonate with Singapore’s unique cultural landscape, connecting global artistry with local relevance.

4. FOSTER ARTS APPRECIATION & CREATIVITY: Ignite a passion for the arts among Singaporeans, fostering a deeper cultural appreciation and stimulating the growth of creativity with lifelong engagement in the arts.

GLOBAL RECOGNITION: Elevate Singapore's finest homegrown plays, musicals, and talents to the world stage, showcasing our artistic excellence internationally. Our vision and mission is to nurture and develop local talents to create world-class stage plays and musicals, contributing to Singapore’s growth as a global city of the arts. We aim to enhance the quality of life for Singaporeans through the appreciation of theatrical works that engage and inspire diverse audiences across all generations. Theatre & Dramatic Arts

name: AGAM LTD.
search_text:
AGAM LTD. AGAM Ltd. or AGAM Theatre Lab as it is more commonly known was started on 30th January 2019 by Subramanian Ganesh, a passionate theatre maker in collaboration of a collective of enthusiasts, AGAM intends to create professional plays with highest quality for our audience here and now.

​Being the newest theatre company of 4G Singapore, it's mission is to stage entertaining, innovative and relevant theatre experiences that stimulate and inspire our community to think critically about the world around them, and to engage in constructive and Creative self-expression.

​Even though the company is new, we are not new to the theatre scene in Singapore, having previously worked with several other theatre companies in previous productions - including commissioned works by the Esplanade Theatres by the Bay. Each and every one of the artiste have contributed to the arts in form or another and their experience spans from 5 to 20 years. With the vast experience of the veterans of intercultural theatre together with the creative and vibrant young theatre enthusiast, this is unification of creative minds in Singapore theatre scene.

​Our artistes come from all sorts of different backgrounds, we have full time practitioners, teachers, accountants, scientists, army men, film producers and everything in between. We also get a large number of 'resting' professional actors who enjoy working with a high quality theatre company that allows them to experiment and try out new things in a less pressured environment than the professional world.

We don't only play the classics. AGAM Theatre Lab actively supports new writing and aiming to stage many plays by relatively unknown writers and bring them to light. We also provide a showcase for our own members' writing to be regularly performed and intending to publish it in both hard and soft forms.

We envision providing a platform for youth and adults to curate, market and stage unique intercultural theatre content. Vision

AGAM ORIGINALS

Fuel the future of new writing by producing and presenting the best new plays, that challenge and change audiences here and now.

AGAM EXPLORATION

Explore relevantly or connection to our roots.

AGAM LABORATORY

Nurture, train and inspire artists, leaders and young people through artiste development programmes

Enable theatre makers to engage creatively with communities and audiences, altering the perception of what theatre can be.

AGAM SERVICES

We, share our knowledge with other organisations and individual artiste who needs assistance in theatre making.

Mission

AGAM seeks to create entertaining, innovative and relevant theatre experiences that stimulate and inspire our community to think critically about the world around them, and to engage in constructive and creative self-expression. name: AHMADIYYA MUSLIM MISSION
search_text:
AHMADIYYA MUSLIM MISSION OBJECTIVE The objectives of the Association are:
(a) The propagation of Islam as interpreted in Ahmadiyyat, i.e. Islam as expounded by the Founder of the Ahmadiyya Movement and his successors, and generally to promote the comparative study of religion.
(b) The advancement of education among the members of the Association and such other persons as the Managing Committee of the Association may from time to time determine;
(c) The devising of ways and means for the members of the Association and others;
(d) The doing of all such other acts incidental to the purposes aforesaid in order to further the objects of the Association as a social, educational. cultural, moral, charitable and spiritual institution, such as:
i) The establishment of libraries for the achievement of the aims and objects of the Association;
ii) The devising of ways and means for the members of the Association and others;
iii) The establishment of branches in Singapore.
iv) The provision of funds as far as possible by way of loans or otherwise, for enabling deserving persons to become more useful members of the Association;
v) The acquisition of movable and immovable properties or rights therein or rights appurtenant thereto which may be necessary or ancillary to its purposes by way of purchase or gift. The propagation of Islam as interprete in Ahmadiyyat. Islam

name: ARANYA SANGHA DANA FELLOWSHIP LIMITED
search_text:
ARANYA SANGHA DANA FELLOWSHIP LIMITED The object for which the Company is established are:
(a) For the advancement of Buddhism through empowering of monastics, regardless of affiliation to different Buddhist traditions;
(b) To enhance the well-being and quality of life, particularly for aging monastics, by addressing the challenges of deteriorating mental and physical conditions through professional social work case management;
(c) To improve quality of life to ageing monastics with life-limiting illnesses;
(d) To bring the Buddhist community together to care and support the ageing monastics;
(e) To alleviate the suffering caused by aging and illness among monastics and provide support in alignment with the propagation of Buddhism, while retaining the discretion to employ diverse means, including addressing poverty and distress, as well as offering financial assistance;
(f) To promote mental well-being and healthy living among monastics, adhering to the Buddhist Vinaya (code of conduct), and providing support for mental health issues; and
(g) To groom and nurture the young monastic community, fostering their spiritual growth, education, and well-being, with the aim of ensuring the continuous propagation of the Dhamma through the teachings and practices of the next generation of Buddhist monks and nuns. The vision of the company is for Monastics in Singapore to age well with the support of the Buddhist community.

The mission of the company is to consolidate the lay Buddhist community’s resources to support Monastics in the fulfilment of their aspirations to practice the Buddha Dhamma, so that they may age in place with dignity. Buddhism

OUR VISION: For every child who comes, to find joy again.

search_text:
ART OUTREACH SINGAPORE LIMITED Established in 2003, Art Outreach is a non-profit arts intermediary with IPC status, dedicated to promoting art appreciation in Singapore and strengthening networks within the local art ecosystem.

Our mission is to increase visual art literacy and foster a deeper understanding and engagement with the visual arts through:

Delivering art education programmes for primary and secondary school students
Organising art events, exhibitions, and community presentations

Conducting public art tours to enhance accessibility to the arts

In addition, we support practitioners in sustaining careers in the arts by providing financial assistance, professional development opportunities, and an independent space to nurture and incubate their work. We also facilitate meaningful dialogues between artists, collectors, and industry leaders to strengthen the broader arts ecosystem.

Through these initiatives, we aim to promote, foster, improve, develop, and inculcate an enduring interest in the visual arts within Singapore’s schools and communities. Our vision is A Singapore where visual arts are an integral part of everyday life, fostering creativity, dialogue, and cultural understanding across communities.

Our mission is to champion visual art literacy and appreciation by providing accessible education programmes, engaging exhibitions, and professional development opportunities. Through fostering strong networks between artists, collectors, and industry leaders, we support practitioners in sustaining meaningful careers while enriching Singapore’s art ecosystem. name: ARTSEQUATOR LTD.
search_text:
ARTSEQUATOR LTD. We provide multiple valuable, unique services that are vital to our three stakeholders and to the overall health of the arts ecosystem:

● ArtsEquator advocates for arts writers, critics and arts media to be acknowledged and supported within the wider arts ecosystem.
We provide multiple valuable, unique services that are vital to our three stakeholders and to the overall health of the arts ecosystem:
● ArtsEquator advocates for arts writers, critics and arts media to be acknowledged and supported within the wider arts ecosystem.
● ArtsEquator develops arts writing and criticism capabilities in arts writers through its editorial process, mentorship programmes and other activities.
● ArtsEquator advocates for the artists, the creative process and the creative product through our online and offline activities.
● ArtsEquator’s in-depth critical content (articles, podcasts, essays, panels) offer a vital feedback loop to artists.
● ArtsEquator creates new audiences by promoting the arts through our web and social media platforms.
● ArtsEquator supports the creation of discerning arts audiences who have the critical capabilities and competencies to engage with the creative product.
● ArtsEquator.com is a living archive that documents arts practice via our articles and other content.
● ArtsEquator promotes intra- and inter-exchange of information between Southeast Asian artists and audiences by developing knowledge and networks about each other.
● As a digital arts media organisation, ArtsEquator uses the power of digital technology for capability development, research and archive.

ArtsEquator Ltd. is an arts media company that values and promotes Southeast Asian regional art practice.
It is committed to developing arts writing and criticism which identify Southeast Asian creative practices as part of, rather than an adjunct to, global contemporary arts. Its principal stakeholders are arts makers, arts critics and arts audiences in Southeast Asia.

name: ASIAN FILM ARCHIVE
search_text: ASIAN FILM ARCHIVE The Asian Film Archive preserves the rich film heritage of Singapore and Asian Cinema, to encourage scholarly research on film, and to promote a wider critical appreciation of this art form.  As an important nexus, it brings together the various segments of the Asian film community in order to open and enrich new intellectual, educational and creative spaces.  To nurture the Asian film community, the Asian Film Archive aspires to be much more than just a repository of film and tape. It is to be a hub for the film community, contributing to culture, scholarship and industry. Our mission is to save, explore and share the art of Asian Cinema. Internationally renowned film archives not only house the gems of the cinematic world, they are also vibrant hubs of activity. Supporting the local film community is an emphasis at the Asian Film Archive. In line with its mission, the Asian Film Archive:	 1. Preserves film prints, digital masters and related materials in a permanent collection focusing on the culturally important works of independent Asian filmmakers.	 2. Promotes appreciation and build cultural value of Asian films through research, education and publication.	 3. Provides access to comprehensive Asian film resources in order to provide sources of inspiration for creative processes, teaching, and research.	4. Nurtures a film community with a passion for Asian cinema and encourage new audiences to fall in love with Asian films through a carefully curated programme of screenings and discussions.	 5. Facilitates the marketing and distribution of Asian films, merchandise and collectibles especially for independent filmmakers. The Asian Film Archive (AFA) is a non-profit organisation founded in 2005 to save, explore and share the art of Asian Cinema. We aspire to be a hub for the Asian film community, through organising ... Historical & Cultural Conservation Arts, culture and heritage Community development Education

name: AWWA LTD.
search_text:
AWWA LTD. The objects of the Company are to empower the disadvantaged to maximize their potential to lead dignified and independent lives. The objects include, but are not limited to, the following:

5(a) to empower and help those who:

(i) are disabled or have special needs;

(ii) are aged and infirm;

(iii) are poor, needy or suffer hardship ; and/or

(iv) are victims of calamities or disasters in Singapore such as accidents, fires, explosives, collapse of houses, floods and earth tremors; and

5(b) to promote positive values such as compassion, humanity and respect for others through its programs and services.

The Company has the power to do all such things as may be permitted under the law and as may be necessary, incidental or conducive to the attainment of all or any of its objects, including but not limited to the following:

6(a) provide services in the areas of elderly care, disability care, nursing, education (including schools), developmental support, caregiver support, youth and family support and to engage in research, dissemination and management of knowledge in any related area;

6(b) to engage in social enterprises which are consistent with its mission and objects;

6(c) help, co-operate and work in close liaison with all other established organizations in the Republic of Singapore;

6(d) purchase land, erect or alter office or other buildings and/or lease premises to carry out the objects of the Company and have power to give security deposits and guarantees and raise and/or borrow money for that purpose on the security of the assets of the Company;

6(e) establish building and other funds to carry out the objects of the Company;

6(f) raise funds in support of the Company’s objects;

6(g) sell, lease, manage, mortgage, improve, develop, dispose of, turn to account or otherwise deal with all or any part of the property or interests of the Company;

6(h) invest the Company’s funds in accordance with investment policies approved by the Board;

6(i) provide financial assistance to persons in need by way of grants, loans or otherwise in accordance with a financial assistance policy approved by the Board;

6(j) subscribe to, be a member or shareholder of any corporation or set up any corporation that has objects similar to any of the objects of the Company;

6(k) set up branches in Singapore and form affiliations or amalgamate with any corporation or acquire all or part of the operations of any corporation that has objects similar to any of the objects of the Company;

6(l) make donations to any corporation or cause that has objects similar to any of the objects of the Company;

6(m) take and accept any donation or gift, to be used for any one or more of the objects of the Company; and

6(n) not make any loans to any person except with the approval of the Board.

Save as is otherwise expressly provided, the objects specified in each of the paragraphs in this Constitution shall be regarded as independent objects, and accordingly, shall be in no way limited or restricted (except when otherwise expressed in such paragraph) by reference to the objects indicated in any other paragraph or the name of the Company.

All references to corporations and corporation shall be deemed to include any corporation, partnership, association, club, or other body of persons whether incorporated or not and wherever incorporated or domiciled and whether now existing or hereafter to be formed.

name: Academy of Chinese Medicine, Singapore
search_text:
Academy of Chinese Medicine, Singapore The objectives of the Academy are:
(1) To promote and safeguard the highest standards of professional practice in TCM and a high code of ethical conduct amongst its members.
(2) To sustain and foster TCM postgraduate education.
(3) To encourage, reward and propel TCM researches.
(4) To foster science-related disciplines so as to boost closer exchange between Traditional Chinese and Western Medicine Practitioners.
(5) To provide a link between local and foreign TCM organizations so as to launch international medical exchanges and co-operation in education, training and research.
Vision:
To be the leading academic organization of Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM), with the aim of becoming one of the highest authorities on TCM in the world.

Mission:
⑴ To promote and disseminate TCM education and knowledge
⑵ To enhance the skills of TCM clinical practice so as to safeguard the health of patients
⑶ To encourage TCM research and to initiate evidence-based TCM to enhance the efficacy of TCM practice, and
⑷ To facilitate collaboration between TCM and Western Medicine (WM).
(5) To grant specialist certificates on TCM to persons who have fulfilled the criteria laid down.
(6) To protect the interests and dignity of the Academy of Chinese Medicine, Singapore

Vision : To teach and preach the whole truth of God as revealed in His written Word, the Bible.


Mission

1. establish and maintain a place for the celebration of the Almighty God and provide Christian fellowship for those of like faith.
2. gather into the fellowship of believers those who respond in faith to the call of the Gospel and to nurture them in the faith through that fellowship.

3. edify the lives of Christians through the teaching of God’s Word and reach-out to the non believers by whatever possible means, including but not limited to organising and conducting life enrichment courses, for all ages, provision of counselling and care to parents and their teenage children or to provide assistance in whatever that is most needed by those families.

6. provide, grant or participate in granting relief to the poor, sick and needy.

7. promote or participate in promoting the welfare of mankind on Christian charitable and benevolent principles.

8. purchase, lease or otherwise, acquire lands, buildings or property, properties and any other property or interest in the same, for the purpose of the Centre.

9. receive any gifts or money or property for any of the purposes and objects herein stated whether subject to any special trust or not but so that the Session may nevertheless decline to accept any gifts or donations or to take over any property which has annexed to it any condition or obligations not approved by the Session.

10. deal in cinematography, films, records, cassettes tapes, video tapes, laser-disc and all other apparatus for recording events by means of sight and sound and produce exhibit or distribute any show by means of films records audio-visual, video recordings or such other methods.

11. undertake and execute any trusts created to provide funds or income for the use and benefit of the Centre in the furtherance of its objects or any of them.

search_text:
Aoxiang Counselling Service The objective is to provide counselling service for individuals and families in areas such as marital, familial and parent-child relationships, individual development, mental/psychological behavioral wellbeing, etc..

The Core Values of the charity are embraced under the acronym “SOAR”:
(a) Strength-based: Emphasizing and building upon the inherent strengths, resilience, and capabilities of each individual, empowering them to overcome obstacles and thrive in their journey towards healing and growth.

(b) Open-hearted: Cultivating a compassionate and empathetic approach, welcoming clients with open hearts and minds, and providing a safe space for them to explore their emotions and struggles.

(c) Authenticity: Fostering genuine and transparent interactions, encouraging clients and counselors alike to be true to themselves and their experiences, promoting honesty and vulnerability in the counseling relationship.

(d) Restoration: Working towards the restoration of individuals and relationships through healing, reconciliation, and wholeness.

The Society shall take into consideration the Environment, Social and Governance (ESG) factors when conducting the charity’s activities.
The Vision is to be socially-conscious and responsible counselling center to serve our community at large through counselling as our clients grow.

The Mission of the charity is to bring hope and healing to the community through professional counseling and care rooted in positive guiding principles.

name: Apex Harmony Lodge
search_text:
Apex Harmony Lodge Our objects are:

a. To provide residential, day care and respite facilities for patients suffering from dementia;
b. To provide medical, nursing, social, psychological, occupational and recreational activities for patients;
c. To provide psycho-social and educational support to family members and care-givers of the patients.

The core business of Apex Harmony Lodge is to provide the best of care for persons living with dementia as articulated in the mission statement. Guided by the vision, mission, values and its operating philosophy, person-centred care (PCC) delivered through Person-Environment-Occupation (PEO) model underpins the practices at Apex Harmony Lodge. PCC focuses on overall well-being, reflecting the elders’ remaining strengths and abilities. The PEO model articulates that ability to carry out any function is the result of the dynamic relationship between the person, his/her environment and his/her activities. Hence, increasingly the emphasis is on normalization and not institutionalisation.

With greater awareness and deeper knowledge, increasingly the key programmes at Apex Harmony Lodge are driven by uniqueness of residents and how best the residents could be a gift in building a gracious community to embrace people with dementia. Programmes are designed to benefit the residents as well as people whom the residents come in contact with. Eventually we envision creating a village in which people with dementia are integrated and respected as who they are and live quality life.
Apex Harmony Lodge's vision & mission

VISION : To be a living model of a home committed to lives affected by dementia.

MISSION : To provide the best of care for persons living with dementia.

Our mission is underpinned by our operating philosophy of unlocking the potential in every resident, staff and volunteer beyond dementia care, and built on our core values of respect, integrity, perseverance, passion, devotion and excellent care. Apex Harmony Lodge (AHL) is a community committed to empowering lives affected by dementia to continue to live well. We strive to do this through the creation of an ecosystem in which Persons with... name: Assemblies of God of Singapore, The
search_text:
Assemblies of God of Singapore, The 3. Nature

3.1 As a people called by God, who shall constitute part of the Body of Christ, built and established upon the foundation of the Apostles and Prophets, Jesus Christ Himself being the chief cornerstone and

3.2 Because members of the Body, the Church of Jesus Christ are enjoined to assemble themselves together for worship, fellowship, counsel and instruction in the Word of God and the work of the ministry, and for the exercise of those spiritual gifts and offices provided for in New Testament church order;

3.3 It is resolved that the Society is a Pentecostal denomination comprised of churches, credential ministers, and associated agencies who voluntarily enter into its membership based on mutual agreements, shared objectives and beliefs as stated in our Tenets of Faith for the purpose of:

a) Encouraging and promoting the spread of the Full Gospel in Singapore and beyond;
b) Providing a basis of fellowship among Christian ministers of like precious faith;
c) Establishing and maintaining Bible Schools and other educational institutions;

d) Promoting medical and other humanitarian institutions and enterprises; and
e) Promoting the distribution of Bibles and other fundamental Christian Literature.

3.4 To recognise and promote scriptural methods and order of worship, unity, fellowship, work, and business for God and to disapprove unscriptural methods, doctrines and conduct.

3.5 To present a unified front that represents Pentecostal believers so there can be a united voice whenever and wherever needed and act in harmony in all matters of national scope which affect our religious life or violate our Tenets of Faith.

3.6 To gather for annual General Council meetings or such Meetings as required to formulate and state broad policies which will guide the Society and other related organisations of the Society.

3.7 To guard the Pentecostal testimony and promote establishment of self-propagating and self-supporting local assemblies of Pentecostal believers in Singapore.

3.8 To provide and conduct religious services of the Christian faith and public worship services.

3.9 To have the right to receive or remove members and to mediate or to arbitrate and to resolve issues involving the members as necessary.

3.10 To grant credentials, to those who have met the requirements as set forth in the Constitution and By-Laws of the Society.

3.11 To have the right to own, hold in trust, use, sell, convey, mortgage, lease or otherwise dispose of, such property real or chattel, as may be necessary for the propagation of its work.

3.12 To have the right to establish and develop churches, departments and institutions as may be deemed necessary in order to fulfil the objectives of the Society.
Mission Statement (Why We Exist)
The AG(S) is committed to establish a Pentecostal fellowship, for the development of progressive local churches, ministries and ministers, to fulfil the Great Commission.

Pentecostal Fellowship
The word ’Pentecostal’ emphasises…
1. our unique distinctive in the baptism of the Holy Spirit, His Work, and the Spirit-filled experiences in our lives and ministries.

2. our intent to uphold an promote Pentecostal distinctive and heritage.
The term ‘Fellowship’ depicts the attributes of koinonia relationship which encompass unity, partnership, communication, networking and sharing of resources among local Pentecostal churches and ministers.

Development
As Christ has given ministry gifts to local churches and ministers, Ephesians 4:11-16, the AG(S) recognises the need to educate, equip and raise competent local church pastors and ministers.

Progressive Local Churches and Ministers
This describes the continuous growth and development of vibrant and credible cutting-edge churches and ministers who have clear visions and strategies which are both current and relevant.

Fulfil the Great Commission
The bottom-line for the Pentecostal empowerment and experience is to carry out the call of our Lord Jesus Christ to “Go and make disciples”, Matthew 28:20, with greater effectiveness and beyond the local churches. Thus, the AG(S) is determined to unite local Pentecostal churches and ministers to impact Singapore and the world with the Gospel of the Lord Jesus Christ without compromising its means.




Core Value Statements (What Holds Us Together)
The following areas present the core values of the AG(S). We desire that they define and drive this fellowship in an atmosphere of faith, grace, hope and love.

1. Tenets of Faith of the Assemblies of God
We acknowledge that these tenets shared by Assemblies of God all over the world distinguish us as uniquely Pentecostals. We will uphold these tenets in our own lives and the life of our church.

2. Prayer
We acknowledge prayer as a privilege granted to us through the work of Jesus. We treasure this privilege and rely on God completely in conception, planning and execution of all ministries and activities in our lives and fellowship through private and corporate prayers.
3 Unity
We acknowledge that God is three persons in one and that He has demonstrated to us the truth of diversity in unity. We seek to work with all our brothers and sisters in what He has called us to in perfect unity.
4. Excellence in Ministry
We acknowledge that God deserves the best from us. He endows us with gifts and talents to do and equip others for the work of services. Therefore we value the importance of training and developing of ministers to excel in their respective calling to service.
Christianity

Mission statement: The Association for Breastfeeding Advocacy (Singapore) is committed to the well-being of both mothers and babies and will do its utmost to promote, protect and support breastfeeding and ensure that babies get the best start in life. search_text:
BEAUTIFUL PEOPLE SG LTD. (a) To catalyse and build a volunteer movement for personal transformation and social change, through building relationships between members of the society with those on the margins.

(b) To build mentoring relationships that enable and empower mentee beneficiaries to discover their fullest potential, and thrive even as they make important life transitions.

(c) To contribute to the less fortunate by providing opportunities for volunteers and mentee beneficiaries to do community service together, either locally or overseas, as a way of paying it forward.

(d) To support volunteers and mentee beneficiaries in achieving their dreams through ‘seed’ funding.

(e) To provide financial assistance or interest free loan to mentee beneficiaries who are in need of financial support when starting out in life, building a career or family.

(f) To provide an effective support system for volunteers by: i) developing hard skills through training and continuous development programmes and ii) nurturing a community of supportive relationships. The aim is to inculcate a sense of empowerment and belonging among the volunteers and mentee beneficiaries by cultivating a spirit of love, respect for self and others, gratitude and contribution so they can live purposeful, productive and dignified lives.

(g) To collaborate and partner with mission-aligned companies and other social welfare organisations in building volunteering communities in support of our mentee beneficiaries, which may include sharing our expertise with other social welfare organisations through consultancy work.

To be a platform for building mentoring relationships that change

name: BGR INTERNATIONAL
search_text: BGR INTERNATIONAL BGR International is a non-profit, non-government, global relief and development organization.  Our goal is to help connect people in need around the world with people who care.  BGR International does this by offering effective and efficient response to acute disaster events around the world as well as offering efficient and effective solutions to the long term problems of poverty, hunger and suffering. The primary services of BGR International include mobilization of financial, physical, personal and spiritual resources from around the world to the place where they are most effective in helping those in need. This can include but is not limited to food, water, health care items, clothing, shelter, etc.  We will promote the physical, mental and spiritual welfare of persons suffering from poverty, infirmity, ill-health, disease or accidents, with due respect to be given disregarding their nationality, race, colour, language, culture or religion. PURPOSE    BGR International wants to connect people in need with people who care.	  VISION    To see people's lives filled with hope and peace, inspiring them to raise their families in confidence, build their communities with dignity and share this life with others. name: BHASKAR'S ARTS ACADEMY LTD
search_text:
BHASKAR'S ARTS ACADEMY LTD Objective:
1. To inculcate, generate, promote, foster and develop an interest in the performance and appreciation of classical Indian music, orchestra, dance and other Indian fine arts in Singapore, and to serve as a platform for talented musicians and dancers.

2. To develop the next generation of Singaporean traditional Indian musicians, composers, dancers and choreographers.

Bhaskars Arts Academy (BAA) is a Singapore-based Indian classical dance, music and theatre company and one of the major institutions in the Indian performing arts scene of Singapore. As a 72-year-old organisation, BAA is a strong and highly regarded brand in the arts sector. For many years the founders, the late Mr & Mrs Bhaskar, have been recognised as significant contributors to the vibrant art scene of Singapore. Bhaskar's Arts Academy has the long-term trust of Singapore's National Arts Council as one of the Major Company grant recipients.

While Bhaskar's Arts Academy is committed to staying true to the traditional techniques and philosophy, the academy also gives credence to the innovative spirit of Singapore through cross-cultural explorations. Our founders were daring trail blazers in the field of cross- cultural collaborations.

Our artistic team is led by:
Artistic Director, Ms. Meenakshy Bhaskar, has performed in several BAA productions. She has performed for dignitaries from all over the world, including Her Royal Highness Queen Elizabeth and received the Singapore Young Artiste Award.

Music Director, Dr. Ghanavenothan Retnam, a wizard on the flute and a much sought-after musician locally as well as abroad. His recordings have been heard on the National Geographic, Animal Planet and the Discovery Travel channels. His stirring and emotive performances for dignitaries and royalty led him to record for a Walt Disney feature film. He is one of the two Cultural Medallion awardees in 2024, and is the first Indian musician to receive the award.
Vision Statement:
Touching lives, by championing Indian performing arts on stage, and beyond.

Mission Statement:
To perform traditional and classic, contemporary dance, music and theatre with artistic excellence to domestic and international audiences; To provide a unique opportunity to advance the professional capabilities of gifted, young Singaporean performers. Artistic Vision: Preservation and performance of traditional forms of Indian dance, music and theatre; Development of Singaporean talents especially in choreography; Produce new repertoire to appeal to wider audiences; Cultivate audience appreciation of the arts increasingly; Collaboration work with suitable synergistic partners to create new perspectives and opportunities; To be the flagship Indian performing arts group for Singapore. Started in 1952 by the late Mr KP Bhaskar, a doyen of Indian traditional arts in Singapore, we are the oldest and premier Indian performing arts group in Singapore. We aim to create, produce, pre... Traditional Ethnic Performing Arts Arts, culture and heritage Community development

name: BOP HUB LTD.
search_text:
BOP HUB LTD. BOP Hub was established in 2011 with a vision to design business to end poverty. A Singapore-based business accelerator platform, we catalyze and strengthen cross-sector collaboration and participation in social entrepreneurship. To that end, BOP Hub serves as a gateway for business ventures, technologists, and manufacturers to access base-of-the-pyramid (BOP) markets. We work to bundle and weave products with distribution, logistics, economies of scale, public policy, design, and technology in order to complete the supply chain and more effectively deliver much needed goods and services to consumers at the base of the pyramid.

BoP Hub’s strategy is to aggregate and scale up the best models across sectors and industries, negotiating collaborative interwoven approaches and cutting wastage in the supply chain. With this approach, BoP Hub seeks to optimize efficiency and extract hidden value by reducing costs and offering better solutions. BoP Hub’s value proposition is to make products and services for BoP markets “cheaper, faster, easier and better,” improving lives and empowering 4 billion consumers, producers, employees, and entrepreneurs at the base of the pyramid. Our vision it to provide everyone opportunities to sustainable livelihood with dignity. We achieve this by forging an Ecosystem of Abundance through system innovation at the base-of-the-pyramid, by supporting entrepreneurship, connecting partners and sharing resources. BoP HUB serves as a gateway between businesses and BoP* markets. We work together with technologists, manufacturers and other non-profit organisations to design products integrated with distributio... name: BRIGHTON COMMUNITY CHURCH
search_text: BRIGHTON COMMUNITY CHURCH a.	To glorify and worship Almighty God;  b.	To preach and teach the gospel of Jesus Christ;  c.	To create an environment conducive for the edification of Christians;  d.	To challenge and equip Christians to fulfill their calling in life and ministry;  e.	To propagate the Christian faith in Singapore;	f.	To do good works to all mankind regardless of their race or religion; and  g.	To do such all things as are necessary or consistent with the achievement of the above objects subject to the approval of the relevant authorities. a.	We believe that the Bible, comprising the Old and New Testaments in their original languages, is the inspired revelation of Almighty God for the salvation of mankind, and is the infallible, inerrant and authoritative guide to all matters of life, faith and practice.    b.	We believe there is one and only one infinite, eternal and perfect Almighty God, the Creator of all things, and that he exists as three Persons: the Father, the Son and the Holy Spirit.    c.	We believe that Jesus Christ is the son of God who took on human bodily form without losing his deity, was born of a virgin, lived a sinless life while on earth, suffered, bled and died as the atoning sacrifice for the sins of the world, resurrected bodily from the dead, ascended into heaven, who shall one day return in power and glory to establish his permanent reign.    d.	We believe that the Holy Spirit is God and He convicts, regenerates, indwells, seals, teaches, guides, comforts, unites, fills, gives spiritual gifts and empowers believers for godly living and effective witness in the world.  e.	We believe that mankind, directly created by God in his image and likeness but alienated from God by sin, stands totally depraved, spiritually dead, eternally condemned and liable to everlasting punishment, unless justified by faith through Jesus Christ and regenerated by the Holy Spirit.    f.	We believe that salvation, whereby a person is forgiven of his sins, declared righteous, given eternal life and assured of bodily resurrection from the dead to an everlasting state of glory and blessedness, is a free and gracious gift of Almighty God bestowed on those who personally repent of their sin, place their faith and trust in Jesus Christ alone, believing that His shed his blood and died vicariously in payment of their sins and receiving him as their Lord.	g.	We believe the local church is a visible community of regenerated believers without limitations as to race, nationality or socio-cultural backgrounds, whose head is Jesus Christ, whose Guide is the Holy Spirit, whose authority for doctrine and practice is the Bible and whose ultimate corporate mission is to glorify God through worship and advancement of the gospel of Jesus Christ in all the world. name: BUSINESS CHINA
search_text:
BUSINESS CHINA The long-term target of Business China is to groom around 20,000 to 30,000 bilingual and bi-cultural Singaporeans who can connect with China and communicate effectively with their Chinese counterparts. To achieve its objectives, Business China will recruit successful, capable, experienced and talented individuals to form a firm foundation and create an effective network. Business China is also keen on involving individuals with special skills and resources to support its organizational framework. Members of the public are welcomed to participate in activities organised by Business China. In the future, Business China will not only pave the way for Singaporeans to establish and expand the economic opportunities in China, but it will also strive to make Singapore a strategic partner of China. Eventually the aim is to establish a mutually beneficial networking platform for both countries in the fields of culture, education and business. Vision
A core pool of Singaporean bilingual and bicultural talents, and enterprises who can engage deeply in economic opportunities with China.

Mission
We develop premier bilingual business programmes and networks, to generate interest in and strengthen understanding of the latest developments in China. Historical & Cultural Conservation

name: Bethany Church (Singapore)
search_text:
Bethany Church (Singapore) a) To worship and serve God; the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit; the one true God in three persons;
b) To spread the Gospel of the Kingdom of God and of Jesus Christ by word and by deeds;
c) To minister to and nurture the members towards spiritual maturity and effective discipleship in Jesus Christ;
VISION:
Raising up disciples of JESUS CHRIST to reach out, and Impact generations, communities, and nations with Kingdom values.

MISSION:
1. Evangelism & Mission
a). Evangelism: To create the urgency of the great commission by equipping & sending members to share and preach the Gospel of Christ to every person (Mark 16:15 and Mat 28:18-19).
b). Mission (apostolic): To mobilize members with compassionate hearts to reach generations, communities, and nations (Luk 4:18-19 and Mat 25:35-36).

2. Pastoral
To care and nurture church members to grow to be mature and fruitful in Christ (Jeremiah 3:15; John 21:15-17).

3. Teaching
a). To reach and teach the community to discover Jesus (Mark 4:1).
b). To teach, equip and empower every member with a strong Biblical foundation for the Great Commission (2 Timothy 3:16, Ephesians 3:14-19, Mat 28:20).

4. Prophetic
a). To lead and empower the congregation to encounter God through Word, worship, and prayer life (Ephesians 4:11-13, John 4:24, Matthew 26:41)
b). To train and mentor members to find their calling and develop their prophetic gifts (1 Corinthians 12:1-2, 1 Corinthians 14:2, 2 Kings 2:10).

5. Apostolic
a). To grow the church as an apostolic capacity church, through Equipping, Empowering, and Sending Missionaries.
b). To plant churches as a House of prayer for all nations, and to Impact Nations.

6. Operational & Support
a). Main Mission: To establish an effective and efficient church management system (Ephesians 4:21)
b). Sub Mission :
- To provide excellent services to support five-fold ministries.
- To establish SOP for effective and efficient ministries.
- To maximize media and technology platforms for church ministries and their vision. Christianity

name: Bethany Emmanuel Church
search_text:
Bethany Emmanuel Church The object of this Church shall be to bring honour and glory to God (I Corinthians 10:31, Ephesians 3:20-21) by:
A) Worshipping God in spirit and in truth (John 4:24).
B) Spreading the Good News about Jesus Christ (Acts 1:8, II Corinthians 5:18-20, I Peter 3:15).
C) Making disciples by leading others to personal faith in Christ and by drawing them into responsible involvement in the local church and by teaching them to obey all that He commanded (Matthew 28:19-20).
D) Encouraging believers to act according to God's will, to let Christ live in them, and to be controlled by the Holy Spirit (Romans 12:1-2, Galatians 2:20, Ephesians 5:15-18).
E) Building up the church spiritually and numerically through the ministry of each individual member (Ephesians 4:11-16, Hebrews 10:24-25).
F) Performing acts of Christian service and love (Matthew 25:34-40, James 2:14-16). Building that Church with walls and without walls by fulfilling the Twin Great Commandments & Great Commission!
Empowerment of the Holy Spirit, through obedience to the Holy Bible and fervent prayers and praise!
Cohesively serving and anticipating the Return of the glorified Christ! Christianity

The establishment and extension of Christian missions and institutions for the furtherance of God's Kingdom on earth;  6 The promotion of Christian relief;  7 The establishment and operation of child care centers and kindergartens; and  8 The doing of all such other things as are incidental or conducive to the attainment of the above objects or any of them, or which may be conveniently carried on and done in connection therewith. 1. The belief in the divine, verbal and plenary inspiration of the Scriptures in the original languages, their consequent inerrancy and infallibility, and, as the Word of God, the Supreme and final authority in faith and life;  2	The belief in one God existing in three co-equal and co-eternal Persons, Father, Son and Holy Spirit;  3	The belief that Jesus Christ, the eternal Son of God, was conceived by the Holy Spirit, born of the virgin Mary, and is true God and true man;	4.	The belief that man was created in the image of God, but sinned through the fall of Adam, thereby incurring not only physical death but also spiritual death, which is separation from God and that all human beings are born with a sinful nature and become sinners in thought, word and deed;  5	The belief that the Lord Jesus Christ died a propitiatory and expiatory death as a representative and substitutionary sacrifice, and that all who repent of their sins and believe in Him are justified before God on the grounds of His shed blood;  6	The belief in the bodily resurrection of our Lord Jesus Christ, in His ascension into Heaven, and in His exaltation at the right hand of God, where He intercedes for us as our High Priest and Advocate;  7	The belief in the personal, visible and premillennial return of our Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ to judge this world and bring peace to the nations;  8	The belief that salvation is by grace through faith, not by works, and that all who repent and receive the Lord Jesus Christ as their personal Saviour are born again by the Holy Spirit and thereby become the children of God;  9	The belief that the ministry of the Holy Spirit is to glorify the Lord Jesus Christ and to convict and regenerate the sinner, indwell, guide, instruct and empower the believer for godly living and service;  10	The belief that Christ instituted the Sacrament of Baptism for believers and their children and the Sacrament of the Lord's Supper, which sacraments shall be observed by His Church till He comes;  11	The belief in the eternal security, bodily resurrection, and eternal blessedness of the saved and in the bodily resurrection and eternal conscious punishment of the lost;  12	The belief in the real, spiritual unity in Christ of all redeemed by His precious blood and the necessity of faithfully maintaining the purity of the Church in doctrine and life according to the Word of God and the principle and practice of biblical separation. name: Beyond Social Services
search_text:
Beyond Social Services The objectives of the Society are

To enhance the ability of young people, families, and communities from low-income backgrounds to live purposeful, satisfying, and hopeful lives despite their poverty-related challenges; and

To present social issues as opportunities for cooperation between the public sector, civil society, and community toward an inclusive and cohesive Singapore society and the common good.
Our Vision
We envision a Singapore where low income individuals, families and communities can marshal goodwill across society to live purposeful, satisfying, and hopeful lives. An inclusive Singapore that strives to remove barriers impeding the dignity, progress, and integration of the poor.

Our Mission
We exist to alleviate poverty and help better the lives of vulnerable families and individuals in Singapore.
We’re here to help build as inclusive a society as possible.
We do this with initiatives that help stabilise families, enable economic wellbeing, promote good health, facilitate education and learning, create safe spaces and nurture caring communities.
We also actively involve mainstream society - individuals and organizations alike - in our mission by inculcating and encouraging the spirit of civic consciousness and compassion.

Values
We believe that every person is a gift, someone of great value. We believe this to be true, regardless of the challenges and complexities people present.
We believe relationships are key to building community.
We believe mutual care and compassion are necessary components of a thriving society.
We believe that social justice and a strong sense of community are critical for our nation’s progress.
These are the values which dictate our actions.


Strategy
We encourage our members to come together around what they care about by enabling their efforts with resources from the wider community.
We value collaboration, and seek to build networks of individuals and organisations to stand in solidarity with our members, and in support of their efforts to improve their circumstances.

Standards
We consciously take steps to produce work of high standards. This begins with the environments we conduct our business with creating a warm, welcoming, and safe environment wherever we work.
Beyond an office, ours is a community workplace built upon open communication – a deeply collaborative day-to-day experience that brings together our staff, our supporters, and our members.

name: Bible Society of Singapore, The
search_text:
Bible Society of Singapore, The A. The Object of the Society is:

(a) to achieve the widest, effective and meaningful circulation of the Holy Scriptures of the Old and New Testaments throughout Singapore and as far as may be possible in other lands;
(b) to equip churches in Singapore and other parts of the world to share the Bible;
(c) to help people understand the message in the Bible by improving literacy and providing relevant readers aids but avoiding all matters of controversial interpretation;
(d) to help people engage, interact with and live out the Bible;
(e) to act as an integrated Bible agency that encourages all aspects of the Bible Lifecycle to help people understand its message, through proper translation, publishing, and distribution, literacy, engagement and advocacy;
(f) to engage in social interest and/or business activities (including but not limited to, art and cultural activities, and humanitarian and charitable causes) which support the advancement of its Object.

B. The Society shall act both independently and in association with the United Bible Societies and with such other Bible Societies as may from time to time be decided. The Society shall where possible co-operate with churches and Bible Societies whose object and principles are in harmony with its own, particularly in the preparation of versions or translations of the Holy Scriptures and in their publication and distribution.
C. The Society may purchase, lease, or otherwise acquire land, buildings, investments and property of any kind movable, immovable, or any interest in the same for or in connection with or to further the abovementioned purposes or any of them.

D. The Society may do all or any of the above things:

(a) as principal, trustee, or agent or otherwise and either alone or in conjunction with any other body or persons, including but not limited to The United Bible Societies and other Bible Societies as the Board may from time to time approve;

(b) either by or through agents, trustees or otherwise, and the trustees' rights and liabilities shall be in accordance with legislation then in force.

Without prejudice to the aforesaid, the Society may also set up separate legal entities and social enterprises to carry out such activities which fall under the above-mentioned Object and/or are complementary to such Object.

name: Breastfeeding Mothers' Support Group (Singapore)
search_text: Breastfeeding Mothers' Support Group (Singapore) BMSG(S) is the only organization in Singapore that promotes, supports and protects breastfeeding by mothers to mothers. Our mission is to increase the incidence and duration of breastfeeding, for the promotion of better health in Singapore. BMSG(S) is a registered charity and a member of the Health Endowment Fund*.     To promote breastfeeding  By raising awareness of the benefits of breastfeeding to both mother and child        through events such as the annual World Breastfeeding Week   through media   through in-house publications such as the quarterly Keeping Abreast newsletter, a book on  Practical Hints  for breastfeeding, breastfeeding booklets for general public and car decals    To support breastfeeding  By offering support and timely information to new mothers and mothers-to-be	  through helpline phone and email counselling	 through in-house library of resource materials and books  through sale of products such as breast pumps, nursing clothes and other essential accessories   through regular support gatherings	To protect breastfeeding  By providing accurate information and protecting against unethical practices in the infant health industry	  through workshops and talks  through working closely with SIFECS (Sale of Infant Food Ethics Committee Singapore) which reports on violations of ethics by formula companies	The Consequence of NOT Breastfeeding   Babies who are not breastfed enjoy less protection from diarrhea, ear and respiratory infections, eczema, influenza and allergies, pre-disposing them to higher risks of getting diabetes, high blood pressure, obesity and cancers as adults.	Mothers who do not breastfeed may experience more postpartum hemorrhage and face higher risks of pre-menopausal breast, ovarian and uterine cancers.   Families whose babies are not breastfed will incur higher costs with purchases of formula and frequent visits to the doctors translating to higher expenses and more childcare leave taken. Others; -

name: CAKE THEATRICAL PRODUCTIONS LTD.
search_text:
CAKE THEATRICAL PRODUCTIONS LTD. The objects for which the Company is established are:-
a) To promote, generate, foster, develop, stimulate and inculcate greater interest in the theatre in particular and the performing arts in general in Singapore through appropriate educational and community programmes, and to provide an avenue for those interested in drama and the arts to develop their potential to the fullest.
b) To produce, present and deal in plays, revues and operas, poetry reading, dance, concerts, exhibitions, pantomimes, pageants, impresario activities, musical and dramatic works, comedians and amusements of kinds in connection with performances at the theatre, cinema, variety, stage and music halls, and broadcasts over radio, television, internet and other means of transmitting sound in pictorial effect, and to enter into any arrangements for the management, conduct, control and carrying on of any such activity or activities, and for the supply of play, opera and scripts, librettos, music, artistes, performers, musicians, materials and all other personnel or things in connection therewith.
c) To carry on all or any of the activities of promoters of the performing arts, artistes, managers and/or representatives in all or any of the spheres of the performing arts, and to employ or commission persons to write, compose or adapt plays, films, radio, television and video productions, sketches, songs, music and dances.
d) To establish, form, and manage a full-time professional theatre company and to provide an outlet for theatre performers and other artistic practitioners as well as support personnel who wish to make a career in the theatre.
e) To present, organise, manage and conduct conferences, festivals and competitions in or related to drama and performing arts in Singapore or overseas either on its own or in conjunction with others.
f) To take such steps and to do such things as may be deemed necessary or convenient for any of the purposes of the company.
g) To carry on any of the business or businesses of traders, distributors, manufacturers, designers, producers, buyers, sellers, lessees, lessors of dance and theatrical equipment, props, costumes, souvenirs and any other objects relating to dance, theatre and the arts.
h) To purchase, take on lease or exchange, hire or otherwise acquire any real and personal estate which may be deemed necessary or convenient for any of the purposes of the Company, provided that in case the Company shall desire to purchase, take on lease or otherwise acquire any land then the Company shall obtain such licence (if any) as may be required by law.
i) To construct, maintain and alter any hall, auditorium, theatre, house, building or works necessary or convenient for the purposes of the Company.
j) To take any gift of property, whether subject to any special trust or not, for any one or more of the objects of the Company.
k) To take such steps by personal or written appeal, public meetings or otherwise, as may from time to time be deemed expedient for the purpose of procuring contributions to the funds of the Company, in the form of donations, annual subscriptions, or otherwise.
l) To sell, manage, lease, mortgage, dispose of or otherwise deal with all or any part of the property of the Company.
m) To borrow and raise money in such manner as the Company may think fit.

name: CAREGIVER SUPPORT HUB LTD.
search_text:
CAREGIVER SUPPORT HUB LTD.
The objects for which the Company, Caregiver Support Hub Ltd, is established are exclusively charitable and for the public benefit, including but not limited to:
1. To provide support, resources, and services to caregivers from all walks of life, regardless of race, religion, age, gender, or background, to promote their well-being, resilience, and mental health;
2. To organise educational programmes, talks, workshops, and outreach initiatives to raise awareness of caregiving needs and challenges in the community;
3. To advocate for the interests and welfare of caregivers in Singapore by collaborating with healthcare, social service, and governmental agencies;
4. To promote volunteerism and community engagement in support of caregivers and their families.  Our Vision
At Caregiver Support Hub Ltd, we imagine a society where caregivers are seen, supported, and empowered to care with confidence and compassion. We hope to build a community where caregiving - a vital but often invisible role - is valued and sustained.
For caregivers, no matter their background, have access to the knowledge, resources, and networks they need to navigate the challenges of caregiving while maintaining their own well-being. By fostering understanding and empathy across society, we aim to create an environment where no caregiver feels alone, overwhelmed, or unappreciated.
Our vision is of a society that celebrates caregiving, strengthens the bonds between caregivers and the community, and provides practical and emotional support so that caregivers can thrive alongside the people they care for. Ultimately, we want caregivers to feel confident, supported, and respected in their invaluable work.


Our Mission
Our mission is to support and empower caregivers through practical resources, education, advocacy, and community engagement. We work to help caregivers enhance their well-being, resilience, and skills, so they can care sustainably and with dignity.
We welcome and support caregivers from all walks of life, regardless of age, race, religion, gender, or background. Through workshops, talks, training programmes, and outreach activities, we provide the knowledge and tools caregivers need to meet daily challenges. Our programmes also help caregivers connect with each other and with volunteers, creating networks of care and support that extend beyond individual households.
Education is at the heart of what we do. We aim to raise awareness of caregiving challenges in the wider community so that society better understands and appreciates the vital role caregivers play. Our initiatives foster empathy, strengthen social connections, and encourage shared responsibility in supporting caregivers and their families.
Advocacy is another key part of our work. We collaborate with healthcare professionals, social service organisations, and government agencies to ensure caregivers’ voices are heard and their needs considered. By working together across sectors, we aim to influence policies, programmes, and practices that improve the lives of caregivers and the people they care for.
Volunteerism is central to our mission. We create opportunities for people to contribute their time, skills, and compassion to support caregivers. Through volunteering, we help build a culture of generosity, community engagement, and shared responsibility, showing that caregiving is not just an individual effort but a societal one. Support Groups

CARITAS SINGAPORE COMMUNITY COUNCIL LIMITED The role of the Caritas Singapore Community Council is to enhance the social and community services provided by the Catholic Church and the Catholic Community in Singapore for the benefit of the broader community in Singapore.
As stated in our M&A, the objectives of Caritas Singapore:
1. To initiate, assist and organize such forms of relief and schemes of social services so as to alleviate distress or poverty amongst people regardless of their race or religion;
2. To better and more effectively serve the poor, the needy and all people by researching, analyzing the needs and developing the strategies and activities of various social and community organizations;
3. To provide leadership and direction and support for the various Catholic social and community organizations to better serve the broader community
4. To synergize resources and coordinate common activities of various social and community organizations, including but not limited to fund raising, volunteer recruitment and matching, capacity building, governance practices, shared services and shared practices; and
5. To provide a network and promote a community for various social and community organizations to facilitate an exchange of ideas, gathering of resources, and promoting research and development of strategies and actions to be undertaken to more effectively serve the poor, the needy and the community generally. OUR MISSION:
To collaborate and provide leadership within the Catholic community in Singapore in fulfilling the Church's social mission especially for the most vulnerable in society.

To be visible sign of God's love for humanity demonstrated by Catholics living the principles and values of the Catholic Social Teaching. General Charitable Purposes

name: CHARACTER & LEADERSHIP ACADEMY LIMITED
search_text:
CHARACTER & LEADERSHIP ACADEMY LIMITED (1) To organise (including partnership, support and co-organise) youth outreach programs and events to train and develop the youth in the area of leadership and character building by providing opportunities for the youth to participate in Youth-related Leadership or Character Education (workshops, symposiums, events and conferences) conducted locally and globally.

(2) To encourage the spirit of volunteerism and build social cohesiveness through social, racial, transcultural integration related programs and youth community projects conducted globally and locally.

(3) To manage all functions (from recruitment, selection, deployment and promotion) of all youth facilitators, volunteers and trainers as well as oversee and manage relevant certification for youth related courses and training programs.

(4) To equip young Singaporeans with necessary leadership skills and cultural sensitivity to take on opportunities at the global stage, encouraging cross cultural dialogues between Singaporean youths and youths of other nationalities through 10 key thrusts:-

- Arts and Culture
- Sports and Adventure
- Community and Service Learning
- Character and Leadership Education
- Environmental and Entrepreneurship Education

5) Mental Health Education & Equipping for Youths, Educators and Parents Character & Leadership Academy is a youth development charity that seeks to engage, empower and equip youths of today, both local and international, with essential personal development and leadership skills, alongside appropriate character traits for an ever-changing world of tomorrow.


CLA was founded based on the belief that Personal Values and Character are the key foundations and life-smith of every youth leader, where in the absence of either would not allow them to effectively lead with pride and integrity.

Our Mission
“Building a community of youth with good character and leadership traits, locally and globally, through Equipping, Engagement and Empowerment.”

Our Vision
“To be the Centre of Excellence in Youth Character Development and Leadership Education, reaching out to 25,000 youths in Asia by 2025.”

name: CHECKPOINT THEATRE LIMITED
search_text:
CHECKPOINT THEATRE LIMITED The objective of the Company is to support, develop and promote the practice of performance and visual arts, and to foster and inculcate an interest in and appreciation of such artistic endeavours. Established in 2002, Checkpoint Theatre is a company of multi-disciplinary storytellers. We focus on creating and presenting original Singapore content with strong writing, performance and direction, across different media, disciplines and platforms.

With honesty and humour, head and heart, we produce and develop vibrant and important contemporary Asian stories that connect with, challenge, and inspire audiences. We are the home of new Singapore playwriting and we nurture the next generation of Singapore theatre-makers and creatives.

What We Do

• We create and present new theatrical and inter-disciplinary work with honesty, depth, and nuance.
• We develop original Singapore stories with outstanding writing through our process of rigorous dramaturgy and incisive direction.
• We mentor emerging theatre-makers and creatives through workshops, internships and collaborations, and through providing professional resources and platforms to showcase new work.
• We publish anthologies of original works written by Singapore storytellers and books on theatre and art-making.

MISSION To create, produce and champion a canon of vibrant and important Singapore content that embraces and empowers our diverse voices and communities. VISION For every individual to experience ... CHILDREN'S CANCER FOUNDATION The objects for which the Company is established are all or any of the following:
(a) to promote the overall well-being of the CCF Child;
(b) to provide the CCF Child and his families emotional and moral support, educational support, through counselling and psycho-social services;

(g) to support research projects into cancer and enhance the medical expertise of medical staff and social workers;
(h) to facilitate a conducive and suitably-equipped hospital and educational environment for the CCF Child;
(i) To do all such other things as are incidental or conducive to the attainment of the above objects, where necessary with the approval of the relevant authorities, and provided that nothing shall be done solely for profit or for commercial reasons, the Company shall be allowed:
(i) to provide and transfer knowledge, skills and/or experience as may be required by corporations and entities in Singapore, which support the objects and charitable work of the Company;
(ii) to collaborate, maintain liaison and coordinate projects or activities with other related organisations, corporations or entities in furtherance of the objects and charitable work of the Company;
(iii) to provide the Affected Child emotional and moral support through counselling and psycho-social services;
(iv) to organise social and recreational activities and programmes for the Affected Child; and
(v) to carry on or to undertake any lawful business or activity, do any act or enter into any transaction, with full rights, powers and privileges for such purposes. Vision:
To become a leading provider of resources and psychosocial services to children and their families impacted by cancer.

name: CHINESE DEVELOPMENT ASSISTANCE COUNCIL
search_text:
CHINESE DEVELOPMENT ASSISTANCE COUNCIL CDAC's priority is to help the less successful individuals of the Chinese community in Singapore to upgrade themselves through education and skills training. The objectives of CDAC are: (i)To support, foster and promote the socio-economic, educational and skills development of the less successful of the Chinese community in Singapore; (ii)To promote the educational performance of the under-achievers in all fields and to participate, aid and assist in educational programmes and undertakings to raise their educational level; and (iii)To formulate ways and means to help working adults to improve their skills and earning capacities.

CDAC continues to focus on its core programmes and adopt a "centric approach" to help our three target groups, namely students, workers and families.

Following a strategic review in 2016, CDAC has re-organised its programmes to better address the emerging needs of the community and sharpen the focus of its programmes and schemes. In addition to providing more support to students & parents and families (including workers), CDAC will also support seniors to age actively and meaningfully through learning, health & wellness and volunteerism. CDAC will also continue to support the expansion of school-based student care centres.

We plan to expand our outreach to our target groups through the 13 CDAC Centres and also through more collaborative projects with network partners. The mission of CDAC is to nurture and develop the potential of the Chinese community in contributing to the continued success of multi-racial Singapore. name: CITRIN FOUNDATION LTD.
search_text:
CITRIN FOUNDATION LTD. Citrin Foundation is a research-driven, not-for-profit organization set up to tackle citrin deficiency, a monogenetic condition which is an inborn error of metabolism, as well as a secondary urea cycle disorder.

Our goal is to ultimately find effective treatments and a cure for the condition. We fund research projects to better understand the condition and develop new therapies. We also provide support to citrin deficiency patients and their families globally. There are two cornerstones to the foundation’s mission, finding a cure for citrin deficiency and providing life-long support to deficiency patients. We believe that a multi-disciplinary, innovative, and long-term approach will deliver breakthrough treatments and address the well-being of citrin deficiency patients holistically. Therefore, we seek to work with top scientists, researchers, and physicians who are in related fields around the world to solve this medical problem together.

We have developed the following list of objectives to achieve this:

- Identify patients with Citrin Deficiency (CD) worldwide
- Study the natural course of disease in large patient cohorts
- Improve the diagnosis of CD
- Understand the basic science and pathogenesis of the disease
- Explore the benefit of existing therapies
- Develop new therapies
- Support affected patients and their families Health Professional Group Diseases/Illnesses Support Group Others; Research

name: CITY CHINESE ORCHESTRA (SINGAPORE) LTD
search_text: CITY CHINESE ORCHESTRA (SINGAPORE) LTD (a) To provide an environment in which people with a love for Chinese classical music and who wish to promote this music can come together and learn more about the music.	(b) To, through object (a), raise the performing standard of the Orchestra.  (c) To, through different forms of musical performance, generate the desire to learn Chinese classical music and to raise the level of appreciation of the music to the general public.  (d) To establish, form and manage music groups which will include choirs and orchestras.	*Please refer to Governing Instrument as attached for more details on the remaining objectives. Organisation Vision Statement    City Chinese Orchestra was set up with the aim of providing a conducive environment for Chinese music lovers to share their interests and engage in the learning and performing of Chinese music. For the past 36 years, the Orchestra has continually trained young Singaporeans in the playing of traditional Chinese instruments and also provides opportunities to expose Singaporeans to the rich Chinese culture. Professional musicians and composers from China are frequently invited to share their knowledge and experiences with the Orchestra.	  In the coming years, the Orchestra seeks to continue in its mission to promote traditional Chinese music, and performing arts in general, among Singaporeans. We hope that amidst the economic developments of the country, music and culture are not neglected and forgotten. The Orchestra will continue to train young musicians and invest in collaborations with local as well as overseas performing artistes and groups, to raise the level of music appreciation and performance in the local music arena. Music & Orchestras

The vision and mission of the College of Paediatrics and Child Health, Singapore is defined through its emblem.	Stated below are the details:	 In the centre of the Shield, the Chevron symbolises the coming-together of   all medical specialties to provide the best medical care for the nation.	  The two children represent paediatrics and healthy children.	  The snake coiling around the rod symbolises  healing .    The four circular red dots joined together by olive leaves represent the   four races living harmoniously in Singapore and in the pink of health.	 Supporting the Shield are two lions which represent Singapore.    The olive branches symbolises anointing and medicinal values. Our vision resonates we meet an unmet need in people to have an impact on their own and others’ future.

Regarding the Company's aforesaid exclusively charitable objects relating to advancement of education, the Company intends to conduct its corporate administrative, financial, and program planning operations from its registered office in Singapore. Our Mission
As a catalyst for advancing education worldwide, we inspire, challenge and equip communities of professionals to act effectively and with integrity to champion the success of their institutions.

Advancing education to transform lives and society.

Our Values
We value professional excellence, integrity and respect for our community of volunteers and staff as partners, stakeholders and leaders. We aspire to be bold, agile and innovative. And we are committed, in all that we do, to be collaborative, inclusive and embracing of diversity.
