name: CRU ASIA LIMITED
search_text:
CRU ASIA LIMITED Cru Asia Ltd (CAL) is an interdenominational evangelical organisation committed to giving people everywhere the opportunity to know and experience God’s love and plan for their lives. We offer spiritual guidance, resources and programmes tailored for people from all cultures in every walk of life.
We endeavor:
• To share the message of Jesus Christ
• To work with all churches and other Christian groups
• To equip Christians with a deeper knowledge of the faith
• To provide a regional training centre and organise regional conferences

Our Vision

Movements everywhere, so that everyone knows someone who truly follows Jesus

CAL firmly believes that a relationship with Jesus Christ is a transformative one. When a community of people realizes the magnitude of God’s love for them, their transformed lives, in turn, transform the world.

Be it helping friends discover meaning and purpose in life, guiding people through difficult times, or traveling to faraway places to build wells or treating the needy, these communities-in-actions become powerful movements, and we hope to see ripple through the world.

We recognize that this world-changing vision is a bold one – one that is possible only with God’s empowerment and guidance.

Our Mission

Reach, build and send Christ-centred multiplying disciples
CAL seeks to reach out in love, with both the message of Jesus Christ and good deeds, in partnership with churches and other Christian groups. Our commitment is to equip believers in deeper knowledge of the faith through mentoring, seminars and formal theological equipping, building them up to be confident, passionate individuals sent out as a blessing to the nations.
To that end, we also hold regional conferences and are launching networks of training centres across various parts of Asia.
The following CAL divisions work together to achieve the above:
• Cru Singapore, inclusive of Media Ministry
• East Asia School of Theology (EAST)

 


Christianity

name: CURIOUS THOUGHTS GIVING LIMITED
search_text:
CURIOUS THOUGHTS GIVING LIMITED Its objects are:

(a) To enhance the psycho-emotional well-being of our beneficiaries through 2 main initiatives:
StoryTime (https://storytime.sg): A community initiative that brings volunteers from the public (children and families) to read to the less privileged elderly from Senior Activity Centres. This aims to promote inter-generational community bonding.
CuriousTime@VWOs: Free structured Literacy or MOE English programmes for children who are living in shelters or are beneficiaries of a Voluntary Welfare Organisation (VWO). This aims to increase the literacy level of beneficiaries through a structured programme.

(b) To provide educational, moral, emotional, social and financial support to 2 groups of beneficiaries:
the elderly who are less privileged; and
children beneficiaries referred from Voluntary Welfare Organisations.

(c) We seek to improve their quality of life in terms of education, recreation and social belonging, and physical and mental health.

(d) To foster community bonding and social interaction between the inter-generations and the Singapore community.
Our vision is:
- To promote inter-generational bonding through reading.
- To empower children from all walks of life through equal and transparent access to quality education.

Our mission is:
- To bring different generations together through a reading session.
- To provide quality education to all children, through experienced and well-trained teachers via a thorough curriculum. Established in 2017, Curious Thoughts Giving is a non-profit charitable organisation that believes in empowering children from all walks of life through equal and transparent access to quality educ... name: Caring for Cambodia Fund
search_text:
Caring for Cambodia Fund 1. To provide education for impoverished children in Cambodia so they can break the cycle of poverty and contribute back to their communities and their country; 2. To create and sustain safe, technologically equipped schools with clean drinking water and a meal every day; 3. to mentor, motivate and professionally train Cambodian teachers according to international best practices and standards as well as Ministry of Education, Youth and Sport guidelines for hte school curriculum of Cambodia; 4. To fund supplies and other tools necessary to teach, including uniforms, transportation, food, medical and dental assistance, and books; 5. to raise awareness in international communities, including the education of children in Singapore and worldwide about the poor and helping those in need, that Cambodia is a country in need and that as Citizens of the World everyone should help in some way. Vision: Caring for Cambodia (CFC) strives to make it possible for every Cambodian child to attend school from preschool to 12th grade and to graduate with the skills required for higher education or for obtaining meaningful employment. This enables our students to support their families, to become productive citizens and to give back to their communities. CFC’s long term goals are:

- To make higher education an attainable choice throughout Cambodia
To improve early childhood education across the country
- To create a sustainable system that will be run and funded by Cambodia

Mission: Caring for Cambodia (CFC) is educating a generation of Cambodian children today, to make a difference for Cambodia’s tomorrow. Our schools are safe and well equipped. We train teachers, provide tools, and remove barriers to learning. We build Cambodian leadership to guide and sustain these schools, so that generations of CFC graduates can reach their highest potential and make valuable contributions to their community.

General Charitable Purposes

name: Caritas Singapore Agape Fund
search_text:
Caritas Singapore Agape Fund The role of Caritas Singapore is to enhance the social and community services provided by the Catholic Church and the Catholic community in Singapore for the benefit of the broader community in Singapore. As stated in our M&A, the objectives of Caritas Singapore are:
1. To initiate, assist and organize such forms of relief and schemes of social services so as to alleviate distress or poverty amongst people regardless of their race or religion;
2. To better and more effectively serve the poor, the needy and all people by researching, analyzing the needs and developing the strategies and activities of various social and community organizations;
3. To provide leadership and direction and support for the various Catholic social and community organizations to better serve the broader community
4. To synergize resources and coordinate common activities of various social and community organizations, including but not limited to fund raising, volunteer recruitment and matching, capacity building, governance practices, shared services and shared practices; and
5. To provide a network and promote a community for various social and community organizations to facilitate an exchange of ideas, gathering of resources, and promoting research and development of strategies and actions to be undertaken to more effectively serve the poor, the needy and the community generally. Vision:
To be the visible sign of God's love for humanity demonstrated by Catholics living the principles and values of the Catholic Social Teaching.

Mission:
To collaborate and provide leadership within the Catholic community in Singapore in fulfilling the Church's social mission especially for the most vulnerable in society. Community

name: Chee Tian Keng Temple
search_text:
Chee Tian Keng Temple OBJECTIVE

The objects of the Charity are as follows:
To carry on with the ancient tradition of philosophy and religious belief of Taoism, that is deeply rooted in the Chinese customs. The main activities that are held annually are the deities’ anniversaries and festival celebrations.

MANAGEMENT

There should be at least 3 governing board members. These include Board Members, Management Committee Members, Directors and Trustees*. The duties, terms of office, appointment and removal procedures of the governing board members should be specified in the governing instrument. It should also state that the Commissioner of Charities or the respective Sector Administrator should be notified of any change in the governing board members.

QUORUM FOR MEETING

For AGM: At least 25% of the total voting membership or thirty (30) voting members, whichever is the lesser, present at a General Meeting shall form a quorum.

For Committee Meeting: At least half (½) of the Committee Members must be present for its proceedings to be valid.

MAGEMENT AND COMMITTEE

Any changes in the Committee shall be notified to the Registrar of Societies and the Commissioner of Charities within two (2) weeks of the change.

The Board shall have the power to remove the Board member who is appointed by the Board before the expiration of his period of office and may appoint another person in his stead." (This is to ensure that the Board is able to remove the Board member that it has appointed.)

CONFLICT OF INTEREST CLAUSES

Whenever a member of the Board/Management Committee is in any way, directly or indirectly, has an interest in a transaction or project or other matter to be discussed at a meeting, the member shall disclose the nature of his interest before the discussion on the matter begins. The member concerned should offer to withdraw from the meeting and not participate in the discussion or vote on the matter. The Board/Management Committee shall decide if this should be accepted.

TRUSTEES

The trustees of the Society shall not effect any sale or mortgage of property without the prior approval of the General Meeting of members.

Notice of any proposal to remove a trustee from his trusteeship or to appoint a new trustee to fill a vacancy must be given by posting it on the notice board in the Society's premises at least two (2) weeks before the General Meeting at which the proposal is to be discussed. The result of such General Meeting shall then be notified to the Registrar of Societies and Commissioner of Charities.

The address of each immovable property, name of each trustee and any subsequent change must be notified to the Registrar of Societies and Commissioner of Charities.

AMENDMENTS TO CONSTITUTION

No alteration or addition/deletion to this Constitution shall be made except at a general meeting and with the consent of two-thirds (2/3) of the voting members present at the General Meeting, and they shall not come into force without the prior sanction of the Registrar of Societies and Commissioner of Charities.

CESSATION OF CHARITY STATUS

In the event that the society ceases to be a registered charity under the Charities Act, all debts, liabilities legally incurred on behalf of the [society] shall be fully discharged, and the remaining funds will be donated to charitable organization(s) with similar objectives in Singapore which is (are) registered under the Charities Act as the members of the society may determine at the General Meeting, unless otherwise allowed by the Commissioner of Charities.

DISSOLUTION

The Society shall not be dissolved, except with the consent of a majority of the total voting membership of the Society for the time being resident in Singapore expressed, either in person or by proxy, at a General Meeting convened for the purpose.

In the event of the Society being dissolved as provided above, all debts and liabilities legally incurred on behalf of the Society shall be fully discharged, and the remaining funds will be donated to charitable organization(s), or Institution(s) of a Public Character, when the Society is an Institution of a Public Character, as the case may be, with similar objectives in Singapore which is(are) registered under the Charities Act, as the members of the Society may determine at the General Meeting.

A Certificate of Dissolution shall be given within seven (7) days of the dissolution to the Registrar of Societies and Commissioner of Charities.
The vision is to showcase to the world, the tradition of philosophy and religious belief of Taoism that is rooted in the Chinese customs.

The mission is to educate and exhibit to the general public, the philosophy and religious belief of Taoism, through holding a series of events, for example the traditional anniversaries and festival celebration. Taoism

name: Church of Joy
search_text:
Church of Joy Church of Joy is a Christ-centred charismatic church that desires to worship God in spirit and truth, and to see God’s glory – His character and purpose – reflected in our unity of community and faithful mission.

We are an autonomous local church that partners with the New Covenant Ministries International apostolic team. Church of Joy believes in the priesthood of all believers and the discipling of all nations. We are a mission-minded church that desires to build a base so that Christians can be trained and resourced to change the character and culture of our city, our region and the world, to reflect Christ’s grace and truth.

We desire to become a house of prayer for all nations, and for the grace and truth of the Gospel to bless all. Christianity

name: City Harvest Community Services Association
search_text:
City Harvest Community Services Association To do good to all people, with every opportunity given to us.
This is the ethos of the City Harvest Community Services
Association (CHCSA). Our work began in 1994 when members of
City Harvest Church began to reach out to communities in need.
These works grew and CHCSA was officially registered as a society
in 1997; we gained charity (IPC) status in 2000.
As the social service arm of City Harvest Church, we are tasked
with doing good in the community through reaching out to and
assisting the needy and the marginalised, regardless of race
or religion.
We focus on three people groups – elderly, youths, families in transition.
We support them with a mindset to find a hurt and heal it, to find
a need and meet it.
By partnering with various organisations and community
stakeholders, we are able to provide a holistic and two-pronged
approach to supporting our community. Doing Good in our Communities as the Social Service Arm of City Harvest Church City Harvest Community Services Association (CHCSA) was registered as a society in 1997, obtained charity status in 2000 and granted IPC status in 2002. name: Civilians Association (Singapore)
search_text:
Civilians Association (Singapore) The objects of the Society are:

a) To carry out charity contributions to the needy residents of Singapore, and to provide social aid and befriender services to the aged and mentally, emotionally, physically and financially challenged residents of Singapore;

b) To promote soccer/golf/cricket or any other sports especially amongst youth to aid in maintaining good mental/physical health and to help in greater integration amongst races, background and upbringing;

c) To promote and organize events / seminars / exhibitions to foster greater family togetherness and bonding, especially amongst 3- tier families, thus enhancing greater interaction and cohesiveness amongst families in Singapore. To consider aiding financially or otherwise challenged needy or less fortunate and deserving single parents on a selective basis

d) Arrange and organize talks, seminars and events to create an opportunity for participants, especially youngsters, to acquire knowledge and skills to upgrade themselves, thus contributing towards a more vibrant and successful Singapore;

e) In furtherance of the above, the Club may carry out the following activities as means to further the organisation’s main objectives:
i. To conduct sports / recreational / health / educational / social activities and events of cultural and social interests for members, their families and public.
ii. To organize events / meetings to recognize outstanding people in Singapore and to confer them in the name of “Silent Heroes” for their cause. The Club may form a Silent Heroes Chapter and from time to time also appoint Advisory Boards, Judging Panels and any other Boards or Committees / Teams in connection with organizing the Silent Heroes Award program.
iii. To collect, compile, print, publish and to sell or distribute leaflets journals and books electronically or otherwise that the Club may consider desirable, for the promotion of its objects.
iv. To campaign for, receive, hold and apply funds, gifts, donations, or the proceeds thereof, to any of the purposes described in the objects.
v. To purchase, lease, take on hire or in exchange or otherwise acquire any movable or immovable property, rights and/or privileges.
vi. To borrow money with interest or otherwise for the purpose of acquiring movable or immovable property, rights and/or privileges, and for repayment of such loan(s) for the purpose of (or) attaining its objectives.
vii. The Club may also consider fund- raising activities to raise funds for the purpose of acquiring movable or immovable properties and for the purpose of fulfilling its objectives and related activities from time to time based on the requirements and to achieve the objectives.
viii. To engage in any other activity designed to promote the aforesaid objects but not in any way prejudicial to the interests of the Club or the General public or in contravention of these Rules.
ix. To obtain affiliation and sanctions with any sports and other relevant bodies to participate and organize sports meets, tournaments or any other events or performances to attain any or all of the objectives of the Club.
Vision: To be a Leading Society in Promoting a Caring and Sharing Community

Mission: To Promote Humanity and Values for Building a Harmonious and Inclusive Society The Civilians Association (Singapore) is a non-profit organization and became a charity in March 2019. name: Club Rainbow (Singapore)
search_text:
Club Rainbow (Singapore) Established in 1992, Club Rainbow (Singapore) is a non-profit organisation and charity with the mission to support and empower children with chronic illnesses and their families. Armed with the firm belief that every child deserves equal opportunities to lead a fulfilling and independent life, Club Rainbow supports our children and their families in more than 30 critical programmes, services and assistance schemes. Adopting a holistic approach based on five core pillars of support - Social Work Intervention, Therapy Intervention, Education Support, Arts Development and Vocational Development - Club Rainbow partners our children and their families in their journey to achieve the following objectives at different stages of their lives.

Key Thrusts and Objectives:
1) Living with Dignity - Building Resilient Families
2) Maximising Developmental Potential - Developing Independent Adolescents
3) Achieving Full Potential - Empowering Individuals as Active Contributors

Objectives :-
1) Building resilient families
2) Developing independent adolescents
3) Empowering individuals as active contributors .

Core Pillars of Support :-
1) Social Work Intervention
2) Therapy Intervention
3) Educational Support
4) Arts Development
5) Vocational Development .

As an independent charity, CRS relies largely on corporates and public donations to support our mission. Granted the Institution of a Public Character (IPC) status by Ministry of Health, monetary donations received may be tax-exempted. Vision:
To be the charity of choice that inspires hope and makes a difference in the lives of children with chronic illnesses and their families.

Mission:
We support and empower children with chronic illnesses & their families by providing compassionate relevant services in their journey towards an enriching life.

Established in 1992, Club Rainbow (Singapore) is a non-profit organisation with the mission to support and empower children with chronic illnesses and their families. name: Coalition Against Bullying for Children and Youth (CABCY)
search_text: Coalition Against Bullying for Children and Youth (CABCY) CABCY is a society comprised of responsible volunteers who believe in providing children and youths with safe and secure environments void of bullying. CABCY will work with individual children, youth, parents, teachers and any authorities or organisations involved with children and youths to:	  Recognize bullying;	Reduce bullying;   Report bullying;   Reform bullying behaviour;   Review policies against bullying;   Reduce the harmful effects of bullying;	 Provide responsible intervention against bullying; and   Reinforce a Bully Free Zone CABCY is a voluntary society set up to help children, youth and adults recognize the different forms of bullying, its effects on the targets of bullying and what can be done to help reduce or prevent bullying. CABCY exists to be a resource for children, youth, parents, teachers, child workers, social workers, counsellors and other people who work with children and youths to obtain help and advice on issues concerning bullying. name: Conservation International Singapore Conservation Trust
search_text:
Conservation International Singapore Conservation Trust To provide awareness and resources to the people of Singapore in promotion and support of education, science and research and urban sustainability. To achieve the main objections, and subject to this Deed, the Operations Manual and Applicable Law, the Trustees shall have the powers to perform the following: (a) plan and participate in fund raising, (b) event planning and management, (c) public, private and non-profit partnership engagement, (d) community outreach, and (e) developing and implementing educational, volunteer and research programs and activities. Vision
Singapore as a resilient and prosperous country providing regional leadership in sustainable development.

Mission
Building upon a strong foundation in science, education and partnership to empower people to recognize both their individual impact and Singapore’s collective environmental footprint. To educate the communities of Singapore on sustainability and biodiversity, thus enabling the protection of natural assets on which both the current and future generations depend. name: Cornerstone Community Church
search_text:
Cornerstone Community Church 1. To propagate the Christian Faith and to spread the Gospel of Jesus Christ as revealed through the Holy Scriptures by means of communication, whether visual, verbal or written.

2. To provide, promote and render counselling services both material and spiritual to the sick, the aged, the homeless and the needy.

3. To do all such things as are incidental or conducive to the attainment of the above object provided that nothing shall be done for commercial reasons or solely for profit, the Church may:

a. Erect and maintain Church buildings, social halls, business offices, school buildings, recreational facilities, parsonages, and such other structures and organize and promote such activities as are deemed necessary for the purposes of the Church and with the approval in writing of the relevant authorities for the upkeep and maintenance of said buildings and facilities.
b. Collect, solicit and accept funds, gifts and other subscriptions; to borrow money, hold in trust, use, mortgage, lease, sell, or otherwise acquire or dispose of property, real or chattel, in keeping with the recited purposes of this Church, and it shall have and exercise all powers that are necessary or convenient to effect any and all of the purposes for which this church is organized.
c. Undertake and execute any trust the undertaking whereof may seem desirable either gratuitously or otherwise.
d. Establish, incorporate or set up:
(a) Agencies and branch churches; and
(b) Any company limited by guarantee or any other non-profit entity, whether in Singapore or in other countries for the purpose of holding any immovable property on trust for the Church
e. Enter into partnership or any arrangement in the nature of a partnership with any person or persons or church or churches engaged or interested or about to become engaged or interested in the carrying on or conduct of any business of a solely Christian nature which this Church is authorised to carry on or conduct or from which this Church would or might derive any benefit, whether direct or indirect.
f. Build a Christ-centered congregation through the Worship of God by the preaching of the Word of God.
g. Encourage a fellowship of believers in a life of fervent prayer and systematic discipleship and the exercise of spiritual gifts.
h. Provide for preaching, teaching and fostering the growth of the Christian Religion; to license and ordain ministers; to promote a ministry of evangelism; to be involved in missionary work by the sending out, receiving and training of missionaries; to carry on the organization of Churches and foster their development and local sovereignty and independence according to this Constitution and its By-laws.
i. Participate in international and interdenominational ministries.
j. Co-operate with other evangelical Christian groups for carrying out any of the above stated objects.
k. Establish and run a child care development Centre.

Coronation Baptist Church OBJECTIVES AND OPERATIONS
The objectives for which the Church is established are:
a. to worship, praise and give thanks to the Triune God: Father, Son and Holy Spirit; b. to teach the Word of God to believers in order that they may be built up in the faith and equipped for the work of the ministry;
c. to preach the gospel of our Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ and to teach and encourage personal witnessing of individual believers to this same end;
d. to support the work of missionaries and agencies of like faith for the furtherance of the gospel;
e. to meet for prayer, fellowship and the administration of the ordinances of Baptism and the Lord's Supper; and
f. to exercise social concern by ministering to the needs of believers and non believers alike in a Christ-like spirit. Build a church spiritually alive with faith, godliness and holiness, where every believer is prepared for ministry, equipped with an understanding of God’s word, lives out the Great Commandment and is committed to the Great Commission. To promote and maintain the true worship of the only true, righteous and holy God of heaven and earth (Exodus 20:1-5; Matthew 4:10;  John 4:23-24).  B.	To preach the gospel of Christ Jesus for the gathering and perfecting of the saints (Matthew 28:19-20; Luke 24:46-47; Romans 10:11-15;	I Corinthians 1:17-31; Ephesians 4:11-16; II Timothy 4:1-2).  C. D.	To promote and maintain the fellowship of the saints in Christ Jesus (John 17:21-24; I Corinthians 12; Ephesians 4:1-16; Hebrew 10:23-25;  I John 1:7).  E.	To exercise Christian discipline in the Church of Jesus (Matthew 16:16-19; Matthew 18:15-20; I Corinthians 5:1-5; II Thessalonians 3:6;   I Timothy 1:20; Titus 3:10).	F.	To minister the mercies of Christ to the needy within and without the fellowship of the Church (Matthew 25:31-46; Acts 6:1-6; Galatians 6:10).	G.	To defend the faith once delivered to the saints (Philippians 1:27;   II Timothy 1:13; Jude 3). Church Theme verse	 For I determined not to know anything among you, save Jesus Christ and Him crucified.     1 Corinthians 2:2	  Our Mission	 As salt and light in this world, we proclaim a sovereign, powerful, saving gospel to a fallen, sin-cursed and dying world, setting forth Jesus Christ crucified as the only hope for mankind, and the Bible alone as the basis, rule and foundation for all of life and faith.        Our Vision    1.	We aspire to be a vibrant and faithful Reformed Church, establishing ourselves as a pillar and ground of biblical truth as they are expressed in the 3 Forms of Unity (Belgic Confession, Heidelberg Catechism and Canons of Dort).	 2.	We seek to be salt and light in this world, setting forth the antithetical, Christian life as the only life worth living.      3.	We will build up the Church with the pure and lively preaching of the Word of God, submitting to the rule of our King Jesus Christ through His properly ordained office-bearers and cheerfully employing our gifts for the salvation of His people. name: Crime Library (Singapore)
search_text:
Crime Library (Singapore) Objectives
1. Locate Missing Persons: Utilize all available resources and collaborate with law enforcement and the community to find missing individuals.
2. Reunite Families: Provide support and services to help adopted children connect with their biological families.
3. Raise Awareness: Increase public awareness about unsolved crimes and the importance of community vigilance through social media, events, and TV documentaries.
4. Facilitate Information Sharing: Create a platform for sharing information and tips related to missing persons, reuniting families and unsolved crimes.
6. Promote Public Safety: Engage with the community to promote safety practices and preventive measures against crime.
7. Provide Resources and Support: Offer resources and support to families of missing persons and victims of crime to help them through challenging times. Vision
A safer and more connected society where every missing person is found, every child knows their origins, and communities are empowered with knowledge to prevent and solve crimes.

Mission
To assist in locating missing individuals and reuniting adopted children with their biological families, while raising awareness about unsolved crimes and promoting public safety through community engagement and information sharing.

search_text:
Cultural Matching Fund The Cultural Matching Fund shall be for the purposes of benefiting the community through the advancement of arts and heritage in Singapore by:

(a) encouraging donations to arts and heritage charities in cultural sector; and

(b) developing capabilities for the long-term sustainability of arts and heritage charities in the cultural sector and the cultural sector as a whole

through the provision of CMF matching grants.
The CMF aims to encourage giving to our arts and heritage sector. This will create a more sustainable arts and heritage scene, and one in which more people in Singapore have a stake. The CMF will match monetary donations received by the eligible organisations, from individuals, foundations and corporations. The CMF doubles the value of every cash donation for the receiving organisation which can be put to various uses, including developing capabilities for the long-term sustainability of the organisation and the cultural sector as a whole. name: DANCE ENSEMBLE SINGAPORE LTD
search_text: DANCE ENSEMBLE SINGAPORE LTD Dance Ensemble Singapore was established in July 1993 under the auspice of the National Arts Council. It evolved from the immensely successful Yan Choong Lian Dance Troupe.	  The Ensemble has been a key player in promoting dance in the Singapore. Under the able direction of its Founder and Artistic Director, Mdm Yan Choong Lian, its membership has increased from a hundred to 700 members to date. The Ensemble aims to nurture young talents through creative and quality dance training and to upgrade the local artistic standard through various arts activities. In addition, it strives to make dance more accessible to school students by participating in the National Arts Council s Arts Education Programmes.     The Ensemble has distinguished itself in the past years with numerous outstanding public performances in both local and overseas major events. It has actively participated in National Day Parades, President s Star Charity night, Chingay procession, Singapore Festival of Arts, Festival of Asian Performing Arts and Chinese Cultural Festivals. It has also represented Singapore at major international events such as the New Zealand TAANZ Conference in Dunedin (1989), ASEAN Dance Festival in Malaysia and Japan (1991 & 1992), 10th National Cultural Festival in Thailand (1995), International Youth Dance Festival in Macau (1993, 1997 & 1999), International Children s Folklore Festival from 1994 - 1997 in Turkey, France and Spain, Commonwealth Cultural Festival in Malaysia (1998), Nan Ying International Folklore Festival in Taiwan (2000 & 2003), Asian Tourism Festival in Japan (1999 - Hiroshima, 2000 - Okinawa & 2005 - Nagoya), Cultural Dance Performance in China (1998), ASEAN Cultural Week in Cambodia (2002), Matsuyama Autumn Festival in Kagoshima, Japan (2005),  Interweave  with Hong Kong Zephyr Dance Troupe (2006)? International Dance Festival, Genting, Malaysia (2007) and "Paradise" with Macau Conservatory of Dance(2008).     In addition, the Ensemble has organised seminars, dance exchange programmes, dance camp, and overseas dance tours to upgrade the members  dance technique and knowledge. It has also invited international acclaimed choreographers and dance instructors from China, Taiwan and the United States to provide other form of dance training for the members.     The Ensemble is a recipient of the National Arts Council s Annual Grant and its lease at the present premise at 60 Waterloo Street under the National Arts Council s Arts Housing Scheme signifies recognition of its continued efforts to bring dance to the masses. In keeping with the objectives of promoting home grown talent and raising the overall artistic standard of dance in Singapore, DES plans to discover new talent from its other activity in arts education. In addition, DES ARTS hopes to train versatile artistes and ensure the sustainability of its professional dance company by improving the efficacy of its education programme from increased training facilities and widened arts education programme from the already well-established dance school, DES Performing Arts Academy.    DES will continue to promote Chinese culture, develop the arts and cultivate the younger generation in arts appreciation. In keeping with the lifelong learning culture, DES will widen its dance education programme for its students and dancers by expanding its syllabus to include martial arts, music and drama. This will create well-rounded artistes for the company and the arts scene in Singapore. Against this backdrop of a widened education syllabus, DES's participation in the National Arts Council's Arts Education Programme will benefit primary and secondary school children in Singapore.    Besides counting on local content, DES will continue to explore international opportunities to raise its profile, generate creative ideas and infuse groundbreaking dance experience. DES aims to collaborate with reputable dance organizations and practitioners in co-production of performances, workshops, exchange programmes, dance camps, and overseas dance tours. It will continue to invite international acclaimed choreographers and dance instructors from countries such as China, Taiwan and the United States. Dance Ensemble Singapore was established in July 1993 under the auspice of the National Arts Council. It evolved from the immensely successful Yan Choong Lian Dance Troupe. name: DRAMA BOX LTD.
search_text:
DRAMA BOX LTD. The objects for which the Company is established are :
(a) The advancement of the arts and culture in Singapore and staging and/or
supporting local and international quality theatre and cultural performances,
productions and events.
(b) To further the skills and training of local arts practitioners by creating opportunities
for them to be exposed to the international arts community.
(c) To promote and raise awareness of the arts amongst the general public. Drama Box believes that art is integral to our society, and plays an important part in building the resilience and maturity of the people. We create socially engaged work that inspires dialogue, reflection and change. We seek to tell stories that are immediate and reflective of our society, promoting a deeper understanding of Singapore’s culture, history and identity. The wisdom of the people, their faces and souls are what is behind the theatre we make.

Our approach to theatre-making is socially engaged. The engagement with the public on social issues inspires, drives and completes our work. In each artistic work, we create a creative and safe space for people to be deeply engaged with the art form as well as the content presented. We use different methodologies and approaches, creating work that involve artists as main creators, involving the related communities in the creation process, or both. We constantly push ourselves artistically, experimenting with new and multi-disciplinary artistic forms. name: DYSLEXIA ASSOCIATION OF SINGAPORE
search_text:
DYSLEXIA ASSOCIATION OF SINGAPORE AIMS

The company’s aims are:
a) to put quality first in delivering a comprehensive and effective professional service for people with dyslexia and other struggling learners;
b) to provide an assessment service for struggling learners;
c) to provide a remediation service for people with dyslexia and other struggling learners, including educational therapies;
d) to raise public and professional awareness of the nature, at risk factors and incidence of dyslexia and other learning struggles;
e) to enable others (teachers, parents and professionals) to help people with dyslexia and other struggling learners;
f) to support and elicit support for people with dyslexia and other struggling learners and their families;
g) to promote and carry out local research on dyslexia and other learning struggles and to disseminate results; and
h) to network with organisations with similar objectives in and outside Singapore.


GOAL

The goal of the Company is to establish a world class organization committed to empowering people with dyslexia and other struggling learners in Singapore. We are driven by a steadfast dedication to provide exceptional support, resources, and opportunities that enable these learners to flourish academically, emotionally, and socially.

At the core of our organisation lies a deep commitment to understanding and addressing the unique challenges faced by struggling learners. By employing evidence-based approaches, innovative interventions, and personalised strategies, we strive to unlock their untapped potential, fostering a sense of belonging, confidence, and purpose.

Our goal extends beyond providing individualised support. We actively cultivate a culture of inclusivity and advocacy, working closely with educators, parents, and the broader community to foster awareness, acceptance, and understanding of struggling learners. By partnering with stakeholders, we aim to influence policy, shape educational practices, and create an environment that values and supports the diverse needs of all learners.

Our ultimate aspiration is to be recognised as a global leader in empowering struggling learners. By championing their potential, we envision a future where every struggling learner can achieve their dreams and contribute meaningfully to society.

MISSION

The Mission Statement of the company is: Empowering people with dyslexia and struggling learners to achieve their true potential. In our efforts to continuously provide and improve our professional services to dyslexics in Singapore, we rely heavily on public donations to sustain our daily operations. name: Dhammakaya Centre (Singapore)
search_text:
Dhammakaya Centre (Singapore) To establish a Buddhist Mission where members can gather together to meditate so as to cultivate compassion & goodwill.

Dhammakaya Centre (Singapore), DCS, was formally registered a Buddhist Meditation Society in 15 Sep 1999. The main objective was to gather members to learn meditation and foster increase understanding on the benefits of meditation on individual physical and mental health, which are essential to building a happy and healthy family that can contribute to the community at large.

The main focus of the activities conducted by DCS is free meditation courses open to people living in Singapore that are interested to learn meditation, regardless of race, language & religions. Our uniquely simple approach to meditation that emphasises on relaxation proven to be easy and applicable to the needs and lifestyle of people in Singapore. Hence, there was exponential growth of the size and frequency of our meditation courses, from 30 pax per class/week at the beginning to 180 pax per class, twice a week at the present. Mission: To promote world peace through the practice of meditation

Vision: To promote in Singapore society a culture of virtue and morality irrespective of gender, race, language or religion Buddhism

name: Disabled People's Association
search_text:
Disabled People's Association The objectives of the DPA shall be:-

a) To encourage and enhance self-help and independent living among persons with disabilities in the community,

b) To facilitate, encourage and further advance the full participation and the equality of persons with disabilities in the community so as to enable them to enjoy the living conditions and improvements in the standard of living resulting from social and economic developments,

c) To do such other things that are incidental or conducive to the attainment of the above objectives as the General Meeting of Members of the Board of Management may from time to time decide, DPA shall:

i. work towards removing all architectural and attitudinal barriers and those barriers that would hinder the full participation of persons with disabilities in the educational, vocational, economic, social, cultural, sports and recreational life by making the necessary recommendations to the relevant authorities or organisations, by taking action to facilitate the full participation of all persons with disabilities, including those who are home-bound in the design, formulation, implementation and evaluation of policies, programmes, and services for their needs, and to monitor, evaluate and review such services; to also provide communication links with all persons with disabilities and to make accessible information about disability, its treatment, correction and prevention;

ii) ensure that the benefits of reform and development programmes in every field, nationally and internationally, also reach citizens with disabilities;

iii) promote or organise and/or to initiate the provision of cultural, educational/vocational, sports and recreational facilities, where none exists as well as social benevolent and other activities for the benefit and welfare of the persons with disabilities;

iv) affiliate with and to further the work or purpose of any national or international organisation having as its objectives the promotion of the interest, welfare and rights of persons with disabilities through social justice;

v) work closely and to provide consultative services on matters relating to persons with disabilities to Governmental and Non-Governmental Organisations engaged in the field of services to persons with disabilities; promote or undertake research in any aspect of prevention, habilitation, rehabilitation and equalisation of opportunities; organise training courses not for profit or commercial reasons for persons with and without disabilities concerned with programmes for persons with disabilities; organise conferences, seminars, study groups of workshops on subjects relevant to persons with disabilities;

vi) run, with the approval of the relevant authorities, business with a social mission to create work activities for people with disabilities and to assist them in achieving financial independence.

Disabled People’s Association (DPA) is Singapore’s only cross-disability non-profit organisation. We represent the disability community, working to build a fairer society where everyone can participate in all aspects of life from education to employment and access to social integration.

What We Do:
A Voice of Our Own: We are an organisation for people with disabilities run by people with disabilities. We listen to the needs of all persons with disabilities in Singapore, their allies, carers and other social service organisations that provide essential services and we ensure their needs are represented at a national level. We provide a platform for all people with disabilities to speak up and ensure their voices are heard, and we represent those less able or comfortable to speak for themselves.

How Do We Do It:
We help the disability community have a voice in society by working with decision-makers in political, commercial and educational institutions so that no decision that impacts us is made without us. We also provide training for both people with disabilities and the public, so we can work together to create an inclusive, accessible society we are all proud of.

Our Aims:
● DPA raises awareness of the needs of people with disabilities to empower people to live independent lives via access to education, transport, paid employment and community activities.
● DPA initiates constructive dialogue about disability so businesses and the public are conscious of the barriers that prevent people with disabilities being full and productive members of society and work with us to overcome them.
● Sharing research and best practice examples that promotes understanding and potential solutions to key issues faced by people with disabilities.
● We build public awareness of all types of disabilities, both visible and invisible. DPA regularly collaborates with like-minded organisations and has worked on various dynamic campaigns such as the nationwide See the True Me campaign in collaboration with the National Council of Social Services (NCSS). Disabled People's Association (DPA) is Singapore's only cross-disability non-profit organisation. Singapore is a destination country.   o	To empower Singaporeans, residents and visitors to take action to help to combat this heinous crime.	Our work is supported by the Singapore Government s InterAgency Taskforce against trafficking in person, and is in line with the National Plan of Action. Raising awareness is a preventive measure. Vision				  Everybody is born free, to live free.     Mission   Our mission is to combat human trafficking by raising awareness, advocating change and empowering communities, businesses and individuals to take action to end this horrific crime against humanity. While human trafficking is a global problem, our region of focus is the Asia-Pacific, with our base operations in Singapore.  Our mission is in line with the Singapore government's national plan to fight human trafficking both in Singapore and the region. An inter-agency task force was formed in 2011 to combat what has been described as  a threat to Singapore's security as well as its economic and social fabric . The national plan has a four-pronged approach, namely,  prevent, prosecute, protect and partnership.  Raising awareness is a preventive measure. name: EQUESTRIAN FEDERATION OF SINGAPORE (E.F.S)
search_text:
EQUESTRIAN FEDERATION OF SINGAPORE (E.F.S) 7. OBJECTS:

7.1. The objects of the EFS shall be as follows :

7.1.1. Promote, develop and increase participation for the sport of Equestrian in Singapore;

7.1.2. Promote physical activity for health and wellness, foster community engagement and bonding for social inclusiveness and integration, and inspire the Singapore spirit through the sport of Equestrian;

7.1.3. To engage communities and provide Equestrian access to vulnerable segments of the community such as youth at risk and the less privileged;

7.1.4. Unify, co-ordinate, sanction and organize Equestrian activities in Singapore, including national and international Equestrian tournaments and events;

7.1.5. Raise the competitive standards of Equestrian athletes in Singapore for sustainable elite level performance at international competitions and multi-sport major games;

7.1.6. Provide sport pathways and opportunities for the progression and advancement of Equestrian athletes, coaches and technical officials in Singapore; and

7.1.7. Raise the technical capability of Equestrian coaches and technical officials in Singapore.


To do all things complementary or incidental to attain the aforesaid objects in Articles 7.1.1. to 7.1.7.
O U R V I S I O N
To develop a Singapore equestrian community, with a deep interest in horses and horse sports, a culture of learning, excellence and achievement, and a strong sense of compassion and welfare, voluntarism and public service.

O U R M I S S I O N
Successful development of a structured pathway of learning and development for Equestrian athletes, coaches, officials and supporters that guides participants from beginners up to Olympic Games standards through competitions, grading, education, qualifications, promotion, scholarships and awards.

search_text: ESSEC BUSINESS SCHOOL To provide higher education to train students of all countries in business administration and more generally in disciplines related to management, including undergraduate, graduate, post graduate, doctoral and executive education.    To confer and award degrees, diplomas and certificates, including honorary degrees or distinctions in the related fields of studies. To institute professorships, lectureships and other posts and offices. To grant scholarships for students as the Board of Directors considers feasible and desirable.    To innovate by the renewal of teaching activities and methods.    To conduct research so as to advance and disseminate knowledge relating to business administration and management, to collect, prepare and distribute information and statistics relating to any type of business, industry or technology, to promote or propose such methods, procedures and measures as my consider appropriate. To produce works and publications of all description pertaining to the concepts and practice of management. To promote the exchange of knowledge and skills in the related field of management with business and industry.    To carry on all or any of the businesses of industrial, business, personnel and public services consultants and to advise on the extending, developing and improving of all types of businesses or industries and all systems and processes relating to the production, storage, distribution and marketing.    To educate business leaders by achieving intellectual influence and translating it into relevant learning for business communities, public or private. The mission of ESSEC Business School is to create and disseminate cutting-edge knowledge, to train and develop bold pioneers and influential leaders for both the business world and society as a whole. ESSEC Business School encourages students and participants in its programmes to anticipate – and meet – the economic, managerial, social, environmental and ethical challenges of an increasingly uncertain world. name: Eden Community Services Centre
search_text:
Eden Community Services Centre 3.1 Its objects are:-

a) To provide counseling services to children, youth, family and elderly inclusive of marital counseling.
b) To initiate, assist and organise such forms of relief and schemes of social service as would alleviate poverty and distress amongst the people of Singapore and beyond, irrespective of race, age, sex nationality or religion.
c) To promote, participate and provide social, emotional, intellectual, physical and financial assistance so as to fulfil all the above needs for mankind.
d) In furtherance of the above objects, the centre may do all such things which are incidental or conducive to the attachment of the objects, and particularly as follows.
I. To establish, maintain and manage, subject to the approval of the relevant authorities, a family service centre.

II. To establish, maintain and manage, subject to the approval of the relevant authorities, creche, kindergarten, child care centre and day care centre for the children, youth and elderly.

III. To cooperate with recognized bodies, religious organizations and other welfare agencies in conformity with the above objects.

IV. To raise, receive and administer or distribute funds, property or goods in kinds as are entrusted to it.

V. To rent or purchase property for use as centres or homes for children, aged, sick or other uses as may deem necessary for the pursuit of the objects of the centre.

VI. To provide School and Chartered Bus Services for the purposes of the society.
Our Vision
To build a gracious society with citizens of good character where every individual contributes actively to the well being of the community.
To celebrate Goodness, encourage Beautiful Living and to build a Glorious Nation.
“Character is Destiny” (Heraclitus)
Our Mission
Working in close partnership with parents, Eden will strive to help every parent and their children (as student) build strong families and every person become a self-directed learner with good character traits.
This is achieved through a systematic training approach in cultivating character excellence and healthy live skills to equip students for service to the community.

Our Core Values
The Management and staff of Eden believes in :

Excellence – We endeavour to give our best in all we do.

Dedication – We approach every day’s life (and work) with zest
and choose to live each day with joy.

Exemplary – We believe good character qualities are more
effectively caught than taught and we strive to serve
as good role models at all times.

Noble living- We celebrate every person’s desire to build a
virtuous life and to achieve their noble-aims in life.

Children/Youth Community Family

name: Emmanuel Reformed Bible College
search_text:
Emmanuel Reformed Bible College The College’s objects are to provide training to prepare godly men and women for the effectual dissemination of the pure and unadulterated Gospel of Jesus Christ and the vigilant defence of the faith both locally and globally to the saving of souls and the setting up and building up of sound, fundamental reformed churches of Christ worldwide. By teaching the inspired, inerrant, infallible Word of God efficiently and training in practical ministry, the College seeks to effectively equip, train and produce graduates who are called of the Lord and who will do the work of evangelists, missionaries, pastors, and theological teachers through the ministry of preaching, teaching, and application of the Word of God by the enabling power of the Holy Spirit.

The College shall not engage in activities outside Singapore which are not in accordance with the laws of the foreign country.

The College shall not engage in any activities that may undermine the racial or religious harmony in Singapore. Its mission is to glorify God by equipping and nurturing men and women called of the Lord in the full-time and lay ministry; offering a Bible-based theological education that is pastoral, practical, and Spirit-filled; and to fulfill the Great Commission of Jesus Christ by upholding and encouraging gospel evangelism, world missions, church growth and planting, pastoral and other ministries, with a Biblical and Christocentric emphasis. name: FAITH INDEPENDENT BAPTIST CHURCH LTD
search_text:
FAITH INDEPENDENT BAPTIST CHURCH LTD The objects for which the Church is established are:
A. Preach the Gospel of Jesus Christ, here, and throughout the world through missionaries.
B. Teach all things which Christ has commanded.
C. Defend the Word of God.
D. Declare the whole counsel of God.
E. To establish and maintain a place for the worship of the Almighty God and to provide for Christian fellowship for those of like faith.
F. To grant or participate in granting relief and aid to the sick, poor and needy particularly among the members of the Faith Independent Baptist Church Ltd or their families and others whether or not members of church as the Church Leadership Council may deem fit.
G. To promote education or participate in schemes calculated to promote education having a Christian emphasis and to establish and maintain an institution for the training of Christian missionaries who shall be entrusted with the extension of this Company whenever there may be a need. To glorify our Lord Jesus Christ by bringing the saints into a closer fellowship with God and with one another through the study of His Word and obedience to its precepts and by seeking every opportunity to evangelize the lost both in Singapore and around the world. name: FAITH MISSION HOME LTD
search_text:
FAITH MISSION HOME LTD To propagate the Christian faith (A) VISION OF FMH
FMH exists to plant church communities in Singapore that are committed to redeeming the world through redeeming all areas of life here and now by intentionally making disciples of Jesus Christ that make disciples to reflect the glory of God as lights of the world. It means being compelling witness of Jesus Christ in the Public Space Persuading others to be followers of Jesus Christ.


(B) MISSION OF FMH:
Towards this end (vision), as a Church, FMH will edify, equip and empower to cultivate its members to:

a. Be intentional about making the Gospel of Jesus Christ tangible and desirable in the social space of the Church (and its members) b. Be faithful in knowing and living by everything Jesus had taught c. Active in sharing our faith so that others will at least want to know Jesus.

name: FAITHACTS
search_text: FAITHACTS The object of the Society is to improve and strengthen the family and community life in Singapore.  To achieve the above objective, the Society may, subject to the prior approval from the relevant authorities, where necessary:  (a) serve the needy in the community through the provision of welfare services including child care, student care, tuition and educational services, youth development, the provision of financial assistance (including bursaries and loans), drop-in centre(s) and service centre(s);  (b) meet the needs of youths, whether through schools or institutions, the activities or programmes of the  Society or otherwise;  (c) provide an information and referral service to tap existing community resources for low income families and to enable the needs of service users to be dealt with by the most appropriate organisation;  (d) provide care, assistance and counselling to individuals and families in distress;  (e) organise educational and awareness programmes that will strengthen the family unit, enhance family life and meet the real needs of individuals;  (f) provide services that aim to help children from disadvantaged families upgrade themselves through tuition programmes, literacy programmes and enrichment programmes;  (g) develop and sustain the participation of volunteers in the programmes of the Society;	(h) develop networks and work in harmony with social and community organisations within and outside the neighbourhood, to better serve the needs of the service users;	(i) provide quality support services to meet the needs of identified target groups in the community;  (j) provide and promote a range of services which emphasises remedial, preventive and developmental programs for members of the family;  (k) promote active involvement of Christians in community work and welfare services so as to demonstrate the love of Christ in the community by living out the gospel so that Christ may be known;   (l) organise and carry out fund-raising projects and campaigns to support the activities of the Society;  (m) generally assist in the relief of poverty, suffering, ignorance or ill health, whether physical or mental, without limitation of age, sex, race, nationality, religion or moral character;  (n) do all things that are conducive, incidental or related to all or any of the objects of the Society. To provide quality and holistic services that address social issues with our distinctive Christian love that meet the changing needs of the community. FaithActs is a community care service that serves the needs of children with learning challenges, youths at-risk, disadvantaged families, and seniors at Queenstown. We aim to positively impact, cha... Children/Youth Community Eldercare Family Community development Education Healthcare Social service and welfare Sports

FAMILY RESOURCE AND TRAINING CENTRE FRTC s object is to enhance the functioning and wellness of professionals and families by providing the following services:
2.1 Training: To initiate, develop, coordinate and conduct training relevant to professionals, families and the community.
2.2 Public Education: To foster and enhance wellness of professionals and families through educational projects, programmes and activities.
2.3 Consultation and Professional Development : To provide consultancy services to professionals, groups and organisations in areas pertinent to personal and professional development, strategic planning, resource management, programme development,
research & evaluation and community needs assessment.

2.4 Research, Documentation and Evaluation To promote and conduct documentation and research as well as to develop resource materials.
2.5 Resource Centre To be a depository and function as a resource centre for publications, audio-visual and related materials on professional practice, the family and organisational professional development.
2.6 Continuing Professional Education To provide continuing professional education and related services for professional development.
2.7 Any Other Related Services To provide any other related services that will fulfill the mission of FRTC. The mission of the Family Resource and Training Centre (FRTC) is: To support, nurture and strengthen professionals in their work with families through leadership in training, public education, research, consultancy and advocacy . name: FOUNDATION FOR THE ARTS AND SOCIAL ENTERPRISE LIMITED
search_text:
FOUNDATION FOR THE ARTS AND SOCIAL ENTERPRISE LIMITED (a) To promote & support the initiation and development of arts & culture initiatives that shall advance the place of creative life in Singapore society & promote Singapore's emerging status as a hub in the global & Asian cultural stage;

(b) To nurture, develop & build on the cultural resources & capital existing in Singapore, particularly to encourage more original and innovative artistic ventures;

(c) To promote cultural exchanges between Singaporean & other nations using creative & performing arts as a medium & to initiate arts & cultural activities in encouraging the involvement of local artistes in forging Singapore's creative edge to the international arena;

d) To act as a platform, forum & network for promoting, establishing & carrying on the operation of events, conferences, conventions, meetings, programmes in any manner of creative arts, performances, theatres, musicals, dances performing arts of all description in Asia & elsewhere & to undertake national, regional or international projects of an all-inclusive nature which have the objectives of providing a platform to engage & improve the creativity, vibrancy, quality of life & enthusiasm of arts & culture initiatives with shared values of compassion, community, cohesion, contribution & commitment towards developing creative arts environment;

e) To establish a network whereby organisations & individuals of like mindedness are engaged in united & co-operative efforts in alliance by promoting public awareness & facilitating, co-ordinating events, conventions, seminars, meetings, exhibitions & programmes through all means of communications, strategic networking & partnership with individuals, organisations, corporations, associations, government bodies & other local authorities in undertaking community projects & outreach in arts & culture;

(f) To aid, sponsor & fund the advancement of education, arts & cultural activities through grants, award, support & assist in providing bursaries scholarships in education, knowledge creation, morality & culture for Singaporeans in the communities & society at large with charitable concern to raise fund in aid of philanthropic work;

(g) To act as proprietors, operators, promoters, advisors, consultants, organisers & managers of all kinds of musical carnivals, festivals, entertainment, theatre, plays, performances, stage & variety shows, conferences, corporate events & public facilities of every description for the furtherance of the objects of the company & to produce recordings, albums, musicals, songs, dances, plays & performing arts in connection with the objects of the company & to raise funds for charitable & philanthropic purposes;

(h) To train, teach, develop & equip individuals, workers, volunteers, participating groups & organisations for like-minded charitable initiatives in all aspects of organising, managing & conduct of events, conventions, conferences, programme, education & training & providing training resources, & for that purpose to conduct classes, hold courses or otherwise provide facilities & opportunities for training, instruction, education & development of the activities for the furtherance of the objects of the company; The vision of the Foundation is aligned with Singapore's national objectives to become an arts hub for Asia as well as globally. The mission is to fill the gap between the government agencies (which provides grants and other support) and the emerging artistes in Singapore's cultural scene. The Foundation hopes to directly nurture the up and coming artistes who have the talent and potential but not the capability to develop themselves in the greater arts scene.

Foundation for the Arts & Social Enterprise (FASE) will be driven by four primary program thrusts or directions: directly promoting arts and culture in the local context, ranging from jazz to other music genre and other artistic endeavours; through these projects, encourage a greater Singapore experience by community building and engagement ; focus on educational initiatives such as scholarships; and finally, foster a network of visionary leaders with the spirit of social enterprise and arts philanthropy. And this is just the start; as our program areas grow, so will the scope and reach of the organisation. The Foundation for The Arts and Social Enterprise (The Foundation) converges the arts and business with philanthropy. An Institutions of Public Character (IPC) charitable arts organization, The Fou... Literary Arts Music & Orchestras Training & Education Visual Arts Arts, culture and heritage

name: FRANCISCAN MISSIONARIES OF MARY
search_text: FRANCISCAN MISSIONARIES OF MARY To share and touch lives with Christ's love, praying and witnessing to the gospel in peace and integrity of creation.    To provide various educational, medical, pastoral and social services to the under-privileged, the poor and the needy regardless of race, religion or status. We, Franciscan Missionaries of Mary choose to share in the life of the people to whom we are sent.  Since the foundation of our congregation, this meant at times being sent to the most distant and dangerous places of mission where Christ is least known.  Prayer and witnessing to the Gospel form the main focus of our lives.  We collaborate with those who seek the truth, the empowerment of women especially those underprivileged, the promotion of justice, Peace and Integrity of Creation, so making real the Reign of God. Christianity

name: FRANCISCAN SISTERS OF MARY (SINGAPORE) LTD.
search_text:
FRANCISCAN SISTERS OF MARY (SINGAPORE) LTD. The objects and purposes for which the Company is constituted are exclusively charitable, being the following:

1. to announce, instruct, promote and spread the love of Jesus Christ amongst all persons especially to the poor, the needy and/or the marginalized following after the example of Jesus Christ, Blessed Mary of the Passion and in the spirit of St Francis of Assisi;

2. to provide spiritual, physical, emotional and charitable relief to the poor, the sick, the needy, the aged and/or the infirmed, irrespective of their race or religion;

3. to foster and promote the Christian way of life, especially its principles of universal love, almsgiving, peace, mutual respect, mercy and understanding and to provide education and formation in the spirit of St. Francis of Assisi;

4. to initiate, assist and/or organize such forms of relief and schemes of service (educational, medical, spiritual or other welfare works) to help alleviate the suffering of the poor, the underprivileged, the old, the sick, the destitute, the marginalized and/or anyone in need regardless of one's race or religion;

5. to relieve persons or children who are in conditions of need, hardship or distress, whether or not by reason of their physical, social or economic circumstances, and to provide such charitable purposes for the general welfare of all persons or children of all races or religion resident in the Republic of Singapore or elsewhere; and

6. to provide, support for the life and missions of the provinces/regions of the Franciscan Missionaries of Mary throughout the world. The mission of the Franciscan Sisters of Mary is to reveal the face of God’s love through actions that are humanizing towards those who suffer and are on the margins. We commit ourselves, with others, to the care of creation and to the service of peace, justice and healing, where it is most needed. Christianity

name: FRONTIER DANCELAND LTD.
search_text:
FRONTIER DANCELAND LTD. (a) To plan, develop and promote the development and appreciation of contemporary dance in Singapore. (b) To conduct workshops, courses, programmes and activities to encourage the formulation of creative dance in Singapore. (c) To provide training for talents and to groom such talents by creating opportunities for them. (d) To publish or distribute information relevant to its objectives. (e) To do all such other things as are incidental or conducive to the attainment of the above objects or any of them provided that nothing shall be done solely for profit. VISION STATEMENT

Frontier Danceland aims to be a versatile contemporary dance company that creates, produces, and exposes our local and overseas audiences to a spectrum of artistically excellent dance works. We strive to inspire the future generation of dancers, choreographers and art lovers by contributing to a culture that we envision to be permeated by a love for movement.


MISSION STATEMENT

Frontier Danceland is committed to creating a sustainable dance platform for Singaporean dance artists, whether locally or internationally trained, cultivating the passion for experimental, relevant and accessible dance creation and performance.

As a company that is constantly evolving, Frontier Danceland is devoted to being a hub for international choreographers to stop by for artist-in-residence periods, such that diverse contemporary dance works are created for our company artists, who are trained to be versatile dancers.

Through local performances and overseas tours, we believe in exposing our local audience to a wide array of contemporary dance works created by both local and international choreographers, and in sharing local creations with our international audience.

We are dedicated to nurturing young local talent by offering quality training programmes that will not only develop the younger generation’s skills, but also build up their appreciation of contemporary dance, such that the love for this art form will spread to their peers and positively influence the future quality of life.



Founded in 1991 by Low Mei Yoke and Tan Chong Poh, Frontier Danceland is a professional contemporary dance company in Singapore that became full-fledged in 2011. Committed to cultivating versatilit... Professional, Contemporary & Ethnic Dance Arts, culture and heritage Community development Education Social service and welfare

name: Fa Hua Monastery
search_text:
Fa Hua Monastery 3.1 The Monastery s objectives are: a) To propagate, teach and disseminate the teachings of the Buddha Dharma in accordance with the teachings of Sakyamuni Guatama Buddha. b) In furtherance of the above object but not otherwise, the Monastery may:- (i) promote activities conducive to Buddhist education, civilization, benevolent affairs and to develop and expand Buddhism. (ii) provide a place of worship and also to propagate and help believers with the teaching and practice of Buddhism. (iii) create awareness by conducting Dharma discussions and meditation classes to achieve spiritual insight. (iv) set up a fund for promoting the works of Buddhism which will generate human compassion and self-enduring determination. (v) establish and support, or aid in the establishment and support or associations, institutions, funds or trusts calculated to disseminate the Buddhist religion or to benefit the aged, destitute, the disabled and to give donations for any charitable and patriotic purposes which promote the welfare of mankind or provide relief from pain and suffering. (vi) do all such other acts, whether alone or in conjunction with others, which may aid in the achievement of these objectives. MISSION : To build a temple with a strong spiritual atmosphere of faith and purity, where devotees are united together with one heart and one voice; and where devotees hearts are filled with peace, joy and happiness. To spread the word of Buddha and preach Buddha's teachings so that devotees can live blessed lives and be peaceful at heart which will impact and help others.
VISION : To.spread the education and practice of body and mind, promote the practice of Chinese Buddhist Tantra, enhance the purification of body and mind and the communication of Buddhism. Buddhism

name: Free Presbyterian Church of Scotland (Singapore)
search_text:
Free Presbyterian Church of Scotland (Singapore) The government of the Church is Presbyterial, i.e. the plurality of elders. The Kirk Session has the spiritual oversight and government of the local Congregation. Meanwhile, the Deacons’ Court shall administer the Church's finances and other temporal affairs. The Deacons’ Court shall consist of duly elected deacons of the Congregation. In cases of petitions or complaints, the matters are brought before the next higher Church Court which is the Presbytery. Singapore belongs to the Asia Pacific Presbytery (previously called  Australia & New Zealand Presbytery) and falls under the jurisdiction of this Presbytery. The highest Church Court in ruling cases will be the Synod in Scotland. The basis of church government is based on the Word of God which is contained in the Scriptures of the Old and New Testaments. For the practical and procedural aspects of the church government the local Constitution, Westminster Confession and the Manual of Practice will be used. The objectives for which the Church is established are:-
- The salvation of souls;
- The edification of Christians through teaching of God’s Word;
- Promotion of godly worship and holy living;
- The preservation and upholding of “the faith which was once delivered unto the saints”;
- The establishment and extension of Christian missions for the furtherance of God’s Kingdom;
- The promotion of Christian relief for the poor and needy;
- All the objectives being subservient to the primary objective of glorifying God.

For more information please refer to the Church website: www.fpchurch.org.uk.
Christianity

search_text: Fu Hui Buddhist Cultural Centre 3(1)	To propagate the Buddhist teachings as a foundamental religious   practice, to cultivate virtues and promote family happiness among its members   as well as the community; name: GARDEN CITY FUND
search_text:
GARDEN CITY FUND The Objectives of the Trust are:
(i) to enhance the enjoyment and appreciation of the Garden City for amenities and special features at the national parks, nature reserves and green areas in Singapore;
(ii) to support projects, community activities and other programmes that promote and enhance nature and ecology, art and culture, landscape and horticulture, animal and greenery management, animal health and welfare, and conservation;
and
(iii) to cultivate a strong interest in the environment and promote a sense of ownership in Singapore's nature heritage. Vision: Let’s Make Singapore our City in Nature

Garden City Fund supports projects that complement NParks’ efforts towards fulfilling its City in Nature vision through:
• Optimising the green spaces in Singapore
• Supporting the urban biodiversity conservation model
• Engaging the community
• Enhancing competencies of the landscape industry in Singapore

Through partnerships with organisations and individuals, GCF provides a platform for the community to be involved in conservation, research, outreach and education initiatives. By engaging the community, we hope to cultivate a love for the environment and promote a sense of ownership in our natural heritage.

Established by the National Parks Board (NParks) in 2002, Garden City Fund is managed independently as a charity, where 100% of donations raised go towards projects it supports. The charity aims to... Environment Environmental protection and sustainability

search_text: GARDENS BY THE BAY The objects for which Gardens by the Bay is established are all or any of the following:    (1) To develop and manage the Gardens by the Bay as a premier leisure destination, to provide a world-class horticultural exposition and an edutainment center, to provide a green space for public enjoyment, to identify, develop,  organise and promote activities, events, and public education programmes, including guided tours for schools and the public.	  (2) To promote and support the advancement of botany, horticulture, conservation and environmental science whether directly or together with other bodies.	  (3) To promote and advance the "City in a Garden" vision in collaboration with the National Parks Board by creating a sense of community ownership through the provision of horticultural experiences and signature recreational spaces for Singaporeans.
