name: GENERATION: YOU EMPLOYED, SINGAPORE LTD.
search_text:
GENERATION: YOU EMPLOYED, SINGAPORE LTD. Generation is an Education-to-Employment, Not-for-Profit Company, founded by McKinsey & Company in 2014. It is the largest and fastest growing Youth Employment organization in the world, with 13 global locations and over 30,000 graduates as of July 2019.

www.generation.org

In each market we:
1. Identify at-need individuals/populations who are struggling to find a meaningful, career-launching job

2. Work with employers to identify roles which they are finding hard to fill or hard to retain good talent in (e.g. digital marketing)

3. Build customized 'bootcamps' of 6-12 weeks duration to intensively train at-need individuals for these specific roles

4. Match graduates into these roles with partner employers

5. Mentor, support, and monitor their progress on-the-job for 6-12 months

Generation began work in Singapore in Feb 2018. We are working in partnership with SkillsFuture Singapore (SSG), all 5 Polytechnics, and the three ITE campuses to educate and employ individuals in 3 custom built bootcamps - Digital Marketing, Customer Service Associate, and Engineering 4.0. In doing so, we also have the joint goal of embedding our employment-focused methodology and ethos into these Institutes of Higher Education.

Our program is co-Branded with SSG as the Work-Learn Bootcamp (WLB).

https://www.skillsfuture.sg/worklearnbootcamp


Generation Singapore is also establishing new programs in partnership with SSG, WorkForce Singapore (WSG), and other private organizations to expand the impact on needful job-seekers in Singapore. Vision:
A career-launching job for every person, anywhere in the world

Mission:
Empower people to build thriving, sustainable careers and provide employers the highly-skilled, motivated talent they need Generation Singapore is an affiliate of Generation: You Employed, a global nonprofit that supports people to achieve economic mobility through employment. name: GERMAN EUROPEAN SCHOOL SINGAPORE (DEUTSCHE EUROPAEISCHE SCHULE SINGAPUR)
search_text:
GERMAN EUROPEAN SCHOOL SINGAPORE (DEUTSCHE EUROPAEISCHE SCHULE SINGAPUR) a. The objectives of the Association are:
i. To establish, run and operate the “German European School Singapore – Deutsche Europäische Schule Singapur”, (‘GESS’), a school for the purpose of providing education in the German language as well as in one of the official languages of Singapore, for the purpose of allowing an education based on German and on international educational targets, curricula and final exams, and for imparting European values and to forge links with the different cultures of Singapore.
ii. To provide pupils with the suitable preschool, primary and secondary and (if appropriate) dual vocational training education with the up-to-date educational methods and principles and to prepare them for final examinations recognised in Germany and respectively other internationally recognised examinations. The integration of the German pupils into the educational system of the Federal Republic of Germany must always be possible.
iii. To familiarise pupils with local languages and cultures as well as to further human and cultural relations outside GESS through extra-curricular activities so as to promote mutual understanding and generally provide a comprehensive and favourable impression of the Federal Republic of Germany.
b. The Association shall have the powers to carry out the following activities and services in furtherance of its objectives:
i. To provide facilities for the education and development of the mental, physical and moral characters of the pupils.
ii. To provide courses and programmes to develop the pupils’ whole personality, their mental and physical health and shall inculcate in them the spirit of tolerance and understanding and appreciation of international affairs in order to promote peace and goodwill in the world.
iii. To purchase or otherwise acquire land and to build thereon - GESS and any additions and improvements thereto and to furnish and equip GESS with such furniture, equipment and educational aids as may be necessary.
iv. To borrow such sum or sums of money as the Association at a General Meeting may approve and to provide security therefore by mortgage or otherwise.
v. To undertake fund-raising.
vi. To offer extra-curricular activities.
vii. To undertake matters furthering the welfare and education of the pupils and staff of GESS.
viii. To act as a channel through which parents may express their opinion and desires through the Board of Governors to the principal and the teachers of GESS.
ix. Subject to this Constitution and any laws in force in Singapore for the time being, to accept not only pupils of German nationality but also those of all other Nationalities.
x. To enter into any arrangements with any government or authority, supreme, municipal, local, or otherwise, that may seem conducive to the Association’s objects, or any of them; and to obtain from any such government or authority any rights, privileges, and concessions which the Association may think it desirable to obtain; and to carry out, exercise, and comply with any such arrangements, rights, privileges, and concessions.
xi. To apply for, secure, acquire by grant, legislative enactment, assignment, transfer, purchase, or otherwise, and to exercise, carry out, and enjoy any charter, licence, power, authority, franchise, concession, right or privilege, which any government or authority, or any corporation or other public body may be empowered to grant.
xii. To invest and deal with the money of the Association not immediately required in such manner as may from time to time be thought fit.
xiii. To purchase, take on lease or in exchange, hire, or otherwise acquire any movable or immovable property and any rights or privileges which the Association may think necessary or convenient for the purposes of its objects.
xiv. To sell, improve, manage, develop, exchange, lease, dispose of, turn to account, or otherwise deal with all or any part of the property and rights of the Association.
xv. To carry on any other activity which may seem to the Association capable of being conveniently carried on in connection with its objects.
xvi. To do all such other things as are incidental or conducive to the attainment of the objects and the exercise of the powers of the Association.
OUR MISSION & VISION
MISSION
Giving our students roots in German and European cultures, we are a community school and, through excellent education, empower our students to contribute positively to tomorrow’s world.

VISION
Giving our students wings, we aspire to develop them into life-long learners who will be successful and make a difference.

Guiding Statements
Celebrating German and European roots
GESS is a high-quality international school that celebrates German and European roots and values.

Empowering our Students
GESS empowers all students to become knowledgeable, skillful and resilient individuals to reach their full potential.

Holistic and Humanistic Education
GESS offers a holistic and humanistic education, a choice of curriculum, with social responsibility, in pursuit of excellence.

Prepare Students to become Global Citizens
GESS prepares students to successfully navigate a changing world as global citizens to create a sustainable future.

Be a True Partner for your Family
GESS is a non-profit community school and a true partner for your family. Foreign Educational Institutions/Funds

name: GESS FUND LTD.
search_text: GESS FUND LTD. (I) TO SUPPORT AND ASSIST THE BOARD OF GOVERNORS OF THE GERMAN EUROPEAN SCHOOL SINGAPORE (HEREINAFTER REFERRED TO AS "THE BOARD") TO ENCOURAGE AND FOSTER THE INTEREST AND FINANCIAL SUPPORT OF THE PAST STUDENTS, PARENTS OF STUDENTS, INDUSTRY PARTNERS AND FRIENDS OF THE GERMAN EUROPEAN SCHOOL SINGAPORE (HEREINAFTER REFERRED TO AS "GESS") THE TO PRESERVE IMPROVE AND DEVELOP ITS STANDARDS SERVICES PROPERTY AND FACILITIES AND IN PARTICULAR AND WITHOUT LIMITING THE GENERALITY OF THE FOREGOING;  (A) TO EXTEND INVITATIONS TO PAST STUDENTS, PARENTS OF STUDENTS, INDUSTRY PARTNERS AND FRIENDS OF GESS TO BECOME MEMBERS OF THE FOUNDATION AND THROUGH SUCH MEMBERSHIP TO PARTICIPATE AND JOIN MORE ACTIVELY AND EFFECTIVELY IN SUPPORTING AND ASSISTING THE BOARD TO PROMOTE AND CARRY OUT THE ACTIVITIES OF GESS;	(B) TO ENCOURAGE THE MAKING OF GIFTS AND TESTAMENTARY DISPOSITIONS TO OR FOR THE BENEFIT OF GESS OR ANY OF ITS FUNDS OR ACCOUNTS OR ANY TRUSTS OR FUNDS ESTABLISHED TO BENEFIT GESS;  (C) TO ENLIST THE SUPPORT INCLUDING THE FINANCIAL SUPPORT OF PAST STUDENTS, PARENTS OF STUDENTS, INDUSTRY PARTNERS AND FRIENDS OF GESS FOR ACTIVITIES CALCULATED TO IMPROVE THE EDUCATION PROVIDED BY GESS INCLUDING THE ENGAGEMENT OF SPECIALISTS WITH QUALIFICATIONS WHICH ENABLE THEM TO CONTRIBUTE TO THE EDUCATION OF THE STUDENTS AND THE TEACHING STAFF, THE ACQUISITION OF BOOKS, WORKS OF ART, PLANT AND EQUIPMENT, LAND AND BUILDINGS AND THE CONSTRUCTION AND MAINTENANCE OF BUILDINGS AND FACILITIES;  (D) TO RAISE MONEY FOR THE PURPOSES AFORESAID BY ANY METHOD THAT SEEMS DESIRABLE (INCLUDING THE UNDERTAKING OF OR PARTICIPATING IN COMMERCIAL VENTURES OF ALL KINDS);	(II) TO PROMOTE THE COOPERATION BETWEEN GESS AND SCHOOLS IN SINGAPORE TO EXCHANGE KNOWLEDGE AND TEACHING TECHNIQUES BY COMMON STUDENT ACTIVITIES AND OTHER MEANS OF EXCHANGE;  (III) TO PROVIDE SCHOLARSHIPS TO STUDENTS OF GESS IN ACCORDANCE OF GESS S ADMISSION AND ENROLMENT POLICIES  (IV) TO INVEST AND DEAL WITH THE MONEY OF THE FOUNDATION IN SUCH MANNER AS MAY BE DEEMED APPROPRIATE IN FURTHERANCE OF THE ADVANCEMENT EDUCATION;  (V) TO ACT AS TRUSTEE OF TRUSTS AND FUNDS WHICH MAY BE ESTABLISHED FOR THE BENEFIT OF GESS;  (VI) TO DO ALL SUCH THINGS AS ARE INCIDENTAL OR CONDUCTIVE TO THE ATTAINMENT OF THE ABOVE OBJECTS AND POWERS OR ANY OF THEM; AND  (VII) TO CARRY ON ANY EXCLUSIVELY CHARITABLE OBJECTS RELATING TO ADVANCEMENT OF EDUCATION PERMITTED UNDER SINGAPORE LAW. The GESS Fund works alongside GESS to enhance our children’s experience above and beyond the classroom and provides for extra equipment, better technology and innovative co-curricular programmes on top of the quality education offered. It forges strategic partnerships with institutions and corporate partners and raises funds for the benefit of children at GESS.  Others; Foreign educational institutions/funds

name: GRACE COVENANT CHURCH LIMITED
search_text:
GRACE COVENANT CHURCH LIMITED 3. The Church is a Christian organisation of a purely and exclusively religious, educational and charitable character. The objects for which the Church is being established (the “Objects”) are:-
3.1 to establish and maintain a place for the worship of God in Trinity – our Heavenly Father, Lord Jesus Christ and the Holy Spirit;
3.2 to promote Christian fellowship;
3.3 to shepherd the believers so that they may know God and be like God;
3.4 to have worship services together;
3.5 to make God known;
3.6 to preach the Gospel in order that men may be brought to faith in the Lord Jesus Christ to fulfill all the commands which the Lord Jesus Christ has entrusted to Christian believers, particularly to make disciples of all nations;
3.7 to conduct its own church services and programs to the standard of the Holy Bible;
3.8 on a non-profit making basis, to support churches, hospitals, social welfare service centers, and other institutions and missionaries financially by allocating, assigning or designating the funds, moneys and properties of the Church for their use in connection with one or more of their operation, management and religious or charitable activities;
3.9 to undertake, organise, support, sponsor and promote charitable activities and projects of all kinds for the benefit of the community in Singapore and other countries as are created or intended for the honouring and glorifying of the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, for the support of other Christians and/or Christian organisations and for relieving persons who are in conditions of need, hardship or distress from disaster or economic circumstances;
3.10 make payment or donations to such persons who deserve help and assistance, or to such charitable organisations or institutions for the furtherance of religion, education, or social welfare on such terms as may be determined by the Church provided that such charitable organisations or institutions shall prohibit the distribution of their income and property amongst their members to an extent at least as great as is imposed on the Church under or by virtue of Clause 6.2 hereof;

name: Galilee Bible-Presbyterian Church
search_text:
Galilee Bible-Presbyterian Church ARTICLE 5 OF CONSTITUTIONS: PRINCIPLES OF GOVERNMENT

The Church, in setting forth the form of government which it maintains as being founded upon and agreeable to the Word of God, reiterates, by way of introduction, several cardinal principles which are basic to and regulative of its form of church government.

5.1 “God alone is Lord of the conscience” and “hath left it free from the doctrine and commandments of men, which are in any thing contrary to His Word, or beside it in matters of faith or worship”. Therefore we consider the rights of private judgment, in all matters that respect religion, as universal and inalienable. We do not even wish to see any religious institution aided by the civil power further than may be necessary for protection and security and, at the same time, be equal and common to all others.

5.2 In perfect consistency with the above principle of common right every Christian church, or union or association of Christian churches is entitled to declare the terms of admission into its communion and the qualifications of its ministers and members, as well as the whole system of its internal government which Christ has appointed. In the exercise of this right it may, notwithstanding, err in making the terms of communion either too lax or too narrow; yet, even in this case, it does not infringe upon the liberty or the rights of others, but only makes an improper use of its own liberty and rights.

5.3 Our blessed Saviour, for the edification of the visible Church, which is His body, appointed officers, not only to preach the Gospel and administer the Sacraments but also to exercise discipline for the preservation both of truth and duty; it is incumbent upon these officers and upon the whole Church, in whose name they act, to censure or cast out the erroneous and scandalous, observing in all cases the rules contained in the Word of God.

5.4 Truth leads to goodness; the great touchstone of truth is its tendency to promote holiness; according to our Saviour’s rule, “by their fruits ye shall know them”. No opinion can be either more pernicious or more absurd than that which brings truth and falsehood upon a level and represents it as of no consequence what a man’s opinions are. On the contrary, we are persuaded that there is an inseparable connection between faith and practice, truth and duty; otherwise it would be of no consequence either to discover truth or to embrace it.

5.5 Under the conviction of the above principle, we think it necessary to make effectual provision that all who are admitted as teachers be sound in the faith. We also believe that there are truths and forms with respect to which men of good character and principles may differ. And in all these we think it the duty both of private Christians and societies to exercise mutual forbearance toward each other.

5.6 Though the character, qualifications, and authority of church officers are laid down in the Holy Scriptures, as well as the proper method of their investiture and institution, yet the election of persons to the exercise of this authority, in any particular society, is in that society.

5.7 All church power, whether exercised by the body in general or in the way of representation by delegated authority, is only ministerial and declarative; that is to say, the Holy Scriptures are the only rule of faith and conduct; no church court ought to pretend to make laws to bind the conscience in virtue of its own authority; all its decisions should be founded upon the revealed will of God. Now though it will be easily admitted, that all synods and councils may err through the frailty inseparable from humanity, yet, there is much greater danger from the usurped claim of making laws than from the right of judging upon laws already made and common to all who profess the Gospel, although this right, as necessity requires in the present state, be lodged with fallible men.

5.8 If the preceding Scriptural and rational principles are steadfastly adhered to, the vigour and strictness of its discipline will contribute to the glory and happiness of any church. Since ecclesiastical discipline must be purely moral or spiritual in its object and not accompanied by any civil effects, it can derive no force whatever but from its own justice, the approbation of an impartial public, and the countenance and blessing of the great Head of the Church universal.
ARTICLE 3 OF CONSTITUTIONS: OBJECTS

The objects for which the Church is established are: -

3.1 The salvation of souls;

3.2 The edification of Christians through the teaching of God’s Word;

3.3 The promotion of godly worship;

3.4 The defence of “the faith which was once delivered unto the saints”;

3.5 The establishment and extension of Christian missions and institutions for the furtherance of God’s Kingdom on earth;

3.6 The promotion of Christian relief for the poor and needy;

3.7 The strengthening of mutual fellowship, encouragement and unity among individual Bible Presbyterian Churches affiliated to the Bible Presbyterian Church of Singapore and among other Bible believing churches of similar stand “for the Word of God and for the testimony of Jesus Christ” (Rev 1:9).

The Church’s vision is “Reaching the World for Christ”. Our motto is: “For to me to live is Christ, and to die is gain.” (Philippians 1:21).

The mission of the Church is summed up in 3 areas:
1. Obedience to Christ’s Command: To go into all nations, making disciples to Jesus Christ, baptizing them by immersion in water and teaching them to obey the doctrines of Christ.
2. Continuing Reproduction of Christ’s Church: To evangelize and establish churches for Christ.
3. Exemplifying Christ’s Love and Compassion: To be a tangible living example of the love and compassion of Jesus Christ and to cooperate with the government without compromising the objectives and primary purpose of the Church mission. Christianity

search_text:
HCA HOSPICE LIMITED The objects for which the Company is established shall be exclusively charitable and are:
(a) to help persons with life limiting illnesses (“patients”) and their families, in Singapore, in the following manner:
(i) to encourage, provide or assist in caring for patients and/or their family members in Singapore according to the principles and practice of hospice care;
(ii) to provide or help in providing financial and social assistance to needy patients and/or their families;
(b) to advance and uphold a high standard of hospice care by:
(i) providing or helping to provide training for doctors, nurses, paramedical and lay persons in all aspects of hospice care;
(ii) providing or helping to provide financial, social assistance and emotional and spiritual support to persons engaged in the provision of hospice care, including officers, employees and volunteers of the Company;
(c) to promote greater awareness of hospice care among the general public and educate related professionals on the current concepts of hospice care;
(d) to promote, financially support and/or conduct research into all aspects of hospice care;
(e) to work closely with existing and future hospices and, where necessary, to support the setting up of new hospices and hospice care services in Singapore;
(f) to establish, operate and maintain facilities for the provision of hospice care in Singapore; and
(g) to engage/participate in all such lawful activities as are or may be necessary, incidental and/ or conducive to the attainment of the Company's objects. Vision - To be the centre of excellence for Hospice Home Care.
Mission - We will ensure the best quality of life for our patients by delivering professional palliative care and providing compassionate support for their families.
- We will nurture the dedicated individuals who make our work possible and serve our community through continued learning and development.
Values - Compassion, Professionalism, Respect, Integrity and Collaboration. name: HEARTWARE NETWORK
search_text:
HEARTWARE NETWORK 3.1 The object of the Society is to promote a culture in Singapore with shared heart values of compassion, community, cohesion, contribution and commitment towards life pursuits, social concern and welfare of mankind on charitable and benevolent principles.

3.2 In furtherance of the above object and provided that nothing is done solely for profit or for commercial reasons, the Society shall:
(a) Establish a network whereby organizations, communities and individuals of like-mindedness are engaged in united and co-operative efforts in alliance.
(b) Provide a forum for the meeting of social organizations, community groups and individuals for the building of social capital or "heartware" in Singapore and
elsewhere by promoting or participate in promoting social values and concerns,
welfare and awareness programmes; by adopting a pluralistic approach for exchange of ideas and leadership to raise public and volunteers' awarenes through strategic and innovative community activities and by providing for support and finance of the projects and programmes.
(c) Promote and advance public education and awareness programmes on community
needs and social responsibility through all means of communication and strategic
networking and partnership in undertaking community projects and outreach to all
sectors of the communities in providing and granting relief, aid and community
services to the delinquents, destitute, orphans, elderly, emotionally troubled, sick,
poor and needy people of the community and generally to promote social and welfare programmes, referral and counseling services, facilitation and support and
to establish support hostels and orphanages.
(d) Encourage volunteerism and charitable involvement in programmes and projects
undertaken by the Society and to inspire like-minded initiatives among the
individuals, volunteers mainly youths and young adults of the community at large
for involvement in social concerns, community services and charity works on
local and at international levels and generally to facilitate the attachment of
volunteers to large scale national and international projects and to provide
consulting services to participating organization.
(e) Develop, inspire, equip and train youths, young adults, volunteers, participating
groups and organizations for like-minded initiatives and effective leadership in
social and community services through instruction, training, role-modeling, talks,
seminars, workshops, conferences, conventions and participate in schemes
calculated to promote education having social concerns, heartware values based
on compassion, community, cohesion, contribution and commitment; and to
encourage them to maintain a long-term partnership in co-operation with
individuals, voluntary groups and organization for a lasting community impact.
(f) Promote and advance community safety and security awareness through
organizing Home Team and national agency-related projects that are aligned to
the overall objectives of the Society.
(g) Promote and participate in fund-raising projects with other charitable
organizations.
(h) Perform due diligence on designated charities to ensure the funds raised will be
optimized towards defraying operating costs and program development.
(i) Provide for support and finance of the projects and programmes.
(j) Provided that nothing is done solely for profit or for commercial reasons, publish,
print, produce, deal in and distribute books, literature, tracts, magazines,
newspapers, newsletters, periodicals or leaflets, cinematography films, records,
compact disc, audio and video cassettes, tapes and all other apparatus that the
Society may deem desirable for the declaration, promotion or advancement of its
objects.
(k) Erect and maintain buildings, social halls, business offices, school buildings,
recreational facilities, hostels and such other structures as are deemed necessary
for the purpose of the Society and to organize and arrange for the upkeep and
maintenance of the buildings and facilities.
(l) Receive and take any gift of money or property for any of the purposes and
objects of the Society whether subject to any special trust or not but the Society may nevertheless decline to accept any gift or donations, or to take over any
property which has been offered to the Society of which has annexed to it any
condition or obligation not approved by the Society.
(m) Establish and execute any trust, the undertaking whereof may seem desirable
either gratuitously or otherwise.
(n) Provide, engage and employ workers, staff, secretaries, agents, solicitors, doctors,
servants and other persons for the purpose of carrying out the objects of the
Society or any of them.
3.3 The property, funds and other income of the Society shall be applied solely in promoting the objects thereof and irrevocably dedicated to charitable, community and educational purposes. name: HEB - ASHRAM HALFWAY HOUSE
search_text:
HEB - ASHRAM HALFWAY HOUSE The objectives of HEB-Ashram Halfway House are: (a) To assist in the rehabilitation and reintegration into society of penal offenders, probationers, drug addicts, residents and clients of halfway houses who are Singaporeans/Permanent Residents from the Hindu/Indian and other communities in Singapore: (b) In furtherance of the above objectives, HEB-Ashram Halfway House, with the concurrence of HEB. may do all such things which are incidental or conducive to the attainment of the objective, and particularly as follows: (i) rehabilitation programmes including but not limited to those based on religious (Hindu) teachings; (ii) social counseling programmes and case work management to address the psvchosocial. physiological, emotional and social needs of" clients, and where appropriate or to the extent possible, that of their family members; (iii) pre-care, in-care (prison counseling) and after-care programmes for clients to address the preventive, developmental and re-integrative aspects of the rehabilitation process respectively; (iv) training programmes (academic and vocational) to develop, upgrade and strengthen the skills-set of clients for their vocation or employment and career progression; (v) meeting with the requirements and specifications of the Singapore Prisons Service and Singapore Corporation of Rehabilitative Enterprises (SCORE) to achieve the desired outcomes and to provide clients with step-down care in the community by or through collaboration with governmental and non-governmental agencies in the hope of rehabilitation and reintegration; (vi) source, recruit and work with dedicated volunteers to provide the services set out above; and (c) To address the well-being of the community at large by engaging in such community service projects (such as "Gift from the Heart") that HEB-Ashram Halfway House may, with the concurrence of HEB. deem desirable or necessary. MISSION STATEMENT To develop a culture of recovery and resilience in offenders by providing quality client-centric rehabilitative and reintegrative programmes through innovation, teamwork and community support

VISION STATEMENT To be the leader in rehabilitative therapy, training and support in collaboration with strategic partners and the community to achieve optimal success in reintegrating offenders into society

VALUES Client-Focus (Acknowledge and accept that each and every client is unique with varying needs and thus structuring our philosophy and operations around our clients to guide them towards their goals) Teamwork (Actively foster interdependence through collaboration with clients, partners and the community to arrive at the common goal of rehabilitating offenders in Singapore) Quality (Strive to achieve the highest standard of value-added care and rehabilitation services with the aim of empowering our clients) Humane (Emphasize benevolence, empathy, (com)passion and a humane approach to rehabilitation) Support Groups

name: HERITAGESG
search_text:
HERITAGESG (a) to provide specialist museum and heritage-related services, sector-development initiatives and programs to promote and develop the arts, culture and heritage sector in Singapore, and to position Singapore as an international leader in the field of museum and heritage-related services;

(b) to operate a children’s museum in Singapore where children can learn about a wide range of themes, including (without limitation) the heritage and culture of Singapore and the region, by playing with interactive and immersive exhibits;

(c) to do such other things as are incidental or conducive to the attainment of the above objects or any of them and particularly as follows, provided that nothing shall be done solely for commercial profit:

i. to provide archaeological, consultancy and related services for the promotion of archaeology, including assessments, surveys, fieldwork, excavations, post excavation specialist services, collections management and care, public education, research and consultancy relating to tangible heritage and historical environment;

ii. to organise, manage and/or run (whether on its own or in collaboration with other organisations) sector-development initiatives and programs including signature heritage festivals and placemaking initiatives, and to develop strategies and models to support such initiatives and programs;

iii. to provide support to NHB and its industry partners in business development to achieve the strategic objectives of the NHB;

iv. to develop and implement technology strategies to enhance museum visitor experience and to achieve the strategic objectives of the NHB;

v.to recruit employees to support the Company’s needs and the wider strategic thrusts of NHB and the arts, culture and heritage sector, on such terms to be mutually agreed in writing between the Company and NHB;

vi. to levy fees and other charges and to solicit or acquire by lawful means and to accept or receive subscriptions, donations of property of any nature and any devise, legacy, annuity, money, contributions or fund for the purpose of promoting and carrying on the objects of the Company or the NHB or any of them and to apply them as well as the income thereof to such objects; and

vii. to generally carry on or undertake any business or activity, do any act or enter into any transaction that are not prohibited under any law for the time being in force in the Republic of Singapore.

name: HINRICH FOUNDATION LIMITED
search_text:
HINRICH FOUNDATION LIMITED The Foundation aims to promote the belief that sustainable global trade promotes peace and this can be achieved by:

(i) Ensuring equality in trade development by encouraging policymakers to take a balanced approach and support open, free, transparent and fair global trade;

(ii) Encouraging both the private sector and governments to drive innovation and competitiveness;

(iii) Sponsoring the education and skill development of individuals who wish to participate in international trade; and

(iv) Providing practical trade services to promote and coach producers on how to participate in global trade.

With these goals in mind, the objects of the Foundation are:

1. To award in any part of the world grants, loans, scholarships, fellowships, endowments or other similar types of direct and indirect assistance designed to encourage individuals and any bodies, incorporated or unincorporated, who are pursuing courses or programs of educational betterment or who are researching and promoting ideas, approaches, or points of view in the fields of international trade, international communications, international law, international transportation and international politics;

2. To make contributions, gifts or other transfers to scientific or exploratory institutions like universities, colleges, vocational schools, research institutes, foundations (public or private) or other organisations in any part of the world with objects and purposes similar to those mentioned in paragraph 1 above;

3. To make contributions, gifts or other transfers to any one or more of the following:

3.1 Individuals in any part of the world: (a) having been accepted at or currently attending an accredited college, university or vocational school; and (b) having demonstrated acceptable progress in his chosen field of study, or having demonstrated the potential to achieve acceptable progress in his chosen field or study; and (c) wishing to pursue further studies in the fields as set out in paragraph 1 above;

3.2 Individuals in any part of the world: (a) currently teaching at or having been accepted to teach at an accredited college, university or vocational school; and (b) wishing to teach, do research, or write and publish in the fields as set out in paragraph 1 above;

3.3 Individuals in any part of the world: (a) having demonstrated expertise in his or her chosen field of study or in fields of study as set out in paragraph 1 above; and (b) wishing to pursue research in the fields of study as set out in paragraph 1 above to result in publication of the research project either publicly or privately, or to result in the publication of a limited distribution report as defined by the Directors of the Foundation;

3.4 Any group of individuals, associations, foundations, corporations or any other bodies whether corporate or otherwise wherever situated or incorporated: (a) promoting an idea, an approach, or a point of view in any one or more of the fields of study as set out in paragraph 1 above; and (b) providing financial or other direct or indirect assistance to pursue further research, publish materials, or promote their ideas, approaches or points of view through private or public media promotions;

3.5 Individuals, groups of individuals, associations, foundations, corporations or any other bodies whether corporate or otherwise wherever situated or incorporated pursuing or providing courses or programs of educational betterment or other support to people in disadvantaged regions in any part of the world in order to enable or promote their participation in the global economy, including practical trade services to promote and coach producers on how to participate in global trade;

3.6 Individuals, groups of individuals, associations, foundations, corporations or any other bodies whether corporate or otherwise wherever situated or incorporated pursuing international cultural, environmental or human welfare goals, such awards not to exceed ten percent (10%) of awards made in any one year; and

4. To do all such things as may be necessary or desirable in furtherance of the objects mentioned in paragraphs 1 to 3 above including without limiting the foregoing the investment and other management of the assets of the Foundation in accordance with the regulations of the Foundation pending the use of such assets for such objects. The Hinrich Foundation is a unique Asia-based philanthropic organization that works to advance mutually beneficial and sustainable global trade. We believe sustainable global trade strengthens relationships between nations and improves people’s lives. We support original research and education programs that build understanding and leadership in global trade. Our approach is independent, fact-based and objective. We are an authoritative source of knowledge, sharp analysis and fresh thinking for policymakers, business, media and scholars engaged in global trade. We are building a network of next-generation trade leaders by partnering with universities and corporations across global value chains. Think Tanks

Rule 2.9  To organize certain projects, e.g.  Flag day, Scholarship Fund Drive, Building Fund, etc. 	  Rule 2.10  The Association shall not engage in any activities that may undermine the racial or religious harmony in Singapore.    Rule 2.11  The Association may, with the approval of the Registrar of Societies, establish any number of branches it deems fit. Such branches shall be under the control of the Association and shall function in accordance with the provisions of this Constitution and the rules made thereunder.    Rule 2.12  The Association shall incorporate a company to conduct its business activities, if any. name: HOLLAND INTERNATIONAL SCHOOL LIMITED
search_text:
HOLLAND INTERNATIONAL SCHOOL LIMITED The Holland International School aims to provide an excellent education with an international dimension. We are confident that with our care and individual attention,we can get the best out of our children. Throughout the school we encourage openness, respect and enthusiasm,and we aim to make the learning experience fun. The learning process is of fundamental importance to us. The objects for which the Company is established are:
(a) To establish, equip, maintain, operate, and conduct, but not for profit, either by itself or in association or collaboration with other institutions having objects wholly or partly similar to this Company, a co-educational non-sectarian school or schools in Singapore.
(b) To provide all appropriate books and equipment and boarding and other accommodation for students and teachers or either of them at any school owned by the Company and doing all such other things as are incidental or conducive to the attainment of the above object.
(c) To arrange, institute or conduct exhibitions or instruction in dancing, music, drama, theatrical performance, swimming, riding or other social sporting or athletic activities of any sort or description.
(d) To offer exhibitions, prizes and rewards and to make grants and allowances to students or prospective students of any school owned by the Company.
(e) To provide playing fields, games, courts, recreation grounds and buildings, swimming baths and other accommodation in connection with sports, games, and pastimes of all kinds at any school owned by the Company.
(f) To carry on farming, dairy and poultry farming, stock breeding, market gardening, fruit farming and nurseries on any property of the Company for the purpose of providing and supplying any school or schools of the Company.
(g) To act as trustees, governors, or managers of any immovable or movable property given or held upon trust for educational purposes.
(h) To levy fees and other charges and to solicit or acquire by lawful means and to accept or receive subscriptions, donations of property of property of any nature and any devise, legacy, annuity, money, contribution, or fund for the purpose of promoting and carrying on the objects of the Company or any of them and to apply them as well as the income thereof to its objects.
(i) The borrow or raise money for the purposes of the Company or any of them in any such manner and upon such terms and security as may be considered expedient, and to issue debentures and other securities and for the purpose of securing any debt or other obligation of the Company to mortgage or charges all or any part of the property of the Company.
(j) Subject to the provisions of Section 19 of the Companies Act, Chapter 50 to purchase, take on lease or in exchange, hire or otherwise acquire for the purposes of the Company any immovable property or interest whatsoever and wheresoever and any rights, privileges, and easements over or in respect of any such property which may be considered necessary or convenient for the purposes of the Company.
(k) To purchase, hire or otherwise acquire for the purposes of the Company any personal property or interest in personal property.
(l) To sell, let, mortgage, dispose of or turn to account all or any of the property or assets of the Company as may be thought expedient with a view to the promotion of its objects.
(m) To invest the moneys of the Company not immediately required for its purposes in or upon such investments, securities or property as may be thought fit.
(n) To amalgamate, affiliate or co-operate with and subscribe to any association, society or corporation whose objects shall be both charitable and educational and to purchase or otherwise acquire and undertake all or any part of the property, assets, association, society or corporation PROVIDED that the Company shall not amalgamate, affiliate with or subscribe to any association, society or corporation which shall not prohibit the distribution of its income and property among its members to an extent at least as greats as imposed on the Company under or by virtue of clause 4 hereof.
(o) Subject to clause 4 thereof to grant pensions, allowances and gratuities to past or present officers or employees of the Company or to the dependents of such persons and to establish and maintain or participate in trust funds or schemes (whether contributory or non-contributory) for providing pensions or other benefits for any such persons as aforesaid.
(p) To do all such other things as are incidental or conducive to the attainment of the above objects or any of them.
(q) Expanding the offering with an International Stream education and enhance to reach a wider audience and give international families in Singapore access to unique Dutch-rooted approach to education on how children learn best. To endorse the international character of the school we inquire about a name change. Foreign Educational Institutions/Funds

name: HUAXIA CULTURAL HUB LTD.
search_text:
HUAXIA CULTURAL HUB LTD. The objectives are as follows:

(a) To nurture and promote the Chinese culture and heritage, including traditional festivals, customs, practices and cultural values, among the Chinese community;

(b) To organise events promoting traditional Chinese festivals such as “Spring in the City” and other related activities and events in conjunction with the celebration of traditional Chinese festivals in Singapore;

(c) To foster good relationships and engage with local and overseas cultural, community, charity and business organisations for the promotion of cultural interactions, cross cultural appreciation and social harmony;

(d) To fund and organise Chinese cultural events and activities including, performing arts, art exhibitions, art appreciation workshops, training courses, forums and charity drives to promote public interest and talent in Chinese culture and the arts;

(e) To conduct classes, workshops, forums and seminars to promote greater interest and appreciation for the Chinese language, literature and history;

(f) To organise exchange programs with relevant organisations in China and other countries with the aim of promoting the formation of cultural networks among interest groups and volunteer groups;

(g) To set up an effective IT system and to establish and maintain a social media presence in order to publicise events and activities, with the aim of maintaining relations between the company and the community, as well as for the production of
publications and videos for publicity and education purposes; and

(h) To support and undertake fund raising activities and any other events and projects in line with the fulfillment of the aforementioned objects.
Mission

To promote Chinese cultural traditions, practices and values through the organisation of cultural and festive celebrations and events, and through the provision of training and educational programs on the Chinese culture and arts for the community.

Vision

To be a source of inspiration and to provide support to the young generation in the preservation and promotion of good Chinese cultural values, festivals, traditions and practices in Singapore. Historical & Cultural Conservation

name: HUG COMMUNITY SERVICES LIMITED
search_text:
HUG COMMUNITY SERVICES LIMITED The organisation's memorandum and articles of association states that the objects for which the company is established are to contribute, promote, initiate, assist, organise and develop activities, programmes and schemes of social and humanitarian service for the alleviation of poverty, sickness, suffering, ignorance or ill-health of whatsoever nature, the advancement of education and strengthening of the institution of family. Further objects are available as stated in the governing instrument. Our Vision:

To create a profound and lasting impact on the lives of youths and their families by providing them with care, guidance, and unwavering hope.

Through our dedicated efforts and support, we aim to uplift and empower them to overcome challenges, fulfil their potential, and build a brighter future filled with promise and opportunity.

Our Mission: 

To foster the holistic development of young individuals by nurturing and cultivating pro-social values within them. We aim to provide them with the tools, guidance, and opportunities needed to seamlessly integrate into the fabric of society. 

Our approach is grounded in the belief that each young person possesses the potential to not only become conscientious but also to contribute significantly to the betterment of our communities and society at large. 

Through mentorship, education, and community engagement, we strive to instil in them a sense of responsibility, empathy, and purpose, ultimately empowering them to lead fulfilling lives and make meaningful contributions to the world around them. HUG Community Services Limited (HCSL) is a IPC-approved non-profit Social Service Agency (SSA) in Singapore that was established in 2014. HCSL is a full member of the National Council of Social S... name: Hankook Mission Church
search_text:
Hankook Mission Church To foster a vibrant and inclusive Christian community that nurtures spiritual growth, promotes social responsibility, and shares the love of Christ with all in Singapore.

This objective encompasses several key aspects:

Spiritual Growth: This refers to helping members deepen their relationship with God through worship, prayer, Bible study, and fellowship.
Inclusive Community: This means welcoming people from all backgrounds, regardless of race, nationality, socioeconomic status, or stage of life.
Social Responsibility: This involves engaging with social issues, serving the community, and promoting justice and compassion.
Sharing the Love of Christ: This includes evangelism, outreach, and demonstrating Christian values through words and actions. If the wandering of life ends by meeting Jesus Christ, then the knot of knowing and believing in Jesus must be tied to become a complete Christian and begin a new growth that resembles Jesus. The Singapore Korean Mission Church is the beginning of this knot-tying. It is a place where worries and anxieties are tied up and true peace in Christ Jesus is found, where worldly things are tied up and a new beginning as a worker in the Kingdom of God is made. This is the Singapore Korean Mission Church. Christianity

name: Heart of God Church
search_text: Heart of God Church The object of the Church is to propagate the Christian faith and to spread the gospel of Jesus Christ as revealed through the holy scriptures by all means of communication, whether visual, verbal or written.	  In furtherance of the above object, the Church may do all such things which are incidental or conducive of the objects, and particularly as follows:	  1.To provide for preaching, teaching and fostering the growth of the Christian religion in all places; to license and ordain ministers; to carry on the work of evangelism; to promote missionary work in all places; to carry on the organisation of churches and foster their development and local sovereignty and independence.	 2.To build a Christ-centred congregation through the worship of God and by strong preaching of the Word of God.    3.To encourage a fellowship of believers in a life of fervent prayer and systematic discipleship and the exercise of spiritual gifts.    4.To promote evangelism and to be involved in missions by the sending out, receiving and training of missionaries and the planting of branch churches both in Singapore and overseas.	5.To do such other things which are consistent with or incidental to the attainment of the above objects provided nothing shall be done solely for commercial reasons or solely for profit. The Church may:    a.Participate in international and interdenominational Christian activities.    b.Establish and run a childcare and development centre.	  c.Establish educational, medical and social schemes and programmes with a Christian emphasis.    d.Co-operate with other evangelical Christian groups for the carrying out of any of the above stated objects.    e.Erect and maintain Church buildings, social halls, business offices, school buildings, recreation facilities, parsonages, and such other structures as are deemed necessary for the upkeep and continuance of the said buildings and facilities.	  f.Collect, solicit and accept funds, gifts and other subscriptions; to hold in trust, use, mortgage, lease, sell or otherwise acquire or dispose of property, real or chattel, in keeping with the recited purpose of this Church, and it shall have and exercise all power that are necessary or convenient to effect all of the purposes for which this Church is organized.    g.Provide for the welfare of the destitute and the delinquent and emotionally troubled persons. Raise up a generation of people who are willing to give God the best years of their lives and He will use this generation to impact all levels of society and all walks of life!		   Beginnings of Leadership: And Jesus increased in wisdom and stature, and in favour with God and men (Luke 2:52)    To build a world class church in a first world nation with a first class spiritual atmosphere Christianity

name: Hebron Bible-Presbyterian Church
search_text:
Hebron Bible-Presbyterian Church The salvation of souls; The edification of Christians through the teaching of God's Word; The promotion of godly worship; The defense of "the faith which was once delivered unto the saints"; The establishment and extension of Christian missions and institutions for the futherance of God's Kingdom on earth; The promotion of Christian relief for the poor and needy; The strengthening of mutual fellowship, encouragement and unity among individual Bible-Presbyterian Churches and among other Bible-believing churches of similar stand. Vision: Our vision is for Hebron to be a radiant church, marked by active proclamation and demonstration of the gospel through our lives. Encapsulated in this vision are four outcomes we shall strive towards:

(1) Hebronites prioritise the God of the Christian gospel, through practising personal and corporate discipleship.

(2) Hebronites internalise the message of the Christian gospel, through inculcating gospel literacy.

(3) Hebronites reciprocate the grace of the Christian gospel, through building community culture.

(4) Hebronites disseminate the offer of the Christian gospel, through inspiring
outreach passion.

Thus, Hebron shall be a church where Hebronites acknowledge God as the Lord of our lives, understand and are able to communicate the gospel message effectively, demonstrate and experience the grace of God in the various sub-community groups, and extend the offer of the gospel as far and wide as God pleases.

Ultimately, our goal is to see the gospel come ALIVE in Hebron, that is, Actively Lived in eVery hEbronite.

Christianity

name: Help Family Service Centre
search_text:
Help Family Service Centre OBJECTIVE To extend assistance to single-parent families who are socially disadvantaged and vulnerable to the demand of coping alone.

From 1 January 2015, HELP FSC has been appointed by the Ministry of Social and Family Development (MSF) as a Divorce Support Specialist Agency (DSSA). As a DSSA, HELP FSC provides specialised services for divorcing and divorced families such as counselling, casework and programmes for the parents and children. VISION A holistic and well-recognised one-stop social organisation fully committed and excelling in meeting the plights and recovery of single parents and their families. MISSION To extend assistance to single-parent families who are socially disadvantaged and vulnerable to the demand of coping alone. To deliver effective social service to support and promote the physical and emotional well-being of single-parent families towards stability and the acceptance of the new family unit. To promote greater awareness through public education in order to foster better understanding and acceptance of single-parent families. Family

search_text:
Himpunan Belia Islam (Muslim Youth Assembly) BASIC PRINCIPLE: Amar Ma’ruf Nahi Munkar.

OBJECTIVE: TO PROMOTE AND TO STRENGTHEN THE AWARENESS OF THE YOUNGER GENERATION IN PARTICULAR AND THE GENERAL PUBLIC IN GENERAL TOWARDS UNDERSTANDING AND REALISING ISLAM, AS IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE TEACHING OF THE QUR’AN AND SUNNAH, IN EVERY ASPECT OF THEIR LIFE.

3.1 Giving and promoting Islamic-oriented education, teaching and culture.

3.5 Co-operating with other organisations in matters and activities which are not in contravention with the basic principle and objective of the Association.

name: Hindu Endowments Board
search_text:
Hindu Endowments Board The Hindu Endowments Board (HEB) is a statutory body set up under the Hindu Endowments Act in 1968. Its role, as set out in the Act, is to administer those endowments placed under its administration.

The word “endowment” is defined in the Act to mean any endowment in land, building or money given for the support of any Hindu temple or other Hindu purpose. The endowments administered by the Board are: Sri Mariamman Temple, Sri Srinivasa Perumal Temple, Sri Sivan Temple and Sri Vairavimada Kaliamman Temple. Vision
Inspire exemplary temple endowments to support religious, cultural traditions in service of the Hindu community and foster harmony amongst all religions.

Mission
1. To manage our temples to the benefit of our devotees, providing a congenial environment for prayers, rituals and ceremonies to meet their religious needs.

2. To enhance social harmony through interfaith understanding.

Hinduism

name: Hong San See Temple
search_text:
Hong San See Temple OBJECTIVES:
1. The objectives of the Temple are to worship Guang Ze Zhun Wang Deity and to promote and impart the religious beliefs of the Temple;

2. In furtherance of the above objects, the Temple shall foster closer relationship between believers of all walks of life irrespective of age and sex.
MISSION
• Hong San See Temple serves as places of worship where devotees pray, perform rituals, and make offerings to Guang Ze Zhun Wang Deity and other deities. The primary goal is to promote spiritual well-being, peace, and harmony through practices that align with Guang Ze Zhun Wang Deity beliefs, such as praying for health, prosperity, and guidance from deities.

• We also function as cultural centers, hosting Taoist festivals, rites of passage, and community events. We organize celebrations for festivals such as the Lunar New Year, Deities Annual Prayers and Celebrations and the Hungry Ghost Festival, fostering a sense of community and cultural identity



• We often engage in charitable work and provide services to the local community. This includes providing free food items and daily necessity items the poor and low income families, or offering educational support to research on Taoism, in line with Taoist principles of compassion and service to others.

VISION
• To be a welcoming and inclusive space where individuals can connect with Guang Ze Zhun Wang Deity religious practice and seek spiritual fulfillment through prayer, meditation, and the cultivation of virtues such as harmony, simplicity, and compassion.

• To promote and preserve Guang Ze Zhun Wang Deity religious practices, and cultural heritage for future generations, ensuring that the teachings of Guang Ze Zhun Wang Deity beliefs and practices continue to be relevant and accessible in modern life.

• To inspire individuals to live in balance with nature and the universe, encouraging the practice of harmony in all aspects of life—personal, social, and environmental.

• To serve as a hub for social welfare, charitable initiatives, and community engagement, reflecting the Guang Ze Zhun Wang Deity values of compassion and service. The temple may seek to uplift the local community by providing aid to those in need and promoting unity through shared cultural practices.
• To act as a bridge between different cultures and religious communities in Singapore, promoting mutual respect, dialogue, and understanding, while maintaining the unique identity of Taoism in Singapore’s multicultural society.

• To provide guidance and support for individuals facing life’s challenges by offering divination, wisdom, and counsel through Taoist practices and spiritual leaders.

Taoism

name: Hope Initiative Alliance
search_text:
Hope Initiative Alliance Hope Initiative Alliance is a charity organisation that provides holistic care for Singapore citizens, residents and migrant workers who are lesser privileged and require social welfare assistance.

With the support of partners from the public, people and private sectors, HIA serves as an aggregator to provide services that improve the lives of people with diverse social welfare needs. In this regard, HIA works closely with over 100 social service organisations, civil society organisations, religious groups, corporations and government agencies to serve the following beneficiaries:
- Children and families from low-income backgrounds and rental housing from lesser privileged families
- Disadvantaged Families and the vulnerable-in-community
- Homeless people and rough sleepers
- Migrant workers living in dormitories
- Migrant domestic workers

HIA also promotes togetherness and unity among people of diverse identities. In this regard, HIA works with religious organisations that partner on interfaith charity efforts. VISION:
To be an alliance of partnerships from people, private and public sectors for the common good

MISSION:
To work with government, civil society groups, religious communities, non-government agencies and private sector companies to provide charity services that will improve people's lives with diverse social welfare needs.

name: Humanitarian Organization for Migration Economics
search_text:
Humanitarian Organization for Migration Economics Humanitarian Organization for Migration Economics [“H.O.M.E.”] is a Singapore-based charity founded in 2004 by the late Ms Bridget Tan. HOME is dedicated to empowering and supporting migrant workers who experience abuse and exploitation.

We provide immediate crisis intervention as well as long-term support such as education and training programmes to achieve our goal of enhancing the wellbeing and employment prospects of migrant workers. HOME also promotes the diverse voices of migrant workers in Singapore through outreach projects, and public education. Three pillars guide us: Welfare, Empowerment and Advocacy. Vision
A world where migrants workers can live and work with dignity and have equal access to rights.

Mission
We care for the welfare of migrant workers by providing casework assistance as well as financial, legal, and medical aid.

We empower migrant workers through skills training programmes.

We work with corporations, government agencies, and community partners to advocate inclusion, access to justice, and equality for all.

name: IIX GLOBAL CHARITABLE LIMITED
search_text:
IIX GLOBAL CHARITABLE LIMITED The objects for which the Company is established are: - (a) to serve social and welfare purposes and advance community development in Singapore by promoting, nurturing and developing a culture of social enterprise in Singapore; (b) to serve social and welfare purposes and advance community development in Singapore by facilitating and promoting opportunities for persons to participate in and foster the development, expansion and funding of social enterprises; (c) to serve social and welfare purposes and advance community development in Singapore by facilitating and promoting the development of Singapore as a regional hub for social enterprises; (d) to serve social and welfare purposes, relieve poverty, provide relief to those in need, and to advance education, health and the environment in Singapore and throughout the Asia Pacific region by facilitating, promoting and assisting the development, expansion and funding of social enterprises so as to promote Singapore as a regional hub for social enterprises; (e) to serve social and welfare purposes and advance community development in Singapore by facilitating, promoting, and establishing networks and platforms in Singapore for social enterprises; and to do all such other things as are incidental in furtherance of the attainment of the above objects or any of them, including, without limitation:- (i) promote, conduct, commission and assist in research on social enterprises and investment in social enterprises and to make available such research; (ii) promote, organize, conduct and assist in talks, seminars, workshops, training programs, exhibitions, conferences and other events by which the culture of social enterprise may be promoted, the organizational capacity of social enterprises may be strengthened and the social and environmental impact of social enterprises may be enhanced; (iii) provide, organize and procure strategic consulting services and technical assistance for social enterprises in the region by which the organizational capacity of social enterprises may be strengthened so as to promote Singapore as a regional hub for social enterprises; (iv) promote, establish, develop and assist in the development of networks and platforms for social enterprises and investment in social enterprises in the region, so as to promote Singapore as a regional hub for the financing of social enterprises, including the development of a social stock exchange in Singapore; (v) promote, produce, and disseminate newsletters and other publications in print or electronic form by which the culture of social enterprises may be promoted nurtured and developed; (vi) liaise, coordinate and cooperate with governmental authorities, professional organisations and other relevant bodies on matters aimed at the promotion of the culture of social enterprise and the attainment of the objects of the Company; and (vii) do all such other lawful things as are incidental or conducive to the attainment of the above objects or any of them Vision: The impact enterprise and impact investment sectors in Singapore and Asia have the potential to address wide-scale social and environmental needs through the application of market mechanisms. When social enterprises are equipped with the knowledge and systems required to raise investment capital, they can scale their operations and magnify their social and environmental impact. IIX Foundation seeks to provide social enterprises with the tools, capacity and knowledge to access investment capital and expand their impact.

Mission: IIX Foundation fosters growth, maturity, innovation, and market readiness in the social enterprise and impact investment sectors in Asia to help address urgent social and environmental problems. (Throughout this document, social enterprise refers to organizations whether for-profit or not-for-profit which have a mission to promote socially or environmentally beneficial goals and use business methods to promote these goals Impact investment refers to actively placing capital in businesses, funds, or other financial vehicles that generate social and/or environmental benefits while having the potential to return nominal principal and an investment return to the investor.) Our mission includes helping to make Singapore a regional hub for impact investing, supporting high-impact social enterprises in Singapore and throughout the region, and building a more inclusive a... name: INDIGO & CO@SG LTD.
search_text:
INDIGO & CO@SG LTD. (a) To provide professional mental health services, expertise and support for individuals, clients, their families, caregivers and/or social networks for any such social issues which may include but shall not be limited to human sexuality, gender identity as well as addiction;

(b) To conduct research, educate, and promote greater understanding as well as awareness in the community in relation to topics relating to human sexuality, gender identity and any other social issues, as may be applicable;

(c) To build competencies amongst mental health practitioners and any such individuals, professionals, institutions or organisations who provide care or support for the wellbeing, mental health, and psychological wellbeing of any such individuals who struggle with human sexuality, gender identity, addiction or any such social issues;

(d) To grant or participate in granting relief and aid to those in crisis or in need;

(e) To do all such other things are incidental or conducive, in the attainment of the above objects, and particularly as follows:

i. To organise and carry out fund-raising projects and campaigns to support the activities of the Company;

ii. To purchase or otherwise acquire property or to hire any room, hall, ship or dwelling house in the Republic of Singapore or elsewhere, and also to hire, purchase or acquire vehicles, offices, household, schools, hospital furniture or other machines or other chattels;

iii. To improve, manage, develop, work, maintain, sell, lease, let, underlet, exchange, surrender, mortgage, charge, dispose of or otherwise deal with and turn into account all or any of the property (real or any personal) for the time being of the Company wheresoever situate, or property in which the Company has an interest and to lend, invest or deposit money or mortgage or create any security, and to erect, construct, alter and maintain any buildings, ships, boats, of other chattels, and to retain, use and occupy or deal with the properties of the Company or any part thereof for all such purposes and in such manner as the Company may deem fit;

iv. To provide, engage and employ ministers, auditors, secretaries, treasurers, agents, solicitors, servants, psychiatrists, psychologists, and other persons for the purpose of carrying out the objects of the Company or any of them;

v. To permit and publish any newspapers, periodicals, books or leaflets that the Company may think desirable for the declaration and promotion of its objects or any of them;

vi. To receive and take any gift of money or property for any of the purposes and objects of the Company whether subject to any special trust or not, but the Company may nevertheless decline to accept any gift or donation or to take over any property which has been offered to the Company or which has annexed to it any condition or obligation not approved by the Company;

vii. To establish and register legal entities such as companies limited by guarantee or societies, of whose objects are aligned to the Company’s objects and provide these legal entities the necessary support monetary or otherwise as well as to support and subscribe to any charitable or public object, and any institution society or club which may be for the benefit of the Company or its members and to make payments towards insurance and to form and contribute to provident and benefit funds for the benefit of any persons who have served the Company or their wives, children or other dependents;

viii. name: INTERNATIONAL COMMUNITY SCHOOL (SINGAPORE) LTD
search_text:
INTERNATIONAL COMMUNITY SCHOOL (SINGAPORE) LTD Mission Definition: ICS endeavors to partner with parents in their role to instruct their children in the way they should go (Proverbs 22:6). In this partnership, ICS commits to providing a dynamic, challenging, and holistic education that recognizes the personal uniqueness of each student and develops them into the person they were created to be. To reach one's God-given potential, there must be an inner transformation that takes place. When that happens, boys and girls, young men and women are not only transformed, but they begin to impact the world around them. Nothing gives us more pleasure and fulfillment than when God receives glory in and through students' lives.
Mission
Educating Minds and Transforming Lives to Impact the World for the Glory of God

Vision Tagline
Every Student, Every Family, Every Day For Christ

Vision
Our authentic international educational community will become a gateway to purpose, equipping and empowering global learners to lead positive change.


Values
There are four core principles or core values by which we operate. These core values are the bedrock of our school: our educational approach, our extra curricular activities, our sports programs, and our family events.

Biblical Worldview
ICS is a Christian school founded upon the belief that God is the source of all truth, and true education is based on His revealed word, the Bible. We are dedicated to establishing a godly environment in which the Bible is actively integrated into all aspects of school life. We strive to encourage students to imitate Christ through a variety of avenues: studying the Bible, age-level chapel services, spiritual emphasis programs, and service trips throughout Asia.
Caring Community
We are family. When students join our school, they join the ICS family. Our international atmosphere is an open door for students of all cultures, races, and nationalities. We proactively work to build relationships among students and between students and teachers that go beyond the classrooms and grade levels. Our goal is to help our students to learn to respectfully interact across age, cultural, and ethnic boundaries. We also strive to reach out to our students by creating opportunities for each family member to participate in school functions.
Academic Excellence
We believe that education is characterized by a dynamic, challenging curriculum coupled with a high expectation for student achievement. We view education holistically. Education includes far more than learning to pass a test. We create opportunities for our students to apply what they have learned while maintaining a focus on in-depth proficiency in all subject matters. Our goal is to instill a love for life-long learning.
Personal Excellence
We believe every person should always pursue excellence. Pursuing excellence means that one is working towards being outstanding. Excellence has a place in the academic world, the professional arena, and our personal lives as well. We encourage all members of our community to strive toward excellence in whatever they do. We want our students to try their best in their academics, collaborate with their peers, and work to improve the ICS community and their world. ICS employs teachers who strive for excellence in their professional lives and find ways to improve student learning in the classroom. Teachers who seek professional development will provide their students with the best educational experience possible. Students will be challenged to develop and pursue personal excellence in their own lives, striving to follow and fulfill their God-given potential.
At the International Community School (Singapore), we believe missions don’t just happen “out there”—they start here. We’re an international school with a Kingdom mindset, and our mission is lived... Foreign Educational Institutions/Funds Education Social service and welfare

name: INTERNATIONAL LUTHERAN SEAFARERS' MISSION
search_text:
INTERNATIONAL LUTHERAN SEAFARERS' MISSION The objective of the Charity are as follows:
• For the promotion of the spiritual welfare of international seafarers and
fishermen that come by the ports of Singapore. Towards this purpose it provides port chaplains and two drop-in centres to serve the physical and spiritual well-being of the seafarers/fishermen that come from various countries.


Vision:
Our mission is to extend Christian love by ministering to the spiritual and practical needs of seafarers. We offer a warm welcome, a helping hand and friendship to seafarers from around the world who enter the ports of Singapore.
As a Christian agency we care for the welfare of seafarers of all nationalities and faiths to provide help and support. We reach out to seamen and fishermen by visiting them on their ships, offering them the hand of welcome and friendship, and caring for them in their hour of need.

Mission:
On our daily ships visit it’s always a pleasure to talk to seafarers from all over the world. So we have a great opportunity to spread the Good News: “Go therefore and make disciples of all the nations.” (Matthew 28.19).

Sometimes on our daily ship visits we are really shocked about the bad living and working conditions of seafarers and fishermen on board. They are facing various dangers in an often merciless working environment, including piracy. It is truly one of the most dangerous jobs in the world.

We know about our restricted possibilities. So we are very thankfully about any kind of support from our church members. We and the seafarers need also your attention, your blessings and your prayers. In life, in death, in life beyond death God is with us. Thanks be to God. Christianity

Without prejudice to the generality of the foregoing, the Company may exercise the following powers, for attaining the objectives.

(c) to invest and deal with the moneys of the Company in such manner as the Board thinks fit;

(d) to carry out Corporate Social Responsibilities, and to give gifts and sponsorships which is of financial or other benefit;

(e) to appoint, employ, remove or suspend management and teaching staff and such other administrative, non-teaching and other officers and staff within the Company as may be necessary or convenient for the purposes of the Company;

(f) to sell, surrender, lease, exchange or mortgage any property, land or building as may be found most convenient or advantageous; or receiving wakaf, which is a voluntary charitable endowment from a person’s belongings or wealth in the form of cash/property as per the Administration of Muslim Law Act (Cap 3);

(g) to borrow money whether by way of bank overdraft or otherwise for such purpose as the Company may from time to time consider desirable;

(h) to support, administer and/or set up other charities of similar objectives as the Company; (i) to provide learning development support for children with needs;

name: Inter-Religious Organisation (Singapore)
search_text:
Inter-Religious Organisation (Singapore) The objectives of IRO are:
- To inculcate the spirit of friendship and co-operation among the leaders and followers of different religions.
- To improve the condition of the adherents of different religious in generally accepted moral principles
- To organize by lawful and peaceful means a movement to eradicate the common evils prevailing among mankind
- To promote mutual respect, assistance and protection among the adherents of different religions.
- To conduct prayers and meditation for the well being of mankind as often as possible and convenient.
- To promote in every way comity and co-operation among the followers of different religions for the good of mankind.
- To organise public lectures, demonstrations, study groups, radio and television publicity in an effort to familiarize the aims of the Organisation.
- To take such steps by personal or written appeals, public meetings or otherwise as may become necessary or expedient in the interests of mankind.
- To publish, circulate and distribute periodicals, leaflets or newsletters that the Organisation may think advisable for the promotion of its objectives.
- To intensify efforts in any other respect towards making the Organisation an effective body of friendship and brotherhood among the adherents of different religions without compromising any of his/her religious values.
- To accept affiliation, if desired, from or with any institution, society or association having objectives similar to those of the Organisation and to co-operate and work with any person or persons in furtherance of the objectives of the Organisation.
- To generally do or perform necessary actions conducive to the attainment of objectives of the Organisation. The Inter-Religious Organisation, Singapore (IRO) is a non-governmental organisation founded by leaders of diverse faiths to work together for religious harmony in Singapore. The understanding between religious groups by developing friendships based on mutual trust and respect.

The members from Hindu, Jewish, Zoroastrian, Buddhist, Taoist, Jain, Christian, Muslim, Sikh and Baha’i religions constantly learn from each other and get to know one another better, fostering and enhancing inter-religious harmony in Singapore. General Charitable Purposes

name: International Sri Krishna Mandir
search_text:
International Sri Krishna Mandir The objects of the Temple are:

1. To provide facilities for the congregation and worship of Sri Sri Radha & Krishna.

2. To preach the Vaishnava philosophy as presented in all Vedic Scriptures, particularly Bhagavad-gita, Srimad Bhagavatam, Caitanya-caritamrta, and
related scriptures as translated by His Divine Grace A. C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada.

3. To train members, devotees, or any favorable members of the public to become Vaishnavas (devotees of Lord Krishna) adhering strictly to the teachings and standards set by His Divine Grace A. C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada in his books.

4. To promote the educational, spiritual, moral, cultural and social interests of its members and the public at large.

5. To preach love of God (Krishna) and control of mind and senses through the chanting of Krishna’s holy names, especially the Hare Krishna maha-mantra.

6. To distribute free Prasadam (spiritually sanctified vegetarian food) to the poor and needy, and to the public in general. The International Sri Krishna Mandir (or, in short, ISKM) has been established to represent His Divine Grace A. C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada (or, in short, His Divine Grace) in carrying out the responsibility of managing the preaching of Krishna consciousness. With the firm understanding that His Divine Grace, who appeared in the authorized disciplic succession of the Brahma-Madhva-Gaudiya Vaishnava Sampradaya (lineage), is the bona fide Acharya (or spiritual master) representing Lord Krishna, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, ISKM accepts as its life and soul the divine instructions of His Divine Grace and recognizes that it is completely dependent on his mercy in all respects. The ISKM has no other function or purpose other than to execute the instructions so kindly given by His Divine Grace and preserve and spread his teachings in their purest form. Hinduism

name: J217 LIMITED

J217 LIMITED The objects for which J217 is established are:-

(ii) To encourage and train volunteers to serve the poor and disadvantaged, so as to nurture more caring citizens for Singapore.

(iii) To collaborate with other organisations with shared objectives to effect positive changes in the society and the individuals that participate in J217 activities.
In obedience to the teachings of the Bible, J217 is to demonstrate the love and teachings of Jesus Christ by acts of service to the poor and disadvantaged regardless of their race, language or religion.
(The name J217 is taken from the Bible: James 2:17 – “Faith without work is dead”)
