name: JAZZ ASSOCIATION (SINGAPORE)
search_text:
JAZZ ASSOCIATION (SINGAPORE) (a) to promote jazz music, jazz music performance, arrangement,
composition and jazz music education in Singapore as well as
raise Singapore’s profile in jazz globally

(b) to collaborate and/or partner with any party, entity,
organisation, or company towards attaining any or all of the
objects stated herein in order to establish an eco-system that
will allow jazz music to flourish in Singapore

(c) to enhance the general public’s appreciation of jazz music

(d) to set up, conduct training and mentorship programmes for
aspiring jazz musicians and create opportunities for
mentorship by and networking with international jazz
musicians

(e) to set up and operate an orchestra based in Singapore
comprising mainly Singapore-based jazz musicians with
adjunct members from overseas as well as guest soloists,
orchestrators, composers and conductors

(f) to set up scholarship and bursary (and other assistance or
support) programmes to help fund local and/or overseas
studies for talented and deserving jazz students (of all ages)
who are Singaporeans and/or Singapore permanent residents.
This shall include, but not be limited to, formal studies,
mentorships and the participation of deserving candidates in
international music conventions, camps and festivals

(g) to provide interim assistance (including cash and in-kind) for
distressed jazz musicians going through a difficult period
including but not limited to circumstances such as loss of
income due to pandemics, acute illness, accident or other
personal tragic incidents

(h) to do such other things as are incidental or conducive to the
attainment of the above objects and provided nothing is done
solely for profit Our vision is to be a centre of excellence in Jazz in Singapore, to bring the joy of Jazz to people from all walks of life and across diverse communities, and to make Singapore a leading global city of Jazz.

Our mission is to play a strong part in lifting the Singapore jazz scene to new heights by:

1. Forming a core pool of established, professional-level local jazz musicians and facilitating continuing education in jazz
2. Playing a major role in bridging the needs of the audience with those of the jazz practitioners and other arts groups' objectives
3. Leveraging jazz as a unifying force to promote interaction, cohesion and inclusiveness among our diverse communities in Singapore
4. Making Singapore a nexus for collaborations between Singaporean and international jazz musicians
5. Developing Singapore into a vibrant city of jazz for Singaporeans, residents and visitors alike
6. Preserving the spirit of jazz by supporting eligible local musicians impacted by crisis with short-term financial aid.

For more information about JASS, please visit www.jazzassociation.sg Jazz Association (Singapore), or JASS, was founded in September 2016 with the aim of promoting the participation, engagement, and excellence of jazz in Singapore. In March 2017, we were accorded ch... Music & Orchestras Training & Education Arts, culture and heritage Community development Education Social service and welfare

name: Jaafari Muslim Association (Singapore)
search_text: Jaafari Muslim Association (Singapore) Please see attached Constitution. In summary, the objects of the Aassociation shall be:	(i)	To promote and advance the understanding of the religion of Islam in accordance with the teachings of Jaafari (Shia Ithna-asheri) school of law and to provide for the upliftment of the social, economic and educational needs amongst members.    (ii)	To foster the spirit of co-operation, fellowship and brotherhood amongst members of the Association through the organisation of discussions, talks and seminars.    (iii)	To act as a service oriented association with the view of providing assistance to charitable causes and help in the raising of funds for worthy institutions such as the Singapore Community Chest and the Yayasan Mendaki.    (iv)	To work in full co-operation with the Majlis Ugama Islam Singapura ("MUIS") and other accredited societies and organisations in the social, educational, moral and spiritual upliftment of Muslims in Singapore.    (v)	To foster good relations with similar bodies of other religions in order to preserve harmony and good understanding of each other s point of view. Mission: To promote the teaching of Islam as revealed through Prophet Mohammed and further explained by the Ahlul Bayt, in fostering a genuine spirit of cooperation and goodwill amongst Muslims and Non-Muslims.   Vision: A learning hub and resource centre for the teaching of Islam (based on the Jaafari school of thought). Islam

name: Jewish Welfare Board, Singapore, The
search_text:
Jewish Welfare Board, Singapore, The a. The encouragement, promotion and coordination of communal, religious, educational and charitable activities of the Jewish Community in Singapore;

b. The administration or assistance in administration of such charitable trusts as the Association may be appointed to administer affecting the Jewish Community in Singapore;

c. The acceptance and administration of donations and bequests to be applied for charitable purposes for the benefit of the Jewish Community of Singapore;

d. The maintenance or assistance in the maintenance of the Maghain Aboth Synagogue and the provision of the services of a Rabbi, Hazan and/or other Synagogue and community personnel who practise Judaism in accordance with Sephardic rites and tradition;

e. The maintenance or assistance in the maintenance of all Jewish Cemeteries in Singapore;

f. The maintenance or assistance in the maintenance of the Abdullah Shooker Jewish Welfare Home and/or other premises used for communal, religious, educational and charitable activities of the Jewish Community in Singapore;

g. The maintenance or assistance in the maintenance of a a Kosher shop and a Kosher restaurant, in conjunction with ; other social and community activities provided by, arranged or encouraged by the Association.

h. The maintenance or assistance in the maintenance of the Ganenu Day School Learning Centre, the Talmud Torah and such other schools as the Association may establish whose teachings shall be in accordance with Orthodox Halachik Standards practice and

i. The maintenance or assistance in the maintenance of the Jacob Ballas Centre. Mission: To build upon the rich heritage of Jewish Community in Singapore and equip community members with the right resources to sustain a vibrant traditional Jewish life in Asia.

Vision: Protect, Nurture, Enlighten and Uplift

name: Jireh Bible-Presbyterian Church
search_text:
Jireh Bible-Presbyterian Church a. save souls.
b. edify Christians through the teaching of God’s word.
c. promote godly worship.
d. defend “the faith which was once delivered unto the Saints.
e. strengthen mutual fellowship, encouragement and unity among other fundamental and bible-believing churches of similar stand” for the Word of God and for the testimony of Jesus Christ” (Rev 1:9)
f. In furtherance of the above objects, the Society may expand its objectives and when the need arises with the written permission from the Registrar of Societies. STATEMENT OF FAITH

1. The doctrine of the Church shall be in accordance with that system commonly called "the Reformed Faith" as expressed in the Confession of Faith as set forth by the historic Westminster Assembly together with the Larger and Shorter Catechisms.

2. In abbreviated form the chief tenets of the doctrine of the church, apart from the Apostles' Creed, shall be as follows:-

2.1. We believe in the divine, verbal and plenary inspiration of the Scriptures in the original languages, their consequent inerrancy and infallibility, and, as the Word of God, the Supreme and final authority in faith and life;

2.2. We believe in one God existing in three co-equal and co-eternal Persons, Father, Son and Holy Spirit;

2.3. We believe that Jesus Christ, the eternal Son of God, was conceived by the Holy Spirit, born of the virgin Mary, and is true God and true man;

2.4. We believe that man was created in the image of God, but sinned through the fall of Adam, thereby incurring not only physical death but also spiritual death, which is separation from God and that all human beings are born with a sinful nature and become sinners in thought, word and deed;

2.5. We believe that the Lord Jesus Christ died a propitiatory and expiatory death as a representative and substitutionary sacrifice, and that all who repent of their sins and believe in Him are justified before God on the grounds of His shed blood;

2.6. We believe in the bodily resurrection of our Lord Jesus Christ, in His ascension into Heaven, and in His exaltation at the right hand of God, where He intercedes for us as our High Priest and Advocate;

2.7. We believe in the personal, visible and premillennial return of our Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ to judge this world and bring peace to the nations;

2.8. We believe that salvation is by grace through faith, not by works, and that all who repent and receive the Lord Jesus Christ as their personal Saviour are born again by the Holy Spirit and thereby become the children of God;

2.9. We believe that the ministry of the Holy Spirit is to glorify the Lord Jesus Christ and to convict and regenerate the sinner, indwell, guide, instruct and empower the believer for godly living and service;

2.10. We believe that Christ instituted the Sacrament of Baptism for believers and their children and the Sacrament of the Lord's Supper, which sacraments shall be observed by His Church till He comes;

2.11. We believe in the eternal security, bodily resurrection, and eternal blessedness of the saved and in the bodily resurrection and eternal conscious punishment of the lost;

2.12. We believe in the real, spiritual unity in Christ of all redeemed by His precious blood and the necessity of faithfully maintaining the purity of the Church in doctrine and life according to the Word of God and the principle and practice of biblical separation from the apostasy of the day being spearheaded by the Ecumenical Movement (2 Cor 6: 14-18; Rev 18:4). Christianity

name: KHETAN FOUNDATION LIMITED
search_text:
KHETAN FOUNDATION LIMITED The objects for which the Company is established shall be purely charitable and in particular all or any of the following:

(1) The improvement of the standard of human life generally and the undertaking of any activity whatsoever which is beneficial or useful to the community.

(2) The improvement of the standard of human life and social well-being, and in particular by:-

(a) doing all things necessary or incidental to the pursuit of the charitable purpose of preventing or relieving poverty in Singapore and Nepal, including the provision of grants to individuals in need and/or charities, or other organisations working to prevent or relieve poverty; and
(b) the encouragement of self-help and the rehabilitation (in any manner whatsoever) of those in temporary or inevitable distress and for the purposes thereof to make grants in aid or render any other financial assistance which the Company may deem fit.

(3) Generally to aid, assist and give relief in any manner and to any extent, including in the absolute discretion of the Board of Directors of the Company, the application of the whole or any part of the assets for the time being of the Company, to any charitable institution whose object or objects are similar to or comparable with the objects of the Company.

(4) To collaborate and cooperate with any other government, not-for-profit or other private agencies and organisations and do all such lawful things which are conducive or incidental to the attainment of the above objects or any of them and provided that nothing shall be done solely for commercial reasons or for profit. Without prejudice to the generality of the foregoing, the Company may:-

(a) enter into any cooperative arrangements with any organisations that may seem conducive to the Company’s objects, or any of them; and obtain from such organisations any rights, privileges, and concessions which the Company may think it desirable to obtain and to carry out, exercise, and comply with any such cooperative arrangements, rights, privileges and concessions;
(b) purchase, take on, lease or in exchange hire or otherwise acquire any real and personal estate or property and improve and maintain the same, which may be deemed necessary or convenient for any of the purposes of the Company;
(c) obtain, collect and receive monies and funds by way of contribution, donation, affiliation fees, subscriptions, legacies, bequests, grants and any other lawful method, and accept and receive gifts of property of any description (whether subject to any special trust or not);
(d) sell, manage, lease, dispose of, transfer or otherwise deal with all or any part of the property of the Company;
(e) invest surplus funds in short-term deposits with Banks, Government Bonds and Securities, Treasury Bills ;
(f) undertake and execute any trust or any agency business which may seem directly conducive to any of the objects of the Company;
(g) subscribe to and promote the aims and objects of any society, institute or organization and to encourage and support any society, institute or organization whose objects are not in variance with those of the Company;
(h) subscribe or otherwise aid benevolent, charitable, national or other charitable institutions which are registered under the Charities Act (Cap. 37) and to grant donations to any institution of a public character approved under the Charities Act (Cap. 37);
(i) pay all costs, charges and expenses incurred or sustained in or about the promotion and establishment of the Company and to remunerate any person or persons for services rendered in the promotion and establishment of the Company; and
(j) to do all such other things as are incidental or conducive to the attainment of the objects and the exercise of the powers of the Company.

(5) The Company shall not support with its funds any political and/or religious organisation.

(6) The Company shall be guided upon the following principles:

GUIDING PRINCIPLES

Individuals, families and societies are progressive in nature, want to be self-reliant and have peaceful and respectable lives through economic prosperity and cultural integration. The biggest hurdles for many people are limited opportunities, limited access to skills and limited financial resources. The lowering or removal of these hurdles can give them the chance to live better lives.

Lotus Life Foundation shall in a focused, scientific and sustained manner, contribute to making a positive impact on the lives of hard working and sincere, yet underprivileged and financially challenged, people and communities.

Lotus Life Foundation shall work together with other organisations towards the cause of empowering the underprivileged populations.

Lotus Life Foundation shall, to the best of its ability and given resources, contribute to help governments, communities and families during natural and other disasters for recovery and reconstruction.

Lotus Life Foundation shall maintain ethical and legal integrity and shall responsibly manage its financial resources.


PROVIDED ALWAYS that the Company shall exist for purposes which are charitable and notwithstanding anything hereinbefore contained nothing shall be an object of the Company which is not a charitable object and that nothing herein contained shall be deemed to empower the Company to carry on the business of banking or insurance.

PROVIDED FURTHER that the Company shall not support with its funds any object or endeavour to impose on or procure to be observed by its members or others any regulation restriction or condition which if an object of the Company would make it a Trade Union (as defined in Trade Unions Act, Cap. 333).

AND IT IS HEREBY DECLARED that save as is otherwise expressly provided, the object specified in each of the paragraphs in this Constitution shall be regarded as an independent object, and accordingly, shall be in no way limited or restricted (except when otherwise expressed in such paragraph) by reference to the objects indicated in any other paragraph or the name of the Company, but may be carried out in as full and ample a manner and construed in as wide a sense as if each of the said paragraphs defined the objects of a separate, distinct and independent company. Individuals, families and societies are progressive in nature, want to be self-reliant and have peaceful and respectable lives through economic prosperity and cultural integration. The biggest hurdles for many people are limited opportunities, limited access to skills and limited financial resources. The lowering or removal of these hurdles can give them the chance to live better lives.

Lotus Life Foundation shall in a focused, scientific and sustained manner, contribute to making a positive impact on the lives of hard working and sincere, yet underprivileged and financially challenged, people and communities.

Lotus Life Foundation shall work together with other organisations towards the cause of empowering the underprivileged populations.

Lotus Life Foundation shall, to the best of its ability and given resources, contribute to help governments, communities and families during natural and other disasters for recovery and reconstruction.

Lotus Life Foundation shall maintain ethical and legal integrity and shall responsibly manage its financial resources. Community

name: KIDZ MEADOW CHILDCARE & DEVELOPMENT CENTRE LIMITED
search_text:
KIDZ MEADOW CHILDCARE & DEVELOPMENT CENTRE LIMITED Kidz Meadow Childcare & Development Centre Limited (KMCDCL), previously owned by Association of Muslim Professionals (AMP), was formed to achieve the following social objectives :

i. To provide quality and affordable infant and childcare programmes for children aged 2 months to 6 years regardless of race, language or religion, so as to support working parents who do not have alternative care arrangements;
ii. To fund and/or subsidise the preschool fees of children from low-income families to ensure that preschool education is made affordable and accessible for all;
iii. To promote the social and emotional well-being of the children enrolled at the Company's preschools by providing proper guidance and purposeful activities so that they will lead a fulfilling life;
iv. To foster, develop and encourage positive values and character development in the children through sponsored parental programmes and strong partnership between the educators and the parents/guardians of the children;
v. To promote continuous education and training to develop the human resource management of passionate educators through training and development programmes that equip them with relevant and necessary skills to educate children from diverse backgrounds and abilities to ensure an inclusive and holistic preschool education programme for the children enrolled at the Company's preschools;
vi. To collaborate with grassroots community organisations to garner resources and develop strategies to promote the quality preschool education for children in their constituencies;
vii. To make good use of national grants and funds to provide a continuum of the Company's programmes so as to develop the children into contributors to the larger community and society;
viii. To bring together persons and organisations passionate in the field of preschool education to facilitate the dissemination and exchange of information and ideas in the field;
ix. To donate a portion of its annual profits to the general community to uplift social and educational levels and promote the good causes of the Company;
x. To undertake, embark on and manage activities and projects and research activities to enhance the objects of the Company; and
xi. To comply with the operators' obligations specified in the funding agreement for the Early Childhood Development Agency Childcare Partner Operator Scheme or any future revisions made thereto.

KMCDCL will partner with corporations and individuals who identify with and supports its mission in society and will support government policies which serve to advance the quality of preschool education for children in Singapore
Our Vision
A First-Class Provider in Early Childhood Care & Education

Our Mission
Provide a quality and innovative programme that nurtures children to be socially responsible citizens, active learners and thinkers.

Our Philosophy
We believe in providing an engaging and positive environment for the children to develop their potential to the fullest in all aspects of development. Children/Youth

name: KRSNA’S FREE MEALS
search_text:
KRSNA’S FREE MEALS 1.Run free meals distribution center for poor,needy Singaporean and needy migrant workers

2.To promote healthy vegetarian food.

3.To enhance healthier youth and family life through organizing various educational,recreational,social and welfare activities.Pick up senior citizens from different locations and have sumptuous vegetarian meals at Krsna;s Free Meals.


4.To contribute to the social stability and well being of Singapore citizens.

(a)organise fund raising projects and publicise its activities through publication,exhibits,audio,video,telecomunications and computer technology,subject to approval from relevant authorities

(b)Provide and organise a series of facilities and activities focused primarily on counselling services to all those seeking assistance regardless of race,religion or financial ability.These including moral and spiritual counselling for troubled youth,persons with marriage and/or family problem as well as former drug/substance addicts without providing detoxification facilities and services.

(c)Follow up on such services eventually with an expansion intended to include a day care centre,free medical consultation etc.,subject to approval from relevant authorities. Projection of Activities

1. Volunteer Pool
1.1 Our immediate need is to expand our volunteer database. We need to increase from our current 120 volunteers to 200.

2. Jalan Besar GRC

2.1 We want to work more closely with Jalan Besar GRC.

2.2 We have observed there’s a pool of 159 elderly members who are part of the Kampong Glam needy group during our Charity Biryani 2020.

3. Gala Dinner 2020

3.1 We plannned to hold a Gala Dinner this year in September. The exact date will depend on the current Coronovirus situation. We will be inviting about 200 elderly beneficiaries to celebrate with us.

4. Project Chulia 2020

4.1 We will also be participating in Project Chulia but in light of the Coronovirus we have decided to postpone our participation to Aug 2020 this year. We will liaise with Mr Shaun, the project manager to find out more details about the project for this year.

5. Special Lunch

5.1 We will continue to commemorate all public holidays with special lunch and also include International Migrants’ Day, World Vegetarian Day, Mother’s Day and Father’s Day.

5.2 We are planning to purchase a passenger cum goods van with the intention to ferry the elderly with limited mobility to our free food centre and also could be used to collect vegetables and groceries for our free food centre.

6.New Activities for 2020

6.1The new activities that we want to launch on 1st July are Talks on healthy Lifestyle, Vegetarian cooking classes and counselling classes for the needy locals and migrant community.

7.Reduce food waste

7.1 Source for vegetables that are still edible from Pasir Panjang Wholesale Centre and turn them into sumptuous meals at Krsna\s Free Meals

7.2 Working with SG food rescue to collect left overs from our venue at the end of the day and redistribute them.

Krsna's Free Meals (previously Krishna's Kitchen) is a soup kitchen in Singapore run by like-minded volunteers who are compassionate and determined to serve those in need. Krsna's Free Meals serves... Community Community development Social service and welfare

name: Kampong Tengah Thian Hou Keng
search_text: Kampong Tengah Thian Hou Keng A physical statue of  MAH CHO  which means godness of the sea was discovered some eighty years back in a kampong in the sea during one the high tide period by of villager staying in the kampong.     This Kampong is currently located somewhere in Seng Kang estate.	  As the main source of living for this Kampong was fishing,  MAH CHO  was the deity, which was worshipped by almost every household. It is general belief that  MAH CHO  will bless and protect fisherman who need to venture into the dangerous sea to earn an income for the family. The crew of fisherman will be blessed to return safely to land with abundance catch each and every trip made.	  After discovering of statue of  MAH CHO , a simple temple made of wood and  attap  was built to house the statue as a place of worship for villagers. It also served an gathering place where villagers of all ages could gather to improve relationship and share experiences.    Due to re-settlement  requirement imposed by government, it had to make way in middle of eighties . Due to the lack of funds, the temple was unable to find a location in Singapore to re-settle and the next best alternative was a vacant plot on Pulau Ubin with makeshift temple. This served as temporary measure for worshippers to continue their respect to deity and prayed for peace and harmony to their families.    Looking for suitable and permanent settlement begun. Through generous contribution of the public, worshippers and sponsors, the temple managed to begin construction on plot of land where it current sits. The temple finally completed it s construction in year 2005.	  It s through the firm belief of  MAH CHO  power by kampong folks in early twenties and this belief passed down from generations that built up the team spirit, cohesiveness that this temple exists today.	This temple will continue to fulfill it s mission of providing a place of worship to bring peace, prosperity and charity to public. To the kampong folks, it s place for gathering to strengthen relationship, contribute ideas to better manage the temple and passing down the values and the   MAH CHO  belief for more generations to come just like what was passed down from for-fathers. Temple as place of worship to bring peace and prosperity to the public and charity works in return to the public Taoism

name: Kew Ong Yah Temple
search_text:
Kew Ong Yah Temple OBJECTIVES:
1. To promote and impart the religious teaching and beliefs of Yew Ong Yah and the other deities worshipped in the Kew Ong Yah Temple;

2. To foster good relationship among believers and encourage cultivation of good moral values.
MISSION
• Kew Ong Yah temple serve as places of worship where devotees pray, perform rituals, and make offerings to Kew Ong Yah (Nine Emperor Gods) and other deities. The primary goal is to promote spiritual well-being, peace, and harmony through practices that align with Kew Ong Yah beliefs, such as praying for health, prosperity, and guidance from deities.

• We also function as cultural centers, hosting Taoist festivals, rites of passage, and community events. We organize celebrations for festivals such as the Lunar New Year, Deities Annual Prayers and Celebrations and the Hungry Ghost Festival, fostering a sense of community and cultural identity



• We often engage in charitable work and provide services to the local community. This includes providing free food items and daily necessity items the poor and low income families, or offering educational support to research on Taoism, in line with Taoist principles of compassion and service to others.

VISION
• To be a welcoming and inclusive space where individuals can connect with Kew Ong Yah religious practice and seek spiritual fulfillment through prayer, meditation, and the cultivation of virtues such as harmony, simplicity, and compassion.

• To promote and preserve Kew Ong Yah religious practices, and cultural heritage for future generations, ensuring that the teachings of Kew Ong Yah beliefs and practices continue to be relevant and accessible in modern life.

• To inspire individuals to live in balance with nature and the universe, encouraging the practice of harmony in all aspects of life—personal, social, and environmental.

• To serve as a hub for social welfare, charitable initiatives, and community engagement, reflecting the Kew Ong Yah values of compassion and service. The temple may seek to uplift the local community by providing aid to those in need and promoting unity through shared cultural practices.
• To act as a bridge between different cultures and religious communities in Singapore, promoting mutual respect, dialogue, and understanding, while maintaining the unique identity of Taoism in Singapore’s multicultural society.

• To provide guidance and support for individuals facing life’s challenges by offering divination, wisdom, and counsel through Taoist practices and spiritual leaders.
Taoism

1. To propagate the Mahayana teachings in Buddhism, that is the Great Conveyance.
2. To foster and develop interest and appreciation in Buddhist Culture and Buddhist Art.
3. To do such other things as are incidental or conducive to the attainment of the above objects and provided that nothing shall be done solely for commercial reasons or solely for profit, the Society may: -
3.1. promote friendship and understanding amongst Members irrespective of race and cultural background and to enhance peace, harmony, co-operation in our multi-religious and multi-racial society of Singapore.
3.2. liaise with other Buddhist organisations in Singapore in connection with Buddhist faith and festivals.
3.3. undertake to pay all or any of the debts and liabilities incurred by the Society;
3.4. accept any gift of property from Donors whether subject to trust or otherwise given to meet the objects of the Society;
3.5. collect funds in connection to the services and facilities provided;
3.6. print and publish any periodicals, books or leaflets that the Society may think desirable or necessary for the promotion of the objects;
3.7. subscribe to any local or other charities and to grant donations for any public purpose. name: Kim Tian Christian Church Singapore
search_text:
Kim Tian Christian Church Singapore 3 Objects We, the Christians of this Church, having received grace, peace and life through the love of God and the salvation of Jesus Christ, and knowing the needs of sinners and our duty to help them, hereby organise ourselves under the guidance of the Holy Spirit to promote the following objectives:
3.1 To preach the Gospel of Jesus Christ.
3.2 To meet regularly on Sundays to worship God in spirit and truth. (John 4:24)
3.3 To help Christians grow in Biblical knowledge and truth.
3.4 To emphasize the power of the Holy Spirit and the need for believers to lead righteous lives.
3.5 To promote social concern and charitable services through the establishment of a Family Counselling Centre and/or Education Centre.
3.6 To publish relevant and helpful materials to promote the cause of the Church (e.g. newsletters, books, tracts, flyers, etc.). Publication content must not contravene the laws of the country.
3.7 To establish a self-supporting, self-reliant, self-propagating and self-governing Church with the aim of fulfilling the Church s mission.
3.8 To establishing Branch Churches in accordance to the regulations of the Societies' Act. OUR VISION: We are committed to growing a loving FAMILY church through the proclamation of the Gospel of Jesus Christ.
OUR MISSION:
Matt 22:37-38 Loving God Love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your mind. This is the first and greatest commandment.
Matt 28:19-20 Loving People Therefore go and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit, and teaching them to obey everything I have commanded you. And surely I am with you always, to the very end of the age. Christianity

In furtherance of the above objectives, KIN may also produce resources that support the preceding two objectives (with the approval of relevant authorities where necessary). Vision: The Church enabled to welcome and disciple people of all abilities

Explanation: Our vision emphasises our conviction that churches are enabled when people with disabilities are included, in line with God’s design for the Church to reflect unity in diversity (cf. 1 Corinthians 12:12).

Mission: Partnering Churches by equipping, resourcing and connecting them in their inclusion journey.

Explanation: Churches are enabled when they celebrate God’s goodness through selfless service that involves everybody whom God has called to Himself, especially people with disabilities. People with disabilities are called to be discipled and to disciple others in the way of Jesus Christ. Christianity

name: LEAP PHILANTHROPY LTD.
search_text:
LEAP PHILANTHROPY LTD. 1. To enhance, improve and empower, with a sense of honour, dignity and self­-respect, the lives of the impoverished and victims of war, unrest, natural and/or man-made disasters and other humanitarian tragedies and disasters by promoting and organising humanitarian relief efforts and encouraging, undertaking, embarking on and managing activities and projects.
2. To render emergency assistance and humanitarian relief to the impoverished and victims of war, unrest, natural and/or man-made disasters and other humanitarian tragedies and disasters.
3. To promote, cultivate and encourage humanitarianism among its members and the public.
4. To promote and encourage a civil life of compassion, care and volunteerism among its members and the public.
5. To educate and promote awareness in the Singapore Community and the public of the suffering of the impoverished and victims of war, unrest, natural and/or man-made disasters and other humanitarian tragedies and disasters by promoting, undertaking and embarking on research activities.
6. To achieve the above objects, the Company shall do all such lawful things which are incidental or conducive to the attainment of the above objects or any of them and provided that nothing shall be done solely for commercial reasons of profit. Without prejudice to the generality of the foregoing, the Company may:-
a. make donations and provide funding and other resources for humanitarian aid programmes;
b. provide research facilities, consultation services and skills training in humanitarian relief work;
c. raise funds through lawful means for the purposes of contributing to any humanitarian aid programme;
d. conduct and carry out activities, projects, and seminars to provide the necessary training and guidance in skills development for the impoverished and victims of war, unrest, natural and/or man-made disasters and other humanitarian tragedies and disasters poor for the purpose of improving and promoting their economic/business activity in the economy, and to provide them with the necessary tools of trade if need be;
e. do all such lawful things as are necessary for the advancement of the interests and lives of the poor and the suffering;
f. collaborate and cooperate with any other humanitarian aid agencies and organisations involved in humanitarian relief programmes and with similar humanitarian goals;
g. enter into any cooperative arrangements with the Government, statutory bodies or any other organisations that may seem conducive to the Company's objects, or any of them; and obtain from the Government, statutory bodies or any other organisations, any rights, privileges, and concessions which the Company may think it desirable to obtain; and to carry out, exercise, and comply with any such cooperative arrangements, rights, privileges and concessions, for the purposes of the Company;
h. purchase, take on lease or in exchange, hire or otherwise acquire any real and personal estate or property which may be deemed necessary or convenient for any of the purposes of the Company;
i. obtain, collect and receive monies and funds by way of contribution, donation, affiliation fees, subscriptions, legacies, grants and any other lawful method, and accept and receive gifts of property of any description (whether subject to any special trust or not);
j. construct, maintain, alter and upkeep any house, building or works necessary or convenient for the purposes of the Company;
k. take all necessary steps for the purpose of procuring contributions to the Company in any form;
l. sell, manage, lease, dispose of, transfer or otherwise deal with all or any part of the property of the Company, for the purposes of the Company;
m. acquire by subscription, purchase or otherwise, to accept and take, hold and sell, shares or stock in any company, corporation or undertaking for the purposes of the Company;
n. undertake and execute any trust or any agency business which may seem directly or indirectly conducive to any of the objects of the Company;
o. establish and aid in the establishment of any charity or institution of a public character and to subscribe to, support, aid in the support of or otherwise aid benevolent, charitable, national or other charitable institutions and to grant donations to any charity or institution of a public character approved and registered under the Charities Act for the purposes of the Company;
p. pay all costs, charges and expenses incurred or sustained in or about the promotion and establishment of the Company and to remunerate any person or persons for services rendered in the promotion and establishment of the Company;
q. accept stock or shares in, or the debentures, mortgage debentures or other securities of any company, corporation or society in payment or part payment for any services rendered or for any sale made to or debt owing from any such company, corporation or society;
r. subject to the approval of the Minister, borrow or raise money from local banks and other financial institutions in such manner as the Company shall think fit and to secure the repayment of any money borrowed, raised or owing by mortgage, charge or lien upon all or any of the property or assets of the Company (both present and future);
s. to bring or defend any litigation proceedings or other court applications that the Company deems necessary to assert and/or protect its legal rights and/or interests, to enable, assist or permit the Company to attain any of the objects of the Company herein expressed and/or any lawful thing incidental or conducive to the attainment of those objects, and/or to vindicate the good name of the Company;
t. to do all such other lawful things as are incidental or conducive to the attainment of the above objects or any of them;
u. to mobilise, organise and coordinate resources to facilitate and assist in humanitarian relief programmes and efforts whether in Singapore or in any other countries in need where the effects of war, unrest, natural and/or man-made disasters, poor economic and/or social conditions have rendered suffering on its people regardless of their religion and ethnicity;
v. to organise, develop and support self-help and humanitarian aid programmes and relief work anywhere in the world through the process of multi-ethnic and multi-religious engagement and cooperation; and
w. to promote the research and development of effective humanitarian policies. Vision: A Southeast Asia where every individual lives with dignity and freedom.

Mission: To empower vulnerable households to break out of the aid dependence and poverty cycle, and to leap above the poverty line of US$2 a day.

Leap partners with like-minded organisations to provide social loans and grants for projects that fundamentally address the challenges faced by vulnerable communities in Southeast Asia. General Charitable Purposes

name: LEW FOUNDATION
search_text:
LEW FOUNDATION The objects for which the Company is established are:
(i) the promotion of health;
(ii) the advancement of education; and
(iii) other purposes beneficial to the community in Singapore and elsewhere including but not limited to:

(a) the relief of poverty;
(b) the advancement of citizenship or community development;
(c) the advancement of arts, heritage or science;
(d) the advancement of environmental protection or improvement; and
(e) the relief of those in need by reason of youth, age, ill-health, disability, financial hardship or other disadvantages.

The objects of the Company shall be carried out for the benefit of persons of all races, language or religion.
VISION

We aspire to be a foundation that makes real impact in improving the lives of the elderly, the poor and disadvantaged groups in the areas of healthcare, education and special education as well as supporting cultural or social activities that promotes better harmony in Singapore.


MISSION
The Foundation’s key areas of focus are healthcare, education, special education and cultural/social activities that promote harmony in Singapore. Within these areas, we are committed to

- Provide relief and financial support to the target groups;

- Develop partnerships with institutions to enhance the provision of facilities and services to the target groups; and

- Be an advocate for greater commitment in training and development of staff or volunteers for such facilities and services; and

- Selectively support cultural and social activities that promote stronger ties amongst all races and improve harmony in Singapore.

General Charitable Purposes

name: LI FOUNDATION LIMITED
search_text:
LI FOUNDATION LIMITED The objects for which the Company is established are the giving of grants for the purposes of:
(a) advancing poverty alleviation, education, health, and welfare through social innovation and impact investment;
(b) promoting the culture of philanthropy and advance community development by supporting public participation in innovative charitable activities through various avenues, including sports, culture, and art; and
(c) developing the capabilities of the executives of the social entrepreneurial teams and empowering them to achieve greater social impact.
Based in Singapore and with a focus on South-East Asia, the Company is committed to providing grants to innovative and impactful projects. The Company also seeks to discover, and work hand in hand together with, young social entrepreneurial teams with the aim of developing innovative approaches to address social problems, as well as to empower such social entrepreneurial teams so as to multiply their potential to build a better world with a brighter future, by adopting a multi-faceted approach and integrating cross-border insights and resources. name: LIAN SHAN SHUANG LIN MONASTERY
search_text: LIAN SHAN SHUANG LIN MONASTERY (a)	To teach and propagate Buddhist doctrines and to promote activities conducive to Buddhist education, civilisation, benevolent affairs and to develop and expand Buddhism.    (b)	To promote the teachings of Buddha by providing a Temple and all other necessary facilities and buildings to be used in perpetuity, for training of monks, the worshipping of Buddha by monks, and others irrespective of race and sex who are Buddhists or Believers in Buddhism, the conducting of classes, seminars and courses of instruction on Buddhism, sutra recitation sessions and the publication of literature on Buddhism.    (c)	To provide all necessary facilities and building incidental to the operation of the Temple, including the provision of accommodation and food for monks and the provision of Buddhists religious services, both for the living and the dead.	(d)	To perform Buddhist religious ceremonies in unity with all Buddhists in Singapore and to observe the Buddha precepts and to put into practice the Salvationism of the Mahayana (ie., the Great Conveyance).    (e)	To carry out such purposes as are charitable including:-    (i)	The relief of poverty, suffering and distress among the poor and needy who by reason of old age are wholly or in part unable to maintain themselves and their dependents by their own exertion and to assist widows and orphans.    (ii)	To provide financial assistance for the education of the dependents of the poor and the needy referred to in paragraph (e)(i) above.	(f)	To do such other things as are incidental or conducive to the attainment of the above objects and provided that nothing shall be done solely for commercial reasons or solely for profit, the Temple may:-    (i)	undertake to pay all or any of the debts and liabilities incurred by the Temple.    (ii)	construct, maintain and alter any building, place of worship or works necessary or convenient for the purposes of the Temple and teaching of Buddhism.	  (iii)	accept any gift of property from Donors whether subject to trust or otherwise given to meet the objects of the Temple.	  (iv)	take such steps by personal or written appeal, public meetings or otherwise, as may from time to time be deemed expedient for the purpose of procuring contributions to the funds of the Temple, in the shape of donations, annual subscriptions or otherwise.	  (v)	sell, manage, lease, mortgage, dispose of or otherwise deal with all or any part of the property of the Temple.    (vi)	collect funds in connection to the services and facilities provided.	(vii)	provide and maintain residential facilities which are of reasonable and befitting standards for monks, temple devotees and guests of the Temple.    (viii)	provide food for worshippers and visitors of the Temple and to make such reasonable charges (if necessary) therefore as may be considered proper.    (ix)	make such reasonable charges (if necessary) as may be considered proper for religious services provided for the urns and ancestral tablets and other receptacles provided by the Temple. (xi)	print and publish any newspapers, periodical books or leaflets that the Temple may think desirable or necessary for the promotion of its objects.    (xii)	subscribe to any local or other charities and to grant donations for any public purpose. (vii)	provide and maintain residential facilities which are of reasonable and befitting standards for monks, temple devotees and guests of the Temple.    (viii)	provide food for worshippers and visitors of the Temple and to make such reasonable charges (if necessary) therefore as may be considered proper.    (ix)	make such reasonable charges (if necessary) as may be considered proper for religious services provided for the urns and ancestral tablets and other receptacles provided by the Temple.    (x)	perform all or any of the religious precepts authorised by Buddhists either alone or in conjunction with or as trustees for any association or person, having the same objects as the Temple.	 (xi)	print and publish any newspapers, periodical books or leaflets that the Temple may think desirable or necessary for the promotion of its objects.    (xii)	subscribe to any local or other charities and to grant donations for any public purpose. Buddhism

name: LIEN FOUNDATION
search_text:
LIEN FOUNDATION The Lien Foundation is committed to Lead, Innovate, Empower and Network for the benefit of society:
Lead: We spearhead strategic partnerships and activities to deliver practical, sustainable solutions for society’s benefit.
Innovate: We mobilise resources, and pushes boundaries for new ways of addressing urgent needs and critical gaps.
Empower: We enable society and individuals to operate at their maximum capacity. Where possible, we equip communities and its leaders with the know-how, initiating change from within.
Network: We are well-positioned to stimulate and spark high-impact idea exchange, high-intensity collaboration, and high-end value creation by leveraging on the people, private and public sectors.
The Lien Foundation conceives and funds services in eldercare and early childhood development in Singapore. The Foundation also commissioned research publications in each of these areas and champions causes such as inclusion in education and tackling frailty in seniors. Overseas, it strives to improve access to palliative care services in low and middle-income countries in South and Southeast Asia. Community Eldercare

name: LIFE COMMUNITY LTD.
search_text:
LIFE COMMUNITY LTD. (A) The objects for which the Company is established (“Objects”) are:

(a) to provide a comprehensive remedial, preventive and developmental service for individuals and families facing or encountering crisis and conflicts;

(b) to provide advice, counselling service, guidance and assistance to students in the areas of education and career development, crisis management, lifestyle management and other related issues or problems by way of school visitation, counselling, workshops, seminars, forums and camps;

(c) to provide, grant or participate in providing or granting relief and aid to the sick, poor and needy; and

(d) to promote education and participate in schemes calculated to promote education.


(B) In furtherance of the above Objects but not otherwise, and provided that nothing is done for commercial reasons or solely for profit, the Company may exercise the following powers:

(a) conduct interviews both for the individuals and groups through psychological tests, home visits, workshops and seminars on issues concerning developmental psychology affecting changes and problems in life;

(b) establish, develop and provide a network between related organizations for comprehensive information resources and referral;

(c) conduct and organize workshops, seminars, forums and training sessions on communication skills, stress management, lifestyle management, self perceptions, inter-personal skills, crisis management, basic financial management and related issues;

(d) act as a catalyst for the training and development of a pool of volunteers for social and community services and the recruitment, selection, training and development of suitably qualified volunteers for such services and to provide internship opportunities and exposures for students involved in social work studies as required in their curriculum;

(e) promote and provide social and community services and to participate in or co-operate with groups or organizations in community-related projects;

(f) establish, maintain and manage, subject to the approval of the relevant authorities, before and after school care centres for needy students;

(g) publish or print books, literature, magazines, bulletins, newsletters, periodicals or leaflets which the Company may deem desirable for the declaration, promotion or advancement of its objects;

(h) receive and take any gifts of money or property for any of the purposes and objects herein stated whether subject to any special trust or not so that the Board may nevertheless decline to accept any gifts or donations or to take over any property which has annexed to it any condition or obligations not approved by the Board; and

(i) make investments, engage in or conduct social enterprise activities or other initiatives for the purposes of raising funds for its maintenance and operations.

PROVIDED ALWAYS that the Company shall exist for purposes which are charitable and notwithstanding anything hereinbefore contained, nothing shall be an Object which is not a charitable object. Vision: Every child, the best that they can be
Mission: Empowering children and families through care and mentoring

LUTHERAN COMMUNITY CARE SERVICES LIMITED Show concern and provide care for the young, family and elderly through remedial, preventive and development work. LCCS envisions “all individuals are engaged to build and sustain healthy relationships.” LCCS seeks to realise its vision through its 4 key thrusts: break the hurt cycle, equip empowered individuals, build connected communities, and advocate a restorative movement. LCCS builds, restores, and strengthens relationships to break the cycle of bullying, financial distress, domestic violence, sexual harm, and criminal offending behaviours. LCCS journeys with individuals and families affected by these issues to work towards their hopes and dreams with their community of care.
The vision serves as a constant reminder to the LCCS team that everyone has worth, and we respect their need for “voice, agency and belonging” when connecting with them.
LCCS' theological guiding principle through faith active in love is fulfilling the mandate of caring for God's creation without any strings attached is important to establish LCCS as a professional social service outfit. This fundamental principle serves the church well as LCCS serves a multi-ethnic group from different religious backgrounds. LCCS practices an inclusive approach to Human Resource Practices as it hires and promote staff based on qualification, attitude and competency. 3. To promote and encourage the praticipation of players,teams, organisations and individuals from Singapore who are interested in lawn bowls for the disabled in International, regional, local or other championships and events.
4. To engender by association a fraternal feeling amongst lawn bowlers especially amongst the disabled and to preverve and promote the best traditions of the sport
5. To conform at all times with the requirements of the laws of the lawn bowls as adopted by the world body known "International Paralympics Committee" or other world governing body controlling the lawn bowls for the disabled.
6. To offer facilites and coaching in the sport of lawn bowls. The Lawn Bowling Association for the disabled (Singapore) was setup with a mission of getting rid of the stigma that a person with a disability is incapable of participating in sports. A disability is just a funcitonal limitation whether it is physical, mental and visual. Lawn bowling is one of the few sports where anyone can participate and enjoy the activity regardless of any disabilities or ages. The game improves fitness, develops new skills, increases social contacts and helps in enjoying the challenges of competitions. Above all, the game gives an intellectual stimulation and it is an ideal sport for Singapore where there is a fast growing number of senior citizens. name: Logos Christian Church
search_text:
Logos Christian Church (a) To worship the Almighty God; (b) To propagate, proclaim and spread the good news that Jesus Christ through His death, burial and resurrection has provided the way of salvation for all mankind and to assist in the extension of His kingdom, respecting the freedom of belief in a multi-religious Singapore; (c) To teach and preach the whole truth of God as revealed in His written Word, the Bible; (d) To provide fellowship and an atmosphere in which believers may be taught and may grow in the grace and knowledge of our Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ; (e) To assist believers in developing spiritual and devotional life towards discipleship through the regular practice of prayer and bible study; (f) To train and send out Christians to carry out Christ s mission in the world; (g) To promote or participate in promoting the welfare of mankind based on Christian, charitable and benevolent principles; (h) To promote education or participate in schemes aimed at promoting education having a Christian emphasis and to maintain a creche, kindergarten or day-care centre; (i) To grant or participate in granting relief and aid to the poor, sick and needy; (j) To publish any periodicals, newsletters, leaflets or books that the Church may deem desirable and necessary for attaining the objects of the Church. Based on the Bible, the Logos Christian Church had received its mission to edify the body of Christ as well as its mission statement. The mission of our church is to make disciples of all nations through evangelizing, teaching and training and to equip the saints so that they can serve and edify the church, and live in the midst of community carrying faith, hope, and love.
Our mission statement is: “Fulfill the mission, Equip the saints, Live out faith, hope and love.”

The Logos Christian Church starts with preaching the gospel to the local and foreign Mandarin or dialect speaking Chinese, as well as those with cultural similarity, making them disciples of Christ, and equipping them to edify the body of Christ; these people live in the midst of community with faith, hope, and love and at the same time reach out to the lost and people from other cultures.

Our Theological Position
Logos Christian Church is an independent church without denominational background, and the forms of worship are relatively free to change, however, it still has established and consolidated principles in respect of basic theological position. The fourth article in the Constitution of the church is the statement of faith, which established and characterized our theological position and standards of faith.

We believe that both the Old and NEW Testaments in the original texts constitute the divinely inspired Word of God, inerrant, infallible and authoritative in all matters of faith and doctrine.
We believe in one God, co-existing eternally in three persons in the same Godhead – God the Father, God the Son and God the Holy Spirit.
We believe in the eternal deity of the Lord Jesus Christ, the Son of God, born of virgin Mary, became man without ceasing to be God, and that He lived a perfect and sinless life on earth and was crucified, buried and on the third day rose again, in order that He might reveal God and redeem sinful man.
We believe that the Holy Spirit came to convict the world of sin, of righteousness and of judgment; and to regenerate, sanctify, comfort and seal those who believe in Jesus Christ, and to empower them with spiritual gifts for the carrying out of the work of the Lord Jesus Christ.
We believe that man is sinful by nature and choice, and is totally unable to remedy his lost condition except by the grace of God.
We believe that salvation of man’s eternal being is the effect of regeneration or spiritual rebirth brought about by the Holy Spirit of God and is the gift of God granted to sinful man by the grace of God through repentance toward God and personal faith in Jesus Christ, whose atoning blood was shed on the cross once and for all for the forgiveness of sins. Christianity

MAN FATT LAM ELDERLY JOY DAY CENTRE To provide day care and rehabilitative service for the aged, to promote their physical, mental and spiritual welfare and for that purpose, to acquire, build and/or maintain premises to facilitate and benefit such persons and services. Adhering to the wish of Master Faquan to provide a loving and caring environment for senior citizens to spend in their old age, the elderly day care centre is established.

In line with the tenet of love and care, we seek to provide a safe and comfortable paradise for the elderly, so that they can live their old age with a peaceful, healthy and active lifestyle. Through our service, we also seek to prevent mental deterioration of the elderly, by ensuring every senior citizen have an optimistic spirit throughout their old age.

We are also committed in supporting and improving the quality of life of the elderly and their ability to live independently to maintain good physical and mental health.

name: MERCY CENTRE LTD.
search_text:
MERCY CENTRE LTD. As per attached Memorandum and Articles of Association. Mercy Centre’s vision is to act justly, love mercy, and walk humbly. Mercy Centre is a non-profit organisation registered as a charity in Singapore. Its objective is to provide all manner of support, relief and assistance to the marginalised and those in need.

Mercy Centre provides training and holistic social empowerment programmes helping its volunteers to eradicate social needs within their communities. We bring good news to the poor, bind up the brokenhearted and proclaim liberty to the destitute.

Mercy Centre believes and recognises that every individual is unique, intrinsically valuable and has a place in community. Its activities and programmes emphasise the importance of relationships, family, community, stewardship, character and integrity. Our philosophy is to address the physical, emotional and spiritual needs of our destitute and less fortunate friends.

Mercy Centre trains its volunteers to be vigilant, caring and respectful while engaging the destitute in their communities. We encourage our volunteers to build friendship relationships with the destitute within their communities. Our volunteers come from all walks of life who passionately act justly, love mercy and walk humbly. Children/Youth Community Family

name: MERCY RELIEF LIMITED
search_text:
MERCY RELIEF LIMITED Mercy Relief is an independent, non-governmental humanitarian organisation established in response to human tragedies in Asia.

We provide timely and effective aid to communities affected by natural or man-made disasters, and implement longer-term development programmes to uplift lives. Our core development work focuses on five key areas: water and sanitation, shelter, sustainable livelihoods, healthcare and education.

Mercy Relief also promotes a spirit of compassion, care and volunteerism within society. We work with individuals and communities to cultivate greater understanding, cooperation and empathy across all backgrounds. Our engagements are grounded in respect for human dignity and a belief in empowering communities to recover, rebuild and thrive.

In addition to humanitarian response and development aid, we conduct training, education, and research to improve public awareness of humanitarian issues. Mercy Relief supports multi-ethnic and multi-religious collaboration, and advocates for humanitarian values and good practices across civil society and the wider public.

We actively collaborate with partners, volunteers and organisations to strengthen capacity and coordination in disaster response and development work. Our efforts are guided by principles of humanity, impartiality, neutrality, independence and accountability.

Mercy Relief serves the less fortunate and needy regardless of country, culture or creed. We are committed to reaching those most in need, and to supporting communities with compassion, dignity, and the tools for long-term resilience.

Through our work, we strive to foster a more humane and responsive world, built on shared responsibility and universal care. Vision: Mercy Relief envisions a compassionate world free from suffering and poverty, where communities support and care for one another.

Mission: Mercy Relief provides timely and effective aid when disasters strike, and promotes a life of compassion and care. We work with communities in Asia to alleviate suffering caused by natural and man-made disasters, and to empower them towards recovery, resilience and long-term development. LATEST ANNOUNCEMENT Mercy Relief has been granted Overseas Humanitarian Assistance Tax Deduction Scheme (OHAS) status by Ministry of Community, Culture & Youth, Singapore (MCCY). Effective 9 Januar... Humanitarian Aid Community development Education Environmental protection and sustainability Overseas humanitarian aid

by vehicles, abuse by humans or other pet animals;
- To treat terminally ill animals and animals abandoned on the
streets;
- To construct adequate infrastructure for taking care of sick
and stray animals;
- To advance literacy and general education and publish books,
periodicals and other literature to promote animal welfare and
stopping cruelty upon animals;
- To cooperate with institutions having similar aims and objects
and to work in coordination with the animal welfare
organizations;
- To offer assistance to other institutions with similar objectives,
professionally and financially; and
- To receive donations for the abovementioned objectives. - improve the quality of lives of stray dogs through rescue, rehabilitaion and re-homing them

- increase public awareness and acceptance of stray dogs as companion animals and they deserved to be treated with compassion and kindness

community;

name: MONTESSORI SENIORS LTD.
search_text:
MONTESSORI SENIORS LTD. The purpose and objects of “Montessori Seniors Ltd” shall be:
a) The acquisition and dissemination of knowledge to the public
at large concerning dementia care.

b) To foster national and international care-giving activities with
a focus on the challenges of coping with Dementia and
Alzheimer’s disease.

c) To use the Montessori approach to educate, enable and
empower seniors and adults with dementia to enjoy quality
of life through meaningful activity.

d) To train caregivers in the Montessori approach, equipping
them with vital skills to care for people with dementia at home
and in a community.

e) To promote the Montessori approach as a model among
organisations, nurturing professionals trained in purposeful,
quality dementia care.

f) To provide support and training to caregivers and healthcare
professionals to equip them in their care of persons with
dementia.
g) To promote successful ageing for elders through programs
and activities based on the Montessori principles

Generally to do all such other things as conducive or incidental to
the attainment of the above objects or any of them:

(i) Develop and formulate training programmes and public
education campaigns or services to benefit the elderly in the
community, persons with dementia, caregivers and healthcare
professionals.

(ii) Provide assistance, information and dementia care to
enhance the quality and well-being of the elderly, persons with
dementia, caregivers, family members, helpers, friends and
volunteers.

(iii) To foster a community support group support among
members and to facilitate mutual assistance on the practice of
care and education for the community that is inclusive for
persons with dementia.

(iv) Raise awareness concerning the community interests and
participation in the general dementia care of elderly sick and
caregivers.

(v) Create opportunities for the community and caregivers to be
trained.

(vi) To render assistance to enable the persons with dementia
and their caregivers greater access to available community

(vii) To provide short-term welfare assistance to persons with

Our Vision

We want to see people with dementia in the community,
engaged with one another and empowered in their own care.

Our Mission

● To provide holistic person-centred care using the Montessori
approach to address the physical, emotional, cognitive and
social aspects of the person.

● To create an environment where seniors and adults with
dementia feel accepted, safe and comfortable; where they as
care recipients are treated with respect, dignity, and are
encouraged to be independent.

● To conduct activities that are well-planned, implemented, and
evaluated to engage, enable and maximise the ability of care
recipients.

● To design and offer activities that care recipients find
purposeful and meaningful.

● To create roles for care recipients and the sense of belonging
to a community, so that they feel that they can still contribute
and are valued.

● To cultivate caring staff who understand and are empowered
to know how to communicate and engage with the people with
dementia.

● To educate and enable family caregivers and foreign domestic
workers in dementia and elder care through the Montessori
approach.

● To train healthcare and eldercare workers to engage people
with dementia through purposeful activities.

Montessori Seniors is committed to serving persons with dementia as well as their caregivers in the community. name: MOTOR NEURONE DISEASE ASSOCIATION (SINGAPORE)
search_text:
MOTOR NEURONE DISEASE ASSOCIATION (SINGAPORE) 1. The Society’s objects are as follows:

1.1 To raise public awareness and understanding of Motor Neurone Disease (“MND”);

1.2 To promote community involvement and provide support for MND patients and their caregivers; and

1.3 To promote the adoption of assistive technologies for communication, respiratory support, mobility and daily living needs of MND patients.

2. In pursuing these objects the Society may carry out the following functions:

2.1 Raise public awareness and understanding of MND through mainstream media, social media and fundraising activities;

2.2 Raise and administer the disbursements of funds to needy MND patients according to qualifying guidelines;

2.3 Coordinate the provision of counselling service programme by social workers and volunteers;

2.4 Formulate and coordinate various programmes, such as outreach to newly diagnosed MND patients, information kits for patients and caregivers, caregivers’ respite, and peer support;

2.5 Organise regular social gatherings, activities or events to promote and strengthen the bonds and support systems available to MND patients and caregivers;

2.6 Organise regular talks, meetings and other such activities to share information and experiences among MND patients, caregivers and healthcare professionals;

2.7 Administer relevant social media platforms for sharing of information and socialising among MND patients and caregivers;

2.8 Promote positivity among MND patients and caregivers with uplifting programmes through stories and messages, mutual encouragement, and birthday wishes;

2.9 Collaborate with interested third partners in the promotion of more user-friendly applications, programs and technologies for MND patients;

2.10 Collaborate with relevant authorities, healthcare providers and welfare organisations to improve and enhance the living conditions of MND patients and caregivers; and

2.11 Promote the advancement of medical interventions and technologies, research studies and methodologies in the field of MND through:

2.11.1 Networking and collaborating with international MND communities; and

2.11.2 Sharing of relevant latest development through seminars, workshops and dialogue sessions.

3. In doing all things necessary for, incidental to, and / or in connection with the attainment of the objects of the Society, the Society recognises that patients with MND are often experts in this area. Therefore, they should be included in any consultations about their needs. All decisions pertaining to the objects set out above should be made in a collaborative manner whenever possible.
Our Vision:
Every MND Patient and Caregiver to live their Lives with Dignity, Care and Hope.

Mission:
To enable persons with MND to live in DIGNITY, with the best possible quality of life, together with family through assistance, technologies and advocacy with enhanced public awareness.

To facilitate the provision of CARE and support by building bonds and empowering through sharing of information and experiences.

To promote HOPE and positivity by encouraging one another and networking with authorities, healthcare providers, local and international communities, so as to live their lives to the fullest.

The objects of the Company shall not extend to the regulation of relations between workers and employers or organizations of workers and organizations of employers TYMFO is a Singaporean orchestra whose mission is to nurture and develop the artistic growth of Singapore’s GEN Y pre-professional orchestral musicians (instrumentalists, composers, and conductors), by providing for them the platform to smoothly transition into the professional music industry. TYMFO is currently led by its Music Director Alvin Seville Arumugam who is pursuing his Masters in Conducting at the Yong Siew Toh Conservatory of Music. TYMFO envisions being Singapore’s next professional home grown orchestra, locally formed for Singapore.

The values in every fiber of TYMFO’s being include, fearlessness in innovation, contribution to the Singapore community, camaraderie in Singapore’s artistic circle, and mastery of the art that we execute.

In harmony with TYMFO’s core values, its objectives are targeted towards two communities: the music community and the public. For the music community, it aims to hone the ensemble skills of our musicians to the highest level so that we can challenge the musical standards of Singapore, and establish true camaraderie and cooperation among the professional and pre-professional artists. For the public community, TYMFO hopes to cultivate an appreciation for orchestral music, by educating its audiences through its musical endeavors. TYMFO also aims to redefine the meaning of orchestral music in Singapore, by daring to innovate in all decisions it makes. Music & Orchestras Training & Education

name: Motor Sports Singapore
search_text: Motor Sports Singapore The objects of the Association shall be:	  (1)	To promote, encourage and develop motor sports.    (2)	To administer and/or govern motor sports in Singapore in its capacity as the National Sports Association for motor sports and, where applicable, in accordance with the sporting powers bestowed upon it by international governing bodies.    (3)	To organize or participate in motor sports events in Singapore or elsewhere and to select individuals or teams to represent Singapore in international motor sports events under the banner of Team Singapore, Motorsports.    (4)	To raise, utilize and maintain funds for the promotion, development, management and control of motor sports.	(5)	To purchase, lease or acquire lands and buildings in Singapore for use by the Association.    (6)	Generally to do all things necessary to promote the objects of the Association whether or not specifically provided for. Vision -   Towards Excellence in Motor Sports    Excellence is describe as :-  Adopting FIAs best practice framework as a ASN  Working with relevant  partners  to create a vibrant local Motor Sports events Calendar  Nurturing of Motor Sports Champions in all 3 disciplines	Mission -  To adopt a three-pronged approach in developing Motor Sports in terms of competitive, recreational and spectator aspects	 Practice  To adopt, develop and enforce national and international sporting codes and regulation for Motor Sports  Strengthen MSS' reputation as the authority for Motor Sports governance  in Singapore  Establish MSS as a knowledge centre for leading practices in Motor Sports     Participation  To engage and increase participation in Motor Sports activities at all levels  Generate public awareness and interest towards Motor Sports through collaboration with schools and community organisation  Organise Motor Sports events targeted at the community at large on a regular basis    Performance	To identity and nurture young Singaporean Motor Sports Talents	Develop and support individual and team entries, and provide organisational capabilities in the participation at international Motor Sports races  Develop a formal career roadmap for competitive racing in consultation with relevant agencies as well as coaching programme NSAs
