name: My Community
search_text:
My Community CONSTITUTION OF

MY COMMUNITY






























INTERPRETATION
1.1 “Community” refers to the physical entities with clear boundaries organised accordingly to principles of self sufficiency.
1.2 “Queenstown” refers to the public housing estates located in the Queenstown Planning Area as stated in the URA Concept Plan 2008.
1.3 “Districts” refer to the public housing districts located in Community as stated in the URA Concept Plan 2008.
1.4 “Residents” refer to the legal occupants in the Community.
1.5 “Visitors” refer to employees of registered companies located in the Community and visitors who use facilities in the Community regularly.
1.6 “Executive Board” refers to the Executive Board of My Community. “Executive Board Member” shall bear a corresponding meaning; “Members” mean members of My Community.

NAME
2.1 This Society shall be known as the “My Community”, hereinafter referred to as the “Society”.

PLACE OF BUSINESS
3.1 Its place of business shall be at Blk 10, Stirling Road #14-02, Queens Condo, Singapore 148954 or such other address as may subsequently be decided upon by the Executive Board and approved by the Registrar of Societies. The Society shall carry out its activities only in places and premises which have the prior written approval from the relevant authorities, where necessary.

OBJECTS
4.1 The objects of My Community are:
a) To integrate residents in the community regardless of race, language, religion and background.
b) To preserve, protect and educate residents and visitors on the heritage, culture and tradition in the community.
c) To research on the demographics profiling of the community and draw analysis from the statistics.
d) To liaise with grassroots and voluntary organisations in providing a better living environment for the community.
e) To instil residents and visitors with a sense of pride and belonging for the community.






















MEMBERSHIP QUALIFICATION AND RIGHTS
5.1 All the residents and patrons within the community are eligible to be members by filling the membership form. A person wishing to join the Society should submit his particulars to the Secretary on a prescribed form. The Executive Board will decide on the membership.
5.2 Only Executive Board Members appointed by the Executive Board who are above 21 years of age shall have the right to vote and to hold office in the Society.
5.3 A copy of the Constitution shall be furnished to every approved Executive Board Member.

5.4 A membership register in a form approved by the Advisor shall be maintained. The membership register shall contain, at the minimum, the following
information of all the Members:
(a) Full name;
(b) NRIC number;
(c) Postal address;
(d) E-mail address;
(e) Contact Number;
(f) Employment Status
(g) Race
(h) Date of Birth

5.5 A Member shall be liable to have his membership terminated:
(a) If he does not observe the Constitution; or
(b) If he is found to be involved in activities prejudicial to the interest and
good name of My Community.



ENTRANCE FEES, SUBSCRIPTIONS AND OTHER DUES
6.1 The entrance fees and subscriptions shall be determined by the Executive Board from time to time.
6.2 Any additional fund required for special purposes may only be raised from members with the consent of the Executive Board or Advisor.
6.3 The income and property of the Society whensoever derived shall be applied towards the promotion of the objects of the Society as set forth in this Constitution and no portion thereof shall be paid or transferred directly or indirectly by way of dividend or bonus or otherwise howsoever by way of profit to the persons who at any time are or have been members of the Association or to any of them or to any person claiming through any of them.











GENERAL MEETINGS
7.1 The supreme authority of the Society is vested in a General Meeting presided over by the President.
7.2 Executive Board Members shall hold office for two years and shall be eligible for re-election or appointment at the next General Meeting.
7.3 A General Meeting shall be held once every two years. The conduct of General Meeting shall be in accordance with the Guidelines. The General Meeting shall:
(a) Consider and approve the minutes of the last General Meeting;
(b) Consider and approve the Annual Report;
(c) Consider and approve the audited statement of accounts;
(d) Appoint Executive Board Members to the new Executive Board; and
(e) Transact any other business for which seven days’ notice in writing must be given to the Executive Board.
7.4 At least two (2) weeks' notice shall be given of an Annual General Meeting Notice of meeting stating the date, time and place of meeting shall be sent by the Secretary to all voting members. The particulars of the agenda shall be emailed to all the participants four (4) days in advance of the meeting.
7.5 The following points will be considered at the General Meeting:
(a) The previous financial year's accounts and annual report of the Executive Board.
(b) Where applicable, the appointment of office bearers and Honorary Auditors for the following term.
(c) Any member who wishes to place an item on the agenda of a General Meeting may do so provided he gives notice to either the President, the Vice-President or the Secretary one (1) week before the meeting is due to be held.
7.6 The quorum at any General Meeting shall be at least 25% of the total voting membership or thirty (30) voting members, whichever is lesser. In the event of there being no quorum at the commencement of the General Meeting, the meeting shall adjourned for half an hour and should the number then present be insufficient to form a quorum, those present shall be considered a quorum, but they shall have no power to amend any of the Constitution.
EXECUTIVE BOARD
8.1 The administration of the Society shall be entrusted to the Executive Board consisting of the following to be appointed at each alternate Annual General Meeting:
A President
A Vice President
A Secretary
A Treasurer
An Assistant Secretary
Ordinary Executive Board Members
8.2 Unless with the prior approval in writing of the Registrar or an Assistant Registrar of Societies, all the Executive Board Members shall be Singapore Citizens. In addition, the President, Secretary, Treasurer and their deputies shall be Singapore Citizen. Foreign Diplomats shall not serve as Executive Board Members.
8.3 Names for the above offices shall be proposed and seconded at the General Meeting and election will follow on a simple majority vote of the Executive Board. All office-bearers, except the Treasurer and Assistant Treasurer may be appointed to the same or related post for a consecutive term of office. The term of office of the Executive Board is two years.
8.4 Election will be either by show of hands or, subject to the agreement of the majority of the voting Executive Board members present, by a secret ballot. In the event of a tie, a re-vote shall be taken and if it still results in a tie, a lot shall be drawn to determine who shall be the successful candidate unless the contesting candidate(s) withdrew in favour of one of themselves.
8.4A Whenever a member of the Executive Board in any way, directly or indirectly, has an interest in a transaction or project or other matter to be discussed at a meeting, the member shall disclose the nature of his interest before the discussion on the matter begins. The member concerned should not participate in the discussion or vote on the matter, and should also offer to withdraw from the meeting and the Executive Board shall decide if this should be accepted.
8.5 An Executive Board Meeting shall be held at least once every 2 months after giving seven (7) days' notice to Executive Board Members. The President may call an Executive Board Meeting at any time by giving five (5) days' notice. Majority of the Executive Board Members must be present for its proceedings to be valid.
8.6 Any member of the Executive Board absenting himself from three (3) meetings consecutively without satisfactory explanations shall be deemed to have withdrawn from the Executive Board and a successor may be co-opted by the Executive Board to serve until the next General Meeting. Any changes in the Executive Board shall be notified to the Registrar of Societies and the Commissioner of Charities within two (2) weeks of the change.
8.7 The duty of Executive Board is to organise and supervise the daily activities of the Society.
8.8 The Executive Board has the power to authorise the expenditure of a sum not exceeding $300,000 per month from the Society's funds for the Society's purposes.
8.9 The Executive Board shall have the power, by a majority vote, to remove a Board member before the expiration of his period of office and may appoint other person in his stead.

DUTIES OF OFFICE BEARERS

name: NALANDA LIBRARY FUND LIMITED
search_text:
NALANDA LIBRARY FUND LIMITED (a) To create a repository of funds to build Nalanda University Library, through sponsorships, donations, gifts and grants.

(b) To provide facilities for study, research, cultivation and teaching as well as to set up the procurement and lending of training and instructional materials, articles, newspapers, journals, books and other literary works.

(c) To found scholarships and exhibitions and to give prizes, certificates and diplomas to staff and students of Nalanda University Singapore’s signal contribution to realising the vision of the new Nalanda will be the
design, building and delivery of Nalanda Library.

Nalanda’s ancient library had been a place where religious as well as secular studies such as public health, mathematics, logic, languages, and law, was discussed, debated and dissected. The Chinese monk Xuanzang, who served as inspiration for the character Tang Sanzang in the classic novel Journey to the West, travelled to Nalanda in the 7th Century in order to learn more. He stayed for five years, studying and teaching at Nalanda. Of the three stone library buildings, he described the nine-storey Ratnodadhi as “soaring into the clouds”. According to other historical accounts of Nalanda, the destruction of the ancient library came in 1193 with a fire that burned for three months, so voluminous were the books in its repository.

From the ashes of that terrible history, a new Library will now arise, phoenix-like, a gift
from Singapore to feed the creative fire of learning and higher education.
The new Nalanda library will recall its ancient role as a key learning resource. It will
advance former Chancellor Amartya Sen’s vision of Nalanda as a modern university of the highest rigour and international standards. The library will be a central feature of the
masterplan for Nalanda University -- in terms of both design and relevance. Through its new collection and by housing collections from individuals and institutions world-wide, the aim is for the library to be one of India’s leading centres for research.
This contribution from yourself in your personal capacity or from your company will be
acknowledged and recognised at the Library with the honour roll of benefactors at its site.

New generations of students to this reborn University will benefit from Singapore’s
privileged opportunity to gift to Nalanda its Library. Your leadership contribution to this
significant gift will be received with gratitude. General Charitable Purposes

name: NEW LIFE STORIES LIMITED
search_text:
NEW LIFE STORIES LIMITED The objects for which the Company is established shall be as follows:-

(1) To promote, generate, foster, and develop among low-income, underprivileged, disadvantaged and/or at-risk women, children and other persons deemed appropriate by the Company (collectively "Beneficiaries") a greater understanding, knowledge and skills for social mobility.


(2) To encourage, support, and advise similarly oriented not for profit organisations to follow the Company's educational model and to deliver a corresponding set of educational and personal development opportunities to Beneficiaries.

(3) To aid, assist, grant donations and provide support to improve the knowledge, capability, and opportunity of Beneficiaries for social mobility.

(4) To develop and provide education, mentoring, and/or development services to Beneficiaries.

(5) To do such other things as are incidental or conducive to the attainment of the above objects for the following purpose:

(a) To involve and encourage the public to make meaningful contributions to Beneficiaries, including through financial or in-kind gifts, providing assistance in technology transfer, education, training, mentoring, or other administrative or development support through the Company's program or otherwise;

(b) To teach and foster and undertake research into any aspects of the objects of the Company and its work and to disseminate educational materials and the results of any research;

(c) To print and publish any newspapers, periodicals, books, journals, papers, monographs or leaflets that the Company may think desirable for the promotion of its objects;

(d) To adopt such means of making known and advertising the Company and its objects and program as may seem expedient;

(e) To encourage governmental and quasi-governmental bodies, private institutions, organisations and individuals to make contributions and donations to the Company, whether directly or indirectly;

(f) To establish and support or aid in the establishment and support of societies, bodies, clubs, companies, associations, funds, trusts and conveniences within Singapore, in any way which the Company shall think proper, which are charitable at law and have objects altogether or mainly similar to those of the Company and prohibit the payment of any dividend or profit to, and the distribution of any of their assets amongst, their members at least to the same extent as such payments or distributions are prohibited in the case of Members of the Company by this Memorandum of Association; to collaborate or network with other charities, corporate partners and community or national organizations for greater efficiency in the use of shared resources and for the advantage of Beneficiaries and Members; and to subscribe or guarantee money for charitable or benevolent objects, or for any exhibition, or for any public, general or useful object;

(g) To hold or arrange competitions and provide or contribute towards the provision of prizes, awards and distinctions in connection therewith;

(h) To confer certificates of participation in the Company's program;

(i) To take such steps by personal or written appeal, public meetings, fundraising events or otherwise, as may be from time to time be deemed expedient for the purpose of procuring contributions to the funds of the Company, in the form of donations, annual subscriptions or otherwise;

(j) To solicit and accept subscriptions, donations, devises and bequests of, and to purchase, take on lease or in exchange, hire or otherwise acquire and hold, any real or personal estate, maintain and alter any of the same as are necessary for any of the objects of the Company and (subject to such consents as may be required by law) sell, lease or otherwise dispose of or mortgage any such real or personal estate;

(k) To take any gift of property, whether in cash or in specie, real, personal or pecuniary and whether subject to any special trust or not, for any one of the objects of the Company;

(l) To make donations for patriotic or for charitable purposes;

(m) To make available and provide assistance in the form of financial grants or loans, equipments or personnel with or without conditions;

(n) To enter into any arrangement or contract with governmental and quasi-governmental bodies, private institutions, organizations and individuals as may be desirable in the interests of the Company;

(o) To buy, sell and deal in all kinds of educational materials in connection with the Company's programmes;

(p) To appoint such staff as may be required for the purposes of the Company and to designate their responsibilities, remove or suspend such staff;

(q) To carry on any of the business or businesses of traders, distributors, manufacturers, designers, producers, buyers, sellers, lessees and lessors of commercial and sale items and any other objects relating to the services offered by the Company (including entering into co-ventures with third parties);

(r) To acquire by purchase, lease, exchange or otherwise and hold by investment land, buildings, moveable and immovable property and assets, and rights and privileges as permitted by law, useful, convenient or likely to advance or benefit directly or indirectly the interest of the Company or otherwise to further the objects of the Company and to sell, manage, lease, mortgage, dispose of or otherwise deal with all or any part of the property of the Company;

(s) To borrow and raise money in such manner as the Company may think fit and secure the same or the repayment or performance of any debt, liability, contract, guarantee or other engagement incurred or to be entered into by the Company in any way and charged upon all or any of the Company's property (both present and future), and to purchase, redeem or pay off such securities;

(t) To invest the monies of the Company not immediately required for its purposes in or upon such investment, securities or property as may be thought fit and for the benefit of the Company, nevertheless to such conditions (if any) and such consents (if any) as may for the time being be imposed or required by law and subject also hereinafter provided;

(u) To apply for, purchase, or otherwise acquire any copyrights, licenses, concessions, and the like, conferring any exclusive or non-exclusive or limited right to use, or any secret or other information which may seem capable of being used for any purposes of the Company, or the acquisition of which may seen calculated directly or indirectly to benefit the Company; and to use exercise, develop, or grant licenses in respect of, or otherwise turn to account, the property rights or information so acquired;

(v) To draw, make, accept, endorse, negotiate, discount, execute and issue promissory notes, bills of exchange, bills of lading and other negotiable or transferable instruments;

(w) To undertake and execute any trust or agency business which may seem directly or indirectly conducive to any objects of the Company;

(x) To lend and advance money or give guarantees to any person or company and give guarantees or indemnities for the payment of money or the performance of contracts or obligations by any person or company; to secure or undertake in any way the repayment of money lent or advanced to or the liabilities incurred by any person or company;

(y) To acquire and undertake the whole or any part of the business, property, and liabilities of any person or entity carrying on any business which the Company is authorised to carry on or possessed of property suitable for the purposes of the Company;

(aa) To acquire by subscription, purchase or otherwise, and to accept and take, hold and sell, shares or stock in any company, society or undertaking, whether the objects of which are, either in whole or in part, similar to those of the Company or otherwise, or such as may be likely to promote the interest of the Company;

(bb) To accept stock or shares in, or the debentures, mortgage debentures or other securities of any company in payment or part payment for any services rendered or for any sale made to or debt owing from such company;

(cc) To apply for and hold any license, permit, approval or authority which may be necessary or desirable in connection with the Company's objects;

(dd) To sell, improve, manage, develop, exchange, lease, dispose of, turn into account or otherwise deal with all or any part of the property and rights of the Company;

(ee) To act as trustee, to hold property or land or to promote any trust for any purpose within the Company's objects;

(ff) To take or hold mortgages, liens and charges to secure payment of purchase price or any unpaid balance of purchase price, of any part of the Company's property of whatsoever kind sold by the Company or any amount due to the Company from purchasers and others;

(gg) To construct, improve, maintain, develop, work, manage, alter or control any houses, buildings, grounds, works or conveniences which may seem calculated directly or indirectly to advancing the Company's interest and to contribute to, subsidize or otherwise assist and take part in the contribution, improvement, maintenance, development, working, management, carrying out, alteration or control thereof;

(hh) To pay all costs, charges and expenses incurred or sustained in or about the promotion and establishment of the Company and the conduct of its business and to remunerate any person or persons (including but not limited to a member of the Company) for services rendered in or about the promotion and establishment of the Company;

(ii) To carry on any other activities which may seem to the Company capable of being conveniently carried on in connection with its purposes or calculated directly or indirectly to enhance the value of or render profitable any of the Company's property or rights;
Our Mission is creating New Life Stories.

New Life Stories fulfils our mandate by supporting families to break the cycle of crime and poverty with upstream intervention programs. New Life Stories support families to creating new life patterns and thus creating new life stories Children/Youth Community Family Support Groups

name: NEXUS CARBON FOR DEVELOPMENT LIMITED
search_text:
NEXUS CARBON FOR DEVELOPMENT LIMITED Our Work: We are mission-driven professionals with experience in debt financing across developing economies and deep expertise in Southeast Asia. We provide Integrated Finance Solutions to support the scale-up and impact of enterprises. Our team brings together diverse backgrounds in engineering, finance, credit analysis, international development, law, marketing, and operations.

Fund management: We address financing gaps by designing and deploying innovative solutions that blend traditional development funding, debt finance, impact investment, and climate finance to provide solutions to market barriers.

Carbon Finance service: We engage implementers that require a key source of funding for their enterprises to scale by leveraging the Carbon Market and providing a full range of support from project certification to carbon asset management.

Consultancy offering: We ensure that the enterprises we support are generating positive social and environmental impacts. We leverage frameworks and methodologies to provide organizations with critical insights and data to support their decision-making process.

Technical consultancy: Our technical consultancy work includes environmental impact studies, market and feasibility studies, carbon footprint assessments, energy efficiency audits, impact measurement. Our Vision: Nexus for Development envisions a world where all communities benefit from access to clean energy and water.

Our Mission: Nexus for Development drives access to finance in developing economies across Asia to increase sustainable energy and water resource development, advance low-carbon solutions, and scale local implementers.

Environment

name: NINE YEARS THEATRE LTD.
search_text:
NINE YEARS THEATRE LTD. • To create high quality stage productions that are consistent, grounded and integrated.
• To research, share and champion the notion and practice of actor training.
• To engage the audience in the discussion of art.
• To continuously develop new modes, methods and models in our work.
• To build and promote a Singapore presence on international platforms.
An award-winning Mandarin theatre company in multicultural Singapore, Nine Years Theatre loves to dream up game-changing ways to do things. Since the beginning, we are invested in actor training in order to create work that is consistent in quality. We believe in entertaining our audience in ways that are honest and thoughtful. Our work is recognised for being relatable and culturally layered, possessing deep sensibility that speaks to audiences from all backgrounds. Fluid in scale and genre, we create performances ranging from re-imaginations of classics and adaptations of literary works, to original plays as well as interdisciplinary and international collaborations.

Nine Years Theatre Ltd. is a charity with IPC status. Nine Years Theatre is supported by the National Arts Council under the Major Company Scheme for the period from 1 April 2022 to 31 March 2025.

Vision
To be a major player who actively contributes to a vibrant and multicultural theatre scene in Singapore and beyond.

Mission
• To promote and raise standards of Singapore Mandarin theatre.
• To be a significant theatre company that engages the audiences and artistic community by continuously redefining the ways artists and audiences make and appreciate theatre.
Theatre & Dramatic Arts

search_text:
NULIFE CARE & COUNSELLING SERVICES LIMITED The objects for which the NCCSL is established are:

(i) To promote and improve the social well-being generally of individuals, through the provision of care and counselling services to family units, including children, parents, youths and any other persons relating to their intellectual, emotional, social and financial needs, without discrimination as to nationality, race, age, ender, language, religion, moral character, social economic background or financial status;

(ii) To develop and promote programmes, services and projects relating to the identification, management, improvement and rehabilitation of behavioural, psychosocial or motional problems; which target children and youths, including but not limited to at-risk youths, ex-youth offenders, youths who reject, have little or no family support, suspended students and out-of-school youths, etc;

(iii) To promote social and academic development of youths generally, and a positive attitude and mindset among youths facing pressures from family, school, peers and/or studies, through organising, and encouraging the active participation of such youths in, educational and recreational programmes, such as tuition classes, sporting activities, character-building workshops, career guidance talks and such other events or activities in furtherance thereof, all of which shall be beneficial wholly or substantially to the community in Singapore. Motto:
WHY - Creating a world where assistance is not a privilege, but a right.


Mission:
HOW - Upholding the dignity of the unseen members of our society through targeted support and advocacy they otherwise struggle to access.


Vision:
WHAT - Taking care of life’s fundamentals through counselling, casework, legal guidance and interim social support.

name: National Arts Council
search_text:
National Arts Council The functions of the Council shall be —

(a) to promote excellence in the arts;

(b) to promote conditions conducive to the advancement of artistic activities in Singapore;

(c) to promote the appreciation, understanding and enjoyment of the arts;

(d) to support and assist in the establishment and development of arts organisations;

(e) to encourage the support of the arts by any persons or bodies or organisations;

(f) to organise and promote artistic activities in Singapore;

(g) to provide, and encourage the provision of, opportunities for persons to practise the arts and to receive training in the arts and arts administration;

(h) to promote incentives for, and recognition of, achievement in the practice of the arts;

(i) to establish and maintain arts facilities including performing arts centres, galleries, theatres, concert halls and rehearsal facilities, and other facilities and conveniences connected with such arts facilities;

(j) to promote the arts of Singapore overseas through artistic exchange and other activities; and

(k) to advise the Government, either of its own motion or upon request made to it by the Minister, on matters connected with the promotion of the arts or otherwise related to the performance of its functions. Vision: A creative city that inspires.

NSS Constitution Clause 3.1
(1) to promote an interest in the flora and fauna of Singapore and the surrounding Southeast Asian region;

(3) to encourage and promote responsible attitudes towards the conservation of our natural heritage and the environment;

NSS Constitution Clause 3.2
To have all such other objectives and do all such other things as are complementary or incidental to the attainment of the foregoing objectives, or any of them and particularly as follows:
(1) prepare, publish and disseminate such books, periodicals, pamphlets, photos, articles and drawings as well as produce such films, videos and recordings in whatever form, electronic or otherwise, as may be necessary or relevant to educate its members and the public;
(2) organise forums, talks, seminars, lectures, shows, recreational and study trips and other like events, so as to help educate its members and the public;
(3) do all things and engage in such lawful activities as are necessary to achieve its objectives, including maintaining its offices, owning/managing of property real or personal, entering into agreements and contracts, signing and executing deeds and undertaking or executing any representation or trust, as well as employing persons and terminating their services.
1. Mission

NSS exists in order to:

• Promote an interest in the flora/fauna of Singapore and the surrounding SE Asia.
• Promote an awareness of nature and the environment and the conservation thereof.
• Encourage and promote responsible attitudes towards the conservation of our natural heritage and the environment.
• Initiate and support research projects relating to the study and conservation of nature and the environment.

These objectives are taken from the Society’s constitution, most recently updated in 2010. Mission is supposed to provide a long-term framework for planning by explaining the organisation’s raison d’etre and is not supposed to be updated every time a plan is prepared. We see no reason for updating this statement of our mission in the current plan.

2. Vision 2021

NSS already has an established reputation in Singapore as a widely respected environmental organisation but popular and governmental commitment to environmental issues remains mixed. By the end of this plan, we want to have led a cultural transformation in Singaporean attitudes to nature conservation and biodiversity and have consolidated a strong position for NSS, not only in Singapore but increasingly overseas, as an environmental advocate. To achieve this, we will have grown significantly by at least doubling membership, income and full-time staff, while protecting our sustainability by maintaining financial reserves at current levels or higher. More of our members will be active as volunteers.

We will also have strengthened the scientific foundations of NSS to enhance our credibility and effectiveness as an advocate. This will mean applying more scientific resources in both full-time and project roles, participating in more research projects funded by others, providing hands-on management of nature conservation/biodiversity sites where feasible, and promoting the concept of citizen science.

Over its 60-year life, NSS has successfully developed its current 5 areas of nature focus (birds, butterflies and insects, marine life, other vertebrates and plants) and its 4 cross-cutting committees and groups (conservation, education, Jalan Hijau and nature rambling). Our plan to grow at a faster rate over the next five years implies some risk. We will mitigate that risk by continuing to focus on these nine areas and being cautious to avoid over-stretching ourselves when major opportunities arise to diversify any further.



Environment

name: Nyingmapa (Buddhist) Meditation Centre
search_text:
Nyingmapa (Buddhist) Meditation Centre To propagate dharma such that it fosters happiness and compassion for all beings.

The modern society prospers in materialism. However, the happiness of all does not keep up with its prosperity. In fact, many are faced with sense of emptiness and anxiety. Why is this so? This is because we are moving further away from the true path in attaining happiness.

Modern society lives in the convenience and efficacy of what science and technology bring. It is thus counter-intuitive that such prosperity in materialism, the convenience and efficacy of science and technology do not necessarily translate to happiness. As the world gets connected seamlessly through the various transportation modes or connected electronically through mobile phone calls, texts, emails or social media, the modern technology does not bring about closer ties among family members or friends. In fact, more and more are more engrossed in one’s own digital worlds and the gaps among loved ones are getting larger instead of closer. So it is time to re-think what goes wrong? What values are important to us?

Considering the many complicating challenges in our daily lives: whether it is work, family, marriage, health etc that one faces every day, buddha dharma comes as relevant in providing the wholistic and right view and thus establishing the right values. With right values, one is in the right direction in his pursuit of happiness, thus, enabling us to live not only a healthy life, both physically and mentally, but also a meaningful and fulfilling life.

Apart from achieving happiness, buddha dharma also promotes compassion for all beings. The world has become more interconnected. If everyone treats others with kindness and respects, the world will be in harmony and bliss.
The propagation is done through dharma study and practical trainings of meditation.

Apart from in-persons studying, propagation of dharma is also done through making use of modern technology such as video and zoom.

Through practical meditation, one contemplates on the teachings of buddha, strives to practise what one learns in his daily life. An individual who is happy, like ripples, brings happiness to those around.

For members who encounter challenges in life, counselling is also provided to impart Buddha’s teachings which one could use to establish right values and right path in pursuit of happiness.
Buddhism

(f) To connect Singapore, Southeast Asia and the world via the practices and trends in contemporary art, so as to make these forms of art accessible to all.
(g) To enhance Singapore's position as an international hub and leader for contemporary arts in Southeast Asia and to develop, when appropriate, a collection strategy, collections network and programs.
(h) To act as trustees, directors or managers of any immovable or movable property given or held upon trust for the purposes of the Company.
(i) To levy fees and other charges and to solicit or acquire by lawful means and to accept or receive subscriptions, donations of property of any nature and any devise, legacy, annuity, money, contributions or fund for the purpose of promoting and carrying on the objects of the Company or any of them and to apply them as well as the income thereof to its objects.
(j) To borrow or raise money for the purposes of the Company or any of them in any such manner and upon such terms and security as may be considered expedient, and to issue debentures and other securities and for the purpose of securing any debt or other obligation of the Company to mortgage or charge all or any part of the property of the Company.
(k) Subject to the provisions of the Companies Act, Cap. 50, to purchase, take on lease or in exchange, hire or otherwise acquire for the purposes of the Company any immovable property or interest and any rights, privileges and casements over or in respect of any such property which may be considered necessary or convenient for the purposes of the Company.
(l) To purchase, hire or otherwise acquire for the purpose of the Company any personal property or interest in personal property.
(m) To sell, let, mortgage, dispose of or turn to account all or any of the property or assets of the Company as may be thought expedient with a view to the promotion of its objects.
(n) To invest the moneys of the Company not immediately required for its purposes in or upon such investments, securities or property as may be thought fit.
-2-
(o) To amalgamate, affiliate or cooperate with and subscribe to any association, society or corporation whose objects shall be charitable and to purchase or otherwise acquire and undertake all or any part of the property, assets, liabilities and engagements of any such association, society or corporation provided that the Company shall not amalgamate, affiliate with or subscribe to any association, society or corporation which shall not prohibit the distribution of its income and property among its members to an extent at least as great as is imposed on the Company under or by virtue of Clause ‎5 below.
(p) Subject to Clause ‎5 below to grant pensions, allowances and gratuities to past or present officers or employees of the Company or to the dependants of such persons and to establish and maintain or participate in trust funds or schemes (whether contributory or non-contributory) for providing pensions or other benefits for any such persons as aforesaid.
(q) To do all such other things as are incidental or conducive to the attainment of the above objects or any of them.

name: ONE (SINGAPORE)
search_text:
ONE (SINGAPORE) Since 2005, ONE (SINGAPORE) has been dedicated to raising public awareness and taking concrete actions to Make Poverty History. We believe in a world where no one is impoverished, be it here at home or overseas.

The ONE Emergency Fund provides financial and in-kind assistance to low-income individuals and families in Singapore who urgently need help, but cannot find it elsewhere or cannot obtain it in time. We have assisted more than 2000 families since the beginning of the pandemic.

Closely linked to the OEF, is the ONE Fresh Food Programme, which provides healthy, fresh groceries to families in need of assistance.

The ONE Livelihoods Fund promotes livelihoods, self-reliance, entrepreneurship and training to reduce inequalities and foster well-being for vulnerable individuals and families in Singapore.

The Concrete Actions Programme (CAP) links corporate and non-profit partners in a variety of projects in support of the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). ONE (SINGAPORE) envisions a just world where no one lives in poverty be it overseas or here at home. It is dedicated to raising public awareness and taking concrete actions to Make Poverty History. ONE (SINGAPORE) also links corporate and non-profit partners in a variety of projects in support of the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) and the movement to end poverty and inequality. In addition, ONE (SINGAPORE) also promotes volunteerism, philanthropy and civic responsibility. General Charitable Purposes

name: ONE CHORAL COMPANY LTD.
search_text:
ONE CHORAL COMPANY LTD. a) To promote choral excellence in Singapore by organising opportunities for training, competitions, and performances for choirs to cultivate higher standards of choral music.

b) To nurture the next generation of choral singers and leaders by creating platforms for education, professional development and leadership
grooming.

c) To elevate the global impact of Singapore's choirs, music, and culture by participating in, and creating opportunities for competitions, exchanges, and performances abroad; and positioning Singapore as a hub and regional leader in choral artistry through international events, collaborations, and meaningful engagements.

d) To encourage innovation and vibrancy in the local choral scene through collaborations across art forms and cultures, affirming Singapore’s position as a multi-cultural society.

e) To bring communities together through choral music by engaging different participants and audiences through our programmes and activities, promoting a spirit of inclusivity and volunteerism.

f) To do all other such activities that are incidental or conducive to the attainment of the above objectives or any of them provided that nothing shall be done solely for profit. Vision: To be a leader in choral artistry in Asia and beyond, to touch hearts and forge communities.

Mission: To uphold choral excellence, nurture the next generation of choral musicians, and foster musical connections and collaborations. Historical & Cultural Conservation Music & Orchestras Training & Education

name: ONE HOPE CENTRE
search_text:
ONE HOPE CENTRE 1. To initiate, assist and organize social service schemes based on Christian principles and teachings to alleviate physical, emotional and mental hardship, oppression, abuse, suffering, and poverty of individuals by providing education and skills training, teaching , rehabilitation, counseling, and welfare services to individuals irrespective of race, language, creed, nationality, age or gender through the saving power of the gospel of our Lord Jesus Christ.

2. In furtherance of the objectives, the Society may :-

i. Operate training and education centers, rehabilitation centers and welfare homes for former drug addicts, alcoholics and former prostitutes, wayward youths , the destitute, sick, aged, delinquent and emotionally troubled people and those at risk who need help and shelter ;

ii. Co-operate and network with recognized government and private organizations, such as welfare agencies and religious organizations;

iii. Raise, collect, solicit, receive, administer, invest or distribute donations, gifts, funds, movable or immovable property or goods in kind as are entrusted or given to it;

iv. Rent, purchase or sell property in pursuit of the Society s objectives;

v. Repair, renovate or build and maintain buildings and structured for use in furtherance of its objectives;

vi. Engage in business activities to financially support the Society s purposes and objectives; and

vii. Provide instruction to foster spiritual maturity in our Lord Jesus Christ and Christian Fellowship amongst individuals in its said schemes.

3. The Society shall not carry out the above objectives and activities without the prior written consent from the relevant authorities, where applicable. Vision: 
For every person affected by addiction to recover and thrive.

Mission: 
To provide professional and accessible help to persons and loved ones affected by addictions. Community

name: PACTA ASIA LIMITED
search_text: PACTA ASIA LIMITED Main objectives: 	 	 The objects for which the Company is established are:-  A.    to advance and promote national and international conflict resolution and reconciliation with a view to relieving suffering, poverty and distress and building social cohesion and trust within and between communities by:-	      (i)  investigating and identifying the probable causes of conflicts;	       (ii) examining probable solutions to the conflict;	    (iii) mediating with parties to the conflict and others together to discuss possible solutions enabling them to build relationships of trust;	    (iv) recommending to the international community and the parties involved the measures which may result in the resolution or prevention of the conflict or the reduction of any humanitarian crisis that may ensue from the conflict;	    (v) publishing reports for the public benefit on the causes and recommended solutions of particular conflicts;     (vi)  implementing and/or coordinating programs and/or activities that promote harmony and build social cohesion and trust within and between communities;   Provided that all activities shall be balanced and shall maintain or enhance the Company s commitment to remaining impartial; and	B.     to further any other exclusively charitable objects in Singapore and elsewhere as the Board of Directors may agree from time to time.  Vision/mission:			  PACTA is dedicated to the resolution of violent conflicts, with a particular focus on Muslim areas of South-East Asia. The name derives from the Latin phrase  pacta sunt servanda    agreements must be kept. This principle provides the guiding philosophy of the organisation which is also underpinned by a belief in the importance of patience, understanding, persistence, sensitivity and imagination.	In PACTA s experience, successful conflict resolution is almost always the result of efforts by multiple actors, national and international often working collectively, sometimes individually but always with common purpose, shared analysis and awareness of each other s approach and particular added value. PACTA reflects this emphasis on partnership in all its endeavours. PACTA actively seeks to work in cooperation with like-minded individuals and organisations, whether governmental or non-governmental.     Our commitment to partners and to those affected by conflict whom we are dedicated to assisting is long-term.  PACTA is conscious that successful peace processes do not end with signed agreements but demand support and nurturing over many years. General Charitable Purposes

name: PANGDEMONIUM THEATRE COMPANY LTD.
search_text:
PANGDEMONIUM THEATRE COMPANY LTD. To present and produce plays and musicals which are challenging, inspiring, relevant, accessible and of the highest artistic, entertainment and production values. These productions may deal with themes that Singaporeans can relate to and identify with,, and at the same time, have a broader, global resonance and universal significance.

To produce plays and musicals written by critically acclaimed playwrights, established classics and new works with the aim of offering audiences opportunities to enjoy a wide variety of theatre, as well as fostering a deeper appreciation for the dramatic arts amongst our community.

To uncover and nurture new talent and provide opportunities to aspiring artists, playwrights and technical practitioners, to hone their respective crafts in a professional arena.
VISION / MISSION

Pangdemonium is a proudly Singaporean, non-profit theatre company with a mission to tell stories on the stage which are challenging, inspiring, relevant, accessible, and above all, of the highest artistic, entertainment and productions values, in a league with the very best of theatre internationally.

Rule #1 of theatre is that it has to be entertaining - but it is capable of achieving so much more. Pangdemonium believes that theatre should strive to enrich, enlighten, and empower; it should surprise, stir, and stimulate; it should be aspirational and inspirational, passionate and compassionate; it should impact on audiences viscerally, intellectually, emotionally. With this in mind, Pangdemonium tells stories that tackle challenging issues, ask difficult questions, provoke constructive debate, and hopefully lead us all on a path towards discoveries of our communal human experience; stories that jolt us all from a collective passivity and make us take positive action, that expose us all for our flaws and frailties, and dare us to change; stories that celebrate life, and champion struggle, survival and the human spirit, that help us see someone else’s point of view, and share in someone’s else’s journey; stories that make us count our blessings, appreciate this world more, and strive to live more meaningful lives; stories that Singaporeans can relate to and identify with, and at the same time, have a global resonance and universal significance.

In short, we tell stories that live up to our motto “Ass-kicking adventures in theatre” – because theatre should give us all a much-needed kick in the nether regions once in a while.

Since our inception in 2010, a key component of our mission has been to uncover new talent and to give opportunities to aspiring artists and technical practitioners to work in a professional, challenging and nurturing environment in our productions. In line with this, we are also creating workshops in Musical Theatre, Playwriting, and other theatre disciplines. We firmly believe that this kind of "sowing seeds" ethos is the way to keep the Singapore arts scene dynamic, fresh and progressive.

Our work is garnering rave reviews from audiences and critics alike (please check out reviews on each production page), and winning prestigious theatre accolades - at the 2014 Life! Theatre Awards our production of NEXT TO NORMAL won the prestigious Production of the Year Award, as well as the award for Readers’ Choice for Production of the Year. As a theatre company we are rapidly making a name for ourselves as a vibrant and vital voice in our community; we are growing in size, strength and stature all the time, recently acquiring Charity status, and our acclaimed annual Season Ticket is steadily motivating more and more people to commit themselves to our work.

Pangdemonium aims to continue to prove that Singapore is able produce theatre that can achieve international standards, theatre that Singaporeans can be proud of, and to inspire more and more people to embrace theatre as a way of life.
Theatre & Dramatic Arts

name: PAVE INTEGRATED SERVICES LTD.
search_text:
PAVE INTEGRATED SERVICES LTD. The purpose of the company are to help and benefit the community by:
a) Providing integrated services for (i) victims of family violence and/or sexual abuse and (ii) child protection;

b) Promoting public awareness and community responsibility in respect of multi- tressed and/or dysfunctional families as well as intra- and inter-personal violence;

c) Developing and contributing to professional knowledge by improving service effectiveness in respect of the foregoing; and

In furtherance of the above purposes but not otherwise, and provided that nothing
is done for commercial reasons or solely for profit, the company may exercise the
following powers:
a) Provide casework, groupwork and community work services, including but not limited to counselling services and public education programmes, to individuals, families and relevant populations experiencing multiple stresses including intra- and/or interpersonal violence;

b) Identify and respond to the changing needs of individuals, families and relevant populations experiencing multiple stresses including intra- and/or interpersonal violence;

c) Contribute to the development of professional knowledge to improve the effectiveness of service providers who deal with multi-stressed and/or dysfunctional families as well as intra and inter-personal violence;

d) Conduct research and document issues relating to multi-stressed and/or dysfunctional families as well as intra- and inter-personal violence; and

e) Share, where appropriate, its experiences and resources. Vision: To be the lead organisation in the development and provision of integrated services to families experiencing interpersonal violence, child abuse and/or sexual abuse.

name: PCF SPARKLE CARE LIMITED
search_text:
PCF SPARKLE CARE LIMITED The objects for which the Company is established are:

1) To promote the welfare and interests of the citizens of, and residents in Singapore without distinction of sex, race, or religion;

2) To advance and improve the standard of life and social well-being in Singapore by fostering, developing and improving all forms of eldercare services, in any such manner as may, from time to time, be decided upon by the Company, including but without prejudice the generality of this provision, the establishment and operation of senior care centres, the provision of community-based programmes for seniors, the establishment of financial-assistance schemes for seniors, and the organisation of initiatives that support the health, well-being and social inclusion of seniors;

3) To establish and maintain or to secure the establishment and maintenance of centres for community development, including PCF Sparkle Care centres for seniors and for elder-care, and to maintain and manage or to co-operate with any governmental or statutory body, association or other local citizen group in the maintenance and management of such Centres for activities promoted by the Company and its constituent bodies in furtherance of the above objects;

4) To aid, assist and provide relief in any manner and to any extent to any charitable institution whose object or objects are similar to or comparable with the objects of the Company, including but not limited to elder-care or senior care centres, community care organisations, aged-care facilities, healthcare providers and other charitable organisations. Provided always that notwithstanding anything heretofore contained no grant or assistance shall be given which shall be in aid of to any political organisation or for any political purpose; and

5) To assist by way of grants, donations or otherwise such charitable, eldercare-related or community-based work, programmes or initiatives in Singapore as the Company considers desirable in the interests of, or in furtherance of, the objects of the Company.
Vision
Sparkling Lives, Redefining Care

Mission

name: PENTECOST METHODIST CHURCH
search_text:
PENTECOST METHODIST CHURCH 1. A local church is a community of all true believers under the Lordship of Jesus Christ. It is the redemptive fellowship in which the Word of God is preached by persons divinely called and the Sacraments are duly administered according to Christ's own appointment. Under the discipline of the Holy Spirit the Church exists for the maintenance of worship, the edification of believers, and the redemption of the world.

2. The Church of Jesus Christ exists in and for the world. It is primarily at the level of the local church that the church encounters the world located in nearly every community. The local church is a strategic base from which Christians move out to the structures of society. The function of the local church, under the guidance of the Holy Spirit, is to help people to personally know Jesus Christ and to live their daily lives in light of their relationship with God. Therefore the local church is to minister to persons in the community where the local church is located, to provide appropriate training and nurture to all, to cooperate in ministry with other local churches, to defend God's creation and live as an ecologically responsible community, and to particpate in the worldwide mission of the Church, as minimal expectations of an authentic church.

3. The local church is a connectional society of persons who have professed their faith in Christ, have been baptised, have assumed the vows of membership in The Methodist Church, and are associated in fellowship as a local Methodist Church in order that they may hear the Word of God, receive the Sacraments, praise and worship the triune God, and carry forward the work Christ has committed to His Church. Such a society of believers, being within The Methodist Church and subject to its Discipline, is also an inherent part of the Church Universal, which is composed of all who accept Jesus Christ as Lord and Saviour, and which in the Apostles' Creed we declare to be the holy catholic church.

4. Each local church shall have a definite evangelistic, nurture, and witness responsibility. It shall be responsible for its present members, and for persons who choose it as their church. WE ARE PENTECOST IN THE COMMUNITY AND IN THE NATIONS CARRYING HIS PRESENCE, POWER, PEACE, and PURPOSES. Christianity

(ii) Establish, promote, co-operate with, become a member of, act as or appoint or be appointed trustees, agents or delegates for control, manage, superintend, or afford financial or other assistance to the work of any associations and institutions and other bodies incorporated, whose objects are similar to the objects of the Company.

(iii) Purchase, build, take on lease or in exchange, hire or otherwise acquire any real or personal property, and in particular any land, buildings, workshops, factories, laboratories, machinery, plant, apparatus, appliances and any rights or privileges necessary or convenient for the purposes of the Company and to construct, erect, alter, improve and maintain any buildings which may be from time to time required for the purposes of the Company and to manage, develop, sell, demise, let, lend, mortgage, dispose of, turn to account or otherwise deal with all or part of the same with a view to the promotion of the objects of the Company.

(iv) Pay all expenses, preliminary or incidental to the formation of the Company and its registration.

(v) Undertake and execute any trusts which may lawfully be undertaken by the Company and may be conducive to its objects.

(vi) Receive money on deposit or loan and borrow or raise money in such manner as the Company shall think fit for the objectives of the Company and to secure the repayment of any money borrowed raised or owing by mortgage, charge or lien upon all or any of the property or assets of the Company (both present and future), and also by a similar mortgage, charge or lien to secure and guarantee the performance by the Company or any other person or company of any obligation undertaken by the Company or any other person or company as the case may be.

(vii) Draw, make, accept, endorse, discount, negotiate, execute and issue bills of exchange, promissory notes and other negotiable or transferable instruments.

(viii) Invest and deal with the moneys of the Company not immediately required for its purposes in or upon such investments, securities or property and in such manner as may be determined by the Company for the purposes of the Company.

(ix) Obtain any order in Council, enactment or Act for enabling the Company to carry out any of its objects for any purpose which may seem expedient and to oppose any proceedings or applications which may seem calculated directly or indirectly to prejudice the Company’s interest.

(x) Establish, promote or assist in establishing or promoting and to subscribe to or become a member of any other association, company, foundation or institution whose objects are similar to the objects of the Company or the establishment or the promotion of which may be beneficial to the Company for the purposes of the Company.

name: PSALT CARE LIMITED
search_text:
PSALT CARE LIMITED Helping the needy who have mental health and addiction issues.

Operating under the motto "Supporting and sustaining mental health recovery", Psalt Care aims to provide peer support to recovering clients to enhance and sustain their recovery and recovery experience Mission Statement
PSALT Care's mission is to enhance the wellbeing of those facing mental health and addiction issues by developing and maintaining self-help support groups to sustain their recovery, as well as provide resources and support for their caregivers and families.

How We Do It
This is achieved by facilitating support group sessions for specific issues such as addictions (alcohol, gambling, sex, cyber, etc), depression, bipolar disorder, and Attention Deficit Disorder (ADD), among others. Separate support group sessions will be organized for caregivers and families of those afflicted with such mental issues.

We also conduct psychoeducation workshops covering a wide range of topics to help the peers to cope with their mental health conditions.

Concurrently, PSALT Care will strive to raise awareness amongst those well-placed to recognise such mental illnesses, so that early detection is optimized. This will include teachers, family members, school counsellors, other medical practitioners, and religious youth groups.

This can be achieved through a multi-pronged communications strategy that will include, mass and online media, printed collaterals, seminars, training and related events.

Why We Do It
There is a rapidly growing sector of the Singapore population that is grappling with the issues related to mental health and addictions and its attending problems and challenges in a society that is only recently coming to terms with the far-reaching devastation such conditions can have on families and caregivers.

Trends in both mental illness and addiction levels have increased in society. Furthermore recent surveys show that majority of Singaporeans do not seek help for their mental health issues, an alarming trend that could result in a severely compromised society with many sectors unable to cope with basic life functions, and requiring fulltime care and support from family members.

This in turn affects the ability of such citizens to contribute to the growth and development of the country.

The issue of addictions is related to mental health problems caused by addictive substances and behaviours.

PSALT Care was created to provide several tiers of service to assist in the process of raising awareness, accurate diagnosis,
treatment and support groups to cover the spectrum of needs that arise with failing mental health.

Through a specialized network of committed organizations and volunteer professionals who also recognize these needs in the community, PSALT Care facilitates the journey back to good
mental health and life style with relevant and affordable help specially designed to support the individuals and their families every step of the way. Support Groups

Erect and maintain Church buildings, social halls, business offices, school buildings, recreational facilities, parsonages and such other structures as   are deemed necessary for the purposes of the Chapel.    ii. Collect, solicit and accept funds, gifts and other subscriptions and hold in trust, use, mortgage, lease, sell or otherwise acquire of and dispose of property real or chattel.    iii. Provide for the welfare of the destitute, rehabilitation of drug addicts, delinquent and emotionally troubled persons.	  iv. Carry on the business of general printers, photographic printers, photo lithographers, engravers, diesinkers, photocopies, stationers and all other substances and materials and processes in connection with printing and reproduction for the purposes of the Chapel.	 v. Engage in the business of publishing and selling of books, booklets, pamphlets and cards for the purposes of the Chapel.	vi Engage in the business of producing and selling of audio tapes, video tapes, compact discs, video compact discs, laser discs, digital video discs and CD-  ROMs for the purposes of the Chapel.    vii. Enter into any arrangement with the government of the Republic of Singapore or elsewhere or authorities supreme, local, municipal or any other and obtain from the government or authority all rights and concessions and privileges that the Chapel deems fit.    viii. Undertake in the purchase, holding, sale, leasing, letting, management or charging of land or building, or any interest therein for the purpose of the Chapel.    ix. Do all or any of the above things as principals, trustees or otherwise and either alone or in conjunction with any other Church, or company, undertaking or persons. The above may be carried out subject to the approval of the relevant authorities. Vision:  To enable all persons to possess PEACE in a complete sense namely:	 - Religious: A state of spiritual harmony with God attained through personal faith in Jesus Christ.  - Psychological: A state of calm, contentment, serenity, tranquillity and rest of the heart, mind and soul as a result of abiding in Christ.  - Psychosocial: A state of concord and harmony with all persons attained by the power of the Holy Spirit.	  Mission:  1. To establish a round the clock counselling and prayer centre in Singapore. Christianity

name: Peace Home Fellowship
search_text:
Peace Home Fellowship The objectives of Peace Home Fellowship are :- (a) To preserve and restore the family in the Church and in the Nation. (b) To promote order, unity, care, love, mutual respect, communication, discipline, commitment, filial responsibility, interdependence, joy and celebration within the nuclear and extended family units in the Church and in the Nation. (c) To provide instruction, teaching, counselling (marital parent-children, in-law etc), encouragement and all forms of physical, mental, emotional and spiritual help to bring restoration to family units in the Church and in the Nation. (d) To promote care, concern and responsibility for the needy, such as the aged, orphans, abandoned babies, the sexually abused, the poor, the retarded, the emotionally depressed etc, in the Church and the Nation. (e) To encourage voluntary participation in the community homes that will be registered independently and set up as autonomous community homes by people who love the needy as listed in 3(d) of the constitution and who will provide the family environment with the love that accompanies it, to bring wholesomeness to the residents of these community homes/institutions. (f) To encourage and train other Christians in the Church and in the Nation to be aware of and practice family values as listed in (b) and show social concern for the welfare of the needy in the Nation as listed in (d). (g) To encourage and promote global preservation and restoration of the family and care for the needy, by training Christians from other countries to bring restoration to family units in their cultures and countries, and to care for the needy (such as the aged, orphans etc) in their respective countries. Vision: As part of the Triune God’s universal Church, His global family, PHF is to be a healing/therapeutic community, consisting of families & singles living in one locality, which is characterised by the following shared values and standards:-
a. there is recognition that each member of the community is needed by everyone in the community and thus very important. This translates into a culture of inclusiveness and respect for all; and one in which discrimination is actively discouraged.
b. there is equality in relationships. All members stand in equal need of love, forgiveness and grace from the Triune God, and have received this common grace from Him. All members are to be equally submissive to His Word, the Bible, and be equally accountable for each other.
c. there is restoration of each member’s personhood as each is unique, and thus every member’s right to freely make choices is respected and encouraged. These choices are ones that are to be good for the community, as well as themselves.
d. there is common care and concern shown for one another. Thus the community will give priority to taking care of the needy (physical, emotional, mental or spiritual) in its midst.
e. there is true fellowship – ie good relationships in which acceptance, forgiveness, compassion and grace are freely extended because all have freely received these first from the Triune God. Such good relationships are to be worked out in the nuclear and extended family units, cell groups as well as in the wider community.
f. there is therapeutic healing experienced by all through the security of enjoying the abovementioned good relationships. As hurts are healed, people are released from the scars left behind from the sins of the past (either their own or sins done to them) and grow to maturity and wholeness within the community, which comprises both the family as well as the local/universal church.
g. there is the recognition that the inculcation of these values and standards by the families and clans in the community is of critical importance as families are the building blocks of the community and society in general. Thus priority is given to the building up of individual families, both Asian and Western.
h. there is the recognition of the critical need to pass on these shared values and standards onto the next generation. Thus priority is given to the modelling of these values and standards to the younger generation in both the family and community settings. This will provide an incentive to the next generation to adopt these values and standards of their own accord. Thus this community will ensure the continual existence of the Church of Jesus Christ in this century and thereafter.
Such a community will produce good and wholesome citizens, contributing to national and global stability.

Mission:
1) To preserve and restore families in the Church and the Nation
2) To promote in these families a culture of order, unity, care, love, mutual respect, communication, discipline, commitment, filial responsibility, interdependence, joy and celebration
3) To promote in their multi-generational (preferably 3 to 4 generations) extended families and clans a culture of order, unity, care, love, mutual respect, communication, discipline, commitment, filial responsibility, interdependence, joy and celebration
4) To promote in local church (community) a culture of order, unity, care, love, mutual respect, communication, discipline, commitment, filial responsibility, interdependence, joy and celebration
5) To promote care, concern and responsibility for the needy in the Church and the Nation
6) To encourage other Christians in the Church, in the Nation and around the world to promote the global preservation and restoration of the family, as well as the care and concern for the needy.

This is to be done by:-
a) Praying for the fulfilment of the above 6-fold mission because we believe that only God alone can do this.
b) Promoting and encouraging families, and singles, to live either together or within walking distance of each other. This makes it more convenient for relationship-building and helping one another.
c) Providing instruction, teaching, counselling (marital, parent-children, in-law etc),
encouragement and all forms of physical, mental, emotional and spiritual help
d) Promoting and encouraging joy and celebration in our family and community life by having social activities such as dining out, recreation, entertainment, celebrations and holidays together.
e) Helping the needy within our neighbourhood and social network.
f) Encouraging and training other Christians in the Church and in the Nation to be aware of and practice family values as listed above in (2), and to show social concern for the welfare of the needy.
g) Encouraging and training Christians from other countries to bring restoration to family units in their cultures and countries, and to care for the needy in their respective countries.
Christianity

4. We render support to charitable and humanitarian causes.

name: Planetshakers (Singapore)
search_text:
Planetshakers (Singapore) The objects for which the Church is established are:

(a) to propagate the message of the Christian faith as expressed in the Bible in accord with the Great Commission of Christ (Mark 16:15 18);
(b) to arrange, organize, conduct retreats, meetings, conventions, conferences, Gospel concerts and otherwise for the purpose of proclaiming the Gospel of our Lord Jesus and sharing in Christian fellowship;
(c) to engage in Evangelism and Christian education by various means, such as public meetings, films, radio, television and printed page, magnetic tapes and other means of communication;
(d) to establish self-governing local churches, according to the New Testament pattern, as the opportunity affords;
(e) to provide Christian education for all age groups and to encourage training for active ministry at home and abroad;
(f) to provide practical Christian assistance to those in need (particularly those members of the Church) by way of food, clothing and shelter, rehabilitation accommodation, homes for the aged and constructive counsel and anything else that the Committee sees as being in harmony with the objectives of the Church;
(g) to support missionary enterprise in other lands with a view to establishing indigenous local assemblies;
(h) to encourage and maintain an attitude of Christian fellowship toward all believers in Christ irrespective of doctrine or denominational differences;
(i) to honour all civil governing authorities accordance to the scriptures;
(j) to conduct any activities for the advancement of religion according to its Statement of Faith;
(k) to preach and teach and generally to further the cause of the Gospel of the Lord Jesus Christ and to advance the Christian faith in Singapore and other Nations and to work for the building up and extension and renewal of the Christian Church;
(l) to meet for the worship of God, the Father, Son and Holy Spirit, the preaching and teaching of the Word of God and the administration of the Sacraments;
(m) to render spiritual oversight, care and assistance (whether material or otherwise) and such other help as may be deemed appropriate to, as far as practicable, meet the needs of persons to whom the church ministers; and
(n) to make donations to, assist, undertake the oversight of or in any way co-operate with other churches, organizations, societies or individuals having similar objects,

provided always that the Church shall exist for purposes which are solely charitable and notwithstanding anything hereinbefore contained nothing shall be an object of the Church which is not a charitable object.

The vision of Planetshakers Singapore is to empower a generation to win a generation through the transforming love and power of Jesus Christ. Christianity

name: Praise Evangelical Church
search_text:
Praise Evangelical Church Praise Evangelical Church (PEC) was birthed in the 1950s by Finnish missionaries, Sister Rasi in particular. Over the years, this legacy was continued in various forms reaching Malaysia, Indonesia and India. Our initial involvement in missions was merely supporting pastors financially. We call it missions by proxy. However since 2007, with a God given vision, our mission involvement became more focused and involved, fostering a close working partnership with local leaders in the area of evangelical and church planting.

We had one main objective. Fulfilling the Great Commission. Praise Evangelical Church is a Bible-believing, God-loving, Christ-centered, Spirit-engaging church. Reaching the unreached for Christ. Christianity

name: Pu Men Buddhist Fellowship
search_text:
Pu Men Buddhist Fellowship To develop Buddhist role models
Vision:
If girded with basic Buddhist education, a having-taken-refuge Buddhist will at least:
1. understand he is adopting a vegetarian diet because he wants to cultivate and grow his innate compassion. And will no longer say Buddhism want us to eat vegetarian.
2. All living beings of the six realms, whether humans or animals, have Buddha nature and is capable of achieving Perfect Enlightenment, Buddhahood; the moment their wisdom and merits reach perfection.
3. Life in the 6-realms of cyclic rebirth is suffering due to the presence of impermanence. Hence, a trained Buddhist should live by the Buddha’s precepts to ensure not getting reborn into especially the 3 lowest realms of animal, hungry ghosts and hells. Having lived a upright and precept-disciplined life one can depart from this world, fearless of what may come after death.

Mission:
To actively propagate the Buddha Dharma so as to promote Right Living and Loving Kindness ie doing no harm to both friend and foe.
This will bring about a more peaceful world.
Buddhism

(b) the advancement of health, medicine and science;

(c) the advancement of education and social mobility;

A more inclusive and resilient society, where everyone has equal access to transformational opportunities that enable them to achieve their full potential.

• Transform Lives.

• Empower Communities.

• Catalyse Change.
Spearheading new approaches and leveraging strategic networks, research and technology, to raise awareness and galvanise change across the sector.

name: Qutbi Charitable and Religious Society (Singapore)
search_text:
Qutbi Charitable and Religious Society (Singapore) 1. The Society is established for the following public charitable and religious purposes in conformity with the laws of Singapore:

(i) To promote and realize the religious tenets of the Dawoodi Bohra denomination of Islam in conformity with al-Quran, Shariat
Mohammediyah, and the Fatemi philosophy, culture and traditions of the denomination, all as interpreted by al-Dai al -Mutlaq.
(ii) To advance education and learning in all forms, including but not limited to, supporting religious and secular educational institutions, awarding scholarships or giving Qardan Hasana and promoting literature and languages, especially the Arabic language and Lisan al-Dawat.
(iii) To provide medical care and relief either by a way of outright donation or Qardan Hasana.
(iv) To promote and aid, in conformity with al-Quran al-Majeed, the
implementation of the concept and philosophy of giving Qardan Hasana (interest free loans) so as to give relief in poverty or distress which includes establishing, maintaining and supporting orphanages, homes for widows and/or the destitute and shelter for the needy in the society; providing and uplifting residential facilities; providing salvage and taking relief measures for persons and in places affected by calamities; assisting the deserving and needy persons by establishing them or helping them in trade, profession and business; and promoting the economic, social and religious welfare of the needy either by way of outright donation or Qardan Hasana.
(v) To assist other trusts, associations, institutions and individuals in promoting the objects of the Society.

2. The Society shall not engage in activities outside Singapore which are not in accordance with the laws of the foreign country.

3. The Society shall not engage in any activities that may undermine the racial or religious harmony in Singapore.
The essence of “Charity” as per the teachings of His Holiness, Dr. Syedna Mufaddal SaifuddinTUS, head of the world-wide Dawoodi Bohra Community is to ultimately make beneficiaries self-sufficient and not dependent on charity. Following the teachings of His Holiness, it is the noble vision of the Society to make beneficiaries self-sufficient not only by giving outright donations but by giving Qardan Hasana so that they are imbued with a sense of giving in the same way that they received assistance from the Society during their time of need and are thus encouraged to assist their fellow brethren in need by in-turn giving Qardan Hasana and where required outright grants.

The activities of the Society will be carried out with the help of volunteers who are ready to donate their time and energy for achieving the objects of the Society.
Islam
