name: RAINBOW CENTRE, SINGAPORE
search_text:
RAINBOW CENTRE, SINGAPORE The objects of the Association are:

1. To empower persons with disabilities through quality intervention, educational and coaching programmes and services.

2. To empower families and caregivers of persons with disabilities with skills and resources so they can enable the full and active participation of the person with disability.

3. To contribute to the building of an inclusive society for persons with disabilities by promoting awareness and engaging partners.

4. To develop the capabilities of disability sector professionals and organisations through quality training and development of programmes and services, locally and internationally.
We envision a world where persons with disabilities are empowered and thriving in inclusive communities.

We work with our partners to create opportunities for persons with disabilities to make the most of their abilities and participate meaningfully in society. Through practical education, meaningful support and effective training programmes, we strive to increase the quality of life for them and their families.

We are committed to enhancing the quality of life of persons with disabilities and their families through:

a. Providing person-centric programmes and services to enable them to achieve their fullest potential;

b. Leading innovation and developing capabilities that better support their needs and aspirations; and

c. Advocating and engaging partners and the public to co-create an inclusive community. Disability (Children)

name: RAMAKRISHNA MISSION-BOYS' HOME, THE
search_text:
RAMAKRISHNA MISSION-BOYS' HOME, THE The primary objective of the Home is to provide education, care and protection to children who lack adequate parental support while respecting the rights and dignity of every child. At the same time efforts are made to inculcate moral and spiritual values in them based on the ideals enunciated by Sri Ramakrishna, Holy Mother Sri Sarada Devi and Swami Vivekananda. A Home away from Home:
The primary intention of our Home is to educate the children while inculcating moral and spiritual values in them based on the ideals of Swami Vivekananda. These ideals evolve around character-building through the development of a child’s physical and mental faculties and moral & spiritual values. This is carried out in the Home through a structured and disciplined life incorporating activities such as physical exercises, games, studies, moral education and spirituality.

The objective of our Home is to re-unite the boys with their families as soon as possible, when both parties are ready. From the point of admission, we are working towards discharge. Our programmes are geared towards the provision of wholesome and therapeutic interventions, holistic care in a nurturing environment and relationship building to prepare the families for the ultimate reunification.

Every Boy developed to his Full Potential:
Residents are provided with a conducive environment to enable them to learn and grow into wholesome individuals. They are provided with opportunities to develop their potentials, up to tertiary level. Children/Youth Community

name: RAW MOVES LTD.
search_text:
RAW MOVES LTD. The objects for which the Company is established are :

(a) To promote and advance dance education and awareness amongst the abled and disabled youth, children and adults in Singapore by conducting workshops, classes, programmes and activities to encourage dance skills and education.


(b) To plan, develop and conduct outreach programmes, performances, events and activities to provide the abled and disabled youth, children and adults with a platform and avenue to showcase their dance skills.


(c) To inculcate, generate, promote, foster and develop, an interest in the performance and appreciation of dance in Singapore, and to serve as an outlet for all levels of dancers to perform and to engage in dance by working on the foundation and belief in what people can achieve rather than what they cannot.


(d) To do all such other things as are incidental or conducive to the attainment of the above objects or any of them provided that nothing shall be done solely for profit. The Company may without prejudice to the generality of the foregoing:

(i) Apply to any governmental or regulatory authorities or other public bodies or to corporations, companies or persons for, and to accept, grants of money, land, donations, gifts, subscriptions, contracts, rights, privileges, concessions and other assistance for promoting charities and the objects of the Company and to conform to any proper conditions upon which such grants and other payments may be made.

(ii) Establish, promote, co-operate with, become a member of, act as or appoint or be appointed trustees, agents or delegates for control, manage, superintend, or afford financial or other assistance to the work of any associations and institutions and other bodies incorporated, whose objects are similar to the objects of the Company.

(iii) Purchase, build, take on lease or in exchange, hire or otherwise acquire any real or personal property, and in particular any land, buildings, workshops, factories, laboratories, machinery, plant, apparatus, appliances and any rights or privileges necessary or convenient for the purposes of the Company and to construct, erect, alter, improve and maintain any buildings which may be from time to time required for the purposes of the Company and to manage, develop, sell, demise, let, lend, mortgage, dispose of, turn to account or otherwise deal with all or part of the same with a view to the promotion of the objects of the Company.

(iv) Pay all expenses, preliminary or incidental to the formation of the Company and its registration.

(v) Undertake and execute any trusts which may lawfully be undertaken by the Company and may be conducive to its objects.

(vi) Receive money on deposit or loan and borrow or raise money in such manner as the Company shall think fit and to secure the repayment of any money borrowed raised or owing by mortgage, charge or lien upon all or any of the property or assets of the Company (both present and future), and also by a similar mortgage, charge or lien to secure and guarantee the performance by the Company or any other person or company of any obligation undertaken by the Company or any other person or company as the case may be.

(vii) Draw, make, accept, endorse, discount, negotiate, execute and issue bills of exchange, promissory notes and other negotiable or transferable instruments.

(viii) Invest and deal with the moneys of the Company not immediately required for its purposes in or upon such investments, securities or property and in such manner as may be determined by the Company for the purposes of the Company.

(ix) Obtain any order in Council, enactment or Act for enabling the Company to carry out any of its objects for any purpose which may seem exped ient and to oppose any proceedings or applications which may seem calculated directly or indirectly to prejudice the Company's interest.

(x) Establish, promote or assist in establishing or promoting and to subscribe to or become a member of any other association, company, foundation or institution whose objects are similar to the objects of the Company or the establishment or the promotion of which may be beneficial to the Company.

(xi) To give pensions, gratuities or charitable aid to past or present employees of the Company and to make payments towards the insurance of and to form and contribute to provident and benefits funds for the benefit of any such persons.

(xii) Employ or appoint consultants, professional or otherwise whether or not remuneration, costs or fees paying terms for the purpose of giving effect or better giving effect or attaining the above objects or any of them.


Provided that:-

(i) In case the Company shall take or hold any property which may be subject to any trusts, the Company shall only deal with or invest the same in such manner as allowed by law, having regard to such trusts.

(ii) The objects of the Company shall not extend to the regulation of relations between workers and employers or organizations of workers and organizations of employers
Established in 2011, RAW Moves is a contemporary dance company which focuses its establishment, connection and direction of work with the spirit of inquiry.

VISION
To be the leading exponent of an inclusive and holistic approach to movement and dance in the Asia region

MISSION
Redefining Movement

RAW Moves’ work is based on the ethos of inquiry and experimentation. It seeks to achieve its mission by:
• Supporting cross-disciplinary experimentation in dance through innovation and research
• Nurturing new talents through collaborative investigation and experiential learning
• Promoting dance in the community
• Eventually developing a centre that allows for research, experimentation, education and performance platforms for all aspects of the word “movement” Professional, Contemporary & Ethnic Dance

name: RAY OF HOPE
search_text:

(i) to build and manage a trusted platform, incorporating casework, counselling as well as programmes for individuals and/or families in need of assistance of those going through hardship in Singapore

(ii) to further any other exclusively charitable objects in Singapore and elsewhere as the Board of Directors may agree from time to time.
The Ray of Hope Initiative Limited seeks to give assistance to deserving individuals and/or families who have been suddenly disadvantaged, displaced or disabled, for example, families that may suffer financially or otherwise as a result of sudden crisis eg. act of God, accidents, death of breadwinner, etc.
Assistance will be given to individuals and/or families regardless of their race or religion or nationality.
The award of such grants is aimed, amongst other things, at yielding a positive social benefit by helping the recipients get back into their usual routine.
Vision: To build a trusted platform, incorporating casework and counselling as well as programmes for the people in our community to help one another. Community Family

name: RESONANCE OF SINGAPORE LTD.
search_text:
RESONANCE OF SINGAPORE LTD. 1) Perform: present high-quality, innovative and world-class multidisciplinary concerts, both locally and abroad, as well as represent Singapore in international arts festivals and competitions. We strive for these performances to uplift, intrigue and enrich the human soul. 
2) Educate: to provide specialised choral training at the junior, intermediate and advanced levels, in the form of workshops, masterclasses, rehearsals and individual voice lessons. At the academic level, we will also conduct new research in our field, and seek to publish our research in peer-reviewed journals, alongside publication of other choral teaching resources and commissioned compositions.
3) Engage the Community: to work with community stakeholders to run community choral programmes for schools and especially for those in need, to use choral music training to instil a sense of resilience, excellence and appreciation for beauty. Scholarships will be offered for youth with low-income backgrounds. We also aim to reach out to various industries and sectors of Singapore, to engage in current and pertinent social causes and issues. Our vision is to build a stronger Singapore through music, strengthen social resilience and uplift lives.  To achieve that goal, we aim to share vocal artistry with singers of diverse backgrounds through performance, education and community engagement. Music & Orchestras

name: RESOUND COLLECTIVE LIMITED
search_text:
RESOUND COLLECTIVE LIMITED (a) To promote the performance, enjoyment and appreciation of music for chamber orchestra and small instrumental ensembles through the presentation of concerts and other music-related event.

Elaboration:

The company is set up to promote chamber orchestra music (classical music for orchestras of 'medium' size, typically 15-45 musicians). In contrast music performed by symphony orchestras typically has 60-100 musicians.

The music performed is primarily from the late Baroque, Classical and early Romantic periods, the 'canon' of Western classical music, which is performed relatively less in Singapore as we have presently only the Singapore Symphony Orchestra and (the mostly) large community orchestras that tend to focus on the larger scale late Romantic orchestral repertoire.

(b) To provide opportunities to our trained local musicians to further enhance their musical skills through the formation of a chamber orchestra and other ensembles that perform for the public at the highest artistic levels.

Elaboration:

With increasing number of local graduates from the Yong Siew Toh Conservatory of Music, NAFA and returnees from overseas music conservatories, there is now a sizable pool of very highly trained Singaporean musicians to form a professional chamber orchestra.

In order for them to retain and improve their artistic standards, it is imperative that they are pushed to perform publicly at the highest levels regularly. Resound Collective's forming of a top tier chamber orchestra provides these musicians the impetus and opportunity to advance artistically, even while they pursue their full time career in teaching or arts management.

c) To grow the appreciation and love of chamber orchestra music to a larger demographic of Singaporeans.

Elaboration:

Exposure to high quality chamber orchestra music, as heard in leading cultural capitals around the world, will broaden local audiences appreciation of classical music, as the music played by this group is core to the Western classical repertoire. (Further details on the repertoire and music we focus on is given in the next section.)

The group will program not only for existing music lovers, but develop programs that attract those who are relatively new to attending concerts, and to re-engage former concertgoers. VISION:

Creating a great little orchestra for Singapore, led by the musicians, for the enjoyment of many.

MISSION:

To tap on our local musical talents to expose and delight audiences with the great music for small orchestra.

To push standards of small ensemble performance to greater heights by working with the best local and international musicians in the field. Music & Orchestras Training & Education

name: Rare Disorders Society (Singapore)
search_text:
Rare Disorders Society (Singapore) 1. Public Advocacy

RDSS engages traditional and new media such as newspapers, magazines, television programs, films, publications, storybooks, facebook, twitter, blog and website to raise public awareness on Rare Diseases. The society also promotes and encourages CSR (Corporate Social Responsibility) and BSR (Blogger Social Responsibility) to help raise funds and create awareness of Rare Diseases.
2. Patient Support

RDSS provides a platform for patients and their families to share their experience and knowledge with each other, as well as to provide support and encouragement. Recreational activities are also organised for patients and their families to provide them a chance to relax, relate and share. We hope to make them feel normal yet special. Some events can be organised with the general public, so that patients can feel understood and accepted.
3. Treatment

RDSS actively seeks sustainable treatment methods for rare disease patients. With public donations, RDSS lend a helping hand to families that need financial support.
4. Hospital Collaboration

RDSS collaborates with hospitals in various initiatives to help rare disease patients.
5. Promote International Alliance

RDSS participates in international rare disease conferences to learn from other societies and foundations to improve our service and support to RD patients in Singapore. Established in 2011, Rare Disorders Society Singapore is an IPC Charity with the mission to raise awareness of the challenges of patients living with rare diseases.  We advocate for equity to empower our beneficiaries to have a good quality of  life. Our vision is to be an empathetic and effective Patient Advocacy Group. Diseases/Illnesses Support Group

name: Riding for the Disabled Association of Singapore
search_text:
Riding for the Disabled Association of Singapore The main objective of RDA is to provide free therapeutic horse-riding programmes that benefit people with physical and learning disabilities. The therapy helps them to gain improved respiration, balance, coordination and mobility of muscles, limbs and spine. It also aids in raising their self-confidence, improve their communication skills, trust, motivation and self-esteem. Vision:
TOGETHER, TOWARDS A MORE INCLUSIVE COMMUNITY INSPIRING HOPE AND IMPROVING LIVES OF PERSONS WITH DISABILITIES

Mission:
ENABLING AND EMPOWERING MINDS, BODIES AND LIVES THROUGH WORKING WITH HORSES

Through dedicating to the provision and development of these equestrian programmes, RDA Singapore aims to enable its beneficiaries to develop psychological and cognitive skills (including improved life skills, independence, confidence, communication, motivation and trust). The rehabilitative scope of our programmes includes improved core muscle tone, respiration, balance, postural control, spatial awareness and increased mobility of muscles and limbs. name: Rose of Sharon Church, Singapore
search_text:
Rose of Sharon Church, Singapore Its objects are:
(a) To build an Apostolic and Prophetic Church that is committed to the Great Commission of the Lord Jesus Christ and to channel all efforts and resources to strategically develop, equip and raise group of people that are effective, strong and dynamic with the main purpose of saving every soul in the marketplace, thereby fulfilling the Great Commission of Jesus Christ.
(b) To propagate the Christian Faith and to spread the Gospel of Jesus Christ as revealed through the Holy Scriptures by means of communication, whether visual, verbal or written.
(c) To provide, promote and render pastoral counselling services both material and spiritual to the sick, the aged, the homeless and the needy.
(d) To build a Christ-centered congregation through the Worship of God by the preaching of the Word of God.
(e) To encourage a fellowship of believers in a life of fervent prayer and systematic discipleship and the exercise of spiritual gifts.
(f) To provide for preaching, teaching and fostering the growth of Christian Religion; to license and ordain ministers; to promote a ministry of evangelism; to be involved in missionary work by the sending out, receiving and training of missionaries; to carry on the organization of Churches and foster their development, local sovereignty and independence according to this Constitution and its By-laws. Vision:
To build an Apostolic and Prophetic Church that is committed to the Great Commission of the Lord Jesus Christ and to channel all efforts and resources to strategically develop, equip and raise group of people that are effective, strong and dynamic with the main purpose of saving every soul in the marketplace, thereby fulfilling the Great Commission of Jesus Christ.

name: Ruanatworkz Musical Arts
search_text:
Ruanatworkz Musical Arts Our main objects are:
(1) To promote instrumental music and other forms of musical arts.
(2) To educate and advocate musical arts.
(3) To contribute to the musical community in Singapore.

For full details, please refer to the attached. Ruanatworkz Musical Arts (R.A.W.) aims to promote the rich history of ancient traditional instrumental music and showcase ancient instruments (eg: ZhongRuan) to the musical art scene through education, performances and creative interactions.

Formed by a group of young traditional instrumental music lovers, R.A.W. aims to create awareness and bring continuity to the traditional instrumental music amongst the local communities especially the young through cultural integration.

We aim to conserve and rejuvenate the culture by organizing performances, lectures, conferences, seminars, workshops and innovative engagement activities.

R.A.W. strives to bring our rich traditional music; with a modern touch, to the hearts of the local communities.

Social Mission:

search_text:
S M JALEEL FOUNDATION LTD. Purpose / Objective

The SM Jaleel Foundation is committed to:

(A) the advancement and improvement of standards of human life and social well-being by:
• fostering, developing and improving education of all kinds, and in any such manner as may, from time to time, be decided upon by the Board of Directors including, but without prejudice to the generality of this provision the founding, creation, maintaining and administration of scholarships and bursaries to needy students;
• promoting child education and other child development activities as decided by the Board of Directors;
• alleviating poverty, human suffering and improvement of standards of living by providing donations to needy persons from time to time as decided by the Board of Directors.

(B) aid, assist, jointly sponsor and grant donations to any institution or organisation in furtherance of the object.

(C) the relief in any manner which the Foundation may in its absolute discretion deem suitable of persons.

(D) the relief of human suffering and improvement of standards of human life by combating, treatment and prevention of sickness and disease, and in particular by providing for, or contributing towards medical and scientific research and teaching, and by providing of medical and surgical facilities and services of all kinds.

(E) aid, assist and give relief in any manner and to any extent including in the absolute discretion of the Board of Directors the whole or any part of the assets for the time being of the Foundation to any charitable institution whose object or objects are similar to or comparable with the objects of this Foundation.

name: SALT & LIGHT FOUNDATION
search_text:
SALT & LIGHT FOUNDATION Salt and Light Foundation is established by private funds with the purpose of honouring God and following His commandments to love God and to love our neighbours. We strive to have a purpose that is bigger than ourselves.

With the establishment of this foundation, we hope to be able to create a positive impact on those whose lives we can touch by working on our objectives:

1. To provide medical assistance and help to finance children whose families are unable to afford the costs of surgeries, hospitalisation, medicine and/or medical equipment;

2. To finance education for the poor, and

3. To support Christian missions and outreach work.

We acknowledge that we cannot change the world but we can change the lives of the one, two, ten and many more who come our way.

As our foundation is a private trust, we do not raise fund from the public. However, we welcome private donations from friends and relatives.
VISION
To impact and transform lives of children and youth from disadvantaged backgrounds.
To support organisations with the heart of God.

MISSION
1. Provide educational grants for children from disadvantaged backgrounds and those who have been abandoned by their families.

2. Impact and transform lives of children and youth from disadvantaged backgrounds by mentorship.

3. Provide medical insurance and secure medical aid for children from disadvantaged communities who require, but have limited access to medical attention.

4. Carry out Christian missions and community work.


Christianity

Dedication to make all types of sarcoma curable

name: SAWL SCHOLARSHIP FUND
search_text:
SAWL SCHOLARSHIP FUND The objective of the Fund shall be to :
(i) award scholarships to physically disabled students annually. The students are from secondary schools to tertiary institutions who demonstrate both financial need and academic promise.
(ii) award scholarships to students with Autism Spectrum Disorder (“ASD”) annually. The students with ASD must:
(a) be so certified by a relevant medical professional or psychologist; and

(b) be attending (i) any special education school registered with the Ministry of Education; or (ii) any early intervention programme(s) registered with SG Enable or the National Council of Social Service.
(iii) award bursaries for students with Autism Spectrum Disorder and/or intellectual disability.
These students must be:
(a) so certified by a relevant medical professional or psychologist; and
(b) attending (i) any special education school registered with the Ministry of Education; or (ii) any early intervention programme(s) registered with SG Enable or the National Council of Social Service.

For students with disabilities to garner an education that they deserve without the need to worry about funds to pay for their education. Foundations & Trusts

name: SG Eco Fund
search_text:
SG Eco Fund This Trust is established for:

a) the purpose of improving the environment or advancing environmental sustainability in Singapore; and

b) other charitable purposes related to, necessary for, or intertwined with the improvement of the environment or the advancement of environmental sustainability in Singapore in the immediate, short, medium, and long-terms; including but not limited to -

i) educating any community on methods and practices which aid in the improvement of the environment or the advancement of envrionmental sustainability in Singapore;

ii) promoting in any community, an awareness of the importance of, and practices to oimprove the environment or advance environmental sustainability in Singapore;

iii) development of the scientific learning and technologies which can aid the improvement of the environment or the advancement of environmental sustainability in Singapore.

The Trust intends to further these purposes by providing grants and/or other resources to eligible applicants to aid them in their development of assets; or their conduct of any activities, projects, or initiatives. Vision: A sustainable and green Singapore for all generations
Mission: To enable the community in Singapore to protect our environment Environment

name: SHADEE.CARE LTD.
search_text:
SHADEE.CARE LTD. The objects of the Company are to provide relief to those in need by reason of youth, age, ill-health, disability, financial hardship or other disadvantages (the “target segment”).
The objects include, but are not limited to the following:-
(a) To be a voice for the marginalized, addressing discrimination, restoring their dignity and improving their quality of life, beginning with mental wellness for Singaporean youths.
(b) To seek innovative ways to create awareness and education for the public and service user so as to reduce the stigma faced by the target segment.
(c) To empower the service user toward self-efficacy, early help-seeking behavior by bringing channels of resources and intervention to the community.
(d) To support and provide young people with opportunities to use their talent and resources to contribute to the object of the organization so as to foster social inclusion of the target segment.
(e) To identify service gaps, collaborate with like-minded partners and those with lived experience to form integrated network of support and sustainable solution for the target segment. Vision and Mission as stated above. Mental Health

name: SHINE Children and Youth Services
search_text:
SHINE Children and Youth Services The objects of the Service, which are to be carried out in Singapore, shall be:

(a) To maintain for children, youths, and their families a social service with professional staff for the advancement of education, relief of poverty, suffering, ignorance or ill health, whether mental or physical irrespective of gender, race, nationality, religion or moral character;

(b) To inculcate healthy attitudes and socially acceptable values in service users/beneficiaries/young persons referred to the Service;

(c) To help service users/beneficiaries/young persons, through their own efforts, to discover and develop their own potential;

(d) To enable service users/beneficiaries/young persons to obtain the necessary financial
assistance and to pursue their education;

(e) In furtherance of the above objects but not otherwise, and provided that nothing is done for commercial reasons or solely for profit, the Service may exercise the following
powers:

(i) Co-operate with relevant government authorities, private agencies, welfare, community and religious organisations and others;
(ii) Raise, receive, administer, invest or distribute funds, properties or goods in kind;
(iii) Carry out such other tasks or activities as may be, or may become appropriate. To be a leading social work organisation in enabling children and youth to maximise their potential. Children/Youth

name: SHREE LAKSHMINARAYAN TEMPLE
search_text: SHREE LAKSHMINARAYAN TEMPLE Shree Lakshminarayan Temple was declared open on Thursday 24th April 1969. The temple is sanctuary of the spiritual values and culture of Sanatan Darm and Vedic Hindu tradition. It is a place of faith, devotion, worship, love, peace and harmony The temple has constantly been upgrading its facilities and services to serve the devotees better. Devotees now have a better and comfortable place for worship. Over the years the role of our temple has changed. Today the temple is not only the place of worship, but it has become an important focal point for the Hindu community to meet and interact. It fulfils the spiritual as well as the cultural needs of the society and fosters positive social values.  The Temple is a vital part of our community - it enriches our lives culturally, religiously and imparts religious values in our children. The Hindu Temple is your temple. We urge every community member to take responsibility and take OWNERSHIP in the growth and beautification of our Temple.It is the responsibility of each and every devotee to share in the care and improvements of the Temple, as the Temple belongs to ALL OF US. Hinduism

name: SHRIMAD RAJCHANDRA MISSION DHARAMPUR (SINGAPORE) LTD.
search_text:
SHRIMAD RAJCHANDRA MISSION DHARAMPUR (SINGAPORE) LTD. (a) To advance a better understanding of the Jain philosophy through the provision of scriptural knowledge, conducting of devotional meditation, prayer sessions, spiritual discourses, lectures, devotional chanting classes, spiritual retreats, celebration of religious festivals, production and / or distribution of literature and other methods to enlighten others about the Jain philosophy.
(b) To provide relief to persons in need by collecting, sending and distributing financial aid as well as food supplies, new and used clothing and books, medical equipment and the like to assist the poor, ill, destitute and the needy. Such aid to be sent directly to existing non-profit charitable organizations, including but not limited to orphanages, hospitals, disaster relief foundations, educational funds for the poor and the like.
(c) To provide relief of the aged, poor, sick or disabled.
(d) Organize programs and activities conducive to the overall development of all living beings by undertaking activities aimed to create and or to improve their living conditions and to relieve them from pains and sufferings to the extent possible.
(e) Restoration, preservation of documents, literature, photographs, Movies, Videos or creating exhibition halls, museums for exhibition of the cultural heritage, preservation of scriptures, historical stories of moral or spiritual values.
(f) To establish, maintain and support a house of worship with services conducted in accordance with the tenets and doctrines of the Jain faith.
(g) To establish and maintain a religious school of instruction for children, youth and adults on the Jain faith; To advance the Jain faith by providing spiritual educational resources to the general public and individuals interested in the Jain faith.
(h) Provided always that notwithstanding anything heretofore contained no grant, aid or donation shall be given which shall be in aid of any political organization or for any political purpose.
(i) The activities of the company are not limited in geographic scope although the primary areas of focus will be Singapore. In Singapore, the objects of the company are to be carried out for the benefit of members of all races resident in the Republic of Singapore. Shrimad Rajchandra Mission Dharampur is a global movement that endeavours to enhance the spiritual growth of seekers and to benefit society.
The Mission’s Statement -“Realise one’s True Self anf serve others Selflessly” is our guiding principle.
Since its inception in 2001 of SRMD, it has grown to 102 Centers worldwide and activities have gained momentum as seekers delve deeper in the Self on one hand and on the other hand the expanse of seva (service) grows with the aim of benefitting society.
Others; Jainism

• Present cross-cultural and multi-ethnic art forms on national, regional, and international platforms.

name: SINGAPORE ASSOCIATION FOR THE DEAF, THE
search_text:
SINGAPORE ASSOCIATION FOR THE DEAF, THE The objectives of the Association shall be:

(a) to promote inclusion and accessibility and manage all aspects of the welfare of the Deaf, Deafblind and Hard-of-hearing, (hereinafter referred to as the “deaf”1) in Singapore;
(b) to develop, provide and maintain welfare, educational and training services to support the deaf in Singapore;
(c) to advocate for and facilitate the full participation of the deaf community in our society;
(d) In furtherance of the above objects but not otherwise, and provided that nothing is done for commercial reasons or solely for profit, the Association may exercise the following powers:

(i) receive gifts in property or in kind, whether subject to any special trust or not, for
any one or more of the objectives of the Association;

(ii) raise funds in aid of the deaf in such manner as the Association may think fit,
including the sale of hearing aids, accessories or other things;

(iii) publish any newspapers, periodicals, books, articles, advertisements in print or
other appropriate medium that the Association may think desirable for the promotion of
its objectives and such other materials;

(iv) establish, support and aid in the setting up of any other entity formed for or any
part of the objectives of the Association;

(v) purchase, develop, maintain, lease or otherwise acquire land or buildings of
whatever kind or any interest in the same and to sell, convey, assign, mortgage, pledge
or otherwise dispose of any land or buildings for the purpose of the Association. Vision:
A Singapore where the Deaf, Deafblind and Hard-of-hearing have equitable opportunities and access to fully participate in and contribute towards a robust and thriving community.

Mission:
To champion inclusion and accessibility, and to empower the Deaf, Deafblind and Hard-of-hearing to have a good quality of life through education, accessibility, hearing health and community service. Disability (Adult)

name: SINGAPORE ATHLETIC ASSOCIATION
search_text:
SINGAPORE ATHLETIC ASSOCIATION The objectives of SAA shall be to:
Promote, develop, and increase participation for the sport of athletics in Singapore.
Promote physical activity for health and wellness, foster community engagement and bonding for social inclusiveness and integration, and inspire the Singapore Spirit through the sport of athletics.
To engage communities and provide athletics access to vulnerable segments of the community such as youth at risk and the less privileged.
Unify, co-ordinate, sanction, and organise athletics activities in Singapore, including national and international athletics tournaments and events.
Raise the competitive standards of athletics in Singapore for sustainable elite level performance at international competitions and multi-sport major games.
Provide sport pathways and opportunities for the progression and advancement of athletes, coaches, and technical officials in Singapore athletics.
Raise the technical capability of coaches and technical officials in Singapore athletics.
Do all things complementary or incidental to attain the aforesaid objectives. VISION: Sgp as S.E.Asia's top athletics nation wih a cohesive & successful system to develop & support athletes, coaches & officials.

A-dvance coaches' & officials' education

name: SINGAPORE BALLET LIMITED
search_text: SINGAPORE BALLET LIMITED SBL exists as an arts organisation to promote the levels of understanding and appreciation of dance as an artform through the performance of high quality classical and contemporary ballet and the running of dance appreciation programmes for both children, youths, adults, and senior citizens. On occasion, we also fly the flag for Singapore as her cultural ambassador to other nations at overseas festivals and showcases Vision Statement:  To be an established professional dance company that is internationally recognised for its strength in both classical and contemporary repertoire and that showcases choreography that crosses boundaries.  Mission Statements:  Develop talents in dance, choreography, and competent arts management. Audience engagement through professional performances, pervasive and persuasive education, and effective communications strategies.	Nurture a growing base of corporate, government, and individual patrons to strengthen the company's financial stability  Create a perpetuating corporate culture based on a dancer's work ethic of dedication, discipline, innovation, passion, and steadfastness.  Evolve into a world class organisation with a sustainable network of international allies and opportunities. Professional, Contemporary & Ethnic Dance

name: SINGAPORE BASEBALL AND SOFTBALL ASSOCIATION
search_text: SINGAPORE BASEBALL AND SOFTBALL ASSOCIATION Objectives :	  1. To uphold, as the Singapore national governing organization for Baseball and Softball, the rules of Baseball and Softball as adopted by the International Baseball Federation (IBAF) and Baseball Federation of Asia (BFA), and the International Softball Federation (ISF) and Softball Confederation Asia (SCA) which are the international authorities;    2. To encourage, promote, develop and control the game of Baseball and Softball in the Republic of Singapore;    3. To promote and arrange local and overseas Baseball and Softball competitions for the benefit of affiliated clubs, associations and teams;    4. To promote better understanding and to integrate and strengthen the spirit of friendship between one another, through Baseball and Softball; and    5. To advance and safeguard generally the interests of the game of Baseball and Softball, and to employ the funds of the Association for this purpose. Our Vision :    SBSA recognises the full benefits which sports bring to our lives; enhancing a society balanced in its physical, mental and social well being. By delivering the Baseball and Softball experience to every doorstep, we will bring sports closer to all. By strengthening the base of the Baseball and Softball fraternity, we will foster world-class athletes that will move, inspire and encourage fellow Singaporeans.	  Our Mission :    SBSA will create a fully integrated organization that will groom, nurture and sustain a pool of Baseball and Softball players and officials, driven by fair play and solidarity, to foster friendship amongst Singaporeans and beyond, enriched by the experience en-route to excellence. NSAs

name: SINGAPORE BOOK COUNCIL LIMITED
search_text:
SINGAPORE BOOK COUNCIL LIMITED a) promoting and encouraging the reading of print and digital books amongst all sections of the population in Singapore for the purpose of education, information and culture;

b) cooperating and liaising with all members of the national and international print and digital book world, including audio book illustrators;

c) leading the community of writers, storytellers, publishers and related institutions and professional institutions to work together to nurture, share, train, research and organise programmes and provide services that are mutually beneficial;

d) supporting the community in developing their literacy skills, enhancing their ability and capacity to be successful in their respective area of interest and expertise;

e) promoting creative writing, storytelling, reading, writing and publishing as a vital part of arts and culture;

f) establishing Singapore as a recognised international hub for Asian content development in the books and literary arts sector; and

g) supporting and providing translation services.
VISION: The power of words, connecting peoples and cultures.

MISSION: To champion Singapore's role in enhancing multicultural literary arts.​

Our programmes run year-round, reaching out to many audiences. They include the annual Asian Festival of Children’s Content (AFCC), a premier festival for children’s books; the prestigious biennial Singapore Literature Prize, the top literary prize in the country; the Singapore Translation Symposium, which promotes literary translation; SBC Campus and Little Campus, our school outreach programmes; as well as our training and mentorship programmes that support and nurture new talents.
Literary Arts

name: SINGAPORE BUDDHA SASANA SOCIETY, THE (SAKYA TENPHEL LING)
search_text:
SINGAPORE BUDDHA SASANA SOCIETY, THE (SAKYA TENPHEL LING) The aims and objectives of the Society shall be: a. To propagate Buddhism by organising educational courses, public lectures, forum etc, on Buddhism. b. To develop and organise a Buddhist Teaching Centre including schools and kindergartens. c. In furtherance of the above objectives, Society may: 1. Train and encourage members to propagate Buddism. 2. Train and encourage members to perform social services such as looking after old folks, handicapped children, creches and kindergartens. 3. Publish periodicals, or any other literature on Buddhist Philosophy. 4. Provide a library on Buddhist and allied literature. 5. Afford members healthy and decent recreational activities. Vision:
Together, through the teachings of the Buddha, we illuminate lives.

Mission:
Following the Sakyapa Tradition, we propagate the Buddha's teachings, bringing about right understanding, moral discipline and mental development to the community. Buddhism

name: SINGAPORE CHINESE CULTURAL CENTRE
search_text:
SINGAPORE CHINESE CULTURAL CENTRE (a) To promote and showcase the local distinctive Chinese culture through various art forms, including plays, opera, recitals, Chinese martial arts, literature and popular entertainment; through performances, international exchanges, art festivals and more, highlighting the multicultural identity of Singapore and also integrating the rich culture of the new immigrants to enrich Singapore s unique Chinese cultural heritage.

(b) To promote and raise awareness of the Chinese culture in Singapore for all races and nationalities and strengthen the cross-cultural and inter-racial integration by providing resources including the operation of venue and platforms. Vision

A vibrant Singapore Chinese culture, rooted in a cohesive, multi-racial society

name: SINGAPORE CHINESE DANCE THEATRE
search_text:
SINGAPORE CHINESE DANCE THEATRE The objectives for which our Company (hereinafter called the “Dance Theatre”) is established are:

a. to establish, promote and maintain the Dance Theatre to preserve and promote Chinese and cultural dance and artistic and creative excellence;

b. to promote the welfare and interests of the citizens of, and residents of the Republic of Singapore without distinction of sex, race or religion;

c. to promote and teach dance, in particular Chinese and cultural dance, as a sustained and vibrant art form;

d. to enhance the general public's appreciation of Chinese and cultural dance and to engage the masses by making Chinese and cultural dance accessible to the general public;

e. to grow and promote the Chinese traditional performance arts and to develop artistic and creative talent, including production and arts management;

f. to instill keen interest and motivation for the learning of Chinese and cultural dance and Chinese culture amongst the youth;

g. to foster traditional technical foundations of Chinese and cultural dance, and to achieve technical artistic excellence amongst youth and interest groups.

h. to develop young dance artistes and choreographers, and create iconic original choreography and productions;

i. to preserve and promote an in-depth understanding of Chinese culture amongst the public;

j. to carry out the objects of the Dance Theatre for the benefit of members of all races resident in Singapore; and

k. to aid, assist and give relief in any manner and to any extent out of the income of the Dance Theatre to any charitable institution whose object or objects are similar to the objects of this Dance Theatre, provided always that notwithstanding anything heretofore contained no grant or assistance shall be given which shall be in aid of any political organisation or for any political purpose. Vision

An innovative yet grounded dance company that aims to be internationally recognised for its distinctive works that showcase Singapore's heritage and cultural identity.


Mission

To inspire, nurture and connect through dance, while contributing to Singapore's vibrant arts scene. Historical & Cultural Conservation Professional, Contemporary & Ethnic Dance Theatre & Dramatic Arts Traditional Ethnic Performing Arts Training & Education

search_text:
SINGAPORE CHUNG HWA MEDICAL INSTITUTION The objects for which the Company is established are:
a. to provide subsidised traditional Chinese medical treatment for the sick regardless of race and religion in Singapore ;

To build a regional centre for Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) treatment, scientific research, rehabilitation care and clinical teaching.

name: SINGAPORE CLAN FOUNDATION
search_text:
SINGAPORE CLAN FOUNDATION (a) To promote, foster and encourage better and closer relationships, co-ordination, co-operation and understanding among the various clan associations in Singapore;

(b) To promote, organize or finance educational, cultural, social and other activities;

(c) To encourage, finance or undertake research related to language, culture and traditions;

(d) To promote better relationships, understanding and co-operation between the Foundation and other organisations in the public and the private sector;

(e) To organize, participate or help in community and welfare services;

(f) To assist and finance the various clan associations in Singapore for the benefit of members of all races resident in Singapore in promoting and implementing the above objects;

(g) To do all such other things as are incidental or conducive to the above objects or any of them, including accepting donations and gifts of any real or personal property for the general purposes of the Foundation or for any particular purpose thereof and investing the moneys of the Foundation not immediately required for its purposes in or upon such investments, securities or property as may be thought fit; and

(h) Provided that nothing shall be done for commercial reasons or solely for profit, to do all such things as are in furtherance of the above objects or any of them,
As above Historical & Cultural Conservation Training & Education

To build and maintain an insurance and related financial services research and information resource centre. - To enhance intellectual depth through creating and developing curriculum content as well as writing and producing publications including textbooks, magazines and journals on insurance and related subjects.- To promote public awareness of insurance and related matters through various activities involving the community as well as public and educational institutions. name: SINGAPORE CONTRACT BRIDGE ASSOCIATION, THE
search_text:
SINGAPORE CONTRACT BRIDGE ASSOCIATION, THE The objects of SCBA shall be as follows:

a) Promote, develop and increase participation for the sport of Contract Bridge in Singapore.
b) Promote health and wellness, foster community engagement and bonding for social inclusiveness and integration, and inspire the Singapore Spirit through the sport of Contract Bridge.
c) To engage communities and provide access to Contract Bridge courses and games to vulnerable segments of the community such as youth at risk and the less privileged.
d) Unify, co-ordinate, sanction and organise Contract Bridge activities in Singapore, including national and international Contract Bridge tournaments and events.
e) Raise the competitive standards of Contract Bridge athletes in Singapore for sustainable elite level performance at international competitions and multi-sport major games.
f) Provide sport pathways and opportunities for the progression and advancement of Contract Bridge athletes, coaches and technical officials in Singapore.
g) Raise the technical capability of Contract Bridge coaches and technical officials in Singapore.
h) Do all things complementary or incidental to attain the aforesaid objects. As the National Sport Association under the Singapore Sports Council for Contract Bridge, SCBA Promotes bridge, encourages member participation in regional and international championships, as well as organises regular bridge events and tournaments to attract local and foreign bridge players. in accordance to promote Sports Participation, Sports Excellence and Sports Industry Singapore as a sports hub. NSAs

3) To engage communities and provide access to the sport of Cycling to vulnerable segments of the community such as youth at risk and the less privileged

4) Unify, co-ordinate, sanction and organise Cycling activities in Singapore, including national and international Cycling tournaments and events

5) Raise the competitive standards of Cycling athletes in Singapore for sustainable elite level performance at international competitions and multi-sport major games

6) Provide sport pathways and opportunities for the progression and advancement of Cycling athletes, coaches, mechanics and technical officials in Singapore

7) Raise the technical capability of Cycling coaches, mechanics, team managers and technical officials in Singapore

8) Do all things complementary or incidental to attain the aforesaid objects Vision :

name: SINGAPORE DRAMA EDUCATORS ASSOCIATION
classifications: Others; Theatre & Dramatic Arts, Training & Education
search_text:
SINGAPORE DRAMA EDUCATORS ASSOCIATION SDEA is a cause and profession driven arts advocacy body.

In relation to its cause :
SDEA advocates for the practice and value of drama and theatre in performance, education and community. It does this through the following:

a. Harnessing its signature events - Celebrate Drama! Connections, SDEA Theatre Arts Conference - to increase the profile and/or use of theatre/drama to different target groups;

b. Collaborating actively with theatre groups and performing arts venues in areas of audience engagement and appreciation of theatre performance and art-making;

c. Connecting with sectors outside of the arts (social service, healthcare, education) to introduce the use of applied theatre/drama to support and/or enhance process of and outcomes in their work.

In relation to the profession :
SDEA looks into the advancement and development of the practitioners who use and /or teach theatre/drama skills and processes, and the applied form through :
A. Policy Dialogues
B. Networking
C. Professional Development
D. Research and Resource Centre



Mission:
Advancing the profession of the drama/theatre educator

Advocating for the practice and value of drama and theatre in performance, education and community

Vision:
Internationally recognised as the organisation for connecting stakeholders in Singapore to inspire change Others; Theatre & Dramatic Arts, Training & Education Theatre & Dramatic Arts Training & Education

name: SINGAPORE GYMNASTICS
search_text:
SINGAPORE GYMNASTICS The objects of the Association shall include the following:
a) To promote gymnastics disciplines in Singapore as defined by the FIG.
b) To increase participation and promote community engagement and bonding through the sport of Gymnastics, to promote health and wellness, inclusiveness and integration, and inspire the Singapore Spirit.
c) To organize and coordinate:
i) Gymnastics Events;
ii) Gymnastics competitions;
iii) Gymnastics coaching and officiating instructional activities; and
iv) Meetings, lectures, demonstrations, film shows and/or other events for directly or indirectly furthering the aims of the Association.
d) To establish friendly and loyal cooperation among all Members for the benefit of gymnastics in Singapore.
e) To arrange to be written or made, and to print, publish, issue, exhibit and circulate freely or otherwise, reports, periodicals, pamphlets, photographs, books, films, or any instructional materials as may by thought desirable in connection with the objects of the Association.
f) To send delegates and teams to conferences and/ or gymnastic competitions held either within or outside Singapore to improve the standard of gymnastics in Singapore and to enable gymnasts to gain international experience.
g) To coordinate efforts for the physical and psychological development of individuals through gymnastics.
h) To take initiative, give directives, define programmes, promulgate rules and regulations, distribute information and technical data, to develop gymnastics in
Singapore with the best spirit of collaboration.
i) To raise funds and to obtain financial assistance through patronship, donations, sponsorship or otherwise for the attainment of any or all the objectives of the Association to the intent that the funds of the Association, howsoever derived or obtained, shall be applied solely towards the costs and expenses incurred in the attainment of any or all of the objects of the Association; and
j) To do all such other things incidental or conducive to the attainment of any one or more of the above objects.
Our Vision
To promote gymnastics as the fundamental sport for everyone that enriches lives, while nurturing world class athletes.

Our Mission
To become a World Class Hub for Gymnastics NSAs

name: SINGAPORE HERITAGE SOCIETY
search_text: SINGAPORE HERITAGE SOCIETY The Society aims to identify and foster the legacy of Singapore's heritage so as to transmit it to future generations. The Society is made up of professional people, scholars, academics, and others with a special knowledge in various relevant disciplines. The Society proposes to study and disseminate among the general public an appreciation of our ecology, our built environment and our way of life in its various spiritual and physical manifestations. name: SINGAPORE HOSPICE COUNCIL
search_text: SINGAPORE HOSPICE COUNCIL The objectives of the Association are to coordinate and promote hospice and palliative care in Singapore, to provide and coordinate training in hospice and palliative care care, to support efforts in improving the quality of hospice and palliative care, to promote public awareness of hospices in Singapore, and to act as the representative body for hospice and palliative care in Singapore and internationally. The SHC is committed to improving the lives of patients with serious life-threatening illnesses and to giving support to the loved ones of these patients. It aims to coordinate and promote hospice and palliative care in Singapore; support the training of doctors, allied-health workers, nurses, caregivers and volunteers; contribute towards research and help establish standards in palliative care practice; and educate the public about hospice and palliative care. It acts as the voice of hospice and palliative care within Singapore and internationally. Hospice Others; Hospice

2. To facilitate contracts among the ASEAN and wider international community of scholars, policy and opinion makers, interested in international issues and problems of regional security.
3. To provide and maintain means of information, study and investigation on international and regional issues through the preparation and publication of books, records, reports and occasional papers and other works as may seem desirable. The Singapore Institute of International Affairs (SIIA) is a non-profit and independent think tank committed to producing policy analysis, fostering in-depth dialogues and bridging gaps between policymakers, private sector decision-makers and experts to shape public policy and social responses. It is dedicated to making Singapore a more cosmopolitan society with a greater interest and engagement in the region and the world. Think Tanks

search_text:
SINGAPORE JUDO FEDERATION 1. Promote, develop and increase participation for the sport of Judo in Singapore.
2. Promote physical activity for health and wellness, foster community engagement and bonding for social inclusiveness and integration, and inspire the Singapore spirit through the sport of Judo.
3. To engage communities and provide access to Judo to vulnerable segments of the community, such as youth at risk and the less privileged.
4. Unify, co-ordinate, sanction and organise Judo activities in Singapore, including national and international Judo tournaments and events.
5. Raise the competitive standards of Judo athletes in Singapore for sustainable elite level performance at international competitions and multi-sport major games.
6. Provide sport pathways and opportunities for the progression and advancement of Judo athletes, coaches and technical officials in Singapore.
7. Raise the technical capability of Judo coaches and technical officials in Singapore.
8. Do all things complementary or incidental to attain the abovementioned objects.
Our Vision

The vision of SJF is for judo to be practiced in all regions of Singapore and for judo to be a safe, clean and exciting sport for Singaporeans of all ages and walks of life.

Our Mission

Our Mission is to develop the core infrastructure of the sport of judo at the national level so that our stakeholders may propagate judo at the school and community levels.

Our Core Values

Our core values are integrity, trust and transparency. We strive to be open and honest to our stakeholders in our dealings and the management of the federation.

name: SINGAPORE NATIONAL PARALYMPIC COUNCIL LTD.
search_text:
SINGAPORE NATIONAL PARALYMPIC COUNCIL LTD. (a) To advance para sport, where para sport promotes health of participants through physical skill and exertion;
(b) To negotiate with similar bodies in other territories in all matters appertaining to the general interest of para sport and in particular in the organisation of inter-territorial meetings, but so as not to limit in any way the activities of existing Associations;
(c) To plan, select, train and administer any Singapore Association, team or organisation whether representing Singapore or as part of a joint team of organisation with any territory or territories, and for that purpose to be affiliated to the lPC and any other organisation of a like nature;
(d) To develop, manage and protect the Paralympic Movement in Singapore;
(e) To ensure the observance of the IPC Handbook in Singapore;
(f) To encourage the development of high performance para sport as well as sport for all;
(g) To organise and encourage educational and cultural activities and exchanges that contribute to the development and promotion of the Paralympic Movement, enhance awareness of disability and drive social inclusion;
(h) To raise, manage and administer funds for the furtherance of the above objects;
(i) To be completely independent and autonomous and to resist all political, religious or commercial pressure;
(j) To fight against the use of substances and procedures prohibited by the lPC or the IFs and, in this regard, to ensure the observance of the World Anti-Doping Code which provisions shall apply, mutatis mutandis, to all persons and competitions under the jurisdiction of SNPC;
(k) To commit itself to taking action against any form of discrimination, segregation and violence in para sport;
(l) To unify all segments within the fraternity and be willing to work with each of these segments for the common good of para sport in Singapore; and
(m) To co-operate with government or non-government bodies in order to fulfil its objectives. Vision: To inspire and promote an inclusive society through para sport in Singapore

Mission: To promote and protect the Paralympic Movement in Singapore, and support our athletes to achieve sporting excellence Disability Sports

name: SINGAPORE OLYMPIC FOUNDATION
search_text:
SINGAPORE OLYMPIC FOUNDATION The objectives of the Foundation are to raise, manage, invest and distribute funds that:
- Support the involvement and promotion of organisations and participants in the Youth Olympic Games;
- Promote and support integrated sports, culture and education activities, programmes and events which inspire athletes and young people to adopt and live by the Olympic values of Excellence, Friendship and Respect;
- Support youth sports and development activities and projects to unite young people through the Olympic spirit and which contribute to the community or benefit the environment;
- Help in training of sports administrators to promote the fundamental principles of Olympism and instil greater professionalism in sport;
- Support the creation of institutions which devote themselves to Olympic education, in particular, the National Olympic Academies, Olympic Museums and cultural programmes related to the Olympic movement;
- Support the fight against the use of substances and procedures prohibited by the International Olympic Committee (IOC) or the International Federations (IFs) and the observance of the World Anti-Doping Code as in force from time to time;
- Promote the general interest of sports in Singapore;
- Support all types of sports people and sporting groups in Singapore, to aid their personal and skill development in their chosen activities;
- Help in the provision of sports and leisure facilities and improve existing sports and leisure facilities in Singapore;
- Encourage the development of high performance sports as well as sports for all;
- Foster the all-round and holistic development of athletes; and
- Encourage and support actions against any form of discrimination and violence in sports.
- Activities and Programmes which contribute to Nation Building Vision

Uphold the legacy of the Youth Olympics in inspiring young athletes and the youth to adopt and live by the Olympic values of Excellence, Friendship and Respect.

Mission

- Enable young and talented Singaporean athletes to achieve their sporting goals
- Support high-performing athletes to represent Singapore on the world stage
- Promote a healthy sporting culture in Singapore through educational activities and programmes Non-NSAs

Singapore Red Cross is a member of the International Federation of the Red Cross and Red Crescent Societies and as such adheres to the Geneva Conventions (1949) and the fundamental principles of humanity, impartiality, neutrality, independence, voluntary service, unity and universality. name: SINGAPORE RUGBY UNION
search_text:
SINGAPORE RUGBY UNION 7. OBJECTS
The objects of SRU shall be to:
7.1 Promote, foster, develop and increase participation in and support for the game
of Rugby in Singapore.
7.2 Promote physical activity for health and wellness, foster community engagement
and bonding for social inclusiveness and integration, and inspire the Singapore
Spirit through the sport of Rugby.
7.3 Engage communities and provide access to Rugby Events for the vulnerable
segments of the community such as youth at risk and the less privileged.
7.4 Unify, co-ordinate, sanction and organize Rugby Events and other activities in
Singapore including national and international Rugby tournaments and events.
7.5 Represent Singapore on Asia Rugby, World Rugby, and to comply with the Laws
of the Game and the by-laws, regulations and resolutions of World Rugby and to
require that the Members of SRU to similarly comply with the same.
7.6 Form and manage Singapore representative rugby teams
7.7 Raise the competitive standards of Singapore players for sustainable elite
performance at international competitions and multi-sport major games.
7.8 Provide sport pathways and opportunities for the progression and advancement
of Rugby players, coaches and technical officials in Singapore.
7.9 Raise the technical capability of Rugby coaches, referees and technical officials
in Singapore
7.10 Do all such other things which are complementary or incidental to the aforesaid
Objects or to promote the interest of Rugby as the SRU may determine from time
to time. SRU VISION
A prominent, influential and respected Union in Asian rugby and Singapore sport. SRU MISSION

Governance: To provide efficient governance and effective leadership for all Rugby Football activity in Singapore

Business Development: To promote, develop and fund growth in facilities, playing standards and participation and provide excellent service and value for money for all partners and stakeholders

High Performance: To provide a competition structure and player, coach and referee development programmes for both sevens and fifteen a side rugby which will improve playing standards and international results and standing

Participation: To optimize accessibility and increase participation in modified and contact rugby through development programmes for players, teachers and coaches NSAs

By creating a distinctive artistic identity, the Orchestra will be recognized internationally as Asia's pre-eminent orchestra. As a strategic partner of the Singapore Conservatorium of Music, the SSO will play a significant role in nurturing musical talent in Singapore and in the region. In its role as a key cultural organization in Singapore, the SSO will broaden the local audience's appreciation of classical music and actively promote local musical talent.

To realize its vision, the SSO will pursue 4 key strategies:

name: SINGAPORE UNIVERSITY OF SOCIAL SCIENCES
search_text:
SINGAPORE UNIVERSITY OF SOCIAL SCIENCES SUSS is a university with a rich heritage in inspiring lifelong education, and transforming society through applied social sciences. We develop students and alumni to be work-ready and work-adaptive, aspiring to reach their full potential, through our 3H's education philosophy – 'Head' for professional competency with applied knowledge, 'Heart' for social awareness to meet the needs of the society, and 'Habit' for passion towards lifelong learning.

We offer more than 100 undergraduate and graduate programmes, available in full- and part-time study modes which are flexible, modular and inter-disciplinary, catering to both fresh school leavers and adult learners. SUSS also offers a broad range of continuing education and training modular courses for the professional skills upgrading of Singapore's workforce. Our VISION: To be a university that inspires learning for life
and impacts lives

Our MISSION: To achieve social good through purposeful continuous learning, applied research and impactful partnerships

Our 'RAISE' Values: Respect | Adaptability | Integrity | Service | Empathy Local Educational Institutions/Funds

name: SOCIETY FOR WINGS
search_text:
SOCIETY FOR WINGS WINGS aims to enable women, aged 40 and above, to embrace ageing with confidence by empowering them to take responsibility for their happiness, health and security so that they can make a difference for themselves and people around them. WINGS has also adopted WHO ICOPE(Integrated Care for Older People) framework to help older women maintain their functional independence and prevent frailty. Affecting Functional Independence would be mainly a woman's cognitive, mental and physical health. We also empower women with financial knowledge to help them lead better lives.

Since its inception in 2006, WINGS has adopted a holistic approach in the design and development of programs in its engagement with women. The approach facilitates the learning, engagement and support of the community of older women, as peers, as they take personal responsibility for their own well-being. Our Vision
Ageing well. Living well.

Our Mission
WINGS empowers women to age well, by staying healthy, being financially secure and staying socially engaged. Community

name: SOUL HEARTFUL LTD.
search_text:
SOUL HEARTFUL LTD. Objective
To empower children and youth by providing comprehensive programs and resources that nurture their emotional intelligence, strengthen their mental health, and foster holistic well-being.

Our mission is to equip young individuals with the knowledge, skills, and support needed to navigate the complexities of their emotions, relationships, and personal development.

Through a combination of educational initiatives, skill-building workshops, and community engagement, we aim to create a multi-faceted approach that encompasses workshops, counselling, and psychotherapy to achieve the following:

1. Enhance Emotional Intelligence:
Workshops: We conduct interactive workshops focusing on self-awareness, empathy, and emotional regulation. These workshops provide a safe space for children and youth to explore their feelings, improve their emotional literacy, and learn effective emotional management strategies.

Counselling: Our counsellors provide one-on-one and group counselling sessions to help individuals delve deeper into their emotional experiences. These sessions facilitate self-reflection and guide them in applying emotional intelligence to their daily lives.

Psychotherapy: For those requiring more intensive support, we offer evidence-based psychotherapy services that address specific emotional challenges. Our licensed therapists work closely with children and youth to develop coping strategies and healing plans tailored to their unique needs.

2. Promote Positive Mental Health:
Workshops: Our mental health workshops raise awareness about the importance of mental well-being, reduce stigma, and teach stress management techniques. Participants gain practical skills for maintaining and improving their mental health.

Counselling: Our counsellors provide a confidential space where children and youth can discuss their mental health concerns. We offer support for issues such as anxiety, depression, and other challenges, helping them develop resilience and coping strategies.

Psychotherapy: For more complex mental health issues, we offer evidence-based psychotherapy to address underlying conditions. Our therapists collaborate with clients to develop treatment plans and promote mental health recovery.

3. Cultivate Self-Awareness:
Workshops: Self-awareness workshops help individuals discover their strengths, values, and areas for growth. These sessions empower them to make informed decisions about their personal development and future goals.

Counselling: Our counsellors guide young individuals in exploring their aspirations and values, aiding them in making choices that align with their true selves.

Psychotherapy: Psychotherapy sessions can address self-identity and personal growth, helping individuals navigate significant life transitions and challenges with a focus on self-awareness and self-acceptance.

4. Build Resilience:
Workshops: Our resilience-building workshops teach participants how to bounce back from setbacks, develop problem-solving skills, and manage stress effectively.

Counselling: Our counsellors help individuals develop personal resilience plans, teaching them to face adversity with courage and determination.

Psychotherapy: For those dealing with complex life challenges, our psychotherapists provide therapeutic strategies to build resilience, develop coping mechanisms, and find strength in times of adversity.

5. Promote Holistic Well-Being
Workshops: Our well-being workshops focus on various aspects of life, from physical health and nutrition to building positive relationships and fostering a sense of belonging within the community.

Counselling: Our counsellors address holistic well-being by considering the interplay of emotional, social, and psychological factors, helping children and youth achieve a balanced and fulfilling life.

Psychotherapy: Psychotherapy can address holistic well-being by exploring the impact of mental health on physical health and relationships, providing a comprehensive approach to individual growth and happiness.

Through these workshops, counselling, and psychotherapy services, we aspire to create an environment that nurtures the emotional intelligence, healthier mental health the overall well-being of children and youth. Our comprehensive approach aims to equip them with the skills and support they need to thrive in today's complex world and to build a foundation for a brighter, healthier, and more promising future.

To empower children and youth by providing accessible art-based therapeutic programs that enhance emotional intelligence, promote mental well-being, and foster resilience. These programs enable them to navigate life's challenges and contribute positively to their communities. Vision
​At Soul HeARTful, we envision a future where every young individual stands tall, equipped with the tools to navigate the complex tapestry of emotions and relationships with grace and wisdom.

Our vision is a world where the youth we nurture today become the catalysts for a more emotionally intelligent, mentally resilient, and harmonious world tomorrow.

With unwavering commitment, we envision a future where the seeds of self-awareness, emotional intelligence, and holistic well-being that we plant today grow into the flourishing forests of tomorrow's well-rounded, resilient, and compassionate individuals.

Mission
HeARTful Transformation, One Child at a Time

Our mission is to ignite the flames of resilience, compassion, and self-discovery in the hearts of children and youth. At Soul HeARTful, we believe that every young individual holds within them the potential to become a beacon of strength and empathy.

We aim to fan those flames, guiding them on a journey of self-awareness, emotional intelligence, and holistic well-being. We are dedicated to fostering a community where the light of hope and understanding shines brightly.

Our mission is to empower the youth to navigate the intricacies of their emotions, build positive relationships, and fortify their mental health. We are committed to providing the knowledge, skills, and unwavering support they need to find their unique path to a fulfilling and resilient life.

Together, we embark on a shared mission to nurture their inner light, so it may radiate not only within, but also outward, illuminating the way for a brighter, more empathetic, and connected future. Mental Health

name: SOULRICH FOUNDATION LTD.
search_text:
SOULRICH FOUNDATION LTD. Soulrich Foundation focuses on the initiatives of Well-Being and Family.

1) Well-being is in line with the first 3 of the 17 Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) as outlined by the United Nations. We believe that sustenance is key to a person’s well-being. As such, we partner organisations who are doing great work in meeting this need. We also believe in caring for practical needs, to enable people to lead lives with purpose and dignity.

These organisations include:
- Willing Hearts
- Food From The Heart
- The New Charis Mission
- Central Singapore CDC

2) In a recent international report from the Social Trends Institute co-sponsored by the Institute for Family Studies, the research showed that retreat from marriage seems to decrease family stability for children in a wide variety of social contexts. Using data from 100 countries around the globe, the study also found that family instability was higher in countries where more children were born to single mothers and cohabiting couples.

It is proven that marriage is associated with more family stability for children across the globe and that strong family relationships build sustainable societies.

Our partner organisations include:
- Focus On The Family
- Centre For Fathering
- Dads For Life Our vision is to improve the well-being and strengthen the family ties of fellow Singaporeans.

Our mission is to educate and empower individuals and corporates on the value of charity and community. Community Family Support Groups

name: SRI KRISHNAN TEMPLE
search_text:
SRI KRISHNAN TEMPLE a) to provide a place of worship for persons of the Hindu faith which is provided for by virtue of Sri Krishnan Temple situated at 152 Waterloo Street Singapore 187961, at which place there can be observed and performed the usual Hindu religious as well as Hindu customary ceremonies;

b) To disseminate and practise Hindu religious knowledge and traditions especially that of dana (giving for charitable purposes) which is an important part of a Hindu’s dharma (religious duty);

c) To conduct Religious, Bhajan and other classes related to Hinduism; and

d) To promote Hindu culture.

e) In furtherance of the above objects, the Charity may do all such things which are incidental or conducive to the attainment of the objects, and particularly as follows:
i. Receive donations or endowments for any of the charitable purpose described in these objects; or
ii. Donate income monies of the Charity for charitable purpose connected with or similar to those of the Charity. The mission of the Sri Krishnan Temple is to be a focal point for the Hindu community worship. It is our mission to develop a congregational responsibility to the community we serve by being mindful of our culture and customs and adhering to the principles of the Hindu Dharma sastras, as laid down in the Vaishnava Paancharaatra tenets of worship.

The vision of the Sri Krishnan Temple is to enhance the religious experience for the community through expanded programs in education, youth and adult activities; encourage appreciation of Hindu sculptures and architecture and promote racial harmony for our devotees and other religious or ethnic organisations. Hinduism

name: SSVP LTD.
search_text:
SSVP LTD. “The objects for which the Company is established are:

(a) to seek out and find those in need, and the forgotten, the marginalized, the victims of exclusion or adversity in Singapore (collectively the “poor”); and

(b) to serve the poor by providing any form of assistance, care, guidance, support, relief and/or training that may alleviate suffering or deprivation and promote human dignity and personal integrity in all their dimensions,


with compassion and for the benefit of all the poor who are resident in Singapore regardless of creed, ethnic or social background, health, gender or political opinion.


Notwithstanding the above, subject to the provisions of the Companies Act (Cap. 50A) and the Charities Act (Cap. 37), or any other statutory modification, amendment or re-enactment thereof from time to time in force, and any other written law and this Constitution, the Company has full capacity to carry on or undertake any business or activity, do any act or enter into any transaction; and for such purposes, undertake and possess full rights, powers and privileges.” The Vision and mission of SSVP Ltd is to:

1. seek out and find those in need, and the forgotten, the marginalized, the victims of exclusion and adversity in Singapore (collectively known as the "poor") and

2. to serve the poor by providing any form of assistance, care, guidance, support, relief and/or education and/or training that may alleviate suffering or deprivation and promote human dignity and personal integrity in all their dimensions,
