name: ST ANDREW'S MISSION HOSPITAL
search_text:
ST ANDREW'S MISSION HOSPITAL The St. Andrew's Medical Mission was founded in 1913 by Dr. Charlotte Ferguson-Davie, who was a medical doctor and the wife of the first Anglican Bishop of Singapore. She saw the plight of the local women and children of that time and the lack of medical services for them. In response, she set up a dispensary along Bencoolen Street for destitute women and children. The Medical Mission continued to grow, with the opening of more dispensaries and eventually St. Andrew's Mission Hospital in 1923 and St. Andrew's Orthopaedic Hospital in 1939.

Today, ST ANDREW’S MISSION HOSPITAL meets its objective through the following services:
- St Andrew’s Mission Hospital Clinic
- St Andrew’s Community Hospital
- St Andrew’s Autism Centre
- St Andrew's Mission School
- St Andrew’s Nursing Homes
- St Andrew’s Senior Care
- St Andrew's Migrant Worker Medical Centre

1) The principal activity of the St. Andrew’s Mission Hospital Clinic at 8 Simei Street 3, Singapore 529895 is to provide outpatient medical care to patients-in-need in the community.

2) The principal activity of the St. Andrew’s Community Hospital (“SACH”) at 8 Simei Street 3, Singapore 529895, is to provide rehabilitative, subacute, dementia, palliative and continuing care for adult and paediatric patients after their hospitalization for acute care at a general hospital. SACH partners with Changi General Hospital and other healthcare providers to provide integrated care to patients. Its range of services include inpatient, outpatient, home care (medical, nursing, therapy), day rehabilitation and community care services.

3) The principal activity of the St. Andrew’s Autism Centre (“SAAC”) is to provide education, training, care and support to children, youths and adults with autism and their families. SAAC currently operates a school, a residential facility and two day activity centres at 1 Elliot Road, Singapore 458686 and 149 Compassvale Bow, Singapore 544690. Its range of programmes and services includes education with a modified curriculum, specialist therapies, training in personal care and independent living, vocational skills training, development of leisure interests, promotion of physical well-being, parent support and networking.

4) The principal activity of the St. Andrew’s Mission School (“SAMS”) at 11 Bukit Batok Street 25, Singapore 658712, is to provide education, training, care and support to children and youths with autism who are able to access the national curriculum at the primary and secondary levels through a holistic education that is founded in the mainstream academic curriculum and enhanced by life and vocational skills training.

5) The principal activity of the St. Andrew’s Nursing Home (“SANH”) is to provide skilled nursing care within a comprehensive recovery-oriented framework. SANH provides a spectrum of services including occupational therapy and physiotherapy to help them develop their potential and maximise their abilities in a safe and supportive environment. Residents' daily activities include physical exercises, social skills training and health education. Residents also engage in recreational activities such as singing, dancing and team games to develop their social interaction skills. Stress and anxiety management programmes are also implemented to help residents effectively manage any challenge they face.
We have four nursing homes:
- St Andrew's Nursing Home (Buangkok) at 60 Buangkok View, Singapore 534012
- St Andrew's Nursing Home (Henderson) at 303 Henderson Road, Singapore 108925
- St Andrew's Nursing Home (Queenstown) at 11 Jalan Penjara, Singapore 149380
- St. John's - St. Margaret's Church Nursing Home at 28 Dover Ave, Singapore 139791

6) The principal activity of the St. Andrew’s Senior Care (“SASC”) is to care and to support our elderly in the community by providing essential day care, dementia day care, day rehabilitation services, centre-based nursing and integrated home care (medical, nursing and therapy) for the elderly requiring such services.
We have five centres:
- St Andrew's Senior Care (JOY Connect) at Blk 5 Beach Road #01-4919. Singapore 190005
- St Andrew's Senior Care (Tampines) at Our Tampines Hub #03-33 and #04-33, Singapore 529684
- St Andrew's Senior Care (Henderson) at 303 Henderson Road, Singapore 108925
- St Andrew's Senior Care (Queenstown) at 11 Jalan Penjara. Singapore 149380
- St Andrew's Senior Care (Dover) at 28 Dover Ave, Singapore 139791

7) St. Andrew’s Migrant Worker Medical Centre (SAMWMC) at 27 Penjuru Walk #01-24 Penjuru Recreation Centre, Singapore 608538, is a purpose-built and pandemic-ready medical centre offering the full-range of primary care services for Migrant Workers. It is the first of six medical centres for Migrant Workers, as part of a new and integrated primary medical care plan for all migrant workers in Singapore. The services provided include: medical and dental consultation and treatment, minor Surgery, mental health services, physiotherapy, occupational therapy, vaccination services, X-ray services, laboratory services, swab services and pharmacy To be a light in society, relieving suffering and enriching lives with the love of Christ

Our Mission
To provide holistic care and healing to the underserved and disadvantaged by ministering to their physical, mental, social and special needs based on Christian values.

Our Values (L.I.G.H.T.)
Love: We care for those in need with love and compassion, following the example of Christ.
Integrity: We commit to the highest moral and ethical standards in word and deed.
Growth: We increase in passion and professionalism, continuously learning and innovating.
Honour: We accord due respect and dignity to every human being as created by God.
Teamwork: We work together in an environment of trust, collaboration, and mutual support.
Nursing Home

search_text:
ST FRANCIS METHODIST SCHOOL (INTERNATIONAL) LTD. The Objects of the Company are to:

(a) establish, operate, maintain and promote the company which shall provide courses of study or instruction pertaining to management, human resource development and any other fields of knowledge;

(b) establish and carry on in Singapore and any other country of the world or on the high seas, schools at or by means of which students may obtain education or instruction (including night tuition classes) in or with regard to languages, geography, history, scripture, ethics, civics, medicine, dentistry, nursing, mathematics, science, management of business, accounting, economics, media studies and production, seamanship, navigation, aviation and all other subjects whatsoever that may be included in a commercial, technical, scientific, classical, religious or academic education or may be conducive to knowledge of or skill in any profession, trade, pursuit or calling and to increase the effectiveness of the young people;

(c) provide a school or schools, lecture class or examination room or rooms, office or offices, board, lodging and attendance and all other necessities and conveniences for or to students, teachers, lecturers, clerks, employees and officers instructed or employed temporarily or otherwise by the company, and to afford them facilities to study, research, cultivation, teaching and performance of the tasks and duties allotted to them respectively. This includes rental of rooms and hall to third parties for examination, church camping and meeting. Vision: To be a school of choice with a vibrant international learning community, rooted in God's Word and inspiring lives to be the best they can be.

Mission: To provide a nurturing environment offering unique opportunities for students to excel through appropriate educational pathways, and to equip them with the necessary skills and strength of character to succeed in this rapidly changing world. Local Educational Institutions/Funds

search_text: STRATEGIC STUDIES FUND The objective of the Strategic Studies Fund (SSF) is the promotion and encouragement of the exchange of information, upon any major international security issues including those of a political, strategic, economic, social or ecological nature, for the benefit of the local community in Singapore. The Strategic Studies Fund (SSF) envisions itself as a leading institution in Singapore sponsoring, in conjunction with The International Institute for Strategic Studies (Asia) Ltd [IISS-Asia], timely, policy-relevant analysis and opportunities for debate on major international security issues of acute interest and importance to the Singapore community as a whole. name: STROKE SUPPORT STATION
search_text:
STROKE SUPPORT STATION (i) To establish, manage and conduct in Singapore a venue or venues to support step-down rehabilitation for stroke survivors, following treatment at community hospitals, with priority to stroke survivors of certain age group(s), as determined or approved by the Board of Directors of the Company.
(ii) To support the rehabilitation and recovery of stroke survivors so as to enable them to regain functional independence, reintegrate into society, obtain (re)empioyment, and achieve their dreams;
(iii) To provide psychological. emotional. and social support to stroke survivors, and their family members and caregivers, and improve their quality of life through rehabilitation, therapy group activities and programmes, and counselling;
(iv) To create an inclusive, friendly. professional and conducive environment in which stroke survivors. and their family members and caregivers can undergo rehabilitation, therapy. and counselling, and participate in group activities and programmes;
(v) To provide subsidies to stroke survivors, and their family members and caregivers. for rehabilitation and participation in activities and programmes organised by the Company, at such amounts as determined or approved by the Board of Directors of the Company Vision
Stroke Support Station (S3) aims to be the primary reference organization for stroke survivors, their caregivers, volunteers, professionals and the public by providing innovative programmes, support services, education and training.

Mission
Stroke Support Station (S3) helps stroke survivors to Re-learn and Enjoy Active Living (REAL) for a better quality of life through a wellness program with social-emotional support that rebuilds confidence and independence. S3 fosters mental resilience in stroke survivors and their caregivers, resulting in social-connectedness among stroke survivors, caregivers, family members and volunteers.
Diseases/Illnesses Support Group

name: SUNCARE SG LIMITED
search_text:
SUNCARE SG LIMITED Suncare SG provides a safe space, at the Lighthouse, for children and youths to freely express themselves and build their sense of community, self-worth and well-being through the programmes offered. Suncare SG is also a trusted place where parents and caregivers can access evidence-based resources and tools to help them optimise the development of their children.
Suncare SG, together with partners, provides expertise, commitment and resources, creating an ecosystem of diverse stakeholders dedicated to helping the children and youths grow to become happy, confident and resilient members of society.
Suncare SG aims to be a provider of good ideas and an incubator for science-based and research-driven community interventions. These initiatives are rigorously evaluated in the community for their potential to scale, so to help even more individuals in the next generation reach their full potential. Suncare SG is a charity focused on supporting the well-being of children and youths. Our approach is science-based and research-driven, drawing on the expertise of our board members and partners. We champion the resilience of Singapore’s youths, embracing their challenges with them in areas like academic pressures, intricate relationships, the demands of the digital age and more.
The rising SUN in Suncare represents our children and youths and the bright future they have ahead of them. CARE is an acronym for Confidence, Awareness, Resilience and Empowerment. These are skills which we hope to equip our children and youths with so they can develop into happy and well-rounded individuals, empowered to achieve their fullest potential. a) to encourage greater public support for the arts;

name: Secretary of the Synod of the Diocese of Singapore
search_text:
Secretary of the Synod of the Diocese of Singapore The Diocese of Singapore is the Anglican church of Singapore, and it is a member of the worldwide Anglican Communion. Apart from local parishes and congregations in Singapore, the Diocese of Singapore has 6 overseas Deaneries, namely Thailand, Indonesia, Cambodia, Laos, Vietnam and Nepal. In addition, the Diocese of Singapore contributes to the community actively through its community service arms (St Andrew's Mission Hospital and Singapore Anglican Community Services) and Anglican schools in Singapore. As a religions body of Christian faith, the objectives of the Diocese of Singapore are:
- To continue in the common life within "the Body of Christ" according to that "which is recorded in the Scriptures of the Old and New Testaments" and received in the Anglican tradition;
- To work with Jesus Christ "for the salvation of men and for the Kingdom of God" by relying "on the guidance of the Spirit" in teaching, guiding and helping people "to know God and His Son Jesus Christ", "to worship Him" and "to serve their neighbours according to his Will". Christianity

bodies and assistance through conciliation and advice for minority shareholders whose

c) To act as a corporate watchdog and promote exemplary standards of transparency and
corporate governance in issuers of securities and other investment products in Singapore
so as to ensure that investor rights and interests are protected. Vision
To build a vibrant, enlightened and empowered investor community by championing investor rights, promotion of financial literacy, and advocacy of progressive industry practices.

Mission
• To advocate sustainable and stable stakeholder relationships in the investment community
• To safeguard and protect investor rights in Singapore
• To empower investors through education and timely information
• To promote fair and transparent corporate governance standards, regulations and practices
General Charitable Purposes

name: Sembawang Bible-Presbyterian Church
search_text:
Sembawang Bible-Presbyterian Church We are a Church serving the spiritual needs of our members and like-minded believers.

The objectives for which the Church is established are:- 
3.1 The salvation of souls; 
3.2 The edification of Christians through the teaching of God’s Word; 
3.3 The promotion of godly worship;
3.4 The defence of “the faith which was once delivered unto the saints”;

3.5 The establishment and extension of Christian missions and institutions for the furtherance of God’s Kingdom on earth;

3.6 The promotion of Christian relief for the poor and needy;

3.7 The strengthening of mutual fellowship, encouragement and unity among individual Bible-Presbyterian Churches and among other Bible-believing churches of similar stand “for the Word of God and for the testimony of Jesus Christ” (Rev 1:9).

OUR VISION-MISSION STATEMENT

As members of the Sembawang/Moriah Bible-Presbyterian Church, we believe and affirm that God has, in His providence, called us to faith and salvation in Jesus Christ and brought us together:
• to worship and serve the Triune God;

• to share the Gospel of Jesus Christ with others;

• to foster the spiritual growth of believers and encourage active participation and expression in service to God and one another;

• to promote the study and understanding of the Word of God comprised in the holy scriptures and the daily habit of personal devotion and prayer;
• to maintain the regular and consistent assembling of ourselves together for worship, prayer, mutual encouragement and exhortation;

• to strengthen believers in overcoming sin and to walk worthily and pleasing to God in obedience to His commands in daily living, being exemplary to others in Christ-like conduct and showing and sharing His love to one another and to all others in word and deed;

• to train up or provide for the training of workers in the service of God in the church and in evangelism and give support to Gospel missions;

• to maintain and defend the purity of our faith and doctrines as set out in the constitution of our church;

• to participate and contribute towards the well-being of the community at large;

and to do all to the praise and glory of God that we may be presented spotless to God for eternity as part of the universal church of all who are redeemed by the precious blood of Christ.

Christianity

name: Sembawang Family Service Centre

Sembawang Family Service Centre - To serve the needy in the community through the provision of relevant community/welfare programmes and services, which may include after school care, educational and enrichment programmes, literacy skills programmes, preventive, developmental and remedial programmes, and services for families; - To provide enhanced information and referral service that identifies and assesses the needs of families or individuals and links them with appropriate community resources; - To provide care, assistance and counselling to individuals and families in need and/or distress; - To provide casework and counseling services to help families or individuals resolve their personal, social difficulties, including marital counseling/pre-marital counseling, behavioral problems, child management, family violence, financial difficulties and interpersonal relationships; - To organise training and educational programmes that will help strengthen the family unit, enhance family life and meet the real needs of individuals; - To develop and sustain the participation of volunteers in the programmes and services of SFSC; - To develop networks and work in harmony with social, community and other relevant organisations, within and outside the neighbourhood, to better serve the needs of the target clients; - To publish newsletters, periodicals, pamphlets or brochures which SFSC thinks fit to promote the objects and activities of SFSC; - To accept free-will offerings and gifts and organise and carry out fundraising projects and campaigns to support the activities of SFSC; and/or - To do such other things as may further the objects or activities of SFSC. Vision: To be a values-driven Centre, building strong families and forming a caring community.

Mission: To reach out and touch lives, meeting the needs of the community and providing relevant services with respect, warmth and professionalism. Family

name: Shalom Baptist Chapel
search_text:
Shalom Baptist Chapel The objects of this Church shall be:

a) Glorifying the God of our Bible
i. Worshipping God (Ps 150:1-6; Matt 4:10; John 4:23; Heb 13:15)
ii. Loving and Obeying God (Deu 6:5; Luke 10:27; 1 John 5:3)
iii. Living Fruitful and Holy Life (John 15:8; 2 Cor 7:1; Heb 2:14; 1 Pet 1:15-16; 2 Tim 1:9; Col 1:10)
iv. Honoring Christ as Lord (Phil 2:11; 1 John 2:23; Matt 10:32; 1 John 4:15)
v. Proclaiming the Gospel (Mark 16:15; Matt 24:14; Ps 96:3; Rev 14:6-7; Matt 28:18-20)

b) Growing the People of our Church
i. Equipping the Members (Eph 4:11-16; 2 Tim 2:2)/ making disciples of Jesus (Matt 28:18-20; Col 1:28-29)
ii. Fellowshipping and Praying in Community (Acts 2:42-47; Heb 10:24-25; Matt 6:5-18; Luke 11:1-13; Matt 21:13)
iii. Practicing Spiritual Disciplines (Mark 1:5; Luke 5:16; Acts 17:11; Josh 1:8; 2 Cor 13:5; 1 John 1:9; Due 5:12)
iv. Exercising Spiritual Gifts and Church Disciplines (1 Cor 12:1-11; Rom 12:6; 1 Pet 4:10-11; Matt 18:15-17; 1 Cor 5:1-13)
v. Mutual support and encouragement (Acts 4: 32-37; Rom 14:19; 1 Thess 5:11; 1 Pet 4:8-10; Eph 4:29; Gal 6:2; Phil 2:3-4)

c) Gracing the Communities of our World
i. Praying for the Community (Jer 29:7; 1 Tim 2:1-2; 2 Chron 7:14)
ii. Promoting or participating in providing social and community services (Gal 6:9-10; Heb 10:24)
iii. Promoting or participating in granting relief and aid to the poor, sick, aged, disabled, incapacitated and needy, whether jointly or severally (Rom 12:20-21; Jas 1:27; Prov 19:17)
iv. Do all such other things as are incidental or conclusive to the attainment of the above objects on the basis that nothing shall be done solely for profit or for commercial reasons.
Our Mission:
To help everyone become, grow & make disciples of Jesus.

Our Vision:
To see everyone redeemed by God & passionate for God so that they might become changemakers in our city & beyond. Christianity

3.2 To cooperate with all Churches and other Christian groups to further Christian objectives.

name: Sheng Hong Temple
search_text:
Sheng Hong Temple (i) To unify all buddhists in Singapore . (ii) To obey Lord Buddha's Precepts and to propagate the Dhamma. (iii) To promote social welfare activities. (VI ) To perform The Lion Dance 杨桃园城隍庙在新加坡有近百年历史，在我国西部也算是规模较大的寺庙之一。成立理事会推动重建庙宇及扩建佛堂以来也有四十多年的光景，从初期一个乡村传统而不算很完善的管理方式逐步发展成今日现代化规模的宗教组织，并建立高透明度、廉洁有系统的管理模式，使庙务、财务执行顺畅，积累出多年宝贵经验。为了让本庙能更进一步的发展，本庙的愿景和使命如下：

愿景

期望庙宇佛堂能和现实社会与时并进，
为社会人群略尽绵力，作出更多贡献，
与同道分享一份荣耀，得到更广泛信徒的支持，
庙务昌盛繁荣

使命

俗语说：“创业难，守业更难”所以必须维护全面透明完整的管理守则，使往后各届理事会能不脱轨执行庙务和财务，留下千古好评

Sheng Hong Temple has been established in Singapore for more than hundred years. The Temple is one of the largest Temple in the western part of Singapore. Since the formation of the Management Committee forty years ago to plan for the development of the Temple to include Buddhism, the Temple has progressed from the initial stage of village type with relatively simple management system to a modernised religious organisation with highly transparent, clean and structured management model, which has ensured an efficient operation of the Temple and healthy cash flow and has accumulated many valuable experience. In order to bring the Temple to the next height, the Vision and Mission of the Temple are our guiding principles:

Vision

To realise the development of the Temple and Buddhism
in tandem with the progression of the society,
pursue endeavours that maximising benefits to the society,
and sharing of success with other religious groups
to gain more supports from devotees
for the prosperity of the Temple


Mission

The saying “It is easy to achieve success; but difficult to sustain it” should always remind us to maintain a complete transparent management system so that any future Management Committee shall not off-track in executing their operation and financial obligations and hence leave an eternal good legacy behind


Buddhism Taoism

name: Silver Ribbon (Singapore)
search_text:
Silver Ribbon (Singapore) Our objects are:
a. To provide information on the types of mental health services available in Singapore.

b. To raise awareness of mental health issues and promote the eradication of stigma and prejudice against persons with mental disorders.

c. To provide basic counselling and facilitate the detection of persons with mental disorders with the purpose of encouraging treatment.

d. To promote positive mental well-being.

e. To assist specific interests of the community to customize mental health programs for different audiences.

f. To assist persons with mental disorders to integrate into community, thus improving their quality of life.


In furtherance to the above objects, the society shall do all such other lawful things as are incidental or conducive to the attainment of it, and particularly as follows:-

a. To measure the level of stigma towards mentally ill community and develop strategies to encourage positive attitudes.

b. To identify level of awareness to shape the activities of mental health promotion.

c. To increase rates of help seeking and decrease delays in treatment, thereby curbing mounting medical costs in Singapore.
Our Vision
Positive attitude towards mental health among the community.

Our Mission
To combat mental health stigma, encourage early treatment and facilitate integration of people with mental illness within the society through innovative means of promoting mental health literacy. Others; -

name: Sing Man Temple (Singapore)
search_text: Sing Man Temple (Singapore) Main objectives: -	a) To Carry on and develop the fine traditional Buddhist values of compassion and benevolence in relieving the distressed.  b) To facilitate and promote Ch'an Meditation so as to relieve stress, promote relaxation and above all, waking up to life, to its choices and possibilities.  c) To practise meditation as an effective method to rest our mind in a peaceful, stable and joyful state, thereby relieving stress due to daily life and work commitments; to purify the mind and develop wisdom.  d) To do all such things as shall be conducive to the attainment of the above objects:  i) To organize dharma classes, lectures and discussions on mattters related to living a more meaningful life.  ii) To organize regular meditation activities, allowing people from different social background to participate and thereby enhance social cohesiveness.  iii) To counsel the distressed to regain, build a joyous, harmonious and healthy lifestyle. Our missions are as follows: -    1) Ch an culture enriches our lives. To encourage more people, regardless of their religious belief to adopt Ch an culture as a way of life.   2) To enhance moral cultivation.  3) Purifying our mind and produce graceful social conducts.  4) Creates a harmonious society. Buddhism

name: Singapore Anglican Community Services
search_text:
Singapore Anglican Community Services The objects of the Society are:
1. To relieve poverty, suffering, ignorance or ill health, whether physical or mental, without limitation of age, sex, race, nationality, religion or moral character, especially by the provision of assistance, shelters, counselling, advisory, training and education.
2. To work with the parishes, congregations and other organisations in the Diocese of Singapore, and to co-operate with other recognised community service organisations, governmental or private, in carrying out community work.
3. To raise, receive, administer, invest or distribute funds, property or goods in kind to those in need.
4. To rent or purchase properties as may be deemed necessary for the pursuit of the Objects of the Society.
5. To enter into contracts for the purposes of the Society. Vision - To be a light in society, relieving suffering and enriching lives with the love of Christ.
Mission - To provide support, care and rehabilitation to the underserved and disadvantaged by ministering to their physical, mental, social needs based on Christian values.

Our Values (L.I.G.H.T.)
• Love: We care for those in need with love and compassion, following the example of Christ.
• Integrity: We commit to the highest moral and ethical standards in word and deed.
• Growth: We increase in passion and professionalism, continuously learning and innovating.
• Honour: We accord due respect and dignity to every human being as created by God.
• Teamwork: We work together in an environment of trust, collaboration, and mutual support.

Psychiatric Services
Residential Rehabilitation Services, Day Rehabilitation Services, Community Intervention Team, Employment Services and Youth Services

name: Singapore Anti-Narcotics Association
search_text:
Singapore Anti-Narcotics Association The objects of the Association to be carried out in Singapore shall be:

(a) to educate the public in preventive drug education, and to reach out to the youth and the community on the dangers of substance abuse;

(b) to garner the community’s support for the Association’s vision of a drug-free Singapore through close cooperation with the Government and community partners;

(c) to support the recovery and reintegration of drug abusers and their families through counselling and aftercare services; and

(d) to advocate for the Association’s vision for a drug-free Singapore at international fora.

4 In furtherance of the above objects (or any of them), the Association may:

(a) maintain and provide exhibition materials on the dangers of drug addiction and substance abuse;

(b) establish a group of like-minded individuals from the community, including volunteers, students, persons in recovery and families of drug abusers for the purpose of championing the Association’s aspirations towards a drug-free society;

(c) organise any activities as the Board deems appropriate for the objects of the Association;

(d) raise funds to acquire, maintain or lease properties and movables in furtherance of the objectives of the Association and to finance the activities of the Association; and

(e) do anything necessary, appropriate, desirable and/or expedient in connection with any of the objects of the Association.
As a leader in drug addiction prevention and recovery, SANA aspires to mobilise the community towards a drug-free Singapore. Children/Youth Community Family Support Groups

name: Singapore Association for the Study of Obesity
search_text:
Singapore Association for the Study of Obesity The Objects of the Association are to:-
a) Promote the study of obesity in Singapore including its causes, manifestations, prevention and treatment.
b) Encourage and support research into all facets of obesity.
c) Provide a better understanding and independent opinion regarding various aspects of obesity to healthcare professionals and to the general public.
d) Seek affiliation with international bodies and organise scientific meetings to promote the exchange of information about the study of obesity.
e) Compile, print, publish and distribute educational materials detailing information about obesity.
f) Receive donations in any form on behalf of the Association and also to borrow or raise money and to invest and deal with money so derived for the purpose of furthering the objectives of the Association.
Our Vision
• To promote the study of obesity in Singapore including its causes, manifestations, prevention and treatment.

Our Mission

• To provide a better understanding and independent opinion regarding various aspects of obesity to healthcare professionals and to the general public.

name: Singapore Badminton Association

Singapore Badminton Association The objectives of SBA shall be as follows:
(a) Promote, develop and increase participation of Badminton in Singapore.
(b) Promote physical activity for health and wellness, foster community engagement and bonding for social inclusiveness and integration and inspire the Singapore spirit through Badminton.
(c) Engage the community and provide vulnerable segments of the community (such as youths at risk and the less privileged) with access to Badminton activities.

search_text: Singapore Baptist Church To proclaim the Gospel of Jesus Christ, to lead others to accept Christ as their personal Saviour, the salvation of souls, the edification of the Christians through the teaching of God's Word, the promotion of Godly worship, and to fulfil the commission Jesus gave to every church. To glorify God by proclaiming the gospel of Jesus Christ, holding fast to the Truth, equipping the saints, serving in unity and loving one another. Christianity

name: Singapore Baptist Convention
search_text:
Singapore Baptist Convention Singapore Baptist Convention is an association of Baptist Churches sharing like-minded passion to work together to fulfill the Great Commission of the Lord Jesus Christ. By bonding together we enjoy the warmth, encouragement and support of an extended family empowering us to widen our outreach and deepen our impact in nurturing and growing our churches for God’s glory. The objects of this Convention according to our statement of faith shall be:

a. Voluntarily to associate Baptist churches of Singapore;
b. To provide a platform for Baptist churches to unite to do local missions in Singapore, to do foreign missions and to assist one another in areas of Christian education, leadership development and ministry;
c. To uphold Christian principles;
d. To encourage the spreading of the Christian gospel;
e. To promote Christian fellowship and cooperation;
f. To plant churches and develop church work;
g. To establish and manage welfare and non-profit making enterprise including but not limited to ministry for the poor, broken family and elderly who lack means to look after themselves;
h. To advance Christian education by the provision of facilities for the publication, sale and purchase (whether through the operation of a bookstore or otherwise) of materials, the instruction, training and development in accordance with the tenets of Christian faith; and
i. To establish and operate a theological seminary for the training up of pastors and church workers. Christianity

-To select and appoint tenpin bowlers to represent the Republic of Singapore in National or International Tenpin Bowling Competitions.
-To arrange matches with controlling bodies of the sport in other countries and with visiting teams.
-To settle and give rulings on all disputed points in connection with the sport and the rules and regulations thereof.
-To affiliate with any International controlling body for tenpin bowling.
-To safeguard, foster, develop and advance a spirit of good fellowship and better relations among bowlers for the betterment of their interests in relation to bowling generally.
-To enforce uniform regulations and rules relating to the sport of tenpin bowling.
- Generally to do such other acts of things, as may be incidental to or connected with or conductive to the attainment of any of the above-mentioned objects.
- To issue certificates to owners and lessees of establishments, to sanction leagues and tournaments and to collect stated fees as provided by the rules and regulations of the Federation.
- To exercise care and supervision over all affiliates bowling activities.
- To govern bowlers, team leagues and tournaments in its membership. VISION:
To be a world leader in the development and training of coaches, athletes, and sports administrators.

MISSION:
To promote and grow the sport of bowling in Singapore, by governing in a responsible and transparent manner.

VALUES:
We strive to be:
Unifying
We will instil pride in our athletes to see their journeys as inspirations for Singaporeans young and old, and, through this, to unify Singaporeans in taking pride in the achievements, struggles, and joys of our athletes

Collaborative
We see our stakeholders as partners and work alongside them to strengthen our bowling ecosystem across all levels, from casual to elite.

World-class
Our athletes are not just at the top of their game, but at the top of the world’s game. Supporting them and innovating the infrastructure for their performance remains at the core of what we do. NSAs

name: Singapore Cancer Society
search_text:
Singapore Cancer Society (1) To foster national and international co-ordination and development of all activities in relation to cancer;

(2) The prevention and control of cancer:

(a) by formulating a plan for the care of needy persons suffering from cancer and the rendering of assistance to the families of such persons;

(b) by formulating and putting into effect a plan of cancer education including:

(i) collection and distribution of information and educational materials relating to cancer;

(ii) organizing activities and educational programmes to create cancer awareness;

(iii) co-operation with other societies and bodies having as their objects the promotion of public health;

(iv) co-operation with the mass media;

(v) co-operation with Government and other public bodies of persons;

(vi) the encouragement and support of inquiries and observations into the origin, growth, prevention and cure of cancer;

(3) To make available technical and therapeutic requirements and facilities for research in respect of cancer;

(4) To accept and hold any lands or buildings whether provided with endowments or not that may be donated to the Society for the purpose of furthering the objects of the Society;

(5) To receive donations, endowments, subscriptions and legacies from persons desiring to promote the objects aforesaid or any of them and to hold funds in trust for the same;

(6) To donate, disburse or expend sums of money in any manner approved by the Council in order to further the objects aforesaid;

(7) To invest the funds of the Society not immediately required in such manner as may from time to time be determined in furtherance of the objects of the Society;

(8) To borrow or raise money required for the furtherance of the objects of the Society and for that purpose to pledge the assets of the Society as security; and

(9) To do all such other lawful things as may from time to time be conducive to the attainment of the objects set forth above or any of them. Vision: To be Singapore’s leading Charity in the fight for a cancer-free community.

Mission: To Minimise Cancer and Maximise Lives through effective programmes. Hospice Others; Diseases/Illnessess Support Group,Hospice,Nursing Home

name: Singapore Charismatic Church
search_text:
Singapore Charismatic Church 3. The aims and objectives of the Church shall be:- (a) To encourage and promote the Charismatic movement in Singapore. "Charismatic Christian" shall mean Christians who believe and practise the gifts of the Holy Spirit with the normal and initial experience of speaking in tongues. (b) To provide a basis for fellowship among Charismatic Christians. (c) To worship, praise and give thanks to the Triune God: Father, Son and Holy Spirit. (d) To provide for teaching and fostering the growth of the Christian Religion, to license and ordain ministers to carry on the work of evangelism; to promote and support missionary work; to carry on the organisation of churches and foster their development and local sovereignty and independence in their service to the community according to this Constitution and its By-laws. (e) To spread the gospel by preaching, performance of signs and wonders, miraculous hearings and deliverance, and to teach and encourage personal witnessing of individual believers to this same end. (f) To provide and maintain training centres and educational institutions and other benevolent institutions as may be deemed necessary for the direct or indirect propagation of the Gospel and the work of' the Church to serve the community. (g) To support the work of missionaries of like faith in every way either in Singapore or abroad. (h) To train, to examine, to confirm, to appoint and to send forth pastors or training pastors who have met the requirements of the Church as set forth in the Constitution and its By-laws. (i) To meet for prayer, fasting, watching and the administration of the ordinance of water baptism by immersion, baptism in the Holy Spirit, the Lord's Supper and for any other purposes incidental to the Statement of Faith as stated in Article 4. (j) To propagate the Christian faith and to spread the Gospel of Jesus Christ as revealed through the Holy Scriptures by all means of communication, whether visual, verbal or written. (k) To provide the opportunity for fellowship, to minister and to provide counselling for the needs of believers and non-believers in the community alike in a Christ-like spirit. (l) To publish and distribute newsletters, periodicals, books, leaflets or any Christian literature in line with the aims and objectives of the Church and its spiritual beliefs. (m) To erect and maintain Church buildings, social halls, business offices, school buildings, or any recreational facilities, parsonages and other structures as are deemed necessary for the purposes of this Church, and to organise and promote such activities as deemed necessary for the upkeep and continuance of said buildings and facilities. (n) To collect, solicit and accept funds and gifts, to hold trust, use mortgages, lease, sell or otherwise acquire or dispose of property, real or chattel in keeping with the recited purposes of this Church, and it shall have and exercise all powers that are necessary or convenient to effect any and all purposes for which this Church organises. Mission: To make disciples of Jesus Christ

Vision: To be a Spirit-filled church Christianity

name: Singapore Cricket Association, The
search_text:
Singapore Cricket Association, The The objects of SCA shall be as follows:
7.1 Promote, control and increase participation for all forms of the game of Cricket in Singapore.
7.2 Promote physical activity for health and wellness, foster community engagement and bonding for social inclusiveness and integration, and inspire the Singapore Spirit through the sport of Cricket.
7.3 To engage communities and provide access to the sport of Cricket to vulnerable segments of the community such as youth at risk and the less privileged.
7.4 Unify, co-ordinate, sanction and organise Cricket activities in Singapore, including national and International Cricket tournaments and events.
7.5 Raise the competitive standards of Cricket in Singapore for sustainable elite level performance at international competitions and multi-sport major games.
7.6 Provide sport pathways and opportunities for the progression and advancement of Cricketers, coaches and officials in Singapore.
7.7 Raise the technical capability of Cricket coaches and officials in Singapore.
7.8 Do all things complementary or incidental to attain the aforesaid objects in Articles 7.1 to 7.7 To develop and promote the sport of cricket so as to raise the level of awareness, understanding and participating within Singapore. To create an infrastructure which is conducive to identifying and nurturing talented cricketers in order to compete and excel at the international level. To raise the standard of cricket in Singapore whilst instilling in our cricketers the games traditional values and ensuring that cricket is played in the true spirit of the game. NSAs

The SGDF is managed purely by volunteers, who are working professionals in major corporates and government sectors with a strong passion for dancesports. name: Singapore Esports Association
search_text:
Singapore Esports Association Constitution of the Singaore Esports Association 4.1.1 To participate as a member of the International Federation and the Singapore National Olympic Council so that Esports can be conducted, promoted and administered in Singapore, and Esports athletes and officials are eligible to represent Singapore at international competitions and major multi-sport games.
4.1.2 To grow, maintain and enhance the standards, quality and reputation of the Esports and the Association, and strive for Government, commercial and public recognition of the Association as the governing and controlling body for all forms and variations of Esports in Singapore.
4.1.3 To conduct, manage and sanction national and international Esports competitions and events in Singapore; and select, prepare and enter Singapore athletes and teams in international Esports competitions.
4.1.4 To provide equal opportunity to Esports players, athletes, coaches, instructors, officials and administrators in Singapore to compete and develop their skills and abilities; and provide them with the appropriate accreditation and certification where required.
4.1.5 To promote the health and safety of the Members and all other Esports participants in Singapore; and organise Esports activities and programmes that promote an physically active-healthy lifestyle and provide opportunities for children and youth-at-risk to develop their social, emotional, communication and behavioural skills.
4.1.6 To apply the property and capacity of the Association towards the fulfilment and achievement of these Objects; and undertake and or do all such things or activities which are necessary, incidental or conducive to the advancement of these Objects. To develop and promote Esports as a sport in Singapore. Esports shall be defined as any video games that are played in a competitive format.
4.2 In furtherance of the above objects, the Association may work with gamers, cyber-athletes, industry players, and relevant authorities. We may also take part in and organise public events, workshops and seminars. We may liaise with international gaming bodies, We may also assist in the public education of gaming and lifestyle balance, as well as facilitate research in gaming and related activities.
. General Charitable Purposes

name: Singapore Floorball Association
search_text:
Singapore Floorball Association It aims to encourage, develop and manage the sport of Floorball at national and international levels in Singapore. It also strives to promote Floorball to countries within the region. The association is constantly working to increase participation in Floorball and provide more playing opportunities for Singaporeans of all ages.

SFA will continue to support its national teams in international competitions such as the World Floorball Championships and Asia Pacific Floorball Championships. It is currently lobbying for Floorball to be included in major games such as the SEA Games and the Commonwealth Games. Through its efforts, SFA believes that Singapore will have the top-ranked men and women national teams in Asia.

SFA manages the development and promotion of the game to more than 15,000 players, more than 200 schools and 100 clubs. Through the organization of Singapore Floorball Leagues, National Inter-Schools tournaments, coaching courses and officiating seminars, SFA aims to promote Floorball to Singaporeans of all ages and continuously improve the standard of the sport locally. Vision: To be the catalyst for Floorball both locally and regionally. To make Singapore Floorball top in the region.

Mission: To be the leading Floorball nation in Asia Pacific in terms of rankings, development and management. NSAs

(c) To engage communities and provide access to the sport of Golf to vulnerable segments of the community such as youth at risk and the less privileged.
(d) To keep abreast with golf developments and to promote golf excellence in Singapore, including promoting golf championships, competitions and tournaments in Singapore. Other than permitted in these Rules, such promotion of golf championships, competitions and tournaments shall not extend to Members’ Championships. To determine the rules of participation and play therein, and to offer, procure, give or contribute towards the prizes and awards for it.
(e) To promote representative matches against other countries and to encourage participation in Open or other Amateur Championships of other countries.
(f) To cooperate to the extent possible and to maintain and cultivate any relations that the Association has with similar associations of other countries.
(g) To uphold the Rules of Golf and the Rules of Amateur Status as approved by the R&A and the Local Rules of Members.
(h) To uphold and maintain the integrity and reliability of the SGA Central Handicap System (“CHS”) that is used by Members.
(i) To provide the means through which disputes and differences concerning golf in Singapore may be settled.
(j) To affiliate with international golf societies and other organisations established for the promotion of the sport of golf.
(k) To borrow or raise funds for the furtherance of the objects of the Association on such terms and on such security as may be thought fit, particularly, by charging any of the Association’s properties.
(l) To administer or invest funds for the purposes of the Association in any investment approved by the Governing Council, with power from time to time to vary such investments and to place money on deposit with banks.
(m) To form and promote any company or body for the purpose of taking over the functions of organising, arranging and promoting the Singapore Open Championship and to purchase, subscribe or otherwise acquire and hold shares and other securities in such company or body and to lend money, indemnify or guarantee the performance of contracts, obligations or otherwise assist such company or body.
(n) To conduct all other activities which the Association considers necessary or desirable in the interests of its Members.
SGA's Mission: To promote and develop the sport of golf in Singapore. Vision: To be the national body for the sport of golf in Singapore. NSAs

search_text: Singapore Hockey Federation The Singapore Hockey Association and the Singapore  Womens Hockey Association who have hitherto been responsible for the control and promotion of the game of Hockey in Singapore for men and women respectively have agreed to merge into one organisation to be known as the Singapore Hockey Federation  (hereinafter referred to as the Federation ). (2) The Federation is desirous of fulfilling the aim and objects recited in this Constitution for the promotion of Hockey in Singapore. (3) The Federation shall from the date or dates, as the case may be, of the passing of the appropriate resolutions of the Singapore Hockey Association and the Singapore Womens Hockey Association assume all rights and obligations of the Singapore Hockey Association and the Singapore Women s Hockey Association including control of all monies in the accounts of  the Singapore Hockey Association and the Singapore Womens Hockey Association which are transferred to it to be utilised  for the purposes set out in this Constitution. Vision & Mission  The objects of the Federation are:-    (a) To encourage, promote, develop and control Hockey in Singapore within and in accordance with the rules of the F.I.H. and in  accordance with the rules of the game as settled and enforced  from time to time by the F.I.H.; (b) To participate in or to arrange for international and other  matches and to select teams to represent Singapore ; (c) To ensure uniformity of interpretation and application of the  rules of Hockey in Singapore ; (d)To act as the co-ordinating body in Singapore to promote  understanding and mutual help amongst Clubs and teams  playing Hockey ; (e)To raise and expend funds for the promotion, development,  management and control of the game of Hockey in Singapore ; (f)Generally to do anything that may be necessary for the benefit of  the game of Hockey in Singapore, including rental purchase of  premises for use by the Federation and the payment of   remuneration to employees and of expenses incurred by any  persons for services on behalf of the Federation NSAs

name: Singapore Japanese Christian Fellowship, The
search_text: Singapore Japanese Christian Fellowship, The "Having been led, as we believe, by the Spirit of God to receive the Lord  Jesus Christ as our Saviour, and on profession of our faith, having been bapti-  zed in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost, we do now   in the presence of God, angels and this assembly, most solemnly and joyfully  enter into covenant with one another, as one body in Christ. We engage, therefore, by the aid of the Holy Spirit to walk together in  Christian love; to strive for the advancement of this Church, in knowledge,  holiness, and comfort; to promote its prosperity and spirituality, to sustain	its worship, ordinances, discipline and doctrine; to contribute cheerfully and	regularly to the support of the ministry, the expenses of the Church, and the  spread of the Gospel through all nations.  We also engage to maintain family and secret devotions; to religiously educa-  te our children; to seek the salvation of our kindred and acquaintances; to walk  circumspectly in the world; to be just in our dealings, faithful in our engage-  ments and exemplary in our deportment; to avoid all tattling, backbiting and  excessive anger and to be zealous in our efforts to advance the kingdom of our  Saviour.  We further engage to watch over one another in brotherly love; to remember	each other in prayer; to aid each other in sickness and distress to cultivate  Christian sympathy in feeling and courtesy in speech; to be solw to take offence  but always ready for reconciliation and mindful of the rules of our Saviour, to  secure it without delay.  We moreover engage, that when we remove from this place, we will as soon as possible unite with some other Church of the same faith and order where we can	carry out the spirit of this covenant, and the principles of God's Word. 1. establish & operate projects & activities to provide support for post-stroke adjustment & to co-operate with all relevant public & private groups, bodies or agencies in providing such services;

2. build up community support for stroke victims & carers;

3. establish a stroke resource centre to gather, compile & disseminate information regarding the causes, prevention, research programmes, support groups bodies or agencies & available aids for stroke & post stroke adjustment & to establish general public education programmes within these fields;

name: Singapore Shooting Association, The
search_text:
Singapore Shooting Association, The The objects of the Association are:

1. To promote the sport of shooting, which includes rifle, pistol, shotgun and other related shooting activities.

2. To organise and manage shooting championships and other related shooting competitions in Singapore.

3. To co-ordinate, regulate, advise on and administer all matters relating to the sport of shooting; act as the National Authority under the International Shooting Sport Federation’s (ISSF) rules and the rules of any other international shooting body or
similar organisation that the Association may be affiliated to; and safeguard the interests of the shooting sport.

4. To select shooters for national representation at international competitions.

5. To regulate standards and certify shooters, coaches and officials. OUR MISSION:

The Singapore Shooting Association (“SSA”) pursues a two-fold purpose namely (a) promote the sport of Shooting in Singapore, and (b) achieve podium success in major regional and international competitions. This purpose encapsulates the constitutional objectives of the Association.


OUR VISION:

The SSA strives to be a World Class Nation in Shooting by creating a vibrant local shooting sport community that (a) endeavors to provide athletes, coaches, officials and sports administrators the opportunities to reach the epitome of the sport of Shooting, (b) enhances the perception and prestige of Singapore Shooting regionally and internationally, and (c)
contributes to the building of a sporting nation using Shooting as a
launching platform, underpinned by a resilient code of conduct, a robust governance system and an excellent Shooting infrastructure.


OUR VALUES:

The SSA’s values of Passion for the Sport of Shooting, Safety, Growth and Learning, and Excellence through Teamwork support the way we serve our community.

a. Passion for the Sport of Shooting. We believe in the power of the sport of Shooting and are passionate that it will inspire pride in
people's lives and a sense of professional decorum.

b. Safety. We always put safety first in the sport of Shooting as nothing else is more important.

c. Growth and Learning. We value our people and endeavor to provide an abundance of learning opportunities to grow and empower them to achieve their fullest potential.

d. Excellence through Teamwork. We celebrate diversity and work
constructively and only. Through team efforts and our collective
strengths, we drive change and achieve our shared goals. NSAs

name: Singapore Sinhala Buddhist Association
search_text:
Singapore Sinhala Buddhist Association 1. To propagate the Buddha-Dhamma and to foster the study and the practice of the Buddha-Dhamma according to the Theravada tradition as practised in Sri Lanka;
2. To promote Buddhist Principles based upon Karunā (Compassion) and Paññā Wisdom);
3. To establish centres of Buddhist learning within the Republic of Singapore;
4. To promote the cultural, physical and intellectual welfare of the members of the Association in particular and the public in general;
5. To celebrate Buddhist festivals and events according to accepted traditions;
6. To encourage friendly intercourse among Buddhists and promote religious harmony with other Faiths;
7. In furtherance of the above objectives and provided that nothing shall be done solely for profit or for commercial reasons, the Association may:
(i) establish day-care centres for children including kindergartens, regardless of race or creed;
(ii) establish homes for the aged, aged sick and disabled persons regardless of race or creed;
(iii) establish free medical clinics;
(iv) partake in community activities and to provide welfare services to all regardless of race and creed;
(v) establish a lending library called the Sri Lankaramaya Library.
Vision
To be a centre of excellence in the practice and promotion of Metta (Loving-Kindness) and Karuna (Compassion) in accordance with the Buddha Dhamma, for a cohesive society, and to foster cooperation within its membership for the common good of the community in terms of education and welfare.

Mission
To foster the study and practice of the Teachings of the Buddha in accordance with Theravada Tradition, and to foster inter-religious and inter-racial harmony in society in accordance with Buddhist principles based on Karuna (Compassion) and Panna (Wisdom).

Buddhism

As the national governing body of skiing and snowboarding in Singapore, SSSA’s mission is to increase general awareness of the sports of skiing and snowboarding, to facilitate Singapore’s participation in regional, international competitions and the Winter Olympics and to create a platform and online community to connect Singaporeans at home and abroad who are interested in skiing and snowboarding. name: Singapore Table Tennis Association
search_text:
Singapore Table Tennis Association Refer to STTA Constitution Art. 4

The objects of the S.T.T.A. shall be:
(a) To increase participation and promote community engagement and bonding through the sport of table tennis, in order to promote health and wellness, inclusiveness and integration, and inspire the Singapore Spirit.
(b) To promote and safeguard the sport of table tennis, and employ when necessary, the funds of S.T.T.A for the said purpose.
(c) To uphold the rules and regulations (with such alterations and amendments as may be made from time to time) of the sport of table tennis as adopted by the International Table Tennis Federation (hereinafter known as “ITTF”) and to notify all members in writing of the same.
(d) To control, conduct or supervise any table tennis tournament in the Republic of Singapore (as may be necessary).
(e) To select players to represent the Republic of Singapore in local and overseas table tennis tournaments or matches.
(f) To arrange friendly or competitive table tennis matches with other countries, local or overseas clubs or associations.
(g) To settle any dispute that may arise between members of S.T.T.A and to clarify any doubt in relation to the rules and regulations of the sport of table tennis (with such alterations and amendments as may be made from time to time) as adopted by the ITTF.
(h) To obtain, collect and receive money and funds by way of contributions, donations, subscriptions, legacies, grants or any other lawful means for or towards the objects of S.T.T.A. Mission
Our mission is to develop world class table tennis players by identifying, nurturing and motivating aspiring table-tennis talents to become the best that they could be.

The STTA is also committed to promoting table tennis vigorously by building a significant number of table tennis players to help make a vibrant sporting Singapore.


Vision
To make table tennis the most popular game in Singapore NSAs

name: Singapore Taekwondo Federation
search_text:
Singapore Taekwondo Federation The Federation is the highest authority for the promotion of Taekwondo in Singapore with the following objectives:
1. Promote, develop and increase participation for the sport of Taekwondo in Singapore.
2. Promote physical activity for health and wellness, foster community engagement and bonding for social inclusiveness and integration, and inspire the Singapore Spirit through the sport of Taekwondo.
3. Unify, co-ordinate, sanction and organise Taekwondo activities in Singapore, including national and international Taekwondo tournaments, demonstrations and gradings.
4. Raise the competitive standards of Taekwondo athletes in Singapore for sustainable elite level performance at international competitions and multi-sport major games.
5. Provide sport pathways and opportunities for the progression and advancement of Taekwondo practitioners, coaches and technical officials in Singapore.
6. Raise the technical capability of Taekwondo coaches and technical officials in Singapore.
7. STF shall be inclusive and shall integrate into STF’s activities any adaptive form of Taekwondo for people with disabilities that has been approved by World Taekwondo or the International Paralympics Committee.
8. Do all things complementary or incidental to attain the aforesaid objects in Articles 1 to 7.

The vision of the Singapore Taekwondo Federation is that the sport makes better persons and enjoys wide participation and the organisation has a tradition of helping potential players realise their dreams of being champions and contributing to the community. The mission of the Federation is to inculcate the right values in its members, promote the art and sport widely, provide participants with optimum opportunities to develop their potential by having sound support, development programmes and services and play a role in supporting the Singapore Government's programmes and helping charitable causes. name: Singapore Tennis Association
search_text:
Singapore Tennis Association The objectives of the Association are:
(i) To uphold the laws of the game of tennis as at present adopted by the INTERNATIONAL TENNIS FEDERATION with such alterations, amendments and additions thereto as may be made from time to time;
(ii) To promote tennis in the Republic of Singapore and to organise tournaments for the benefit of Affiliated Members as well as Associate Members;
(iii) To arrange and organise tournaments with other countries;
(iv) Generally, to advise on and safeguard the interests of the game and employ, wherever necessary, the funds of the Association for that purpose; and
(v) To do all other things as the Committee shall deem necessary or desirable to achieve the Association s objects above. Mission
We are committed to promoting the continuous development of tennis and its community in Singapore. We are passionate about tennis. We aim to deliver more tennis to our whole tennis community and see the standard of tennis raised for all players. Ultimately, we strive for excellence so that Singapore tennis can compete at the highest international levels.

Vision
We appreciate the broader community that make up tennis in Singapore: players of all ages; their supportive families; the tennis clubs; coaches; sports manufacturers and sponsors, the media and other constituents. We are responsive to all our participants and partners. We aim to be proactive in leading the development of tennis in Singapore so that we can inspire and bring about continuous improvement in our community.
NSAs

name: Singapore Thomson Road Baptist Church, The
search_text:
Singapore Thomson Road Baptist Church, The The purpose of this church is to worship God and serve Him, preach the gospel and win souls for Christ, cultivate believers’ spiritual lives and make disciples, in order to fulfill the mission entrusted to all believers by Jesus Christ our Lord. name: Singapore Universities Trust
search_text: Singapore Universities Trust The Government wishes to ensure the continued affordability of undergraduate education in Singapore while providing strong incentives for universities to exercise cost discipline and develop new sources of income. To underscore its commitment to and financial support for university education and to signal the role that alumni and the philanthropic community must play, the Government intends to match community donations to the universities and build up the universities  endowment funds.  	The Government intends, for this purpose, to transfer into the names of the Trustees, monies to establish the Singapore Universities Trust Fund to be held by the Trustees and their successors for the time being of this Deed on and with and subject to the following trust powers and provisions. name: Singapore Weightlifting Federation
search_text: Singapore Weightlifting Federation a.	To organize, promote and develop the sport of weightlifting on a national scale in accordance with the objective of the International Weightlifting Federation (hereinafter referred to as  IWF ).    b.	To promote an interest in the sport of weightlifting as a way of fitness and healthy living.	c.	To develop and intensify friendship and co-operation among members of the weightlifting fraternity.    d.	To groom, train and develop potential competitive weightlifters for international competitions.    e.	To assist national athletes in pursuit of excellence in the sport of weightlifting.    f.	To resolve disputes, if any, that may arise within the Federation or the athletes.    g.	To set up the rules and regulations of weightlifting in accordance with IWF.	h.	To issue weightlifting proficiency, coaching certificates and other standards from time to time.    i.	To own, lease, sub-lease and operate facilities necessary for all or any of the objects of the Federation. MISSION STATEMENT	It is the mission of the Singapore Weightlifting Federation is to establish the necessary programmes to facilitate and encourage increased participation in the sport as well as maximizing the resources available to ensure the competitiveness of the athletes with a strong and efficient management team	  VISION  The Singapore Weightlifting Federation believes in maintaining an open relationship with its stakeholders in pursuit of win-win solutions, which all stakeholders   athletes, coaches and partners   can benefit from. Integrity must be maintained in all its activities and initiatives. NSAs

name: Singapore Wind Symphony (SWS)
search_text:
Singapore Wind Symphony (SWS) 1. To perform music at a world-class level and quality, advocating for wind band music to local and international audiences;

2. To present concerts and performances of wind band music, and chamber music, with a strong emphasis on Singaporean and regional composers, as well as Singaporean instrumentalists;

3.To present educational and outreach events, including workshops, seminars and master classes, for the benefit of young musicians and the general public to develop new audiences for music;

4. To commission and give premiere performances of original or arranged works by Singaporean composers and arrangers, representing such works to local and international audiences;

5. To engage in any other activities, such as fund-raising, publishing, audio/video recordings or to support Affiliated Ensembles, that support and promote the aforementioned objectives; and/or

6. Any other objective as may be decided by the Board and approved by the Registrar ofSocieties. Music & Orchestras

3. To work together with other Christian rehabilitative organisations and government departments ('related organisations') who have the same objectives above to achieve common purposes.
4. To work with related organisations to achieve standardisation of programmes and systems within the framework of Christian principles and to form an accreditation body to regulate basic practices. To enable ex-offenders to rehabilitate and assimilate into society. Support Groups

search_text:
SkillsFuture Jubilee Fund The intent for the Fund is to use the monies to administer SkillsFuture Fellowships and SkillsFuture Employer Awards. These are awards given to:

(a) recognise and develop Singaporeans who embody characteristics aligned with the SkillsFuture objectives and support them in developing skills mastery in their respective fields of work; and

(b) recognise employers who made significant effort to invest in employee training and supported the SkillsFuture effort to develop structured skills-based career pathways for their employees. General Charitable Purposes

name: Society Against Family Violence
search_text:
Society Against Family Violence Prevent and reduce the incidence of family violence Encourage and provide support and therapeutic services for the victims of fmily violence Encourage and provide rehabilitative services for perpetrators of family violence Our mission is to provide services that prevent and/or reduce the frequency of violence in Singaporean families.
Our goals are:
1. To develop and implement programmes that support frontline professionals working with families in crisis.
2. To develop and implement training/consultancy programmes for personnel to service victims and survivors of family violence.
3. To develop a clearing house facility which will facilitate the coordination of existing services for victims and survivors of family violence. Family

name: Special Olympics, Singapore
search_text:
Special Olympics, Singapore (a) To provide persons with intellectual disabilities opportunities to develop physical fitness, demonstrate courage, experience joy and share skills and friendship with their fellow athletes, families and the community.
(b) To promote and provide year-round sports training and competitions in a variety of Olympics-type sports for all persons with intellectual disabilities.
(c) To do such other things as are incidental or conducive to the attainment of the above objectives, and in furtherance of the above objectives, the Association may, with the approval of the relevant authorities where necessary:
(i) encourage and recruit volunteers to train athletes and to organise sporting activities.
(ii) conduct training courses for coaches and officials.
(iii) establish international good relationship with other national Special Olympics organizations.
(iv) promote public education and awareness of the capabilities of persons with intellectual disability.
(v) encourage community and corporate participation.
(vi) participate in Special Olympics regional and world games.
d) In addition, the Association seeks to:
(i) be completely independent, autonomous and to be devoid of all political, religious or commercial pressure and
(ii) commit to taking action against any form of discrimination and violence in sports
(e) To take such steps by personal or written appeal, public fund raising campaigns, meetings or seminars or otherwise as may from time to time be deemed expedient for the purpose of procuring or raising contributions to the funds of Special Olympics Singapore, in the form of donations, annual subscriptions or otherwise, provided that in so doing Special Olympics Singapore shall conform to all relevant statutory regulations, conditions and consents as for the time being shall be applicable by law.
(f) To do all such other lawful things as are necessary for the achievement of the Vision and Mission. Vision: Enhancing the potential of athletes with intellectual disability and celebrating the human spirit common to all.

Mission: To promote and provide year round sports training and athletic competition in a variety of Olympic type sports for children and adults with intellectual disabilities, giving them continuing opportunities to develop physical fitness, demonstrate courage, experience joy and participate in a sharing of gifts, skills and friendship with their families, other Special Olympics athletes and the community. Disability Sports NSAs

name: Special Oral Care Network
search_text:
Special Oral Care Network SOCN's Objects are to:

Advocate oral health equity in those who are vulnerable or marginalised through projects and initiatives;

Improve oral health competency for healthcare workers, nursing home staff, caregivers, and members of the general public - focusing on disability and elderly oral care;

Provide professional training, recommendations and maintain standards within this field of oral health;

Support communication and collaboration through a network of members including healthcare professionals/workers, service providers, oral health professionals, medical suppliers - to affect service development, research, and care provision;

Establish a network of persons and organisations in the SouthEast Asia region to exchange oral care expertise and support continual learning. Mission:

SOCN seeks to improve oral care of vulnerable populations in Singapore through supporting locally-sensitive solutions - through advocacy, providing training, supporting standards, create platforms for communication and collaboration, advocacy, and synergising with friends in the wider ASEAN region. Others; Special care oral health

name: Sree Narayana Mission (Singapore)
search_text:
Sree Narayana Mission (Singapore) a) To uphold the Guru’s teachings which include:
‘Whichever be the religion, it suffices, if it makes a better man’,
‘One in kind, one in faith and one in God is man’,
‘Acts that one performs for one own sake should also aim for the good of the other me’,
‘Gain freedom through education’,
'Gain strength through organisation',
'Gain prosperity through industry'.
b) To promote the interests of the Mission and to encourage generosity.
c) To promote and encourage service to mankind, carrying out good deeds, humanitarian and charitable activities so as to relieve physical, emotional and mental pain and suffering.
d) To provide opportunities and create goodwill to enhance unity and friendship among members and human society through social, cultural, spiritual and recreational activities.
e) To celebrate the birth anniversary of the Guru, observe Guru’s Samathi Day and celebrate other days of significance including the National Day of Singapore and other special occasions as the Committee may deem fit and appropriate.
f) To have such other objects and so such other things as are complimentary or incidental to the attainment of the foregoing objects or any of them including the raising of funds and donations, the purchase or acquisition of movable and immovable properties, the provision of accommodation, facilities and service for the aged, destitute and persons in need of care, and to provide financial assistance, educational assistance, counselling and referral services and food relief to the needy.
Our Vision: Empowering people to live dignified lives.
Our Mission: To provide social and community services based on Sree Narayana Guru’s teachings. Community Eldercare

name: Sri Guru Nanak Sat Sang Sabha
search_text:
Sri Guru Nanak Sat Sang Sabha The objects of the Sabha shall be to promote the teaching of the ten Sikh Gurus and Sri Guru Granth Sahib by organising such religious, cultural, educational and social activities as my be decided upon by the Management Committee from time to time. Vision Statement

"To be a beacon of Sikh spirituality and community service, fostering the principles of Guru Nanak Dev Ji by promoting unity, education, and selfless service within and beyond the Sikh community."





Mission Statement

Quality Religious Services
To propagate Sikhism by providing quality religious services that inspire and guide the Sangat in their spiritual journey.

Create Awareness
To create awareness and inculcate the practice of the Sikh way of life, ensuring the teachings of Guru Nanak Dev Ji reach every corner of our community.

Learning of Gurbani & Kirtan
To organise and promote the learning of Gurbani and Kirtan, enriching the spiritual experience and knowledge of our Sangat.

Joining Sangat to Guruji
To lead the Sangat to become and remain AmritDhari Sikhs, deepening their commitment to the faith and its values. Others; Sikhism
