name: Sri Krishna Mandir Welfare Society
search_text:
Sri Krishna Mandir Welfare Society 1. To organize various educational, recreational, social and welfare activities in enhancing a healthier youth and family life.

2. To provide and organize a series of facilities and activities focused primarily on counseling services to all those seeking assistance regardless of race, religion or financial ability. These including moral and spiritual counseling for troubled youth, persons with marriage and/or family problem as well as former drug/substance addicts without providing detoxification facilities and services.

3. To follow up on such services eventually with an expansion intended to include a daycare centre, free food distribution, free medical consultation, etc., subject to approval from the relevant authorities.

4. To contribute to the social stability and well being of the citizenry of Singapore by way of the above objects.

In furtherance of the above objectives, the Society may organize fundraising projects, publicize its activities through publications, exhibits, audio, video, telecommunication and computer technology, subject to approval from the relevant authorities. Our Mission / Vision:

1. To serve 500,000 healthy, pure vegetarian meals annually.

2. To increase awareness about the benefits of vegetarianism on physical and mental health.

3. To conduct cooking classes to make it easy and interesting for people to switch over to vegetarianism without having to crave for meat.

4. To provide relief by way of counseling to troubled youth, broken families, substance addicts, and prisoners.

5. To create an educational framework based on the rich and ancient science of traditional Indian wisdom to help prevent psychological trauma across all age groups of individuals of the society and transforming them by replacing depression with inspiration.

6. To promote the inclusion of authentic traditional Indian culture and wisdom as at least a vocational option in primary, secondary and tertiary education of Singapore so that the youth of the nation have easier access to it irrespective of race, creed and religion. Children/Youth Community Disability (Adult) Eldercare Family

name: Sri Ruthra Kaliamman Temple
search_text: Sri Ruthra Kaliamman Temple OBJECTS :	  	The objects of the Temple are:	   a)	To administer and manage the affairs of the Temple.  b)	To conduct prayers and religious and cultural functions.  c)	To disseminate religious knowledge.  d)	To promote the educational, religious, spiritual, moral, cultural and social interests of members and their families and others associated with the Temple.  e)	To provide grants and assistance for deserving children/students in the pursuit of education.  f)	To carry out charitable and welfare work among the people of Singapore irrespective of race or religion.  g)	To collect, compile, print, publish and to sell or distribute gratuitously or otherwise, journals, periodicals, leaflets and books the Temple may consider desirable for the promotion of its objects.	h)	To campaign for, receive, hold and apply funds, gifts, donations, bequests and endowments, or the proceeds thereof, to any of the purposes described in these objects.	i)	To purchase, lease, take on hire or in exchange or otherwise acquire any immovable property, rights and/or privileges.	j)	To borrow money on interest or otherwise for the purpose of acquiring immovable property, rights and/or privileges, and for repayment of such loan(s).	k)	To permit the use of the Temple and/or its facilities for weddings, conferences/meetings and other functions or activities with or without charge.  l)	To engage in any other activity designed to promote the aforementioned objects but not in any way prejudicial to the interests of the Temple or in contravention of these Rules. To administer and manage a Hindu religious / charitable organisation set up in 1978 to conduct Hindu religious prayers and to provide religious services to devotees, to disseminate religious knowledge and to promote the educational, religious, spiritual, moral, cultural and social interests of members / devotees and their families and others associated with the Temple. name: Sri Sathya Sai Prema Nilayam
classifications: Others; teachings of Bhagawan Sri Sathya Sai Baba
search_text:
Sri Sathya Sai Prema Nilayam OBJECTIVES

The object of the Society is to study and practice the teachings of Bhagawan Sri Sathya Sai Baba based on the precepts of Sathya (Truth), Dharma (Right Conduct), Shanthi (Peace), Prema (Love) and Ahimsa (Non-Violence) through organised activities in three areas, namely, Education in Human Values, Community Service and Spirituality.

To Achieve the above object, the society shall:
a. Provide members the opportunities to do charitable work in their daily life.
b. Provide members opportunities to serve their fellowmen regardless of race or religion.
c. Promote inter-racial and inter-religious understanding in Singapore and to strive for the recognition of the unity of ideals of all faiths.
d. Hold discussions, talks, lectures, prayers and meditation lessons, meetings and spiritual studies and the organise service activities, seminars, retreats, conferences and symposiums for the purpose of providing opportunities and facilities for spiritual advancement.
e. Print, publish, sell or distribute books, periodicals, pamphlets, bulletins or other literature for the promotion of the objects of the Society herein before stated and in furtherance thereof to produce, sell, distribute or exhibit audio-visual tapes, films and such like material.
f. Utilise funds, gifts, benefactions in cash or kind donated to the Society for the promotion of the objects of the Society.
g. Generally do all other acts necessary for or incidental to the attainment of the objects of the Society herein stated.

Note:- The above objects are approved by the Registrar of Societies and The Commissioner of Charities and it appears in our Society's Constitution Rule 4.
OBJECTIVES

The object of the Society is to study and practice the teachings of Bhagawan Sri Sathya Sai Baba based on the precepts of Sathya (Truth), Dharma (Right Conduct), Shanthi (Peace), Prema (Love) and Ahimsa (Non-Violence) through organised activities in three areas, namely, Education in Human Values, Community Service and Spirituality.

To Achieve the above object, the society shall:
a. Provide members the opportunities to do charitable work in their daily life.
b. Provide members opportunities to serve their fellowmen regardless of race or religion.
c. Promote inter-racial and inter-religious understanding in Singapore and to strive for the recognition of the unity of ideals of all faiths.
d. Hold discussions, talks, lectures, prayers and meditation lessons, meetings and spiritual studies and the organise service activities, seminars, retreats, conferences and symposiums for the purpose of providing opportunities and facilities for spiritual advancement.
e. Print, publish, sell or distribute books, periodicals, pamphlets, bulletins or other literature for the promotion of the objects of the Society herein before stated and in furtherance thereof to produce, sell, distribute or exhibit audio-visual tapes, films and such like material.
f. Utilise funds, gifts, benefactions in cash or kind donated to the Society for the promotion of the objects of the Society.
g. Generally do all other acts necessary for or incidental to the attainment of the objects of the Society herein stated.

Note:- The above objects are approved by the Registrar of Societies and The Commissioner of Charities and it appears in our Society's Constitution Rule 4.
Hinduism Others; teachings of Bhagawan Sri Sathya Sai Baba

name: Sri Sathya Sai Society, Singapore

name: St Andrew's Cathedral
search_text:
St Andrew's Cathedral St Andrew's Cathedral is a Parish within the Diocese of Singapore.

The Diocese of Singapore is the Anglican church of Singapore, and it is a member of the worldwide Anglican Communion. Apart from local parishes and congregations in Singapore, the Diocese of Singapore has 6 overseas Deaneries, namely Thailand, Indonesia, Cambodia, Laos, Vietnam and Nepal. In addition, the Diocese of Singapore contributes to the community actively through its community service arms (St Andrew's Mission Hospital and Singapore Anglican Community Services) and Anglican schools in Singapore. As a religious body of Christian faith, the objectives of the Diocese of Singapore are: - To continue in the common life within "the Body of Christ" according to that "which is recorded in the Scriptures of the Old and New Testaments" and received in the Anglican tradition; - To work with Jesus Christ "for the salvation of men and for the Kingdom of God" by relying "on the guidance of the Spirit" in teaching, guiding and helping people "to know God and His Son Jesus Christ", "to worship Him" and "to serve their neighbours according to his Will". name: St. John Singapore
search_text:
St. John Singapore Main objectives include the following:-
1. To have a trained first aider in every school, home, organization and workplace in Singapore.
2. To be the preferred Charity serving mankind through life saving skills of first aid, nursing, occupational safety, health care training and emergency assistance.
3. To be the preferred Uniformed Group in Singapore. 4. To be an organization adopting best practices and leveraging on information technology skills and tools, in the pursuit of our goals.
5. To provide and manage affordable health care services. Our Vision: To be a leading humanitarian and charitable organization, and to serve humanity by relieving pain and suffering.

Our Mission:
(a) To promote and encourage the work of St. John Ambulance in all its aspects; and
(b) To promote and encourage all works of humanity and charity for the relief of distress, suffering, sickness and danger without any distinction as to race, class or creed, and the extension of the great principles of the Order of St. John embodied in its mottoes “For the Faith” and “In the Service of Humanity”.
(c)To serve mankind through training in life saving skills and health care in schools, homes organisations and the workplace in Singapore.

name: T.H.E DANCE COMPANY LTD.
search_text:
T.H.E DANCE COMPANY LTD. The objectives for which the company is established are:

(a) To achieve the highest standard of excellence and originality in Singapore in the technical and creative areas of dance making in Singapore.

(b) To pursue the creation of an ever changing and dynamic body of work in Singapore that provides a documentary voice for our time in Singapore.

(c) To provide an aesthetic and artistic environment in which Singaporean choreographers, dancers and theatre workers can continue to strive and develop their craft in Singapore.

(d) To recognise and co-operate with public and private bodies which support the performing arts in Singapore.

(e) To respond to the needs of young people including young dance professionals in the performing arts in Singapore. Our Vision:
To build human connections through dance.

Our Mission:
Through the creation and performance of our original work, as well as through our research, practice and training of the HollowBody, we seek to actively engage society and promote thoughtful living.

Our Core Beliefs:
We believe in:
- The body as the vessel that carries our thoughts and emotions.
- Movement as intrinsic and necessary in life.
- Embracing human perfection and imperfection in our work.
- Dance as the power to change our experiences with the world.

Our Goals:
- To be recognised as a Singapore professional dance company of international standing.
- To create distinctive and original works by local choreographers and international collaborators.
- To continue on our research, practice, and archival of the “HollowBody” methodology, and to strengthen our efforts in expanding its reach.
- To nurture a new generation of outstanding dance artists and choreographers as core figures of Singapore’s dance scene.
- To actively engage audiences of diverse backgrounds by challenging perceptions, rousing curiosities, and enabling them to identify with the work that we do.
- To establish and promote a dance ethos that is inclusive and transcends boundaries.
Professional, Contemporary & Ethnic Dance

name: T.H.E. Buddhist Nunnery
search_text:
T.H.E. Buddhist Nunnery CONSTITUTION OF T.H.E. BUDDHIST NUNNERY

OBJECTS
3.1 Its objects are:
a) To promote Buddhism, including to learn, share and practise Buddha’s teachings.
b) To promote moral education to foster goodwill in our society.
c) To support causes for charity.

3.2 In furtherance of the above objects, the Society may conduct seminars, classes, dharma events and hold other activities at appropriate locations.

3.3 The Society shall not engage in any activities that may undermine the racial or religious harmony in Singapore. Vision:
Sow seeds of wisdom, compassion and virtue in every heart and witness the rise of generations of positive individuals who are motivated to help self and others realise their fullest potential inside out, leaving no one behind.

Mission:
T.H.E. Buddhist Nunnery Mission Statement encapsulates the ethos and heartbeat of our community:
We endeavor to build a virtuous community of Buddhists who walk the talk.
We endeavor to serve humanity and provide assistance and relief to those in need, in the spirit of compassion for all.
We endeavor to build a network of altruistic and exemplary individuals who are driven by the purpose of developing self and others.
We endeavor to preserve and promote the diversity of tradition and culture that makes us who we are, anchors us to our roots, and ignites inspiration for the future. Buddhism

name: TABERNACLE OF JOY
search_text: TABERNACLE OF JOY 3.1	The objects of the Tabernacle shall be:    (a)	To practice, preserve and propagate the teachings of the Tabernacle in accordance with the Article of Faith in Article 4 of this Constitution.	  (b)	To bring those who have problems with drug addiction, and other related social difficulties into a personal relationship with Jesus Christ so that they may lead productive and responsible lives, an asset to their community and society.    3.2	In furtherance of these objectives:-	(a)	To be involved in Christian counseling at DRC s (Drug Rehabilitation Centres), providing spiritual and social support to those desiring a new life.    (b)	To provide aftercare. To teach and train new converts in what it means to be disciples and to follow the pathway of discipleship, using hostel facilities as necessary to provide development opportunities.	(c)	To help them secure employment, complete their education and become useful citizens.	(d)	To restore family relationships by providing ministry to residents  family members.    (e)	To provide information and services that will prevent other youth from becoming involved in life-destroying problems.	 (f)	To work in cooperation with the Singapore Anti-Narcotics Association through the Christian Counseling Service in the care of former drug addicts in after-care hostels.    3.3	In furtherance of these overall objectives, the Tabernacle shall be at liberty:-    (a)	To require voluntary contribution of funds from the members and to borrow money;    (b)	To organize religious, educational, evangelistic, charitable or other related activities;    (c)	To employ, supervise, discipline or dismiss workers to carry on its affairs;	(d)	To enter into contracts, sue and be sued in its own name;    (e)	To rent, hire, purchase, hold, develop, buy or otherwise acquire; and to sell, mortgage, charge, let, dispose of or otherwise deal with such movable or immovable properties of any portion thereof;	(f)	To establish, manage or wind-up theological seminaries with the prior approval of the relevant authorities;    (g)	To establish branches with the prior approval of the Registrar of Societies;	(h)	To affiliate with any local or foreign organization having compatible objectives;    (i)	To permit and publish any newspapers, periodicals, books or leaflets that the Tabernacle may think desirable for the declaration and promotions of its objects.    (j)	To do all such other things as may be necessary, incidental, or conducive for the attainment of its objects. Antioch of the East, Impacting Billions of Lives Christianity

name: TAN NGAK BUAY & KEE MENG LANG FOUNDATION LIMITED
search_text:
TAN NGAK BUAY & KEE MENG LANG FOUNDATION LIMITED The objects for which the Foundation is established are:

(A) To advance the cause of education, whether general, professional or technical by the building of schools, institutions of learning and educational facilities, providing scholarships, grants, aids, and financial assistance to persons of all races and religion wheresoever situated in the world and in particular needy children at schools, colleges and universities, and fostering, developing and improving education of all kinds and in any such manner as the Foundation may, from time to time, deem fit.

(B) The relief of human suffering and improvement of human living conditions and standards by the combatting, treatment, alleviation and prevention of sickness and disease for persons of all races and religion wheresoever situated in the world and in particular the needy including but not limited to those who are terminally ill or physically handicapped, by providing for their maintenance and/or funeral expenses, by contributing towards medical and scientific research and teaching and by the provision of medical, surgical and educational facilities and services of all kinds and in any such manner as the Foundation may, from time to time, deem fit.

(C) To contribute or donate to in any manner and to any extent to any religious institution, charitable and/or social welfare institutions wheresoever situated in the world which the Foundation may, from time to time, deem appropriate.

(D) The relief of poverty, suffering and distress and the provision of financial assistance of poor and needy persons of all races and religion wheresoever situated in the world including but not limited to victims of fire, flood, famine, war, pestilence or other calamity or persons who by reason of age, ill-health, misfortune, infirmity or otherwise are wholly or in part unable to maintain themselves and dependants by their own exertions.


(E) Any other objects with a charitable purpose that the members of the Foundation may decide from time to time.

(F) To do all such other things as are incidental or conducive to the attainment of the above objects or any of them and/or in furtherance of the above objects and provided that nothing shall be done for commercial reasons or solely for profit. VISION AND MISSION

1. Our Foundation’s activities are guided by its vision and mission, which are set out as follows:

Vision:
(1) We seek to enable an ecosystem of sustainable support and care for all who care and are being cared for.

(2) We believe in the dignity of all humans, regardless of race, religion, nationality.

Mission:
(1) We care for those who give their strength, time, and resources to care for the vulnerable in society, those in need, in poverty, afflicted with illnesses, disabilities, or suffering from discrimination.

(2) We are dedicated to enable every individual to lead their lives fully and brightly, unlocking the great potential within each person to be self-reliant and fully integrated in all aspects of society and community living.

We seek to achieve the above through the funding of programs that span across education, healthcare and the alleviation of poverty, suffering and distress.
General Charitable Purposes

5. Other objects which are charitable under the laws of Singapore including but not limited to increasing operating space, mindshare and goodwill, promoting health, advancing and improving environmental and wildlife protection, climate action and sustainability, and other purposes that are beneficial to the community in Singapore. TF IPC Ltd supports, funds and implements programmes to strengthen social
resilience, foster international exchange and regional capabilities, and advance science and nature. TF IPC Ltd also galvanises the philanthropic, non-profit and/or civic sector in

name: THE ASSEMBLY OF CHRISTIANS OF SINGAPORE LTD
search_text:
THE ASSEMBLY OF CHRISTIANS OF SINGAPORE LTD The objects for which the Church is established are:-

1 To propagate and spread the Gospel of the Lord Jesus Christ and to observe and practise the teachings of the Bible.

2 To provide opportunities for believers to enjoy fellowship, prayer, worship, instruction and mutual love and to build up God's family of warmth and love.

3 To promote the welfare of mankind and to help relieve human suffering on Christian charitable and benevolent principles.

4 To promote education and to assist in schemes of education having a Christian emphasis.

5 To establish and maintain evangelical centres for the propagation of the Gospel and the worship of God Almighty.

6 To do all such other things as are incidental or conducive to the attainment of the above objects or any of them and particularly as follows:-

6.1 To publish newspapers, books, periodicals, magazines or tracts for the furtherance of the objects of the Church.

6.2 To purchase, lease or otherwise acquire lands, buildings and real estate or other immovable property or movable property or any interest in the same, or to hire any premises, schools, hospitals or other immovable property in the Republic of Singapore or elsewhere.

6.3 To improve, manage, develop, work and maintain, or to sell, lease, let, underlet, exchange, surrender, borrow, mortgage, charge, dispose of or otherwise deal with and turn to account, all or any of the property for the time being of the Church wheresoever situate, or property in which the Church has an interest and to lend, invest, or deposit money an mortgage or any other security, and to erect, construct, alter and maintain any buildings and other immovable or movable property, and to retain, use, occupy or deal with the properties of the Church or any part thereof for all such purposes and in such manner as the Church may deem fit, and in particular to permit any agents of, or any persons authorised by the Church to use and occupy the same and on such terms and conditions as the Church may deem fit.

6.4 To provide, engage and employ ministers, auditors, secretaries, treasurers, agents, solicitors, servants and other persons for the purpose of carrying out the objects of the Church or any of them.

6.5 To receive and take any gift; of money or property for any of the purposes and objects of the Church whether subject to any special trust or not, provided that the Church may decline to accept any gift; or donation or to take over any property which has annexed to it any condition or obligation not approved by the Church.

6.6 To undertake and execute any trusts created to provide funds or income for the use of Church in the furtherance of its objects or any of them.

6.7 To support and subscribe to any charitable or public object and any institution, society or club which may be for the benefit of the Church or its members; to make payments towards insurance and to establish and contribute to provident and benefit funds for the benefit of any persons who have served the Church or of their wives, children or other dependents who are in great financial needs.

6.8 To establish or promote any other churches, fellowships or companies for the purpose of acquiring the business and undertaking of all or any of the property, rights and liabilities of this Church or for any other purpose which may seem directly or indirectly calculated to benefit the Church.

6.9 To draw, make, accept, endorse, discount and negotiate cheques, promissory notes, bills of exchange, bills of lading, charterparties, warrants, debentures and other negotiable or transferable instruments.

6.10 To amalgamate with any church, fellowship or company having objects similar to those of the Church so as directly or indirectly to benefit the Church.

6.11 To enter into any arrangement with the Government of the Republic of Singapore or elsewhere, or authorities supreme, local, municipal or otherwise, in pursuance of the objects of the Church and to obtain from the Government or authority all rights, concessions and privileges that may seem conducive to the Church's objects or any of them.

6.12 To acquire properties and investments which the Church deems fit, and to use the properties and investments for the time being of the Church and the proceeds of sale of such properties and investments and any other moneys of the Church for the general purposes of the Church as the Church may think fit, or for such charitable objects as the Church may be directed to use the same under any special trust, but entirely unconnected with and apart from any political ends.

6.13 To do all or any of the above things either alone or in conjunction with any other church, fellowship, company, undertaking or person.
The vision and mission are embedded in the objectives above. Christianity

name: THE ASTRONAUTS COLLECTIVE LTD.
search_text:
THE ASTRONAUTS COLLECTIVE LTD. The Astronauts Collective (TAC) is a non-profit organisation that helps youths explore the world of work and discover careers they find meaningful to pursue, just like how astronauts explore and discover new worlds!

TAC believes that every youth should be able to aspire towards a meaningful career, regardless of their backgrounds or challenges. Given that Singaporeans spend so much of our lives working, we believe that meaningful careers = happier lives.

Founded in 2015, TAC has been designing experiential career exploration programmes to help provide youths with both the process to explore and the access to resources that many youths lack. Our programmes aim to help motivate youths, generate more career options and pathways they can consider, and develop a direction of next steps towards a meaningful career. Mission - Support youths to explore and discover meaningful careers.

Vision - Meaningful careers, happier lives. Children/Youth

name: THE COMMUNITY JUSTICE CENTRE LIMITED
search_text: THE COMMUNITY JUSTICE CENTRE LIMITED The objects for which the Community Justice Centre (CJC) is established are all or any of the following:	 (1)	To provide assistance to Litigants in Person who appear before the Subordinate Courts of Singapore ( LIPs ) to avoid disadvantaging such LIPs as a result of not being represented by lawyers;	   (2)	To assist and help LIPs in understanding the relevant legal rules, terminology, court procedures and other aspects of the legal system to enhance their access to the legal system;    (3)	To provide an integrated one-stop hub to provide and facilitate various support services to LIPs;     (4)	To provide various services and programmes for the purposes of supporting and assisting LIPs;	  (5)	To develop and implement public outreach and educational programmes and community services and programmes targeted at the community at large in Singapore who may be potential LIPs or have other interests in the court or legal systems; and	  (6)	To develop other services and programmes for the assistance of LIPs or for the furtherance of any of the other abovementioned objects of the CJC as may be decided upon by the Directors of the CJC. Access to Justice for Litigants-In-Person Through Community Partnership General Charitable Purposes

name: THE COMPASSIONATE NETWORK LTD.
search_text:
THE COMPASSIONATE NETWORK LTD. The objects for which The Compassionate Network Ltd (TCN) is established are:
(a) To equip family caregivers, lay helpers and para professionals with training, tools, and resources to provide themselves and their care recipients holistic care, in particular, psychosocial and spiritual care;
(b) To form networks of community helpers in support of family caregivers and their care recipients who are nearing the end of life;
(c) To equip the community, health and social care workers and volunteers with training to incorporate spiritual care into their services to support the sick, the dying and the bereaved; and
(d) To conduct other related charitable activities. Vision: Let every life be cradled in compassion.

Mission: To nurture a compassionate Singapore society that supports caregivers of persons nearing the end of life to fulfill their roles and achieve peace of mind. Other Community-based Services

name: THE CROSSING CHURCH LTD.
search_text: THE CROSSING CHURCH LTD. As stated in the Memorandum of Association Our Vision  To glorify God by planting churches in Singapore and Southeast Asia which will be committed to the expository preaching of His word so that many will come to know Him, live for Him and enjoy Him forever.    Our Values  A Word-based Community  It is our firm conviction that God speaks to us through His living and abiding word, the Bible. It is as the Holy Spirit illuminates God s word to us that we are convicted of our sin and our need to turn to Christ to be born again (1 Pet 1:23; John 16:8-11). We also believe that our knowledge of God as revealed in the Bible is sufficient for all things pertaining to life and godliness (2 Pet 1:3). Furthermore, all Scripture is breathed out by God and is useful for teaching, reproof, correction and training in righteousness so that we can be thoroughly equipped for the service of God (2 Tim 3:16). As such, everything from the pulpit to community groups to kid s ministry is explicitly and unashamedly based on the word of God. We have no message, no voice, and no hope if we don t proclaim the message of the Bible in which God s voice is heard and real hope is found.  A Spiritual Community  God s great goal in history is to gather to Himself a people for Himself (Gen 12:1-3). God s desire is that we will worship Him by offering all of ourselves, all of the time (Rom 12:1-2). But God has also bound us together as His body so that we may exercise the gifts that He has given us to serve and encourage one another (1 Cor 12:4-31). As the people of God, we are God s new family, called to contend together for the truth of the gospel and to stir up godliness in one another as the Day draws near (1 Tim 3:15; Heb 10:19-25). We therefore seek to be an authentic Christian community which exists to both worship God with all of our lives and edify one another in all of our interactions as we see the Day approaching. Church is not functional, it is relational; it is not observed, it is lived; it is not social, it is family. We see ourselves as an inter-dependant, inter-connected spiritual family brought together by the blood of Christ.  A Training Community  God s all-sufficient word is given to us to make us wise for salvation through faith in Jesus Christ and to fully equip the church for every good work (2 Tim 3:15-17). Not surprisingly then, the ascended Christ gives to his church key leaders   apostles, the prophets, evangelists, pastors and teachers   so that they might teach the word of God to equip the saints for the work of ministry. This in turn edifies the body of Christ and allows it to stand firm in the face of falsehood (Eph 4:11-16). It is therefore imperative that every saint be equipped by the word of God for service. Men, women and children will be taught to understand and live out their God-given roles; Fathers, mothers and singles will be encouraged to pursue godliness in the gift that God has given them; Those able to teach will be discipled, and some trained for full-time gospel ministry.  A Missional Community  The missionary nature of the church is implied by the very nature of the gospel which is a proclamation of the good news of Jesus Christ (Rom 1:16-17). Making disciples of all nations is also a command of the risen Christ to all of his followers (Matt 28:16-20). Furthermore, proclaiming the excellencies of God is a direct consequence of our calling as God s people (1 Pet 2:9). Evangelism then cannot be seen as just one ministry among many. Instead we seek to shape everything we do in our preaching-teaching, corporate worship, discipleship and community to be engaged with the non-Christian world around us. We don t just engage in missions, we are missional. Christianity

name: THE CULINARY INSTITUTE OF AMERICA, SINGAPORE, LTD.
search_text:
THE CULINARY INSTITUTE OF AMERICA, SINGAPORE, LTD. (a) to establish, operate and deliver Singapore-based undergraduate educational programmes of the Culinary Institute of America for Singapore citizens and permanent residents of Singapore; (b) to engage in activities relating to teaching and the advancement of education including providing credit and non-credit education and training in the culinary arts and related fields, and undertaking consultancy, production and dissemination of educational materials and research findings (if any) in relation to culinary arts management and related areas; (c) to offer and provide education and degree courses leading to conferment of bachelor degrees, standing and/or accreditation granted by the Culinary Institute of America; and (d) to do such other things which are incidental or conducive to the attainment of all or any of the above objects. for the exclusive benefit of the community of Singapore as a whole and not confined to sectional interests or group of persons based on race, creed belief or religion. OUR VISION
Food is at the heart of our existence. Beyond sustenance, it is fundamental to cultures and deserves to be a source of joy for all people, families, and communities. We seek
to better understand and promote its relationship to health, the environment, and a vibrant, equitable economy.

OUR MISSION
The Culinary Institute of America is dedicated to bettering the nation and the world by means of outstanding education, practice, and scholarship on all aspects of food and the enterprises related to it. Through a transformative learning experience, the CIA prepares future food leaders, innovators and experts for personal and professional success.

OUR VALUES
The CIA is a private, not-for-profit college, guided by the core values of Excellence, Leadership, Professionalism, Ethics, and Respect for Diversity.

name: THE FOOD BANK SINGAPORE LTD.
search_text:
THE FOOD BANK SINGAPORE LTD. Established in 2012, The Food Bank Singapore is the nation’s only accredited member of The Global FoodBanking Network (GFN), an organisation which supports food banks in more than 50 countries. As an IPC-registered charity (non-government funded), we collect surplus food from manufacturers, distributors, retailers, restaurants, and consumers, and channel them to vulnerable communities through our network of beneficiary partners. We also procure food as needed to meet the food support requirements of our beneficiary partners. Beyond providing vital food support to improve food security, we champion food sustainability through advocacy, reducing food waste, and driving education programmes that inspire action to reduce food waste and build a more sustainable future. OUR VISION: To achieve food security for all in Singapore.

OUR MISSION: To connect food surpluses with communities to enhance food security, while championing food sustainability through education, advocacy, and efforts to reduce food loss and waste. Community

name: THE GOOD PROTEIN NETWORK LIMITED
search_text:
THE GOOD PROTEIN NETWORK LIMITED 1. To address some of the most pressing environmental challenges on our planet, including the emission of greenhouse gases, land, water and air pollution, water shortages and loss of biodiversity;

2. To advocate for a more sustainable, secure and just food system;
To advance the markets of plant-based, fermentation-derived and cultivated meat, eggs and dairy products;

3. To engage and help government bodies and multilateral organisations to develop regulatory frameworks that relate to novel food ingredients and to create a level playing field for alternative proteins; and

4. To identify knowledge gaps in the alternative protein sector, to drive funds and brainpower to support the academic fields related to alternative proteins, and to groom talent to accelerate scientific research and innovation in the alternative protein sector.
The Good Protein Network Limited is operating in its brand name GFI APAC. At GFI APAC, we envision a food system that can feed 10 billion people while meeting global climate goals, protecting biodiversity, and maximising public health. By working with scientists, policymakers, and industry leaders, we are singularly focused on creating a world where alternative proteins are no longer alternative. As a field catalyst for the alternative protein sector, we are developing the roadmap for a stronger and more equitable global food supply. We identify the most effective solutions, mobilise resources and talent, and empower partners across the food system to make alternative proteins accessible, affordable, and delicious.

name: THE GROUND CO LIMITED
search_text:
THE GROUND CO LIMITED The governing instrument serves as a reference point for the organisation to keep its practices in check. The Artground – A Curious Place to be is managed by The Ground Co Limited, a registered arts charity dedicated to children from birth to 9 years old. Our vision ‘To let art do, what art does’ defines what we do. As the first multi-disciplinary children’s arts centre in Singapore, we aim to provide positive arts experiences across a variety of art forms, and build bridges between artists, educators, parents and children from various communities so that they may discover and co-create meaningful arts experiences together.

Since our opening in July 2017, we have welcomed more than 175,000 visitors to our Visual Arts Space where we provide tiered levels of access. As part of The Artground’s core programmes, we present interactive installations that children can climb through, over and under, and access for free, and these installations change annually. We believe that children learn best through embodied experiences and we provide opportunities for this to happen in an open-plan space with artists who share our philosophy.

On the weekends, we offer a selection of 30-minute drop-in and ticketed programmes that families with young children can engage in together. These include gardening-inspired arts workshops, performing arts (music, dance/movement and drama) as well as art and craft activities. The Weekend Programmes form the 2nd tier of arts access for families, and allow children to engage further with an artist if they wish to during their exploration of the Visual Arts Space.

Another core programme that caters to schools is our artist-led Learning Journeys which offer a similar range of workshops as the Weekend Programmes. We work with educators and artists to create age-appropriate resource materials for young learners, as well as pre and post programme guides for the teachers and carers who attend the Arts Learning Journeys. The Artground also actively seeks to introduce skills development through Teacher Training and Corporate Bonding Programmes in order to inspire teachers and/or any participant with an interest in the arts to continue the arts engagement back in the classrooms, the home and/or the workplace.

Finally, the Artist Development Programmes enable The Artground to forge strong partnerships with the artist community in its bid to support the development of arts programmes for young audiences in Singapore. Through the open call for the design of the Visual Arts Space, incubation projects, artist sharing practices and dialogues, and professional development opportunities both locally and internationally, The Artground has become a space for artists to inspire and be inspired, to explore, create, question and delight in meaningful aesthetic expressions. It is also a space where teachers, children and their families can feel at ease to come to, to discover and to be curious.

name: THE INTERNATIONAL INSTITUTE FOR STRATEGIC STUDIES (ASIA) LTD
search_text: THE INTERNATIONAL INSTITUTE FOR STRATEGIC STUDIES (ASIA) LTD The International Institute for Strategic Studies (IISS) is the world s leading authority on political-military conflict. It is the primary source of accurate, objective information on international strategic issues for politicians and diplomats, foreign affairs analysts, international business, economists, the military, defence commentators, journalists, academics and the informed public. The Institute is independent, owing no allegiance to any governments or any political or other organisations. The IISS stresses rigorous research with a forward-looking policy orientation and places particular emphasis on bringing new perspectives to strategic discussions. The Asia office based in Singapore (IISS-Asia) seeks to further the Institute's work in the Asia-Pacific region where one-third of its members are based.  IISS-Asia organises the annual Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore, which has become a key feature in the calendars of not only defence ministers, but also their top officials, military chiefs and heads of intelligence. Provide, from an international perspective, to IISS members and the wider public, through publications and other activities, the best possible objective information on military and political developments relevant to the prospects, course, and consequences of conflict having an important military dimension.    Provide, to the same audience, and by similar means, the best possible analysis of the policies to be pursued by various governments and other actors to further and maintain international peace and security.	  Convene government ministers, officials, international civil servants, independent analysts, business people and journalists in different formats, public and private, to advance understanding of political, military, technological, business, economic, environmental, social, religious and other trends that could have an impact on the prospects, course and consequences of conflict having an important military dimension.	  Maintain, nurture and continually enlarge an international network of influential and knowledgeable individuals, corporate entities, governments and other bodies to ensure the effective dissemination of information, analysis and understanding of the subjects and activities addressed by the Institute's work.	   Aim, through these activities, to influence and promote the adoption of sound policies to maintain and further international peace and security and civilised international relations by all actors able to realise this aim. MHF supports the research, documentation and advocacy efforts on Malay society, heritage and culture. Working in partnership with the National Heritage Board (NHB), MHF has been providing strategic direction to the management and programming at the Malay Heritage Centre (MHC) located on the grounds of the Istana Kampong Gelam. In 2015, this historically significant building was gazetted as the 70th National Monument. The MHC Museum showcases exhibitions, interactive
workshops and talks, docent training and guided tours all year round. Historical & Cultural Conservation

name: THE MOH FAMILY FOUNDATION LIMITED
search_text: THE MOH FAMILY FOUNDATION LIMITED The objects for which The Moh Family Foundation Limited ('TMFFL') is established are to carry out charitable and non-profit activities in general, including but not limited to: (a) Engaging in education, vision care, medical social service and community building and social work as TMFFL's primary area of focus; (b) Making grants, donations and providing scholarships and funding of charitable programmes for beneficiaries within the TMFFL's primary area of focus; and (c) Without prejudice to the generality of the foregoing, to do such other things incidental or conducive to the attainment of the above objects or any of them. Vision: To create an opportunity of transformation for anyone who desires a better quality of life. Mission: We believe people can reach their full potential if they are able to gain a clear vision of their future through education, positive mental health, and the ability to see. Through our giving, we hope to inspire deeper awareness and empathy in those we help. The Moh Family Foundation Limited ('TMFFL') supports organizations and leaders with the ability to catalyze new solutions for recurring social challenges in their communities. We are willing to take risks and be patient in testing and scaling effective, replicable models. We work in areas where we have personal experience and insight: China, Southeast Asia, and the United States. In furtherance of TMFFL's mission, TMFFL's main objectives is to carry out charitable and non-profit activities in general, including but not limited to: (a) Making grants to community-based organizations ('CBO') to provide direct service to people who lack opportunities to improve their well-being or health; (b) Providing scholarships and granting awards to students who are financially challenged; (c) Funding quality empirical data based action research to pilot and identify effective solutions for social challenges; (d) Supporting capacity building in the non-governmental organization ('NGO') sector to enable partners to serve a larger community; and (e) Fostering cross sector partnerships to synergize collective efforts in promoting effective philanthropy. As set out above, the main area of focus and field of interest of TMFFL is education, vision care, community building and social work in medical and community settings. Theory of Change: We build long-term partnerships based on shared values of trust, integrity and accountability with leaders who are adaptive, resilient and compassionate. By equipping them with the resources to design social projects, we seek to achieve transformational social change together. name: THE NECESSARY STAGE LTD
search_text: THE NECESSARY STAGE LTD Non-profit theatre company that stages an average of 2 main season productions each year as well as organise the annual M1 Singapore Fringe Festival. The company also runs its developmental initiative, TNS Devising Platform, which incorporates training and mentorship programmes for emerging theatre practitioners (including Playwrights' Cove and Devising with Actors and Playwrights), as well as laboratory initiatives focused on thematic and interdisciplinary explorations. As part of its education and outreach arm, the company creates youth-oriented commissioned productions and conducts school workshops for skills development. It has previously organised workshops and staged performances by its Theatre for Seniors. A full online archive of its history since 1987 can be accessed by the public at at tnsarchives.com.  Formed in 1987 by our Artistic Director Alvin Tan, The Necessary Stage (TNS) is a non-profit theatre company with charity status. Our mission is to create challenging, indigenous and innovative theatre that touches the heart and mind. TNS is supported by the National Arts Council under the Major Company Scheme for the period from 1 April 2020 to 31 March 2022, and is also the organiser of the annual M1 Singapore Fringe Festival. TNS also has the honour of being the only arts company in Singapore helmed by two Cultural Medallion recipients: Artistic Director Alvin Tan and Resident Playwright Haresh Sharma. Theatre & Dramatic Arts

search_text: THE PHILHARMONIC WINDS (SINGAPORE) LTD. The objects for which the Company is established are : (a) To plan, develop, organise and conduct performance, education, outreach and fund-raising programmes and activities mainly through the medium of wind music. (b) To pursue and promote excellence in wind music, locally and internationally, and arts-related education and public awareness in Singapore by conducting workshops, programmes, concerts and activities. (c) To encourage, nurture and further musical and artistic appreciation, development and education. To provide a platform to build a spirit of philanthropy and volunteerism within the wind music community as well as Singapore’s artistic community. (d) To do all such other things as are incidental or conducive to the attainment of the above objects or any of them provided that nothing shall be done solely for profit. The Philharmonic Winds aims to promote the wind orchestra and its repertoire as a serious and distinctive medium of expression, and also aims to be amongst the finest wind orchestra in the Asia-Pacific region. Artistic Thrusts: To perform high quality wind orchestra literature not commonly performed in Singapore To constantly probe wind orchestra classics with greater depth and thought To continually develop artistically via working with the best international conductors and performing overseas To promote the works of Singapore composers, serving as a resource and platform for them to develop their musical ideas To proactively engage in the creation and performance of new wind music Goals and Objectives: To raise the profile of wind bands and wind musics in Singapore, and to develop a larger audience base for wind music To encourage, participate in and organise creative, experimental and innovative collaborations with local and international artists To reach out to the general public, to educate them and encourage their involvement in the arts To participate and contribute actively to Singapore’s art scene Music & Orchestras

name: THE PURPLE PARADE LTD.
search_text:
THE PURPLE PARADE LTD. People with special needs are part of our community and should have equal access to opportunities and good quality of life.
The Purple Parade Ltd aims to sustain the Purple Parade movement which supports the inclusion and celebrates the abilities of persons with special needs. We are making a movement towards a more inclusive society by opening our eyes to people with Special Needs in our community.
Mission
The Purple Parade Ltd will provide opportunities for the Special Needs Community, supporters and partners to partake in The Purple Parade movement in Singapore.
Vision
Aside from promoting awareness and changing mindsets towards the Special Needs Community in Singapore, the core of The Purple Parade movement is to make sure that people with Special Needs are included in the main chapter of Singapore’s growth and have equal access to opportunities in education, employment, transport, and social networks. Our vision is for the spirit of the movement to continue for many more years to come, and for the Special Needs community to be fully included, valued and celebrated in our country.

name: THE SELECT CENTRE LTD.
search_text:
THE SELECT CENTRE LTD. The Select Centre is a not-for-profit organisation limited by guarantee incorporated in Singapore. We are a recipient of the Singapore National Arts Council's Seed Grant for the the period from 1 April 2015 to 31 March 2018.

Our main objective is to advance and facilitate the interflow of ideas and knowledge among different languages, cultures and disciplines through translation, adaptation and other forms of intercultural practice. We organise a year-round calendar of activities, including readings, lectures, workshops, mentorships, residencies and other development and outreach programmes, with three strategic thrusts:
1. Capacity Development: To develop writers and translators through intercultural and interdisciplinary programmes;
2. Advocacy and Outreach: To promote intercultural practice among various stakeholders, including policy-makers, schools and the community; and
3. Internationalisation: To develop Singapore as a regional hub and gateway of ideas and knowledge to the world.

The values that we hold vital to the organisation are: Curiosity, conviction, innovation, integrity, openness, respect, responsibility, and trust. Vision: To build a world where linguistic and cultural diversity is a powerful and effective driver of human understanding and progress.

Mission: To advance and facilitate the interflow of ideas and knowledge among different languages, cultures and disciplines through translation, adaptation and other forms of intercultural practice. Literary Arts Training & Education

name: THE SILENT FOUNDATION LTD.
search_text:
THE SILENT FOUNDATION LTD. (A) Relief and Assistance to Silent Victims and Sufferers: To help, aid, assist and give relief to the poor and needy persons in Singapore and elsewhere who by reason of ill health, age, misfortune or infirmity are wholly or in part unable to reach out for help or otherwise unable to maintain or help themselves or their dependents by their own exertions; (B) Preservation of Nature and the Environment: To preserve the diversity and abundance of life on Earth and the health of ecological systems by protecting and conserving natural areas, environment, plants and animals (including endangered species), to promote sustainable use of renewable natural resources, more efficient use of energy and resources and reduction of pollution and to advance the education of the public in the conservation, protection and improvement of the physical and natural environment; (C) Care and Protection of Animals: For the benefit of the public, (i) to relieve the suffering or to improve the living conditions and welfare of animals in need of care and attention (including animals in captivity) and, in particular, to provide and maintain rescue homes or other facilities for the reception, care and treatment of such animals; and (ii) to promote humane behaviour towards animals by providing appropriate care, protection, treatment and security for animals which are in need of care and attention by reason of sickness, maltreatment, poor circumstances or ill usage and to educate the public in matters pertaining to animal welfare in general and the prevention of cruelty and suffering among animals; Our definition of silent sufferers include both animals, the environment and human beings as long as they are overlooked and are not getting sufficient assistance from the public at large. As animals and the environment cannot speak for themselves, they never get sufficient care and attention. On the human front, there are also causes which have less commercial value to corporate donors and as a result, are being ignored. The Foundation has identified migrant workers, minority races and women’s rights as the common causes often being overlooked by the society. The Foundation devotes its resources equally to both animals, environment and human causes.

The Foundation provides funding in two ways. Wherever possible, the Foundation tries to achieve maximum impact by engaging in activities that have maximum outreach and multiplier effect. It may fund activities which are involved in advocacy, education, publicity through press, road shows and social media. The Foundation may originate its own programs and/or partner with other charitable organisations to undertake such activities.

The Foundation also allocates part of its fund to provide relief to victims of natural disasters and social abuses. Animal Welfare Environment General Charitable Purposes

name: THE SMILE MISSION LTD.
search_text:
THE SMILE MISSION LTD. I. to research and advance developments and care in the areas of plastic, reconstructive and specialized surgery by such methods as the Directors shall from time to time deem feasible and appropriate for people in necessitous circumstances;

II. to improve training and education programmes for medical doctors and other professionals to expand the base of qualified persons in Singapore;

III. to encourage volunteerism and awareness in Singapore;

IV. to sponsor and/or coordinate special projects to deliver services, health care, and education where necessary, in the areas of plastic, reconstructive and specialized surgery to patients otherwise unable to afford or obtain such services;

V. to encourage or fund relevant research in the field of cleft and craniofacial surgery for the purpose of identifying etiological factors, improving outcomes and preventing disease;

VI. to sponsor and/or coordinate fundraising projects to raise funds for various projects, medical missions and other interests of the Company. These fundraising projects are to include car washes, dinner functions, outings and any other fundraising activities or projects necessary for the raising of funds for the purposes of expanding and developing the interest of the Company;

VII. to develop the necessary organisational structures of volunteers, so that they may provide health care and sustain the Company’s programmes in coming years;

VIII. in the achievement of the Objects:
– the Company shall send volunteer doctors and others to collaborate in the organisation of educational missions and programmes;
– the beneficiaries of instruction and training programmes provided by the Company shall return the benefits they receive by rendering volunteer work for the projects conducted by the Company as approved by the relevant government authorities;
– the acts referred to in Clause 3(3) shall be carried out by the Company through missions organised preferably in local public hospitals;

IX. to strictly adhere to appropriate care standards necessary to the care of patients with craniofacial abnormalities;

X. to take such steps and to do all such things as may from time to time be deemed necessary expedient or desirable to make known the Objects of the Company and to procure and encourage contributions subscriptions donations or otherwise; and

XI. to do all such other things as are incidental or conducive to the attainment of the abovementioned objects or any of them provided that nothing shall be done for commercial reasons or solely for profit and particularly as follows:
– subject to the provisions of Section 23 of the Companies Act (Chapter 50) or any statutory modification thereof for the time being being in force, to purchase, take on lease, hire or otherwise acquire any land then the Company shall obtain such license (if any) as may be required by the law;
– with the approval of at least a majority of the members and in accordance with the Articles of Association, to sell, manage, lease, dispose of or otherwise deal with all or any part of the property of the Company for the purposes of the Company;
– with prior approval of at least a majority of the members and in accordance with the Articles of Association, to raise money for the objects and purposes of the Company in such manner as the Company may think fit;
– to invest the monies of the Company not immediately required for its purposes in or upon such investment, securities or property as may be thought fit, subject nevertheless to such conditions (if any) and such consents (if any) as may for the time being be imposed or required by law and to appoint from time to time professional fund managers for the purposes of managing and investing the monies of the Company;
– to construct, maintain, alter and upkeep any house, building, or works necessary or convenient for the purposes of the Company;
– to accept subscriptions, donations (whether of real or personal estate) and demises and bequests and collaborating with charity and/or charitable medical organisations in fund-raising exercises;
– to invite, issue, appeal for, and solicit subscriptions, benefactions, donations, and bequests to or for the Company for the purposes of the Company;
– to establish and maintain such banking account or accounts as it thinks fit into such of which as may be appropriate shall be paid forthwith all monies for the time being belonging to the Company; and
– to pay all costs, charges and expenses incurred or sustained in or about the promotion and establishment of the Company and to remunerate any person or persons for services rendered in the promotion or establishment of the Company. VISION
Raise standards of medical care by creating opportunities for collaborative learning through the delivery of free surgery to children with facial deformities.

MISSION
- Initiate platforms of learning through medical volunteering
- Deliver free surgical care to children with facial deformities
- Establish training & education programmes for volunteers
- Build a knowledge depository with an open-source model
- Instil a sense of volunteerism in the community Others; -

name: THE SOPHIA FOUNDATION LIMITED
search_text: THE SOPHIA FOUNDATION LIMITED (a)	The relief in any manner which the Foundation may deem suitable of persons falling within any of the following categories:    (i)	The poor and needy who by reason of age, ill-health, misfortune or infirmity are wholly or in part unable to maintain themselves and their dependents by their own exertions and in particular widows and orphans.   (ii)	Those in need of moral or social rehabilitation or welfare including prostitutes, juvenile delinquents, prisoners and ex-convicts.    (iii)	Victims of fire, flood, famine, war, pestilence or other calamity. Provided always that the person who is to be the object of such relief is, in the opinion of the Board (which shall be conclusive) a suitable and worthy object of such relief or appears capable of deriving permanent benefit therefrom, bearing in mind that the object of the Foundation is the encouragement of self-help and the rehabilitation of those in temporary or inevitable distress and not the encouragement of idleness or vice.	(b)	The relief of human suffering and improvement of the standard of human life by the combating, treatment and prevention of sickness and disease, and in particular by providing for, or contributing towards medical and scientific research and teaching, and the provision of medical and surgical facilities and services of all kinds.    (c)	The advancement and improvement of the standard of human life and social well-being by      (i)	Fostering, developing and improving education of all kinds, and in any such manner as may, from time to time, be decided upon by the Foundation including but without prejudice to the generality of this provision the founding and maintenance of Scholarships and Chairs at Schools, Colleges and Universities.    (ii)	Organising and carrying out and supporting schemes for the improvement of housing conditions amongst the lower income groups, with particular reference to the provision of flats and houses to be let to the working classes at low, non-profit rents.   (iii)	Providing playing fields and recreation grounds for the public, and fostering and developing by such means as may, from time to time, be determined upon, all health, sporting and recreational activities.   (d)	To aid, assist and give relief in any manner and to any extent including the whole or any part of the assets for the time being of the Foundation to any charitable institution whose object or objects are similar or comparable with the objects of the Foundation including hospitals, sanatoria, homes or places of refuge or shelter, schools, orphanages, industrial welfare establishments and other charitable organisations.	Provided always that notwithstanding anything heretofore contained no grant or assistance shall be given which shall be in aid of any political organisation.	 (e)	The advancement of citizenship or community development, including without limitation, the undertaking of economic development projects.    (f)	To carry out the objects of the Foundation for the benefit of members of all races resident in the Republic of Singapore and the rest of South-East Asia. We aspire to reduce the suffering of elderly persons of limited means, and to that extent, to encourage medical research or related charities / institutions that will contribute to this cause, particularly in Singapore.     We aim to reduce poverty through enabling financial economic independence. We will do this by supporting educational and sustainable economic development projects in lesser developed regions of South-East Asia and assisting charitable / non-profit institutions in Singapore that are believed to make exceptional contributions to society and disaster relief projects. name: THE TAPESTRY PROJECT LIMITED
search_text:
THE TAPESTRY PROJECT LIMITED Our Objectives:
(a) To make mental health conditions less abstract and more relatable to the general
public so as to encourage empathy and early intervention.
(b) To connect with, educate and empower the general public through first-person
narratives.
(c) To raise awareness on issues relating to delayed diagnosis and treatment of mental
health conditions.
(d) To restore hope and dignity to individuals and families that have been affected by
mental health conditions.
(e) Other related charitable activities.

About The “Tapestry” Metaphor

A tapestry is a form of textile art often depicting narratives of people and places.

Like a tapestry, mental health and wellbeing are multifaceted and complex, influenced by various interconnected factors. Just as a tapestry is created by weaving together different threads, mental health and wellbeing are shaped by a combination of physical, emotional, psychological, social, and environmental aspects.

Each thread in a tapestry represents a person’s unique and personal journey with mental health. By sharing our stories, we not only dignify the existence of our journeys, we also create strong bonds that weave together new meaning for our diverse experiences. This in turn supports and strengthens those around, creating a tapestry that represents hope and inclusion. Our Vision
A stronger social fabric woven together by hope, kindness and dignity.

Our Mission
To knit communities together to strengthen capabilities for better mental well-being.

Our Purpose
We interweave diverse perspectives to increase mental health literacy, bring awareness to community resources and promote early help-seeking.

Our Work
We boost access to mental health support in the community by publishing local mental health stories and we facilitate narrative programmes that strengthen personal agency, identity and social connection. Mental Health

Our Vision: Connecting All with Divine Hearts
The vision of our temple is a special wish for all students and devotees to cultivate the divine heart of Lord Buddha. This means to practise and put into action the Buddhadharma with an open heart so as to become fully enlightened for the benefit of all sentient beings.

Our Mission: Empowering all to Have Meaningful Lives
As aspiring Bodhisattvas, we strive to reach out and empower everyone with love, compassion and wisdom, which are the essence of Buddha’s teachings. Therefore, our activities include:

1. Spreading the Buddha’s teachings and preserving rich Buddhist tradition, such as its culture and architecture;

name: TOA PAYOH CHINESE METHODIST CHURCH
search_text: TOA PAYOH CHINESE METHODIST CHURCH The objectives of the Church are to propagate and promote the christian faith, through church and evangelistic meetings, rallies, literature and music so as to spread the Gospel of Jesus Christ and to assist in the extension of His Kingdom. A Local church is a community of all true believers under the Lordship of Christ. It is the redemptive fellowship in which the Word of God is preached by persons divinely called and the Sacraments are duly administered according to Christ's own appointment.  Under the discipline of the Holy Spirit, the Church exists for the maintenance of worship, the edification of believers, and the redemption of the world.  The Church of Jesus Christ exists in and for the world. It is primarily at the level of the local church that the church encounters the world located in nearly every community.  The Local church is a strategic base from which Christian move out to the structures of society.  The function of the local church, under the guidance of the Holy Spirit, is to help people to personally know Jesus Christ and to live their daily lives in light of their relationship with God.	Therefore,  the local church is to minister to persons in the  community where the local church is located, to provide appropriate training and nurture to all, to cooperate in ministry with other local churches, to defend God s creation and lives as an ecologically responsible community, and to participate in the worldwide mission of the Church, as minimal expectations of and authentic church.  The local church is a connection society of persons who have professed their faith in Christ, have been baptized, have assumed the vows of membership in The Methodist Church, and are associate in fellowship as a local Methodist Church  in order that they may hear the Word of God, receive the Sacraments, praise and worship the triune God, and carry forward the work which Christ has committed to His Church.  Such a society of believers, being within The Methodist Church and subject to its Discipline, is composed of all who accept Jesus Christ as Lord and Saviour, and which in the Apostles  Creed we declare to be the holy catholic church.  Each local church shall have definite evangelistic, nurture, and witness responsibility.  It shall be responsible for its present members, and for persons who choose it as their church. Christianity

name: TRADITIONAL ARTS CENTRE (SINGAPORE) LTD.
search_text:
TRADITIONAL ARTS CENTRE (SINGAPORE) LTD. Kindly please refer to the attached Constitution.
The Traditional Arts Centre (TAC) was established in 2012. In 2014, TAC received the Seed Grant from the Singapore National Arts Council (NAC). In our continuous effort to further improve our organisational capabilities, in 2016 we successfully made the transition into a professional arts group. And in 2017, TAC was promoted from a recipient of NAC’s Seed Grant to a Major Company Scheme. This was a great honour bestows upon us by the NAC in recognition of our work done in the field of traditional arts and had given us new impetus to drive our mission even further. TAC was established by Ms Cai Bi Xia, a recipient of the NAC 2008 Youth Award, as a non-profit arts educational organization devoted to promote, train and improve the standards of Chinese Opera in Singapore. We actively participate in cultural exchange overseas, as a means to an end in creating a unique Singapore artistic identity.

The TAC has tasked itself with the role of promoting and developing Chinese opera in Singapore; training new opera practitioners, especially in the development of young talents. Our team of passionate and committed instructors has to date taught in more than a hundred primary and secondary schools all over Singapore. Our school programmes include conducting classes, staging performances, engaged in the transmission of Chinese opera knowledge, and conducting numerous multi-faceted talks and workshops on Chinese opera. These creative and value added programmes had been well received by local educational establishment. Besides continuing to transmit Chinese core cultural values and preserving the unique art of Chinese opera, the center had created new operatic works with local flavours, as well as injecting new elements in order to attract new audience. We hope with these innovative efforts we can reach out to a wider audience and attract them to the wonderful world of Chinese opera.

The TAC is committed to constantly develop new platforms for young Chinese opera talents, develop new audience, and actively work with world renowned institutes to establish high quality programmes in Chinese opera training and research. We strive to stage quality programmes like the “Singapore Youth Chinese Opera Showcase”, and other large scale Chinese opera activities. We will continue to organise international academic conferences on Chinese opera thereby synergizing the strengths of academics from Singapore and abroad. This will increase international cooperation in the cultural arenas, as well as opening up new frontiers for Chinese opera. Its Vision is to create a four-dimensioal art organization that combines teaching ,research , performance , and international exchange .
Traditional Ethnic Performing Arts Training & Education

name: TRANSIENT WORKERS COUNT TOO (TWC2)
search_text:
TRANSIENT WORKERS COUNT TOO (TWC2) Transient Workers Count Too (TWC2) promotes fair treatment for migrant workers. We envision a society that recognizes and values the important contribution they make to our households, economy and country. All labour is dignified and should be treated with respect and consideration. Ethnicity, colour, gender, language, religion or class are irrelevant no worker should be subjected to inhumane or degrading treatment. Employers should conduct their business honourably, responsibly and compensate their staff promptly and fairly. Through ground research and engagement with policy makers and employers, we at TWC2 advocate a more enlightened policy framework for migrant labour in Singapore. We shall extend assistance to workers in need to ensure that they have fair resolution of their cases, dignity in work and living conditions, access to medical care, and protection of their rightful autonomy. By engaging in public education, we shall promote the social conditions in which exploitation, abuse and injustice become history. The mission of TWC2 is:
1) through ground research and engagement with policy makers and employers, to advocate a more enlightened policy framework for migrant labour in Singapore;
2) to extend assistance to workers in need to ensure that they have fair resolution of their cases, dignity in work and living conditions, access to medical care, and protection of their
rightful autonomy; and
3) through public education, to promote the social conditions in which exploitation, abuse and injustice become history. Community

name: TRIATHLON ASSOCIATION OF SINGAPORE
search_text:
TRIATHLON ASSOCIATION OF SINGAPORE Triathlon Association of Singapore (TAS) is the National Governing Body for the sport of Triathlon, Duathlon, Aquathlon and its related multi-sport in Singapore. We're a Not-for-Profit Organization registered with the Registry of Societies (ROS) on 10 September 1992. TAS is gazetted as a National Sports Association for the Sport of Triathlon in Singapore. TAS obtained it's Charity Status since 1 June 2011.

Our Objectives are:

1) Promote, develop and increase participation for the sport of Triathlon in Singapore.

2) Promote physical activity for health and wellness, foster community engagement and bonding for social inclusiveness and integration, and inspire the Singapore Spirit through the sport of Triathlon.

3) To engage communities and provide Triathlon programs access to vulnerable segments of the community such as youth at risk and the less privileged.

4) Unify, co-ordinate, sanction and organise Triathlon activities in Singapore, including national and international Triathlon tournaments and events.

5) Raise the competitive standards of Triathlon athletes in Singapore for sustainable elite level performance at international competitions and multi-sport major games.

6) To develop, select, support and manage Triathlon athletes at these international competitions and major games.

7) Provide sport pathways and opportunities for the progression and advancement of Triathlon athletes, coaches and technical officials in Singapore.

8) Raise the technical capability of Triathlon coaches and technical officials in Singapore.

9) Develop and enforce rules and regulations, and coordinate technical information covering all aspects of Triathlon in Singapore

10) To exercise complete jurisdiction of the sport of Triathlon in Singapore, over all matters pertaining to participation, including programmes and events. This is to ensure safety, fair and evenness of the conduct of Triathlon in Singapore.

11) Do all things complementary or incidental to attain the aforesaid objects above.
Vision
To be an internationally acclaimed nation who makes multi-sports a lifestyle that's leads to excellence.

Mission
TAS strives to exercise fair and judicious supervision of multi-sports events in Singapore and internationally and to promote multi-sports among all and achieve excellence for international recognition. name: TRINITY ANNUAL CONFERENCE
search_text:
TRINITY ANNUAL CONFERENCE The purpose of Trinity Annual Conference is to make disciples for Jesus Christ by equipping its local churches for ministry and by providing a connection for ministry beyond the local churches. TRINITY ANNUAL CONFERENCE
OUR VISION

On TRAC Together with GOD:
Word, Worship, Welcome, Witness and Wonder

Our Vision of the Trinity Annual Conference (TRAC) for the Quadrennium (2021-2024) Our Core Values

Disciples of Christ are committed to 5 Ws :

- the Word of God: disciples learn and share the wisdom of the Bible with each other

- the Worship of God: disciples desire and encourage communion (relationship) with God through personal and corporate prayer

- the Welcome of God: disciples receive and share God's warmth and welfare

- the Witness of God: disciples accept differing roles in being positive witnesses to God's love and good news for the whole world
- the Wonder of God: disciples believe in God's wonderful acts of physical, emotional and spiritual healing through both prayers and physicians

(Adopted at TRAC 37th Special Session 2013).

Objective

1. The purpose of the Annual Conference is to make disciples for Jesus Christ by equipping its local churches for ministry and by providing a connection for ministry beyond the local church; all to the glory of God.

2. The purpose of the Executive Board of the Annual Conference, as part of the total mission of the Church, is to develop quadrennial and/or annual plans and facilitate the programme for the development, administration, and evaluation of the programme of the Annual Conference and to encourage, coordinate, and support the conference boards, districts, and local churches in their ministry and various programmes in accordance with the mission of the church.

Policies

1. Finances: The Board of Finance (BOF), elected at the Annual Conference, prepare the annual budget for presentation and acceptance by the Annual Conference. The BOF secures full information regarding all conference needs and programmes, presented to it by programme boards and organisations, including support for the President of the Annual Conference (including salary, housing, travel, and office expenses, where applicable). The BOF then recommends to the Annual Conference for action and determination of the apportionment by local churches of the Conference.

2. Programmes: The programmes of the Annual Conference are carried out by the various programme boards, namely, the Board of Discipleship and Nurture, the Board of Missions, the Board of Outreach and Social Concerns, the Board of Witness and Evangelism, the Board of Worship and Music, the Board of Youth Ministry, the Board of Children Ministry, the Board of Senior Ministry.
