name: TRUST FUND OF THE CLEFT LIP AND PALATE ASSN OF SINGAPORE (CLAPAS), THE
search_text:
TRUST FUND OF THE CLEFT LIP AND PALATE ASSN OF SINGAPORE (CLAPAS), THE The objective of the Trust Fund of the Cleft Lip & Palate Association of Singapore is:
1. Promoting the interests of and improvement of the medical care of cleft lip/palate and craniofacial patients of Singapore
2. Promoting the education, research, promotional activities of the CLAPAS Parents Support Group, volunteers and the general public and their knowledge and education of cleft lip and palate patients of Singapore
3. Encouraging research in cleft lip/palate and craniofacial patients for the better understanding of its causation and outcomes
4. Providing for the establishment and endowment of scholarships, fellowships, travel grants, exhibitions and prizes for members of the medical and allied professions. Vision Statement
To become the leading organization in Singapore for advancing the medical care, education, and research of cleft lip/palate and craniofacial conditions. We envision a future where all patients receive the highest standard of care, families are empowered and informed, and continuous research leads to groundbreaking discoveries in understanding and treating these conditions.

Mission Statement:
To enhance the quality of life for cleft lip/palate and craniofacial patients in Singapore through the promotion of superior medical care, comprehensive education, and impactful research. We are dedicated to supporting the CLAPAS Parents Support Group, volunteers, and the general public by fostering awareness, providing educational resources, and facilitating community engagement. name: TSAO FOUNDATION
search_text: TSAO FOUNDATION 1.	The objects for which the Foundation is established are :   (1)	To contribute for charitable purposes towards the establishment and maintenance of medical services to provide good quality medical treatment and care for the benefit of all persons in particular the poor, needy, aged, handicapped and demented.  (2)	The relief of human suffering and improvement of human living conditions and standards by the treatment, prevention, alleviation and combating of sickness and disease and in particular by providing for or contributing towards medical research and teaching, and by the provision of medical, surgical and educational facilities and services of all kinds.  (3)	The relief in any manner whatsoever which the Foundation may in its absolute discretion deem suitable including but not limited to the relief of poverty, suffering and distress and the provision of financial assistance, among persons falling within any of the following categories :   (a) The poor, needy, aged, handicapped and demented, who by reason of age, ill-health, misfortune, infirmity or otherwise are wholly or in part unable to maintain themselves and their dependants by their own exertions;  (b)	Those in need of moral or social rehabilitation or welfare including prostitutes, juvenile delinquents, drug-addicts, prisoners and ex-convicts;   And victims of fire, flood, famine, war, pestilence or other calamity Provided always that the person who is to be the object of such relief is, in the opinion of the Board of Directors of the Foundation (hereinafter referred to as the Board)(which shall be conclusive) a suitable and worthy object of such relief or appears capable of deriving permanent benefit there from, bearing in mind that the Foundation seeks to encourage self-help and rehabilitation among those in temporary or permanent distress and not idleness or vice Vision - Our vision is to foster a society for all ages that supports active ageing and values the contributions of older people.    We foster this vision by advancing services, skills and knowledge of individuals and communities in eldercare; by facilitating older people's participation in society; and promoting their views for policy planning.    Mission	We are dedicated to enhancing the quality of life for older people by alleviating the hardships of ageing through our community health services; promoting successful ageing; and pioneering new approaches to ageing and eldercare throughout Singapore and the region.    We aim to be a catalyst for constructive change by addressing pouplation ageing and eldercare issues at the policy level	We actively pursue our mission by focusing our work on these three pillars off eldercare: Service, Education and Collaboration. name: TT FOUNDATION ADVISORS SINGAPORE LTD.
search_text:
TT FOUNDATION ADVISORS SINGAPORE LTD. To provide philanthropy advisory and management services to philanthropic foundations, family offices, business corporations, philanthropists, and charities in support of:
a) relief of poverty;
b) advancement of education;
c) other purposes beneficial to the community, including but not limited to:
(i) promotion of health;
(ii) advancement of citizenship or community development;
(iii) advancement of arts, heritage or science;
(iv) advancement of environmental protection or improvement;
(v) relief of those in need by reason of youth, age, ill-health, disability, financial hardship or other disadvantages;
(vi) advancement of animal welfare; and
(vii) advancement of sport, where the sport promotes health through physical skill and exertion
TT Foundation Advisors Singapore Ltd seeks to become Asia’s one-stop provider of philanthropy advisory and management services. Its target audience includes includes individual philanthropists, private foundations, family offices, and corporate foundations, by offering:

Expert philanthropy advisory services, addressing every stage of philanthropy from strategy formulation and programme curation, to innovative financing and impact management.

Professional philanthropy management services, serving foundations’ holistic needs including governance, endowment management, and grantmaking execution – to preserve their reputations and deliver impact in the communities they serve.

Access to partners and platforms across the Temasek Trust ecosystem, allowing foundations to leverage innovative and catalytic platforms, our global community of impact partners, and other best-in-class and evidence-based programmes across the globe.
General Charitable Purposes

name: TT IPC LTD.
search_text:
TT IPC LTD. The Company’s aim is to build social and economic resilience that underpins peace and progress in Singapore by pursuing the following charitable objects:

a) To invest in people in Singapore through education, research and training, particularly in the next generation in Singapore to increase their knowledge and build their capabilities, skills and values so that they can contribute in turn to their communities as productive and responsible members of their community;

b) To promote the advancement of good citizenship and for community development, including facilitating organising and promoting events, training and activities in furtherance of this objective;

c) To advance education in Singapore, including facilitating organising and promoting events, training and activities in furtherance of this objective;

d) To provide relief of those in need by reason of youth, age, ill-health, disability, financial hardship or other disadvantages, including providing relief in connection with post disaster recovery or pandemics;

(a) Healthcare services to patients determined to be in need of financial assistance;

(b) Programmes and additional sponsorship for the furtherance of medical, nursing, paramedical, allied healthcare personnel and other health-care related education and training programmes for healthcare workers;

(c) Programmes to improve patient-care services and patient safety through pilot projects and quality improvements initiative;

(d) Community care and outreach programmes and enhancement of care integration initiatives;

Adding Years to Healthy Life

name: The Life Church and Missions
search_text:
The Life Church and Missions The objective of The Life Church and Missions is to preach, practise and spread everywhere the teachings of the Bible with the Salvation of Jesus Christ for those who believe in Him as its most basic teaching (Article 3.1 of Constitution). STATEMENT OF FAITH
The sole basis of our beliefs is the Bible, God's infallible written Word, the sixty-six books of the Old and New Testaments. We believe that it was uniquely, verbally and fully inspired by the Holy Spirit, and that it was written without error in the original manuscripts. It is the supreme and final authority in all matters on which it speaks.
We accept those large areas of doctrinal teaching on which, historically, there has been general agreement among all true Christians. Because of the specialized calling of our movement, we desire to allow for freedom of conviction on other doctrinal matters, provided that any interpretation is based upon the Bible alone, and that no such interpretation shall become an issue which hinders the ministry to which God has called us.
We explicitly affirm our belief in basic Bible teachings, as follows:
1. There is one true God, eternally existing in three persons -Father, Son and Holy Spirit -each of whom possesses equally all the attributes of Deity and the characteristics of personality.
2. Jesus Christ is God, the living Word, who became flesh through His miraculous conception by the Holy Spirit and His virgin birth. Hence, He is perfect Deity and true humanity united in one person forever.
3. He lived a sinless life and voluntarily atoned for the sins of men by dying on the cross as their substitute, thus satisfying divine justice and accomplishing salvation for all who trust in Him alone.
4. He rose from the dead in the same body, though glorified, in which He lived and died.
5. He ascended bodily into heaven and sat down at the right hand of God the Father, where He, the only mediator between God and man, continually makes intercession for His own.
6. Man was originally created in the image of God. He sinned by disobeying God; thus, he was alienated from His creator. That historic fall brought all mankind under divine condemnation.
7. Man's nature is corrupted, and he is thus totally unable to please God. Every man is in need of regeneration and renewal by the Holy Spirit.
8. The salvation of man is wholly a work of God's free grace and is not the work, in whole or in part, of human works or goodness or religious ceremony. God imputes His righteousness to those who put their faith in Christ alone for their salvation, and thereby justifies them in His sight.
9. It is the privilege of all who are born again of the Spirit to be assured of their salvation from the very moment in which they trust Christ as their Savior. This assurance is not based upon any kind of human merit, but is produced by the witness of the Holy Spirit, who confirms in the believer the testimony of God in His written Word.
10. The Holy Spirit has come into the world to reveal and glorify Christ and to apply the saving work of Christ to men. He convicts and draws sinners to Christ imparts new life to them, continually indwells them from the moment of spiritual birth and seals them until the day of redemption. His fullness, power and control are appropriated in the believer's life by faith.
11. Every believer is called to live so in the power of the indwelling Spirit that he will not fulfill the lust of the flesh but will bear fruit to the glory of God.
12. Jesus Christ is the Head of the Church. His Body, which is composed of all men, living and dead, who have been joined to Him through saving faith.
13. God admonishes His people to assemble together regularly for worship, for participation in ordinances, for edification through the Scriptures and for mutual encouragement.
14. At physical death the believer enters immediately into eternal, conscious fellowship with the Lord and awaits the resurrection of his body to everlasting glory and blessing.
15. At physical death the unbeliever enters immediately into eternal, conscious separation from the Lord and awaits the resurrection of his body to everlasting judgment and condemnation.
16. Jesus Christ will come again to the earth -personally, visibly and bodily –to consummate history and the eternal plan of God.
17. The Lord Jesus Christ commanded all believers to proclaim the gospel throughout the world and to disciple men of every nation. The fulfillment of that Great Commission requires that all worldly and personal ambitions be subordinated to a total commitment to "Him who loved us and gave Himself for us". Christianity

name: The Stephen A. Schwarzman Scholars Trust
search_text:
The Stephen A. Schwarzman Scholars Trust The Trust plans to raise funds in Singapore and to fund scholarships allowing for Singapore students to attend the Schwarzman Scholars Program (the “Program”) at Tsinghua University. The Program, which is similar to the Rhodes, Gates and Fulbright scholarships, is a one-year graduate program. It has been created by the Stephen A. Schwarzman Education Foundation (the "Foundation") has, in collaboration with Tsinghua University in Beijing, China.

The Foundation will administer and fund scholarships to deserving candidates from around the world to participate in the Program.

The funds raised by the Trust will be used to sponsor scholarships for one to two Singapore students per year.
Leadership for a cross-cultural world

American and European interest dominated the 20th Century. But China's emergence as a global political and economic power center is changing the dynamics of the world.

It will be up to a new generation to overcome differences and lead with confidence and sensitivity. It will require a broader knowledge of the policies, economies and cultures that are shifting the balance. And, more than ever, it will require deeper personal experience, exposure and relationships.

Tomorrow's leaders must not simply shed old biases. They must also have the understanding to see opportunities for mutual advancement, and to strike a healthy balance between cooperation and competition.

Schwarzman Scholars is creating a historic new path for the best and brightest in politics, business, technology and the social sciences to guide us towards greater prosperity and progress in the future.

(c) provide instructions and teaching from the Bible to equip its members for ministry and service;

Having true unity by embracing diversity in Christ.

(a) relief of poverty,

(d) such other charitable purposes which help and benefit the community not falling into any of the preceding heads, namely:
(i) the advancement of health;
(ii) the advancement of citizenship or community development;
(iii) the advancement of arts or heritage or science or education;
(iv) the advancement of environmental protection or improvement;
(v) the relief of those in need by reason of youth, age, ill-health, disability, financial hardship or other disadvantages; and
(vi) the advancement of animal welfare

in any country, region or area of the World.
 
We envision a world where those in need and the marginalised will be empowered to improve and sustain their living situations thus providing opportunities to contribute to society and positively impacting the future.

name: UNLOCKING ADHD LTD.
search_text:
UNLOCKING ADHD LTD. The Company Objectives are to:

(i) Promote ADHD Literacy and build a supportive community and ecosystem for ADHDers and their families in Singapore and

(ii) Provide access to practical information and support to the ADHD community and develop tools to equip ADHDers with knowledge, skills, and strategies to identify their strengths and unlock their full potential.

In furtherance of the above objects but not otherwise, and provided that nothing is done for commercial reasons or solely for profit, the Company may exercise the following powers:

(i) Improve support systems for the ADHD community through engaging with relevant stakeholders (including government ministries, granting organizations, schools, professionals, corporations and the general public) about the nature, incidence and impact of ADHD; and

(ii) Carry out any other activities to achieve the Mission and the charitable objectives of the Company
The Mission Statement of the Company is to empower individuals with Attention Deficit/Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD) and their families to live life to the fullest.

Many individuals with ADHD (collectively, “ADHDers”) struggle with symptoms causing impaired relationships and underperformance in work and life leading to lost potential. The Company enables ADHDers and their families to thrive by providing greater Awareness, Accessibility, Achievement, and Advocacy through the Objectives
Children/Youth Community Disability (Adult) Disability (Children) Family Support Groups

(a) to establish and operate a university.

(b) to engage in activities relating to teaching, research and the advancement of education including undertaking consultancy, production and dissemination of educational materials and research findings.

(c) to confer and award degrees, diplomas and certificates, including honorary degrees and other distinctions.

(d) to take actions designed to ensure the health, safety and welfare of faculty, staff and students.

(e) to award scholarships to Singapore Citizens and/or, Permanent Residents to pursue Undergraduate and Graduate studies in Hospitality offered by the University of Las Vegas Nevada, USA by attendance in person on campus, online or, via other approved modes of instructions.

(f) to do such other things which are incidental or conducive to the attainment of all or any of the above objectives .
 

UNLV Singapore intends to enhance and promote the development of the tourism and hospitality industry in Singapore by developing and enhancing the knowledge and exposure of industry professionals through training and education.

To achieve its Vision and Mission, UNLV Singapore will provide scholarships to Singapore Citizens and Singapore Permanent Residents to undertake and obtain a Bachelor’s Degree and/or, a Masters Degree in Hospitality at the University of Las Vegas, Nevada, USA. UNLV Singapore will also provide scholarships for executive education offered through the Sands Center, which is located in the Harrah College of Hospitality at UNLV.

name: UPLIFTERS (SINGAPORE) LIMITED
search_text:
UPLIFTERS (SINGAPORE) LIMITED (1) to assist migrant workers (and other poor and socially distressed persons in Singapore and other countries) in need in respect of financial literacy, life skills and vocational education;
(2) to relieve poverty and social distress among the poor in Singapore and other countries through enhancing their financial literacy, life skills and vocational competitiveness;
(3) to promote social harmony and inclusiveness for the benefit of the Singapore community by facilitating communication, knowledge sharing and skill training between migrant workers (and other poor and socially distressed participants of the Company’s educational programmes) and their employers and families;
(4) to provide a platform for interaction between underprivileged communities and the wider society with a view to furthering the objects of the Company but not otherwise;
(5) to organize educational, cultural and social welfare events for public participation with a view to furthering the objects of the company but not otherwise;
(6) to establish, co-operate with and maintain relationships with other charitable institutions, corporations, associations, organizations, societies, clubs or persons with a view to furthering the objects of the Company but not otherwise; and
(7) to do all such other things as are incidental or conducive to the attainment of the above Objects or any of them, provided that nothing shall be done for commercial reasons or solely for profit.
Our Vision
A world where every person—regardless of background or circumstance—can shape and uplift their lives.

Our Mission
Our mission is to enable migrant domestic workers in Asia and the Middle East to achieve successful migration outcomes for themselves and their families by combining accessible online education with community support through innovative and scalable solutions.

name: United Hebrew Congregation (Singapore)
search_text:
United Hebrew Congregation (Singapore) Its objectives are:-
a) To provide, support and maintain a means of worship for Jewish people and their families according to the practices of, but not limited to, Reform, Progressive, Liberal, Reconstructionist and/or Conservative Judaism, but generally non-Orthodox Judaism ;
b) To stimulate, encourage and assist Jews and their families in the study and learning of all aspects of Judaism;
c) To stimulate, encourage and assist Jews and their families in all other aspects of living a Jewish life, including such aspects as celebrating Jewish holidays and developing fellowship among Jews and their families;
d) To maintain or assist in the maintenance of a place of worship and the services of a Rabbi and/or other synagogue personnel;
e) To undertake other religious, cultural, social, educational, recreational and charitable activities for the purposes of promoting and advancing the beliefs and practices of Judaism.
f) To do all such other things as are conducive or incidental to the
attainment of the above objects. The Association may raise
subscriptions, contributions and/or donations for or towards the purpose of carrying out all or any of the above objects.
Vision:
To be a vibrant and inclusive center of Jewish life in Singapore and the region, where individuals of diverse backgrounds come together to explore, celebrate, and embrace progressive Jewish values and traditions.



Mission:
We provide a welcoming and supportive environment for Jews of all backgrounds to worship, learn, and engage with Judaism. Through dynamic educational programs, we stimulate curiosity and foster deep understanding of Jewish heritage. We promote active participation in Jewish life, encouraging meaningful observance of holidays and fostering fellowship among our community members. We maintain a sacred space for worship and offer the services of knowledgeable leaders. Additionally, we organize diverse religious, cultural, social, educational, recreational, and charitable activities to promote and enrich Jewish life. Others; Jewish

name: VARIETY - THE CHILDREN'S CHARITY OF SINGAPORE LIMITED
search_text:
VARIETY - THE CHILDREN'S CHARITY OF SINGAPORE LIMITED The objects for which the Company is established are:-

(a) To raise funds to wholly or substantially aid children of Singapore who are in need, regardless of any race, colour or creed.

(b) For the purpose of carrying out the object in clause 4 (a) above:-

(i) To raise funds to further the aims of the Company and to secure sufficient funds for the purposes of the Company.

(ii) To receive any funds and to distribute these funds in a manner that best attains the objects of the Company.

(iii) To enter into any arrangements with any ministry or authority in Singapore that may be conducive to the Company’s objects and to maintain from any such ministry or authority, any rights privileges and concessions which may be desirable to obtain and to exercise and comply with any such arrangements, rights, privileges and concessions.

(iv) To act as a trustee of any trust which has been established for charitable purposes.

(v) To subscribe or guarantee money for charitable or benevolent objects.

(vi) To make donations for charitable purposes for the benefit of children residing within Singapore or to children of Singapore citizens and permanent residents situated outside Singapore.

(vii) To make donations, in so far as the same is permitted by law, for charitable purposes for the benefit of children in other countries provided that such donations do not at any time exceed 30% of the nett tangible asset of the Company.

(c) To do all such things as are incidental or conducive to the attainment of all or any of the objects of the Company.
Vision

“To improve the lives of the children of Singapore who are sick, disadvantaged or have special needs, empowering them to live, laugh and learn.”

 
Mission Statement

“Driven by generous individual and corporate supporters, respected partners and a team of passionate volunteers, Variety offers freedom, care and a future to children of Singapore who are sick, disadvantaged or have special needs.

Freedom through equipment and services providing mobility, care through the provision of medical assistance and family support, and future by supporting children in educational, sporting and artistic endeavours and experiences”

name: WHOLETREE LIMITED
search_text:
WHOLETREE LIMITED The objects of the company are to identify, initiate, partner worthwhile social innovation projects to effect positive transformations in areas of youth mental wellness and personal wellbeing including health and education primarily in Singapore and potentially globally as well. The company is exclusively charitable and may raise funds, conduct seminars and events and carry out all other legally permitted activities to achieve its objects. We envision a world where mental wellness is a top life priority, and where every young person is given the opportunity to realize their life potential via the journey of self-discovery, healing and transformation, to attain true happiness and optimal wellbeing.

Our vision in mental wellness begins with good self-care habits that is holistic and transformative — complemented by a community-based model of care with trained wellbeing champions leading the way, whether at home, in the school, out in the community or in the workplace—supplemented by the traditional face-to-face counselling and psychotherapy for those who need acute mental health care. 

We aspire to connect all the dots in the mental health landscape by taking an ecosystem approach to the grand challenge that is mental health - and where prevention and preemption takes precedence over clinical intervention. Mental Health

By producing and touring productions that are distinctively local in flavour and yet universal in vision and concerns, the company creates memorable experiences for audiences in Singapore and the world.

WILD RICE's mission is to provide an open forum for the shared experience of theatre; celebrating our diversity, reflecting on the problems and possibilities of our times, and presenting productions that inspire, challenge and entertain. Theatre & Dramatic Arts

name: WOMEN OF RESILIENCE LTD.
search_text:
WOMEN OF RESILIENCE LTD. Objective: To enhance the lives of single mothers and women by providing them with the necessary resources and support to achieve self-sufficiency and well-being.

Specific Objectives:

Education and Skill Development:

Provide access to vocational training and educational programs for single mothers and women.
Offer literacy and numeracy programs to improve basic skills.
Provide mentorship and career guidance to assist women in achieving their career goals.
Economic Empowerment:

Establish microfinance programs to support women in starting their own businesses.
Offer business training and management skills development workshops.
Create job placement opportunities for single mothers and women.
Holistic Support:

Provide counseling and psychological support services to address the emotional and mental well-being of single mothers and women.
Offer healthcare and wellness programs, including reproductive health services.
Establish support groups for single mothers to foster a sense of community and shared experiences.
Advocacy and Awareness:

Advocate for the rights and needs of single mothers and women through policy advocacy and public awareness campaigns.
Collaborate with government agencies and other organizations to address the challenges faced by single mothers and women.

By achieving these objectives, Wondrously Woman Gem Limited aims to empower single mothers and women to become self-reliant, contribute to their communities, and break the cycle of poverty. Vision: A community of successful individual with pride and dignity to achieve it's goals.

name: X-Pact Society
search_text:
X-Pact Society a. To train and equip Christians to carry out Christ's mission. b. To provide assistance in any form to any Christian worker, missions organization and churches of like faith; c. To promote or participate in promoting the welfare of mankind on Christian, charitable and benevolent principles; d. To challenge and mobilize like-minded Christians and churches for prayer and service; and e. to teach and preach the truth of God as revealed in His Word the Bible. f. In furtherance of the above objects, the Society may work in partnership with other organizations with similar vision. Vision of X-Pact Society:
X-Pact is a mission organisation which embraces the call of our Lord Jesus Christ to go and make disciples of all nations through church planting ministries in unreached areas or people groups. Working primarily through strategic partnerships, X-Pact’s vision is encapsulated in our motto – “Planting Able Churches Together with C(Ϫ)hrist”.

Mission of X-Pact Society
The mission of X-Pact is framed in our 3 P’s model which expounds on the key guiding principles of our X-Pact’s work:
 Plant Churches
X-Pact’s fundamental ministry is to establish communities of believers, and empower them to grow. The target is to plant able churches with potential for church multiplication. This can be pioneering work or with existing ministries which need help with its growth.
 People Groups
X-Pact’s primary outreach focus is in areas or amongst people groups which are largely unreached. The target is to seek out areas where there are no churches or people groups which are neglected/rejected by other Christian communities.
 Partnerships
X-Pact’s key strategy is to work in close co-operation with local and overseas Christian leaders and groups. The target is to establish partnership with like-minded Christians or groups with a heart and calling to plant churches amongst unreached people groups

name: Y.W.C.A. PRESCHOOL LTD.
search_text:
Y.W.C.A. PRESCHOOL LTD. The object for which the Company is established is to provide childcare services to pre-school children, including those from lower income families, at-risk and with special needs. In furtherance of this objective but not otherwise, and provided that nothing is done for commercial reasons or solely for profit, the Company may exercise the following powers:

(a) Adopt and operate, as a separate legal entity, all or any number of the existing childcare centres of the Young Women's Christian Association of Singapore (YWCA) approved by the Early Childhood Developemnt Agency (ECDA) under the Pre-school Anchor Operator Scheme.
(b) Set up and run additional pre-school centres including those allocated by ECDA, mainly for child care services, but may include infant care and kindergarten classes.
(c) Conduct or facilitate educational research into the behaviour of preschoolers.
(d) Produce age-appropriate teaching materials, curriculum and resources.
(e) Conduct training and facilitate the leadership and professional development of educators and carers.
(f) Provide opportunities for all pre-school children, including those from lower income families to participate in care and development.
(g) Provide and inclusive preschool programme taking into account the educational, rehabilitative, and social needs of all children with or without special needs and those at risk.
(h) Undertake any other business to promote and provide affordable, accessible and quality pre-school education and services.
(i) Support community services and youth development work. Same as Objectives Others; Preschool

search_text:
Yishun Evangelical Church 3.1 To bring the Christian message of Salvation from sin by faith in Jesus Christ to one and all.

3.12 To provide Social and Religious counselling in the Government Drug Rehabilitation Centers and Day Release Scheme and the prisons.

Imparting the importance of the Great Commission to believers and
Impacting the Indian Community in Singapore and beyond with the Gospel

(a) To practise and to disseminate MAHIKARI NO WAZA the belief that spirits exist and that a person may be freed from the harmful influence of malignant possessing spirits by the art of spiritual purification of a person’s own spirit and the spirits through an understanding of God’s spiritualism by holding liturgies and other activities.

(b) To guide any member professing any religion to witness the Creator God as the centre in his daily life, to lead a happy life and a life of prayer and to strengthen and improve his spiritual state.

(b) To study how to lead happy lives based on the three fundamental factors of health, wealth and harmony and in particular to study God’s natural healing powers and how to acquire perfect health, how to attain harmony by living in a surrounding of love and understanding in one’s family and among others without any conflicts.

(d) To integrate members by the cultivation of the love, truth and beauty of God by living faith in God.

(e) To experience union with God, men of flesh and spirits by the living faith.

(f) To witness the teachings of God and to be a light in one’s workplace, home, society and in international relations and

(g) To serve God by establishing Dojos or Teaching Places to fulfil the above objects.
Vision
Create a heavenly civilization with health, harmony and prosperity.

Mission
• Be Yokoshi and create a world of happiness for others.
• Construct SUZA in every land and home to welcome SU No Kami Sama.
• Shine divine lights together and manifest altruistic love.

name: Youth With A Mission Training Centre
search_text:
Youth With A Mission Training Centre Please refer to the Constitution. YWAM’s vision is to be an international discipling community committed to raise a harvest force for missions into Asia. YWAM seeks to serve the Christian churches and organisations in Singapore to train and equip missionaries.

YWAM is founded on biblical principles and we are affiliated to the University of the Nations (U of N) for the accreditation of the courses that we run. The U of N employs a modular system with 12 weeks of classroom coursework. Some courses require field internships or filed assignments in their area of study.

Founded on biblical principles, the University of the Nations (U of N) fulfils its commitment to Christ and His Great Commission by equipping men and women with spiritual, cultural, intellectual and professional training, and inspiring them to both continually grow in their personal relationship with God while also seeking to make Him known among all peoples in all nations. The U of N employs a modular system with 12 weeks of classroom coursework. Some courses require field internships or field assignments in their area of study.
Some of the distinct characteristics of the U of N include:
• A live-learn lifestyle
• Intensive modular courses & immediate field application
• A creative atmosphere in the classroom
• One-on-one contact between staff and students
• Cross-cultural and multicultural training
• “Multiplier for Missions”

The University YWAM seeks to broaden the scope of evangelistic endeavours by equipping students to serve in all spheres of society and in all nations, being both ‘salt’ and ‘light’ to the world (Matthew 5). To achieve this, we teach students to think biblically, discern spiritually and apply scriptural truth to every area of life. The U of N offers Associate’s degrees, Bachelor’s degrees, Graduate Diplomas and Master’s degrees from its seven faculties. These faculties are:
• Arts and Sports
• Christian Ministries
• Communication
• Counseling and Health Care
• Education
• Humanities and International Studies
• Science and Technology

name: ZERO WASTE SG LIMITED
search_text:
ZERO WASTE SG LIMITED The Company is a not-for-profit, non-governmental entity established and located in Singapore for charitable purposes to help Singapore eliminate the concept of waste, and accelerate the shift towards zero waste and the circular economy.

The objects for which the company is established shall be as follows:-
(1) To promote education and engagement on the 3Rs (Reduce, Reuse and Recycle) among individuals and households
(2) To increase waste minimisation and recycling among businesses and organisations
(3) To reduce specific waste such as food waste, single-use disposables, and other waste as the Board of Directors may agree from time to time. Vision: A nation where individuals, communities, businesses and organisations value and conserve resources, and embrace the circular economy.

Mission: Leading the drive towards zero waste in Singapore. Environment

traditional Chinese beliefs and customs and Taoism.
